bio_en:
Abu Abdullah Muhammad ibn Abdullah al-Kharashi al-Maliki (c. 1019-1101 AH / c. 1610-1690 CE) was an Egyptian Maliki jurist who served as the first Grand Imam of al-Azhar to be formally appointed from the Maliki legal school, serving as Sheikh al-Azhar from 1671 to 1686. Born in Kharas, a village in Egypt, he studied in Cairo and became one of the leading Maliki scholars of Egypt.

Al-Kharashi is best known for his monumental commentary on Khalil ibn Ishaq al-Jundi's al-Mukhtasar, known as Sharh al-Kharashi ala Mukhtasar Khalil. This large multi-volume work is one of the most comprehensive commentaries on al-Mukhtasar and became a standard reference in Maliki jurisprudence, particularly in Egypt and North Africa. His exposition of Khalil's concise legal text provided detailed explanations, juristic reasoning, and citation of earlier authorities.

He also authored Hashiyah ala Mukhtasar Khalil (marginalia to Khalil's Mukhtasar), further expanding on issues in the commentary. His juristic output reflects deep mastery of the Maliki legal tradition and the scholarly methodology of al-Azhar in the late 17th century.

Al-Kharashi served as Sheikh al-Azhar during a period when al-Azhar was becoming increasingly institutionalized as the leading center of Sunni scholarship. His tenure as the first Maliki Grand Imam and his comprehensive commentary on Khalil's Mukhtasar are his most enduring contributions. He passed away in Cairo in approximately 1101 AH.
bio_id:
Abu Abdullah Muhammad ibn Abdullah al-Kharashi al-Maliki (sekitar 1019-1101 H / sekitar 1610-1690 M) adalah seorang ahli fiqh Maliki dari Mesir yang menjabat sebagai Grand Imam al-Azhar pertama yang secara resmi diangkat dari mazhab Maliki, menjabat sebagai Syaikh al-Azhar dari tahun 1671 hingga 1686. Lahir di Kharas, sebuah desa di Mesir, ia menuntut ilmu di Kairo dan menjadi salah satu ulama Maliki terkemuka di Mesir.

Al-Kharashi paling dikenal melalui karyanya yang monumental berupa syarah atas kitab al-Mukhtasar karya Khalil ibn Ishaq al-Jundi, yang dikenal dengan nama Syarh al-Kharashi 'ala Mukhtashar Khalil. Karya besar yang terdiri dari beberapa jilid ini merupakan salah satu syarah paling komprehensif atas al-Mukhtasar dan menjadi referensi standar dalam fiqh Maliki, khususnya di Mesir dan Afrika Utara. Penjelasannya atas teks hukum yang ringkas karya Khalil menghadirkan uraian terperinci, penalaran fikih, dan kutipan dari para ulama terdahulu.

Ia juga menulis Hasyiyah 'ala Mukhtashar Khalil (catatan pinggir atas al-Mukhtasar karya Khalil), yang semakin memperluas pembahasan berbagai permasalahan dalam syarah tersebut. Karya-karya fikihnya mencerminkan penguasaan yang mendalam atas tradisi hukum Maliki serta metodologi keilmuan al-Azhar pada akhir abad ke-17.

Al-Kharashi menjabat sebagai Syaikh al-Azhar pada masa ketika al-Azhar semakin terstruktur sebagai pusat utama keilmuan Sunni. Jabatannya sebagai Grand Imam Maliki pertama dan syarahnya yang komprehensif atas al-Mukhtasar karya Khalil merupakan warisan ilmiahnya yang paling abadi. Ia wafat di Kairo sekitar tahun 1101 H.

name_full_en: Abu Isa al-Mahdi ibn Muhammad al-Wazzani
bio_short_en: Moroccan Maliki mufti and author of 'al-Mi'yar al-Jadid,' a massive collection of Maliki fatwas from North Africa.
bio_en:
Abu Isa Muhammad ibn al-Mahdi ibn al-Talib ibn al-Mustafa al-Wazzani (1266-1342 AH / 1849-1923 CE) was a Moroccan Maliki jurist and mufti who produced one of the most extensive collections of Maliki legal opinions in North African history. He served as the chief mufti (qadi al-qudat) of Fez and was one of the leading legal authorities in Morocco during the late Alawid period.

Al-Wazzani studied under major Moroccan scholars and became deeply grounded in the Maliki legal tradition. He served as a judge and mufti in Fez for many years, producing an enormous number of legal opinions addressing the practical questions of Moroccan society in the late 19th and early 20th centuries.

His most important work is al-Mi'yar al-Jadid al-Jami' al-Mu'rib an Fatawa Ulama' Ifriqiya wal-Andalus wal-Maghrib (The New Standard: A Comprehensive Collection of the Fatawa of the Scholars of North Africa, al-Andalus, and the Maghreb), which continues and updates the earlier compilation by al-Wansharisi (al-Mi'yar al-Mu'rib). This multi-volume work is one of the most comprehensive collections of Moroccan and North African Maliki legal opinions, invaluable for understanding the application of Maliki law in historical context.

He also authored al-Nawazil al-Jadidah al-Kubra (The Major New Unprecedented Legal Cases), another large collection of fatawa. Al-Wazzani passed away in 1342 AH. His fatawa collections are primary sources for both legal scholars and historians of Moroccan society in the 19th-20th centuries.
bio_id:
Abu Isa Muhammad ibn al-Mahdi ibn al-Talib ibn al-Mustafa al-Wazzani (1266-1342 H / 1849-1923 M) adalah seorang ahli fiqih dan mufti Maliki dari Maroko yang menghasilkan salah satu kumpulan pendapat hukum Maliki paling luas dalam sejarah Afrika Utara. Beliau menjabat sebagai mufti tertinggi (qadi al-qudat) kota Fez dan merupakan salah satu otoritas hukum terkemuka di Maroko pada periode akhir dinasti Alawiyah.

Al-Wazzani menimba ilmu dari ulama-ulama besar Maroko dan menjadi sangat kokoh dalam tradisi hukum Maliki. Beliau bertugas sebagai hakim dan mufti di Fez selama bertahun-tahun, menghasilkan sejumlah besar fatwa yang menjawab pertanyaan-pertanyaan praktis masyarakat Maroko pada akhir abad ke-19 dan awal abad ke-20.

Karya terbesarnya adalah al-Mi'yar al-Jadid al-Jami' al-Mu'rib an Fatawa Ulama' Ifriqiya wal-Andalus wal-Maghrib (Standar Baru: Kumpulan Komprehensif Fatwa Ulama Afrika Utara, al-Andalus, dan Maghreb), yang meneruskan dan memperbarui kompilasi sebelumnya oleh al-Wansharisi (al-Mi'yar al-Mu'rib). Karya berjilid-jilid ini merupakan salah satu kumpulan pendapat hukum Maliki Maroko dan Afrika Utara yang paling lengkap, sangat berharga untuk memahami penerapan hukum Maliki dalam konteks sejarah.

Beliau juga mengarang al-Nawazil al-Jadidah al-Kubra (Kasus-Kasus Hukum Baru yang Besar), sebuah kumpulan fatwa besar lainnya. Al-Wazzani wafat pada tahun 1342 H. Kumpulan fatwa beliau menjadi sumber primer bagi para ahli hukum maupun sejarawan masyarakat Maroko pada abad ke-19 hingga ke-20.

name_full_en: Abu al-Fadl Iyad ibn Musa ibn Iyad al-Yahsubi as-Sabti
title: Qadi
bio_short_en: Maliki judge of Ceuta and author of 'ash-Shifa bi Ta'rif Huquq al-Mustafa,' the most celebrated work on the rights and honor of the Prophet Muhammad.
bio_en:
Abu al-Fadl Iyad ibn Musa ibn Iyad al-Yahsubi al-Sabti (476-544 AH / 1083-1149 CE), commonly known as al-Qadi Iyad, was a Maliki jurist, hadith scholar, and judge from Ceuta (as-Sabta, in modern Morocco). He is one of the most revered scholars in the Maliki tradition and a celebrated figure across the broader Muslim world.

Al-Qadi Iyad studied in Ceuta, Cordoba, and other Andalusian cities under leading Maliki scholars of his time, becoming the foremost religious authority in the western Islamic world. He served as a judge in Ceuta and later in Granada. He was known for his piety, his scholarly rigor, and his eloquence.

His most celebrated work is ash-Shifa' bi-Ta'rif Huquq al-Mustafa (The Cure by the Recognition of the Rights of the Chosen One), a comprehensive work on the status, rights, and attributes of the Prophet Muhammad ﷺ. This work, universally known simply as ash-Shifa', became one of the most beloved books in Islamic literature, read across all legal schools and regions of the Muslim world. Its detailed documentation of the Prophet's ﷺ attributes and the obligations Muslims have toward him made it a standard reference in this genre.

He also authored Tartib al-Madarik wa Taqrib al-Masalik li-Ma'rifat A'lam Madhhab Malik (a comprehensive biographical dictionary of Maliki scholars), Mashariq al-Anwar (on hadith terminology), Ikmal al-Mu'allim bi-Fawa'id Muslim (a commentary on Sahih Muslim), and other works.

Al-Qadi Iyad passed away in Marrakesh in 544 AH. His ash-Shifa' remains among the most widely read and beloved books in the Islamic world.
bio_id:
Abu al-Fadl Iyad ibn Musa ibn Iyad al-Yahsubi al-Sabti (476-544 H / 1083-1149 M), yang dikenal luas sebagai al-Qadi Iyad, adalah seorang ahli fikih Maliki, ulama hadits, dan hakim dari Ceuta (as-Sabta, di Maroko modern). Beliau merupakan salah satu ulama paling dihormati dalam tradisi Maliki dan tokoh yang diagungkan di seluruh dunia Islam.

Al-Qadi Iyad menuntut ilmu di Ceuta, Cordoba, dan berbagai kota Andalusia lainnya di bawah bimbingan ulama-ulama Maliki terkemuka pada zamannya, hingga menjadi otoritas keagamaan tertinggi di dunia Islam bagian barat. Beliau menjabat sebagai hakim di Ceuta, kemudian di Granada. Beliau dikenal karena ketakwaannya, ketelitian ilmiahnya, dan kefasihan bahasanya.

Karya beliau yang paling masyhur adalah ash-Shifa' bi-Ta'rif Huquq al-Mustafa (Penyembuh melalui Pengenalan Hak-Hak al-Mustafa), sebuah karya komprehensif mengenai kedudukan, hak-hak, dan sifat-sifat Nabi Muhammad ﷺ. Kitab ini, yang secara universal dikenal cukup dengan nama ash-Shifa', menjadi salah satu buku paling dicintai dalam khazanah sastra Islam, dibaca di seluruh mazhab fikih dan berbagai penjuru dunia Muslim. Dokumentasinya yang terperinci mengenai sifat-sifat Nabi ﷺ dan kewajiban-kewajiban umat Muslim terhadap beliau menjadikannya rujukan utama dalam genre ini.

Beliau juga mengarang Tartib al-Madarik wa Taqrib al-Masalik li-Ma'rifat A'lam Madhhab Malik (kamus biografi komprehensif para ulama Maliki), Mashariq al-Anwar (mengenai terminologi hadits), Ikmal al-Mu'allim bi-Fawa'id Muslim (syarah atas Shahih Muslim), dan karya-karya lainnya.

Al-Qadi Iyad wafat di Marrakesh pada tahun 544 H. Kitab ash-Shifa' beliau tetap menjadi salah satu buku yang paling banyak dibaca dan paling dicintai di dunia Islam hingga hari ini.

name_full_en: Abu Ishaq Ibrahim ibn Ali ibn Yusuf ash-Shirazi
title: Imam
bio_short_en: Shafi'i jurist and first teacher at the Nizamiyyah Madrasa in Baghdad, author of 'al-Muhadhdhab' and 'at-Tanbih' in Shafi'i fiqh.
bio_en:
Abu Ishaq Ibrahim ibn Ali ibn Yusuf ash-Shirazi al-Firuzabadi (393-476 AH / 1003-1083 CE) was one of the most important Shafi'i jurists of the classical period. Born in Firuzabad, Fars (Iran), he studied jurisprudence in Shiraz before moving to Baghdad, where he became the leading Shafi'i scholar of the city and the pre-eminent teacher at the Nizamiyyah academy founded by the Seljuk vizier Nizam al-Mulk in 459 AH.

Ash-Shirazi's teaching at the Baghdad Nizamiyyah was one of the most influential in the history of Islamic education. He attracted students from across the Muslim world and trained many of the leading scholars of the subsequent generation. Among his notable students was al-Ghazali, who studied under him at the Nizamiyyah before being sent to study under Imam al-Haramayn al-Juwayni in Nishapur.

His most important works in Shafi'i jurisprudence are al-Muhadhdhab (The Refined), a comprehensive Shafi'i legal text that was later the subject of an important commentary by an-Nawawi (al-Majmu'); and at-Tanbih (The Reminder), a concise Shafi'i legal primer that became one of the standard teaching texts in the madhhab. He also authored works on legal methodology (usul al-fiqh) and biographical works on Shafi'i scholars.

Ash-Shirazi was known for his piety, his accessibility to students, and his mastery of jurisprudential debate. He passed away in Baghdad in 476 AH, widely mourned. His al-Muhadhdhab remains one of the most important reference texts in the Shafi'i tradition.
bio_id:
Abu Ishaq Ibrahim ibn Ali ibn Yusuf ash-Shirazi al-Firuzabadi (393–476 H/1003–1083 M) adalah one dari most important Syafi'i ahli fiqihs dari klasik periode. Lahir di Firuzabad, Fars (Iran), ia belajar fikih di Shiraz sebelum moving ke Bagdad, di mana ia menjadi leading Syafi'i ulama dari city dan pre-terkemuka guru di Nizamiyyah academy founded oleh Seljuk vizier Nizam al-Mulk di 459 H.

Ash-Shirazi's teaching di Bagdad Nizamiyyah adalah salah satu yang paling berpengaruh di history dari pendidikan Islam. He attracted murids dari di seluruh dunia Islam dan trained many dari leading para ulama dari subsequent generasi. Among his terkemuka murids adalah al-Ghazali, yang belajar kepada him di Nizamiyyah sebelum being sent ke study under Imam al-Haramayn al-Juwayni di Nishapur.

His most important works di Syafi'i fikih adalah al-Muhadhdhab (The Refined), comprehensive Syafi'i legal text yang adalah later subject dari important commentary oleh -Nawawi (al-Majmu'); dan di-Tanbih (The Reminder), concise Syafi'i legal primer yang became one dari standard teaching texts di mazhab. He juga mengarang works pada legal metodologi (ushul fiqih) dan biographical works pada Syafi'i para ulama.

Ash-Shirazi adalah dikenal karena his kesalehan, his accessibility ke murids, dan his mastery dari jurisprudential debate. He wafat di Bagdad di 476 H, widely mourned. His al-Muhadhdhab remains one dari most important reference texts di tradisi Syafi'i.

name_full_en: Abu al-Qasim Abd al-Karim ibn Muhammad ar-Rafi'i al-Qazwini
title: Imam
bio_short_en: Major Shafi'i jurist from Qazwin whose 'al-Aziz' served as one of the two foundational texts that an-Nawawi later refined in his works.
bio_en:
Abu al-Qasim Abd al-Karim ibn Muhammad ar-Rafi'i al-Qazwini (555-623 AH / 1160-1226 CE) was a major Shafi'i jurist from Qazwin (in modern Iran). Along with an-Nawawi, he is considered one of the two foundational pillars of the later Shafi'i jurisprudential tradition.

Ar-Rafi'i studied in Qazwin and traveled to study under major scholars of his era, including Imam al-Baghawi. He became the foremost Shafi'i authority of his time and spent most of his scholarly career in Qazwin.

His main work, al-Aziz fi Sharh al-Wajiz (also known as ash-Sharh al-Kabir or Fat'h al-Aziz), is a comprehensive multi-volume commentary on al-Ghazali's al-Wajiz (a concise Shafi'i legal text). This massive work systematically presents and evaluates the opinions of Shafi'i scholars on every legal question, documenting the development of the madhhab up to his time. It became one of the two foundational reference texts for determining the relied-upon (mu'tamad) positions of the Shafi'i school — the other being an-Nawawi's al-Majmu'.

An-Nawawi based much of his own work on ar-Rafi'i's scholarship and frequently references and evaluates his views. The pair ar-Rafi'i and an-Nawawi are collectively called the Two Shaykhs (ash-Shaykhani) in the Shafi'i school. When their views agree, their joint opinion represents the strongest position. He also authored Fath al-Aziz and Muharrar, another important Shafi'i legal text.

Ar-Rafi'i passed away in Qazwin in 623 AH. His scholarship defined the parameters of the late classical Shafi'i tradition and established the standards for determining the madhhab's authoritative positions.
bio_id:
Abu al-Qasim Abd al-Karim ibn Muhammad ar-Rafi'i al-Qazwini (555–623 H/1160–1226 M) adalah seorang tokoh utama Syafi'i ahli fiqih dari Qazwin (di modern Iran). Along dengan -Nawawi, ia adalah dianggap one dari two foundational pillars dari later Syafi'i jurisprudential tradisi.

Ar-Rafi'i studied di Qazwin dan melakukan perjalanan ke study under major para ulama dari eranya, including Imam al-Baghawi. Ia menjadi foremost Syafi'i otoritas dari masanya dan spent most dari his ulamaly career di Qazwin.

His main work, al-Aziz fi Sharh al-Wajiz (juga dikenal sebagai ash-Sharh al-Kabir atau Fat'h al-Aziz), adalah seorang comprehensive multi-jilid commentary pada al-Ghazali's al-Wajiz ( concise Syafi'i legal text). This massive work systematically presents dan evaluates pendapats dari Syafi'i para ulama pada every legal question, documenting development dari mazhab up ke masanya. It became one dari two foundational reference texts untuk determining relied-upon (mu'tamad) positions dari mazhab Syafi'i — other being -Nawawi's al-Majmu'.

An-Nawawi based much dari his own work pada ar-Rafi'i's para ulamahip dan frequently references dan evaluates pandangan-pandangannya. The pair ar-Rafi'i dan -Nawawi adalah collectively called Two Syaikhs (ash-Syaikhani) di mazhab Syafi'i. When their views agree, their joint pendapat represents strongest position. He juga mengarang Fath al-Aziz dan Muharrar, another important Syafi'i legal text.

Ar-Rafi'i wafat di Qazwin di 623 H. His para ulamahip defined parameters dari late klasik tradisi Syafi'i dan established standards untuk determining mazhab's authoritative positions.

name_full_en: Shihab ad-Din Ahmad ibn al-Husayn Abu Shuja' al-Isfahani
bio_short_en: Shafi'i jurist from Isfahan, author of 'al-Ghayah wat-Taqrib,' the most widely studied introductory Shafi'i fiqh manual.
bio_en:
Shihab ad-Din Ahmad ibn al-Husayn Abu Shuja' al-Isfahani (434-500 AH / 1042-1106 CE) was a Shafi'i jurist from Isfahan known for authoring one of the most beloved and widely taught introductory texts in Islamic legal education. He served as a judge in Basra and was known for his learning and piety.

Abu Shuja' is best known for his al-Ghayah wat-Taqrib (The Limit and Approximation), also known as Matn Abi Shuja', a concise primer of Shafi'i jurisprudence covering the essential topics of worship, personal and commercial law, family law, and related subjects. This text is written with remarkable precision and economy, summarizing the basic Shafi'i positions on each topic in minimal words.

Al-Ghayah wat-Taqrib became one of the most memorized and studied legal texts in Islamic history. It has been the subject of numerous commentaries, the most widely used of which is Fath al-Qarib al-Mujib by Ibn Qasim al-Ghazzi and Hashiyat al-Bajuri (by al-Bajuri). These commentaries made Abu Shuja's text the basis for a rich pedagogical tradition. The text continues to be memorized and taught in Islamic schools across the world.

The reason for its enduring popularity is its clarity, conciseness, and comprehensiveness as an introduction to the fundamentals of Shafi'i fiqh. Beginning students of Islamic law in the Shafi'i tradition typically start with this text before moving on to longer works like at-Tanbih and al-Minhaj.

Abu Shuja' passed away in approximately 500 AH. His al-Ghayah wat-Taqrib is his enduring legacy — one of the most influential introductory legal texts in Islamic history.
bio_id:
Shihab ad-Din Ahmad ibn al-Husayn Abu Shuja' al-Isfahani (434–500 H/1042–1106 M) adalah seorang Syafi'i ahli fiqih dari Isfahan dikenal karena authoring one dari most beloved dan widely taught introductory texts di hukum Islam pendidikan. Ia berkhidmat sebagai as hakim di Bashrah dan adalah dikenal karena his learning dan kesalehan.

Abu Shuja' adalah best dikenal karena his al-Ghayah wat-Taqrib (The Limit dan Approximation), juga dikenal sebagai Matn Abi Shuja', concise primer dari Syafi'i fikih covering essential topics dari ibadah, personal dan commercial law, family law, dan related subjects. This text adalah written dengan remarkable precision dan economy, summarizing basic Syafi'i positions pada each topic di minimal words.

Al-Ghayah wat-Taqrib became one dari most memorized dan studied legal texts di sejarah Islam. It telah menjadi subject dari numerous commentaries, most widely used dari yang adalah Fath al-Qarib al-Mujib oleh Ibn Qasim al-Ghazzi dan Hashiyat al-Bajuri (oleh al-Bajuri). These commentaries made Abu Shuja's text basis untuk rich pedagogical tradisi. The text continues ke be memorized dan taught di Islamic schools di seluruh dunia.

The reason untuk its enduring popularity adalah its clarity, conciseness, dan comprehensiveness as introduction ke fundamentals dari Syafi'i fikih. Beginning murids dari hukum Islam di tradisi Syafi'i typically start dengan this text sebelum moving pada ke longer works like di-Tanbih dan al-Minhaj.

Abu Shuja' wafat di sekitar 500 H. His al-Ghayah wat-Taqrib adalah his enduring warisan — salah satu yang paling berpengaruh introductory legal texts di sejarah Islam.

name_full_en: Abdullah ibn Alawi ibn Muhammad al-Haddad
title: Imam
bio_short_en: Yemeni Ba'Alawi scholar and Sufi master from Tarim, author of influential spiritual works including 'Risalat al-Mu'awanah' and 'an-Nasa'ih ad-Diniyyah.'
bio_en:
Abdullah ibn Alawi ibn Muhammad ibn Ahmad al-Haddad (1044-1132 AH / 1634-1720 CE) was a Ba'Alawi scholar, Sufi master, and one of the greatest spiritual figures of Hadhramaut, Yemen. Born in Tarim, Hadhramaut, into the distinguished Alawide sayyid lineage (descendants of the Prophet ﷺ through Imam al-Husayn), he lost his eyesight as a child due to illness but compensated with an extraordinary inner development.

Al-Haddad studied under the leading scholars and Sufi masters of Tarim and became a recognized master of the Shadhili-Alawi Sufi path — the distinctive spiritual tradition of Hadhramaut that emphasizes love of the Prophet ﷺ, continuous remembrance (dhikr), and adherence to the Sharia. He became one of the most important figures in the history of this tradition.

His works are among the most widely read in Islamic spirituality. His Nasaih ad-Diniyyah wal-Wasaya al-Imaniyyah (Religious Counsels and Admonitions of Faith) is a collection of spiritual counsels. His al-Hikam (Spiritual Aphorisms) are profound meditations on the spiritual life. His Wird al-Latif (The Gentle Litany) is one of the most widely recited wird (litanies of remembrance) in the Muslim world to this day.

Al-Haddad's teaching spread from Tarim across the Indian Ocean world — to East Africa, India, and Southeast Asia — through the extensive Hadrami diaspora network. He is known as the Qutb (spiritual pole) of his era in the Ba'Alawi tradition. He passed away in Tarim in 1132 AH and is buried there. His spiritual writings and his wird continue to be recited by millions across the Muslim world.
bio_id:
Abdullah ibn Alawi ibn Muhammad ibn Ahmad al-Haddad (1044–1132 H/1634–1720 M) adalah seorang Ba'Alawi ulama, mursyid Sufi, dan one dari greatest spiritual figures dari Hadhramaut, Yaman. Lahir di Tarim, Hadhramaut, ke dalam terpandang Alawide sayyid lineage (descendants dari Nabi ﷺ melalui Imam al-Husayn), he lost his eyesight as child due ke illness but compensated dengan extraordinary inner development.

Al-Haddad belajar kepada leading para ulama dan mursyid Sufis dari Tarim dan became recognized master dari Shadhili-Alawi jalan Sufi — distinctive spiritual tradisi dari Hadhramaut yang emphasizes love dari Nabi ﷺ, continuous remembrance (dhikr), dan adherence ke Sharia. Ia menjadi one dari most important figures di history dari this tradisi.

Karya-karyanya adalah among most widely read di Islamic spirituality. His Nasaih ad-Diniyyah wal-Wasaya al-Imaniyyah (Religious Counsels dan Admonitions dari Faith) adalah seorang collection dari spiritual counsels. His al-Hikam (Spiritual Aphorisms) adalah profound meditations pada spiritual life. His Wird al-Latif (The Gentle Litany) adalah one dari most widely recited wird (litanies dari remembrance) di dunia Islam ke this day.

Al-Haddad's teaching spread dari Tarim di seluruh Indian Ocean dunia — ke Afrika Timur, India, dan Asia Tenggara — melalui extensive Hadrami diaspora network. Ia adalah dikenal sebagai Qutb (spiritual pole) dari eranya di Ba'Alawi tradisi. He wafat di Tarim di 1132 H dan adalah buried there. His spiritual writings dan his wird continue ke be recited oleh millions di seluruh dunia Islam.

name_full_en: Abu Abd ar-Rahman Habib ibn Umar ibn Ahmad Mashur al-Ba'Alawi
bio_short_en: Hadhrami Ba'Alawi scholar who authored 'Simthut Thahabin,' a devotional poem for the Prophet Muhammad widely chanted in Southeast Asia.
bio_en:
Habib Ali ibn Muhammad ibn Husayn al-Habshi (1259-1333 AH / 1843-1915 CE) was a Hadrami Ba'Alawi scholar who settled in the Indonesian archipelago and became one of the most beloved religious figures among Southeast Asian Muslims. Born in Tarim, Hadhramaut, Yemen, into the distinguished Alawide lineage, he traveled to Java in his youth as part of the wave of Hadrami scholar-merchants who shaped Islam in Southeast Asia.

He eventually settled in Solo (Surakarta), Central Java, where he spent most of his life teaching, guiding disciples, and providing spiritual leadership to the Muslim community. He was a master of the Ba'Alawi Sufi tradition — the Shadhili-Alawi path of Hadhramaut — and attracted followers from across the Malay-Indonesian archipelago.

Al-Habshi is best known for composing Simthut Thahab fil-Alfiyat Mawlid Sayyid al-Arab (The Necklace of Gold in the Thousand Verses on the Mawlid of the Master of the Arabs), commonly known as Simthut Thahab — a praise poem on the Prophet ﷺ in the tradition of mawlid literature. This poem is recited at celebrations marking the Prophet's birthday ﷺ and at other religious gatherings across Southeast Asia, particularly in Indonesia, Malaysia, and surrounding regions. Its recitation became a central part of the religious and communal life of Hadrami-influenced Muslim communities.

Beyond his literary contribution, his personal spiritual presence, his teaching, and his guidance of a generation of students and disciples left a deep imprint on Javanese and broader Indonesian Islamic practice. He passed away in Solo in 1915 and is buried there. His tomb is visited by devotees, and his memory is revered among the Ba'Alawi tradition in Southeast Asia.
bio_id:
Habib Ali ibn Muhammad ibn Husayn al-Habshi (1259–1333 H/1843–1915 M) adalah seorang Hadrami Ba'Alawi ulama yang settled di Indonesian archipelago dan became one dari most beloved religious figures among Asia Tenggaran kaum Muslimin. Lahir di Tarim, Hadhramaut, Yaman, ke dalam terpandang Alawide lineage, ia melakukan perjalanan ke Jawa di masa mudanya as part dari wave dari Hadrami ulama-merchants yang shaped Islam di Asia Tenggara.

He akhirnya settled di Solo (Surakarta), Central Jawa, di mana ia menghabiskan most dari kehidupannya teaching, guiding murids, dan providing spiritual leadership ke Muslim komunitas. Ia adalah master dari Ba'Alawi tradisi Sufi — Shadhili-Alawi path dari Hadhramaut — dan attracted pengikuts dari di seluruh Malay-Indonesian archipelago.

Al-Habshi adalah best dikenal karena composing Simthut Thahab fil-Alfiyat Mawlid Sayyid al-Arab (The Necklace dari Gold di Thousand Verses pada Mawlid dari Master dari Arabs), commonly dikenal sebagai Simthut Thahab — praise poem pada Nabi ﷺ di tradisi dari mawlid literature. This poem adalah recited di celebrations marking Nabi ﷺ's birthday ﷺ dan di other religious gatherings di seluruh Asia Tenggara, khususnya di Indonesia, Malaysia, dan surrounding regions. Its recitation became central part dari religious dan communal life dari Hadrami-pengaruhd Muslim komunitas-komunitas.

Beyond his literary contribution, his personal spiritual presence, his teaching, dan his guidance dari generasi dari murids dan murids left deep imprint pada Jawanese dan broader Indonesian Islamic practice. He wafat di Solo di 1915 dan adalah buried there. His tomb adalah visited oleh devotees, dan his memory adalah revered among Ba'Alawi tradisi di Asia Tenggara.

name_full_en: Abu al-Wafa Ali ibn Aqil ibn Muhammad al-Baghdadi
title: Imam
bio_short_en: Hanbali jurist and theologian of Baghdad, author of 'al-Funun,' reportedly the largest book ever written in Arabic.
bio_en:
Abu al-Wafa Ali ibn Aqil ibn Muhammad al-Baghdadi (431-513 AH / 1040-1119 CE) was one of the most brilliant and original Hanbali scholars of Baghdad. Born in Baghdad, he studied under the leading Hanbali scholars of the city including al-Sharif Abu Ja'far and Ibn Qudwah. He eventually became the head of the Hanbali school in Baghdad.

Ibn Aqil's most remarkable scholarly achievement was al-Funun (also called Kitab al-Funun), a massive encyclopedic work said to have run to over 800 volumes in his original manuscript, making it one of the largest single-authored works in Islamic history. It was a vast compilation of reflections, observations, questions, and answers across virtually every field of knowledge — theology, jurisprudence, philosophy, ethics, literature, and more. Much of it is now lost.

He was known for his bold intellectual curiosity and his willingness to engage with the philosophical currents of his time, which at points brought him into conflict with traditionalists, particularly in the context of the Hanbali establishment. He reportedly associated with Mu'tazili intellectuals in his youth and was required to publicly repent. His later works, however, are firmly within the Hanbali tradition.

Ibn Aqil authored works on Hanbali jurisprudence and legal methodology, and was a significant figure in the intellectual life of Baghdad during the Seljuk period. He passed away in Baghdad in 513 AH. He is remembered as one of the most intellectually expansive scholars the Hanbali tradition produced.
bio_id:
Abu al-Wafa Ali ibn Aqil ibn Muhammad al-Bagdadi (431–513 H/1040–1119 M) adalah one dari most cemerlang dan original Hanbali para ulama dari Bagdad. Lahir di Bagdad, ia belajar under leading Hanbali para ulama dari city including al-Sharif Abu Ja'far dan Ibn Qudwah. He akhirnya became head dari mazhab Hanbali di Bagdad.

Ibn Aqil's most remarkable ulamaly achievement adalah al-Funun (juga called Kitab al-Funun), massive ensiklopedis work said ke memiliki run ke over 800 jilids di his original manuskrip, making it one dari largest single-mengarang works di sejarah Islam. It adalah seorang vast compilation dari reflections, observations, questions, dan answers di seluruh virtually every field dari ilmu pengetahuan — teologi, fikih, filsafat, ethics, literature, dan more. Much dari it adalah now lost.

Ia adalah dikenal karena his bold intellectual curiosity dan his willingness ke engage dengan philosophical currents dari masanya, yang di points brought him ke dalam conflict dengan tradisialists, khususnya di context dari Hanbali establishment. He reportedly associated dengan Mu'tazilahh intellectuals di masa mudanya dan adalah required ke publicly repent. His later works, namun, adalah firmly di dalam tradisi Hanbali.

Ibn Aqil mengarang works pada Hanbali fikih dan legal metodologi, dan adalah seorang tokoh penting figure di intellectual life dari Bagdad selama Seljuk periode. He wafat di Bagdad di 513 H. Ia adalah dikenang sebagai one dari most intellectually expansive para ulama tradisi Hanbali produced.

name_full_en: Muhammad ibn al-Husayn Abu Ya'la al-Farra' al-Baghdadi
title: Qadi
bio_short_en: Chief Hanbali judge of Baghdad and systematizer of Hanbali theology, author of 'al-Ahkam as-Sultaniyyah' and 'al-Mu'tamad fi Usul ad-Din.'
bio_en:
Muhammad ibn al-Husayn ibn Muhammad Abu Ya'la al-Farra' al-Hanbali (380-458 AH / 990-1066 CE) was the chief Hanbali judge of Baghdad and one of the most important systematizers of Hanbali jurisprudence and theology. He studied under major Hanbali scholars of Baghdad and became the foremost Hanbali authority of his time, serving as the qadi al-qudat (chief judge) for the Hanbali community.

Abu Ya'la's most important contribution to Hanbali jurisprudence is his Kitab al-Mu'tamad fi Usul ad-Din (The Relied-Upon in the Foundations of Religion), a systematic treatment of Hanbali theology (aqeedah) in the mode of usul ad-din, engaging with the theological questions of divine attributes, prophethood, and related issues from a Hanbali perspective. He also authored al-Ahkam as-Sultaniyyah (The Ordinances of Government), a major work on Islamic political theory and constitutional law from the Hanbali perspective — closely parallel to and contemporary with the famous work of the same name by al-Mawardi from the Shafi'i school.

He also wrote Kitab al-Masail al-Fiqhiyyah, an extensive collection of Hanbali jurisprudential questions and answers; and works on hadith. His approach to Hanbali theology, particularly on the divine attributes, helped define the Hanbali theological tradition in the 5th century AH.

Abu Ya'la passed away in Baghdad in 458 AH. His al-Ahkam as-Sultaniyyah and his theological works are important contributions to the Hanbali tradition and to the history of Islamic political thought.
bio_id:
Muhammad ibn al-Husayn ibn Muhammad Abu Ya'la al-Farra' al-Hanbali (380–458 H/990–1066 M) adalah ketua Hanbali hakim dari Bagdad dan one dari most important systematizers dari Hanbali fikih dan teologi. Ia belajar under major Hanbali para ulama dari Bagdad dan became foremost Hanbali otoritas dari masanya, serving as qadhi al-qudat (hakim agung) untuk Hanbali komunitas.

Abu Ya'la's most important contribution ke Hanbali fikih adalah his Kitab al-Mu'tamad fi Usul ad-Din (The Relied-Upon di Foundations dari Religion), systematic treatment dari Hanbali teologi (aqidah) di mode dari usul ad-din, engaging dengan theological questions dari divine attributes, prophethood, dan related issues dari Hanbali perspective. He juga mengarang al-Ahkam as-Sultaniyyah (The Ordinances dari Government), major work pada Islamic political theory dan constitutional law dari Hanbali perspective — closely parallel ke dan kontemporer dengan famous work dari same name oleh al-Mawardi dari mazhab Syafi'i.

He juga wrote Kitab al-Masail al-Fiqhiyyah, extensive collection dari Hanbali jurisprudential questions dan answers; dan works pada hadith. His pendekatan ke Hanbali teologi, khususnya pada divine attributes, helped define Hanbali theological tradisi di abad ke-5 H.

Abu Ya'la wafat di Bagdad di 458 H. His al-Ahkam as-Sultaniyyah dan his theological works adalah important contributions ke tradisi Hanbali dan ke history dari Islamic political thought.

name_full_en: Shams ad-Din Abu Abdullah Muhammad ibn Muflih al-Maqdisi
title: Imam
bio_short_en: Leading student of Ibn Taymiyyah and author of 'al-Furu' and 'al-Adab ash-Shar'iyyah,' major references in Hanbali fiqh and Islamic ethics.
bio_en:
Shams ad-Din Abu Abdullah Muhammad ibn Muflih ibn Muhammad al-Maqdisi al-Hanbali (712-763 AH / 1312-1362 CE) was one of the foremost Hanbali jurists of the 14th century and one of the most distinguished students of Ibn Taymiyyah. Born in the Maqdisi region near Jerusalem and raised in Damascus, he studied under Ibn Taymiyyah, who reportedly said of him that there was no one more knowledgeable of his madhhab under heaven than Ibn Muflih.

Ibn Muflih's magnum opus is al-Furu' (The Branches), a massive encyclopedic work of Hanbali jurisprudence that presents the questions of the madhhab systematically and comprehensively, documenting the opinions of major Hanbali scholars and the evidential basis for their positions. Al-Furu' became one of the most important reference works in the Hanbali tradition and remains a standard citation in Hanbali legal scholarship.

He also authored al-Adab ash-Shar'iyyah (Canonical Conduct), a comprehensive guide to Islamic ethical and social conduct grounded in the Hanbali tradition, which combines legal rulings with ethical guidance and remains a valued reference. His Usul al-Fiqh (Legal Methodology) is another important contribution.

Ibn Muflih was known for his meticulous scholarship and his deep grounding in both the textual and the legal dimensions of the Hanbali tradition. He passed away in Damascus in 763 AH. Al-Furu' and al-Adab ash-Shar'iyyah remain indispensable references in the Hanbali legal tradition.
bio_id:
Shams ad-Din Abu Abdullah Muhammad ibn Muflih ibn Muhammad al-Maqdisi al-Hanbali (712–763 H/1312–1362 M) adalah salah satu yang paling utama Hanbali ahli fiqihs dari abad ke-14 dan one dari most terpandang murids dari Ibn Taymiyyah. Lahir di Maqdisi region near Yerusalem dan raised di Damaskus, ia belajar under Ibn Taymiyyah, yang reportedly said dari him yang there adalah tidak one more ilmu pengetahuanable dari his mazhab under heaven than Ibn Muflih.

Ibn Muflih's karya agung adalah al-Furu' (The Branches), massive ensiklopedis work dari Hanbali fikih yang presents questions dari mazhab systematically dan comprehensively, documenting pendapats dari major Hanbali para ulama dan evidential basis untuk their positions. Al-Furu' became one dari most important reference works di tradisi Hanbali dan remains standard citation di Hanbali legal para ulamahip.

He juga mengarang al-Adab ash-Shar'iyyah (Canonical Conduct), comprehensive guide ke Islamic ethical dan social conduct grounded di tradisi Hanbali, yang combines legal hukum-hukum dengan ethical guidance dan remains valued reference. His Usul al-Fiqh (Legal Methodology) adalah another important contribution.

Ibn Muflih adalah dikenal karena his meticulous para ulamahip dan his deep grounding di both textual dan legal dimensions dari tradisi Hanbali. He wafat di Damaskus di 763 H. Al-Furu' dan al-Adab ash-Shar'iyyah remain indispensable references di Hanbali legal tradisi.

name_full_en: Mansur ibn Yunus ibn Salah ad-Din al-Buhuti
title: Sheikh
bio_short_en: Egyptian Hanbali jurist and author of 'Kashshaf al-Qina' and 'Sharh Muntaha al-Iradat,' the two definitive references of late Hanbali jurisprudence.
bio_en:
Mansur ibn Yunus ibn Salah ad-Din al-Buhuti (c. 1000-1051 AH / c. 1591-1641 CE) was an Egyptian Hanbali jurist whose works became the definitive references in the Hanbali legal school. Born in Buhut, Egypt, he studied in Cairo and became the leading Hanbali authority of the Ottoman period, his works gaining rapid acceptance as the most authoritative expressions of the madhhab.

Al-Buhuti's two most important works are Kashshaf al-Qina' an Matn al-Iqna' (Removing the Veil from the Text of al-Iqna') and ar-Rawd al-Murbi' Sharh Zad al-Mustaqni' (The Fertile Garden: Commentary on Zad al-Mustaqni'). Kashshaf al-Qina' is a multi-volume comprehensive commentary on al-Iqna' by al-Hajjawi that became the most authoritative reference work in Hanbali jurisprudence. Ar-Rawd al-Murbi' is a shorter commentary on the equally important primer Zad al-Mustaqni'. Both works are still used as primary references in Hanbali legal education today.

He also authored Sharh Muntaha al-Iradat (Commentary on Muntaha al-Iradat), another comprehensive reference in Hanbali law. His trilogy of major Hanbali reference works effectively defined the late classical Hanbali tradition and represent the synthesis of Hanbali scholarship in the Ottoman period.

Al-Buhuti passed away in Cairo in approximately 1051 AH. The Kashshaf al-Qina' in particular remains the single most cited reference work in contemporary Hanbali legal scholarship and is essential for any serious study of Hanbali fiqh.
bio_id:
Mansur ibn Yunus ibn Salah ad-Din al-Buhuti (c. 1000–1051 H / c. 1591–1641 M) adalah seorang Mesirian Hanbali ahli fiqih whose works became definitive references di Hanbali legal school. Lahir di Buhut, Mesir, ia belajar di Kairo dan became leading Hanbali otoritas dari periode Ottoman, karya-karyanya gaining rapid acceptance as most authoritative expressions dari mazhab.

Al-Buhuti's two most important works adalah Kashshaf al-Qina' Matn al-Iqna' (Removing Veil dari Text dari al-Iqna') dan ar-Rawd al-Murbi' Sharh Zad al-Mustaqni' (The Fertile Garden: Commentary pada Zad al-Mustaqni'). Kashshaf al-Qina' adalah seorang multi-jilid comprehensive commentary pada al-Iqna' oleh al-hajiawi yang became most authoritative reference work di Hanbali fikih. Ar-Rawd al-Murbi' adalah seorang shorter commentary pada equally important primer Zad al-Mustaqni'. Both works adalah still used as primary references di Hanbali legal pendidikan today.

He juga mengarang Sharh Muntaha al-Iradat (Commentary pada Muntaha al-Iradat), another comprehensive reference di Hanbali law. His trilogy dari major Hanbali reference works effectively defined late klasik tradisi Hanbali dan represent synthesis dari Hanbali para ulamahip di periode Ottoman.

Al-Buhuti wafat di Kairo di sekitar 1051 H. The Kashshaf al-Qina' khususnya remains single most cited reference work di kontemporer Hanbali legal para ulamahip dan adalah essential untuk any serious study dari Hanbali fikih.

name_full_en: Abu al-Hasan Muhammad Hayat ibn Ibrahim as-Sindhi
title: Sheikh
bio_short_en: Sindhi hadith scholar based in Medina who taught Shah Waliullah Dehlawi and Muhammad ibn Abdul-Wahhab, influencing major reform movements.
bio_en:
Abu al-Hasan Muhammad Hayat ibn Ibrahim as-Sindhi al-Madani (c. 1102-1163 AH / c. 1690-1750 CE) was a hadith scholar originally from Sindh (in modern Pakistan) who settled in Medina and became one of the most influential teachers of the 18th century. Born in Sindh, he traveled to Medina to pursue advanced studies in the hadith sciences and remained there.

As-Sindhi was deeply grounded in the hadith tradition and was known as an outstanding teacher who combined technical mastery of the sciences of narration with a commitment to following the evidence of the Sunnah. He was influenced by the scholarly tradition of Shah Waliullah's family and in turn influenced others toward a hadith-oriented approach.

His significance lies particularly in the remarkable students he trained. Among his most notable students were Shah Waliullah ad-Dehlawi — the great Indian scholar and reformer who became one of the most important Islamic thinkers of the century — and Muhammad ibn Abd al-Wahhab, the reformer of the Arabian Peninsula whose teachings gave rise to the Wahhabi/Salafi movement. The encounter of both these figures with as-Sindhi's emphasis on direct engagement with hadith and ijtihad had profound implications for Islamic reform movements across the Muslim world.

As-Sindhi also wrote commentaries on hadith texts. He passed away in Medina in approximately 1163 AH. His influence was disproportionate to his written output, transmitted primarily through his teaching and the transformative effect he had on the most important students of his era.
bio_id:
Abu al-Hasan Muhammad Hayat ibn Ibrahim as-Sindhi al-Madani (c. 1102–1163 H / c. 1690–1750 M) adalah seorang ahli hadits pada asalnya dari Sindh (di modern Pakistan) yang settled di Madinah dan became salah satu yang paling berpengaruh gurus dari abad ke-18. Lahir di Sindh, ia melakukan perjalanan ke Madinah ke pursue advanced studies di ilmu-ilmu hadits dan remained there.

As-Sindhi adalah deeply grounded di hadith tradisi dan adalah dikenal sebagai outstanding guru yang combined technical mastery dari sciences dari riwayat dengan commitment ke following evidence dari Sunnah. Ia adalah pengaruhd oleh ulamaly tradisi dari Shah Waliullah's family dan di turn pengaruhd others toward hadith-oriented pendekatan.

His signifikansi lies khususnya di remarkable murids he trained. Among his most terkemuka murids adalah Shah Waliullah ad-Dehlawi — great Indian ulama dan reformis yang became one dari most important Islamic thinkers dari abad — dan Muhammad ibn Abd al-Wahhab, reformis dari Semenanjung Arabia whose ajaran-ajaran gave rise ke Wahhabi/Salafi movement. The encounter dari both these figures dengan as-Sindhi's emphasis pada direct engagement dengan hadith dan ijtihad memiliki profound implications untuk reformasi Islam movements di seluruh dunia Islam.

As-Sindhi juga wrote commentaries pada hadith texts. He wafat di Madinah di sekitar 1163 H. His pengaruh adalah disproportionate ke his written output, meriwayatkan terutama melalui his teaching dan transformative effect he memiliki pada most important murids dari eranya.

name_full_en: Abu al-Hasan Ali ibn Abd al-Hayy al-Hasani an-Nadwi
title: Sheikh
bio_short_en: Indian Islamic scholar and author of 'Madha Khasira al-Alam bi Inhitat al-Muslimin' (What the World Lost by the Decline of Muslims), a seminal work on Islamic civilization.
bio_en:
Abu al-Hasan Ali ibn Abd al-Hayy al-Hasani an-Nadwi (1332-1420 AH / 1914-1999 CE) was one of the most prominent Indian Islamic scholars and thinkers of the 20th century. Born in Rai Bareli, Uttar Pradesh, into a scholarly family descended from the Prophet ﷺ through Imam al-Hasan, he studied at Nadwat al-Ulama in Lucknow and under private tutors, mastering the Islamic sciences as well as Arabic and Urdu literary traditions.

An-Nadwi became the rector of Nadwat al-Ulama and one of the most eloquent Arab-language writers among non-Arab scholars. He wrote in Arabic with a mastery that earned him recognition from Arab scholars and his election to membership in the Arabic Language Academies of Cairo and Damascus.

His most celebrated work is Madha Khasira al-Alam bi-Inhitat al-Muslimin (What Did the World Lose with the Decline of Muslims?), a sweeping analysis of Islamic civilization's contributions to humanity and the consequences of its decline. This book was acclaimed across the Muslim world and translated into numerous languages. He also authored as-Sirah an-Nabawiyyah (a Seerah work), al-Arkan al-Arba'ah (a book on the pillars of Islam), and numerous other works on Islamic thought, history, and spirituality.

An-Nadwi was deeply committed to the spiritual revival of Muslims and critical of Western materialism. He was a member of the Qadiriyyah Sufi order. He passed away in Rai Bareli in 1999, having spent his life promoting a vision of Islam that was simultaneously intellectually engaged, spiritually grounded, and globally engaged.
bio_id:
Abu al-Hasan Ali ibn Abd al-Hayy al-Hasani -Nadwi (1332–1420 H/1914–1999 M) adalah salah satu yang paling terkemuka Indian ulama Islams dan thinkers dari abad ke-20. Lahir di Rai Bareli, Uttar Pradesh, ke dalam ulamaly family descended dari Nabi ﷺ melalui Imam al-Hasan, ia belajar di Nadwat al-Ulama di Lucknow dan under private tutors, mastering ilmu-ilmu Islam serta Arabic dan Urdu literary tradisis.

An-Nadwi became rector dari Nadwat al-Ulama dan one dari most eloquent Arab-language writers among non-Arab para ulama. Ia menulis di Arabic dengan mastery yang earned him recognition dari Arab para ulama dan his election ke membership di Arabic Language Academies dari Kairo dan Damaskus.

His most terkenal work adalah Madha Khasira al-Alam bi-Inhitat al-Muslimin (What Did World Lose dengan Decline dari kaum Muslimin?), sweeping analysis dari peradaban Islam's contributions ke humanity dan consequences dari its decline. Tkitabnya adalah acclaimed di seluruh dunia Islam dan translated ke dalam numerous languages. He juga mengarang as-Sirah -Nabawiyyah ( Seerah work), al-Arkan al-Arba'ah ( kitab pada pillars dari Islam), dan numerous other works pada pemikiran Islam, history, dan spirituality.

An-Nadwi adalah deeply committed ke spiritual revival dari kaum Muslimin dan critical dari Western materialism. Ia adalah member dari Qadhiriyyah tariqahh Sufi. He wafat di Rai Bareli di 1999, having spent kehidupannya promoting vision dari Islam yang adalah simultaneously intellectually engaged, spiritually grounded, dan secara global engaged.

title: Sheikh
bio_short_en: Syrian Shafi'i scholar and professor at Damascus University, known for his accessible fiqh textbooks and scholarly editions of classical hadith works.
bio_en:
Mustafa Dib al-Bugha (1357-1441 AH / 1938-2020 CE) was a Syrian Shafi'i scholar and professor of Islamic jurisprudence at the University of Damascus. Born in Damascus, he studied under leading Syrian scholars and earned advanced degrees in Islamic law at Damascus University and al-Azhar.

Al-Bugha is best known for his widely used fiqh textbooks that became standard university teaching materials across Syria and beyond. His most influential works are al-Wafi fi Sharh al-Arba'in an-Nawawiyyah (a commentary on Imam an-Nawawi's Forty Hadiths), Nuzhah al-Muttaqin Sharh Riyad as-Salihin (a commentary on Imam an-Nawawi's Riyad as-Salihin), and al-Fiqh al-Manhaji ala Madhhab al-Imam ash-Shafi'i (co-authored with Mustafa al-Khin and Ali ash-Sharbaji), a systematic presentation of Shafi'i jurisprudence in five volumes designed for university teaching.

Al-Fiqh al-Manhaji became one of the most widely used Shafi'i fiqh textbooks in the Arab world and in Southeast Asian Islamic universities. Its clear organization, academic style, and comprehensive coverage made it suitable for modern higher Islamic education while remaining faithful to the Shafi'i tradition. His Nuzhah al-Muttaqin similarly became a widely used classroom text.

He was known as a dedicated and accessible teacher who served the University of Damascus for decades. He passed away in Damascus in 2020. His textbooks remain in widespread use in Shafi'i-tradition Islamic education.
bio_id:
Mustafa Dib al-Bugha (1357–1441 H/1938–2020 M) adalah seorang Suriahn Syafi'i ulama dan professor dari fikih Islam di University dari Damaskus. Lahir di Damaskus, ia belajar under leading Suriahn para ulama dan earned advanced degrees di hukum Islam di Damaskus University dan al-Azhar.

Al-Bugha adalah best dikenal karena his widely used fikih textkitabs yang became standard university teaching materials di seluruh Suriah dan melampaui. His most berpengaruh works adalah al-Wafi fi Sharh al-Arba'di -Nawawiyyah ( commentary pada Imam -Nawawi's Forty Hadiths), Nuzhah al-Muttaqin Sharh Riyad as-Salihin ( commentary pada Imam -Nawawi's Riyad as-Salihin), dan al-Fiqh al-Manhaji ala Madhhab al-Imam ash-Syafi'i (co-mengarang dengan Mustafa al-Khin dan Ali ash-Sharbaji), systematic presentation dari Syafi'i fikih di five jilids designed untuk university teaching.

Al-Fiqh al-Manhaji became one dari most widely used Syafi'i fikih textkitabs di dunia Arab dan di Asia Tenggaran Islamic universities. Its clear organization, academic style, dan comprehensive coverage made it suitable untuk modern higher pendidikan Islam while remaining faithful ke tradisi Syafi'i. His Nuzhah al-Muttaqin similarly became widely used classroom text.

Ia adalah dikenal sebagai dedicated dan accessible guru yang served University dari Damaskus untuk decades. He wafat di Damaskus di 2020. His textkitabs remain di widespread use di Syafi'i-tradisi pendidikan Islam.

title: Sheikh
bio_short_en: Egyptian Islamic scholar and thinker, author of numerous influential books on Islamic ethics, Quran interpretation, and social reform.
bio_en:
Muhammad al-Ghazali Ahmad as-Saqqa (1335-1416 AH / 1917-1996 CE) was one of the most influential Egyptian Islamic scholars, preachers, and public intellectuals of the 20th century. Born in Nakla al-Inab, Beheira Governorate, he studied at al-Azhar University and became one of its most prominent graduates, receiving his doctorate there.

He joined the Muslim Brotherhood in his youth and was associated with it through the 1940s, but later maintained an independent scholarly position. He was known throughout the Arab world as a preacher of extraordinary eloquence and persuasion. His Arabic style — combining classical scholarship with accessible clarity — made him one of the most widely listened to Muslim voices of his era.

His works addressed Islamic thought, ethics, and the defense of Islam against secularism and Western cultural influence. Among his most important works are Fiqh as-Sirah (Jurisprudence of the Prophet's ﷺ Biography), a widely read Seerah work; Kayf Nafham al-Islam (How to Understand Islam); ad-Da'wah al-Islamiyyah Tastaqbal Qarnahah (The Islamic Call Enters its New Century); and his controversial Fiqh al-Hadith (Understanding Hadith), which generated debate over his approach to hadith authentication.

Muhammad al-Ghazali was sometimes controversial for positions that traditional hadith scholars disputed, but his influence as a preacher and public intellectual was enormous. He passed away in Saudi Arabia in 1996 during his return from Hajj. He is remembered as one of the most gifted Islamic communicators of the 20th century.
bio_id:
Muhammad al-Ghazali Ahmad as-Saqqa (1335–1416 H/1917–1996 M) adalah salah satu yang paling berpengaruh Mesirian ulama Islams, dais, dan public intellectuals dari abad ke-20. Lahir di Nakla al-Inab, Beheira Governorate, ia belajar di Universitas al-Azhar dan became one dari its most terkemuka graduates, receiving his doctorate there.

Ia bergabung Muslim Brotherhood di masa mudanya dan adalah associated dengan it melalui 1940s, but later maintained independent ulamaly position. Ia adalah known throughout dunia Arab as dai dari extraordinary eloquence dan persuasion. His Arabic style — combining klasik para ulamahip dengan accessible clarity — made him one dari most widely listened ke Muslim voices dari eranya.

Karya-karyanya addressed pemikiran Islam, ethics, dan defense dari Islam against secularism dan Western cultural pengaruh. Among karya terpentingnyas adalah Fiqh as-Sirah (Jurisprudence dari Nabi ﷺ's ﷺ Biography), widely read Seerah work; Kayf Nafham al-Islam (How ke Understand Islam); ad-Da'wah al-Islamiyyah Tastaqbal Qarnahah (The Islamic Call Enters its New Century); dan his controversial Fiqh al-Hadith (Understanding Hadith), yang generated debate over pendekatannya ke hadith authentication.

Muhammad al-Ghazali adalah sometimes controversial untuk positions yang tradisial ahli haditss disputed, but pengaruhnya as dai dan public intellectual adalah enormous. He wafat di Arab Saudi di 1996 selama his return dari haji. Ia adalah dikenang sebagai one dari most berbakat Islamic communicators dari abad ke-20.

name_full_en: Abu al-Falah Abd al-Hayy ibn Ahmad ibn al-Imad al-Hanbali
bio_short_en: Hanbali scholar and author of 'Shadharat adh-Dhahab,' a major biographical history organized chronologically by year of death.
bio_en:
Abu al-Falah Abd al-Hayy ibn Ahmad ibn Muhammad ibn al-Imad al-Hanbali ad-Dimashqi (1032-1089 AH / 1623-1679 CE) was a Hanbali scholar from Damascus known primarily for his major biographical and historical encyclopedia. Born in Damascus, he studied under local scholars and became a respected figure in the city's scholarly community.

His major work is Shadharat adh-Dhahab fi Akhbar man Dhahab (Flakes of Gold: Reports of Those Who Have Passed Away), a comprehensive Islamic biographical dictionary organized by year of death, covering figures from the beginning of Islamic history through his own era. This massive multi-volume work provides death notices and biographical information for scholars, rulers, and other important figures across Islamic history, making it one of the most consulted general historical references in Arabic Islamic biographical literature.

Shadharat adh-Dhahab draws on numerous earlier historical and biographical works, synthesizing information from a vast range of sources. It is particularly valuable for the later periods of Islamic history, from the 10th century AH onward, where the sources it drew upon are less readily available. It has been used by historians and scholars for centuries as a reference for basic biographical information.

Ibn al-Imad also authored other works on history and Islamic sciences. He passed away in Istanbul in 1089 AH, where he had traveled. His Shadharat adh-Dhahab is his enduring contribution — a comprehensive reference that has served Islamic scholars and historians for over three centuries.
bio_id:
Abu al-Falah Abd al-Hayy ibn Ahmad ibn Muhammad ibn al-Imad al-Hanbali ad-Dimashqi (1032–1089 H/1623–1679 M) adalah seorang Hanbali ulama dari Damaskus known terutama untuk his major biographical dan historis ensiklopedia. Lahir di Damaskus, ia belajar under local para ulama dan became respected figure di city's ulamaly komunitas.

His major work adalah Shadharat adh-Dhahab fi Akhbar man Dhahab (Flakes dari Gold: Reports dari Those Who Have Passed Away), comprehensive Islamic biographical kamus organized oleh year dari death, covering figures dari beginning dari sejarah Islam melalui his own era. This massive multi-jilid work provides death notices dan biographical information untuk para ulama, rulers, dan other important figures di seluruh sejarah Islam, making it one dari most consulted jenderal historis references di Arabic Islamic biographical literature.

Shadharat adh-Dhahab draws pada numerous earlier historis dan biographical works, synthesizing information dari vast range dari sources. It adalah khususnya valuable untuk later periods dari sejarah Islam, dari abad ke-10 H onward, di mana sources it drew upon adalah less readily available. It telah menjadi used oleh sejarawans dan para ulama untuk centuries as reference untuk basic biographical information.

Ibn al-Imad juga mengarang other works pada history dan ilmu-ilmu Islam. He wafat di Istanbul di 1089 H, di mana he memiliki traveled. His Shadharat adh-Dhahab adalah his enduring contribution — comprehensive reference yang memiliki served ulama Islams dan sejarawans untuk over three centuries.

name_full_en: Shams ad-Din Muhammad ibn Ahmad ibn Abd al-Hadi al-Maqdisi
title: Imam
bio_short_en: Hanbali hadith scholar and student of Ibn Taymiyyah who died young but left important works on hadith criticism and Hanbali jurisprudence.
bio_en:
Shams ad-Din Muhammad ibn Ahmad ibn Abd al-Hadi ibn Qudama al-Maqdisi al-Hanbali (704-744 AH / 1305-1343 CE) was a Hanbali hadith scholar from the prominent Maqdisi family of Damascus and one of the prominent students of Ibn Taymiyyah. Despite dying at approximately 38 years of age, he left a significant scholarly legacy.

Ibn Abd al-Hadi studied under Ibn Taymiyyah, from whom he absorbed the Hanbali hadith-oriented scholarly methodology, and under the leading hadith masters of Damascus and Egypt. He became a distinguished authority in hadith sciences and biographical literature.

His most important works include as-Sarim al-Manki fi ar-Radd ala as-Subki (The Sharp Sword in Refuting as-Subki), a detailed response to Taqiyy ad-Din as-Subki's defense of the permissibility of visiting the Prophet's ﷺ tomb and seeking intercession — a work that represents an important contribution to a major theological debate within Sunni Islam. He also authored al-Muharrar fil-Hadith (The Purified Work in Hadith), an important hadith anthology used in legal rulings, and al-Uqud ad-Durriyyah fi Manaqib Shaykh al-Islam Ahmad ibn Taymiyyah, a biographical work on his teacher Ibn Taymiyyah.

He also authored a substantial biographical dictionary of hadith scholars. Ibn Abd al-Hadi was known for his precision and thoroughness in hadith scholarship and his loyalty to the Hanbali-Athari tradition of his teacher. He passed away in Damascus in 744 AH, mourned as a scholar of exceptional ability who died too young.
bio_id:
Shams ad-Din Muhammad ibn Ahmad ibn Abd al-Hadi ibn Qudama al-Maqdisi al-Hanbali (704–744 H/1305–1343 M) adalah seorang Hanbali ahli hadits from the terkemuka Maqdisi family of Damaskus and one of the terkemuka murids of Ibn Taymiyyah. Despite dying at sekitar 38 tahun of age, ia meninggalkan a significant ulamaly warisan.

Ibn Abd al-Hadi belajar kepada Ibn Taymiyyah, from whom he absorbed the Hanbali hadith-oriented ulamaly metodeology, and under the leading pakar haditss of Damaskus and Mesir. Ia menjadi a terpandang otoritas in ilmu-ilmu hadits and biographical literature.

His most important karya-karyanya meliputi as-Sarim al-Manki fi ar-Radd ala as-Subki (The Sharp Sword in Refuting as-Subki), a detailed response to Taqiyy ad-Din as-Subki's defense of the permissibility of visiting Nabi ﷺ's ﷺ tomb and seeking intercession — a work that represents an important contribution to a major theological debate within Sunni Islam. He also mengarang al-Muharrar fil-Hadith (The Purified Work in Hadith), an important hadith anthology used in legal hukum-hukum, and al-Uqud ad-Durriyyah fi Manaqib Syaikh al-Islam Ahmad ibn Taymiyyah, a biographical work on his guru Ibn Taymiyyah.

He also mengarang a substantial biographical kamus of ahli haditss. Ibn Abd al-Hadi adalah dikenal karena his precision and thoroughness in ahli haditsship and his loyalty to the Hanbali-Athari tradisi of his guru. He wafat in Damaskus in 744 H, mourned as a ulama of exceptional ability who wafat too young.

name_full_en: Diya' ad-Din Abu Abdullah Muhammad ibn Abd al-Wahid al-Maqdisi
title: Imam
bio_short_en: Hanbali hadith scholar from the Maqdisi family of Damascus, compiler of 'al-Ahadith al-Mukhtarah,' a collection of authentic hadiths selected through rigorous criteria.
bio_en:
Diya' ad-Din Abu Abdullah Muhammad ibn Abd al-Wahid ibn Ahmad al-Maqdisi (569-643 AH / 1173-1245 CE) was a Hanbali hadith scholar from the renowned Maqdisi family of Damascus. He was a leading hadith master of the 7th century AH and founded the Dar al-Hadith ad-Diya'iyyah on Jabal Qasiyun in Damascus, an institution that became an important center of hadith learning.

Diya' al-Maqdisi studied under the major hadith scholars of Syria, Iraq, the Hijaz, and other regions, collecting narrations and receiving ijazahs from the leading masters of his era. He traveled extensively in pursuit of hadith, a hallmark of the muhaddithin tradition.

His most important work is al-Ahadith al-Mukhtarah (The Selected Hadiths), also known as al-Mukhtarah, a collection of hadiths he authenticated as sound (sahih) or good (hasan) — hadiths not found in the Sahihayn (Bukhari and Muslim) but which he considered authentic. This work is a major addition to the authenticated hadith literature and is cited by later hadith scholars as a source of acceptable narrations.

He also authored Fadail al-A'mal and numerous other hadith compilations and works on the biography of narrators. His Dar al-Hadith trained many students in the sciences of hadith transmission and criticism.

Diya' al-Maqdisi passed away in Damascus in 643 AH. His al-Mukhtarah is his most significant scholarly legacy, representing an important contribution to the authenticated hadith literature and the Athari-Hanbali scholarly tradition in Damascus.
bio_id:
Diya' ad-Din Abu Abdullah Muhammad ibn Abd al-Wahid ibn Ahmad al-Maqdisi (569–643 H/1173–1245 M) adalah seorang Hanbali ahli hadits dari renowned Maqdisi family dari Damaskus. He adalah seorang pemimpin pakar hadits dari abad ke-7 H dan founded Dar al-Hadith ad-Diya'iyyah pada Jabal Qasiyun di Damaskus, institution yang became important center dari hadith learning.

Diya' al-Maqdisi belajar kepada major ahli haditss dari Suriah, Irak, Hijaz, dan other regions, collecting riwayats dan receiving ijazahs dari leading masters dari eranya. Ia melakukan perjalanan extensively di pursuit dari hadith, hallmark dari muhaddithin tradisi.

His most important work adalah al-Ahadith al-Mukhtarah (The Selected Hadiths), juga dikenal sebagai al-Mukhtarah, collection dari hadiths he authenticated as sound (sahih) atau good (hasan) — hadiths tidak found di Sahihayn (Bukhari dan Muslim) but yang he dianggap authentic. This work adalah seorang major addition ke authenticated hadith literature dan adalah cited oleh later ahli haditss as source dari acceptable riwayats.

He juga mengarang Fadail al-A'mal dan numerous other hadith compilations dan works pada biografi dari perawis. His Dar al-Hadith trained many murids di ilmu-ilmu hadits periwayatan dan criticism.

Diya' al-Maqdisi wafat di Damaskus di 643 H. His al-Mukhtarah adalah his most significant ulamaly warisan, representing important contribution ke authenticated hadith literature dan Athari-Hanbali ulamaly tradisi di Damaskus.

name_full_en: Muhammad ibn Ali ibn Muhammad ibn Abdullah ash-Shawkani
title: Imam
bio_short_en: Yemeni scholar and advocate of ijtihad, author of 'Nayl al-Awtar,' a masterful commentary on hadiths of legal rulings, and 'Fath al-Qadir' in tafsir.
bio_en:
Muhammad ibn Ali ibn Muhammad ibn Abdullah ash-Shawkani (1173-1255 AH / 1759-1839 CE) was one of the most important Islamic scholars of the 18th-19th centuries. Born in Shawkan, Yemen, he studied in San'a under the leading scholars of Yemen and became the chief judge (qadi al-qudat) of San'a under the Qasimi imams — the highest judicial position in the country — a post he held for nearly thirty years.

Ash-Shawkani advocated for direct engagement with the Quran and Sunnah and criticized rigid taqlid (blind following of a legal school), arguing that qualified scholars should engage in ijtihad (independent reasoning) based on the primary sources. He was influenced by the tradition of independent hadith-based scholarship in Yemen.

His most important work is Nayl al-Awtar Sharh Muntaqa al-Akhbar (Achieving the Aims: Commentary on the Selection of Reports), a comprehensive multi-volume commentary on a hadith collection focused on legal rulings, demonstrating his methodology of deriving rulings directly from the hadith evidence. He also authored Fath al-Qadir, a five-volume Quran commentary; Irshad al-Fuhul ila Tahqiq al-Haqq min Ilm al-Usul (Guidance for Scholars on the Science of Legal Methodology); and al-Badr at-Tali' (a biographical dictionary of scholars).

Ash-Shawkani passed away in San'a in 1255 AH. His Nayl al-Awtar and Irshad al-Fuhul are particularly important contributions to hadith-based jurisprudence and legal methodology, and continue to be widely used across the Muslim world.
bio_id:
Muhammad ibn Ali ibn Muhammad ibn Abdullah ash-Shawkani (1173–1255 H/1759–1839 M) adalah salah satu ulama Islam terpenting of the 18th-19th abad-abad. Lahir di Shawkan, Yaman, ia belajar in San'a under the leading para ulama of Yaman and became the hakim agung (qadhi al-qudat) of San'a under the Qasimi imams — the highest judicial position in the country — a post he held for nearly thirty tahun.

Ash-Shawkani advocated for direct engagement with Al-Al-Qur'an and Sunnah and criticized rigid taqlid (blind following of a legal school), arguing that qualified para ulama should engage in ijtihad (independent reasoning) based on the primary sources. He adalah pengaruhd by the tradisi of independent hadith-based para ulamahip in Yaman.

His most important work adalah Nayl al-Awtar Sharh Muntaqa al-Akhbar (Achieving the Aims: Commentary on the Selection of Reports), a comprehensive multi-jilid commentary on a koleksi hadits focused on legal hukum-hukum, demonstrating metodenyaology of deriving hukum-hukum directly from the hadith evidence. He also mengarang Fath al-Qadhir, a five-jilid tafsir Al-Al-Qur'an; Irshad al-Fuhul ila Tahqiq al-Haqq min Ilm al-Usul (Guidance for Scholars on the Science of Legal Methodology); and al-Badr at-Tali' (a biographical kamus of para ulama).

Ash-Shawkani wafat in San'a in 1255 H. His Nayl al-Awtar and Irshad al-Fuhul adalah khususnya important contributions to hadith-based fikih and legal metodeology, and continue to be widely used across dunia Islam.

name_full_en: Abu al-Ala Muhammad Abdurrahman ibn Abdurrahim al-Mubarakfuri
title: Sheikh
bio_short_en: Indian hadith scholar, author of 'Tuhfat al-Ahwadhi,' the most comprehensive commentary on Jami at-Tirmidhi.
bio_en:
Abu al-Ala Muhammad Abdurrahman ibn Abdurrahim al-Mubarakfuri (1283-1353 AH / 1860-1935 CE) was an Indian hadith scholar from Mubarakpur, Uttar Pradesh. He is best known for his monumental work Tuhfat al-Ahwadhi bi-Sharh Jami' at-Tirmidhi (The Gift of the Resolute: Commentary on the Jami' of at-Tirmidhi), the most comprehensive commentary on Sunan at-Tirmidhi.

Al-Mubarakfuri was associated with the Ahl al-Hadith movement in India, which emphasized direct derivation of rulings from hadith texts rather than adherence to a particular legal school. He studied under the scholars of the Indian hadith tradition and dedicated his scholarly life to the commentary on at-Tirmidhi's collection.

Tuhfat al-Ahwadhi is an extensive work in approximately ten volumes that provides detailed explanations of each hadith in Sunan at-Tirmidhi, including technical analysis of the narrators, comparative jurisprudential discussions, and at-Tirmidhi's own comments on each hadith. The commentary draws on a wide range of earlier scholarship and is considered one of the most important reference works for understanding the hadiths in at-Tirmidhi's collection. Its depth and comprehensiveness made it the standard reference commentary on Sunan at-Tirmidhi.

Al-Mubarakfuri also authored Sirat ar-Rasul (a Seerah work) and other works. He taught at Jamia Salafiyya in Varanasi, India. He passed away in Mubarakpur in 1353 AH. His Tuhfat al-Ahwadhi remains his most significant contribution and a major reference in hadith scholarship.
bio_id:
Abu al-Ala Muhammad Abdurrahman ibn Abdurrahim al-Mubarakfuri (1283–1353 H/1860–1935 M) adalah seorang Indian ahli hadits dari Mubarakpur, Uttar Pradesh. Ia adalah best dikenal karena his monumental work Tuhfat al-Ahwadhi bi-Sharh Jami' di-Tirmidhi (The Gift dari Resolute: Commentary pada Jami' dari di-Tirmidhi), most comprehensive commentary pada Sunan di-Tirmidhi.

Al-Mubarakfuri adalah associated dengan Ahl al-Hadith movement di India, yang emphasized direct derivation dari hukum-hukum dari hadith texts rather than adherence ke particular legal school. Ia belajar under para ulama dari Indian hadith tradisi dan dedicated his ulamaly life ke commentary pada di-Tirmidhi's collection.

Tuhfat al-Ahwadhi adalah seorang extensive work di sekitar ten jilids yang provides detailed explanations dari each hadith di Sunan di-Tirmidhi, including technical analysis dari perawis, comparative jurisprudential discussions, dan di-Tirmidhi's own comments pada each hadith. The commentary draws pada wide range dari earlier para ulamahip dan adalah dianggap sebagai salah satu yang terpenting reference works untuk understanding hadiths di di-Tirmidhi's collection. Its depth dan comprehensiveness made it standard reference commentary pada Sunan di-Tirmidhi.

Al-Mubarakfuri juga mengarang Sirat ar-Rasul ( Seerah work) dan other works. Ia mengajar di Jamia Salafiyya di Varanasi, India. He wafat di Mubarakpur di 1353 H. His Tuhfat al-Ahwadhi remains his most significant contribution dan major reference di ahli haditsship.

name_full_en: Abd al-Hayy ibn Fakhr ad-Din al-Hasani an-Nadwi
title: Sheikh
bio_short_en: Indian scholar and author of 'Nuzhat al-Khawatir,' an eight-volume biographical dictionary of Islamic scholars in the Indian subcontinent.
bio_en:
Abd al-Hayy ibn Fakhr ad-Din al-Hasani an-Nadwi (1286-1341 AH / 1869-1923 CE) was an Indian Islamic scholar and historian, and the father of the celebrated scholar Abu al-Hasan an-Nadwi. Born in Lucknow into a family tracing their lineage to Imam al-Hasan ibn Ali, he studied under the scholars of Lucknow and Bhopal and became a distinguished authority in Islamic biographical literature and history.

His most important work is Nuzhat al-Khawatir wa-Bahjat al-Masami' wan-Nawazir (The Walk of the Minds and the Delight of Listeners and Beholders), also known as al-I'lam bi-man fi Tarikh al-Hind min al-A'lam (Notice of the Distinguished Figures in the History of India), a comprehensive multi-volume biographical dictionary of Muslim scholars, rulers, and notable figures of the Indian subcontinent from the earliest Islamic presence there through his own era. This massive work is the most comprehensive single-authored biographical encyclopedia of the Indian subcontinent's Islamic history.

Nuzhat al-Khawatir documents thousands of figures associated with Islamic scholarship, mysticism, and public life in India, many of whom would otherwise be difficult to trace. It is an indispensable reference for the social and religious history of Islam in the Indian subcontinent.

Abd al-Hayy also taught at Nadwat al-Ulama and contributed to its development as a center of traditional Islamic scholarship with a broader literary vision. He passed away in Lucknow in 1923. His biographical encyclopedia is his most enduring scholarly legacy and remains the foundational reference for Islamic biographical history in South Asia.
bio_id:
Abd al-Hayy ibn Fakhr ad-Din al-Hasani -Nadwi (1286–1341 H/1869–1923 M) adalah seorang Indian ulama dan sejarawan Islam, dan father dari terkenal ulama Abu al-Hasan -Nadwi. Lahir di Lucknow ke dalam family tracing their lineage ke Imam al-Hasan ibn Ali, ia belajar under para ulama dari Lucknow dan Bhopal dan became terpandang otoritas di Islamic biographical literature dan history.

His most important work adalah Nuzhat al-Khawatir wa-Bahjat al-Masami' wan-Nawazir (The Walk dari Minds dan Delight dari Listeners dan Beholders), juga dikenal sebagai al-I'lam bi-man fi Tarikh al-Hind min al-A'lam (Notice dari Distinguished Figures di History dari India), comprehensive multi-jilid biographical kamus dari Muslim para ulama, rulers, dan terkemuka figures dari anak benua India dari earliest Islamic presence there melalui his own era. This massive work adalah most comprehensive single-mengarang biographical ensiklopedia dari anak benua India's sejarah Islam.

Nuzhat al-Khawatir documents thousands dari figures associated dengan ulama Islamship, sufiism, dan public life di India, many dari whom akan otherwise be difficult ke trace. It adalah seorang indispensable reference untuk social dan religious history dari Islam di anak benua India.

Abd al-Hayy juga taught di Nadwat al-Ulama dan contributed ke its development as center dari tradisial ulama Islamship dengan broader literary vision. He wafat di Lucknow di 1923. His biographical ensiklopedia adalah his most enduring ulamaly warisan dan remains foundational reference untuk Islamic biographical history di Asia Selatan.

title: Sheikh
bio_short_en: Palestinian Shafi'i judge and prolific author of works defending traditional Islamic scholarship and collecting prophetic praises.
bio_en:
Yusuf ibn Isma'il ibn Yusuf an-Nabhani (1265-1350 AH / 1849-1932 CE) was a Palestinian Islamic scholar, Shafi'i judge, and prolific author. Born in the village of Ijzim near Haifa, Palestine, he studied at al-Azhar in Egypt and served as a judge in the Ottoman court system in Beirut, Janin, and other cities. He was a devoted follower of the Sufi tradition and the Ba'Alawi path.

An-Nabhani is best known for his prolific writing in defense of the Prophet ﷺ, the status of the Sufi masters, and the practice of seeking the Prophet's ﷺ intercession. He authored numerous works on the Prophet's ﷺ attributes and the proper veneration of the Prophet ﷺ. His most widely read work is Hujjat Allah ala al-Alamin fi Mu'jizat Sayyid al-Mursalin (The Evidence of Allah upon the Worlds: The Miracles of the Master of the Messengers), a comprehensive collection of the prophetic miracles.

He also authored Jawharat al-Kamal (Pearl of Perfection), a praise poem for the Prophet ﷺ that is widely recited in traditional circles; as-Sa'adah al-Abadiyyah fi al-Ta'rif bi-Mashahir al-Hadratin-Nabawiyyah (on the saints and scholars of the Prophetic city); and numerous other works. He was a strong defender of traditional Sufi-Sunni practices including the mawlid celebration.

An-Nabhani is a revered figure in Sufi-oriented Sunni circles for his defense of the veneration of the Prophet ﷺ. He passed away in Beirut in 1932. His works continue to be read across traditional Muslim communities, particularly those connected to the Ba'Alawi and Shadiliyyah traditions.
bio_id:
Yusuf ibn Isma'il ibn Yusuf -Nabhani (1265–1350 H/1849–1932 M) adalah seorang Palestinian ulama Islam, Syafi'i hakim, dan penulis yang produktif. Lahir di village dari Ijzim near Haifa, Palestina, ia belajar di al-Azhar di Mesir dan served as hakim di Ottoman court system di Beirut, Janin, dan other cities. Ia adalah devoted pengikut dari tradisi Sufi dan Ba'Alawi path.

An-Nabhani adalah best dikenal karena his produktif writing di defense dari Nabi ﷺ, status dari mursyid Sufis, dan practice dari seeking Nabi ﷺ's ﷺ intercession. Ia mengarang numerous works pada Nabi ﷺ's ﷺ attributes dan proper veneration dari Nabi ﷺ. His most widely read work adalah Hujjat Allah ala al-Alamin fi Mu'jizat Sayyid al-Mursalin (The Evidence dari Allah upon Worlds: The Miracles dari Master dari Messengers), comprehensive collection dari prophetic miracles.

He juga mengarang Jawharat al-Kamal (Pearl dari Perfection), praise poem untuk Nabi ﷺ yaitu widely recited di tradisial circles; as-Sa'adah al-Abadiyyah fi al-Ta'rif bi-Mashahir al-Hadratin-Nabawiyyah (pada saints dan para ulama dari Nabi ﷺic city); dan numerous other works. Ia adalah strong defender dari tradisial Sufi-Sunni practices including mawlid celebration.

An-Nabhani adalah seorang revered figure di Sufi-oriented Sunni circles untuk his defense dari veneration dari Nabi ﷺ. He wafat di Beirut di 1932. Karya-karyanya continue ke be read di seluruh tradisial Muslim komunitas-komunitas, khususnya those connected ke Ba'Alawi dan Shadiliyyah tradisis.

name_full_en: Muhammad Rashid ibn Ali Rida al-Husayni at-Tarabulsi
bio_short_en: Syrian-Egyptian Islamic reformer, student of Muhammad Abduh, and author of 'Tafsir al-Manar,' an influential rationalist Quran commentary.
bio_en:
Muhammad Rashid ibn Ali Rida al-Qalmuni (1282-1354 AH / 1865-1935 CE) was a Syrian Islamic scholar, reformer, and public intellectual who became one of the most influential Muslim thinkers of the early 20th century. Born in Qalamoun near Tripoli, Lebanon, he was deeply influenced by the reformist writings of Jamal ad-Din al-Afghani and Muhammad Abduh, whose student and most devoted disciple he became.

Rashid Rida emigrated to Cairo in 1897 and founded the journal al-Manar (The Lighthouse) in 1898, which he edited until his death. Al-Manar became the most influential Islamic reform journal of its era, circulating across the Muslim world and shaping the discourse of Islamic intellectual life for decades. It addressed theology, jurisprudence, social issues, and political questions from a reformist perspective, calling for the renewal of independent reasoning (ijtihad) and the reform of Islamic education.

His most important scholarly work is Tafsir al-Manar (the Manar Commentary), a continuation and completion of Muhammad Abduh's Quranic commentary, which is the most important exegetical product of the Islamic modernist movement.

He also authored al-Khilafah aw al-Imamah al-Uzma on the Islamic caliphate, Yusr al-Islam (The Ease of Islam), and numerous other works. Rashid Rida's thought evolved over his career, moving from pure Islamic modernism toward engagement with Salafi and Wahhabi thought in his later years. He passed away in Cairo in 1935, having been one of the central figures in modern Islamic intellectual history.
bio_id:
Muhammad Rashid ibn Ali Rida al-Qalmuni (1282–1354 H/1865–1935 M) adalah seorang Suriahn ulama Islam, reformis, dan public intellectual yang became salah satu yang paling berpengaruh Muslim thinkers dari awal abad ke-20. Lahir di Qalamoun near Tripoli, Lebanon, ia adalah deeply pengaruhd oleh reformist writings dari Jamal ad-Din al-Afghani dan Muhammad Abduh, whose murid dan most devoted murid ia menjadi.

Rashid Rida emigrated ke Kairo di 1897 dan founded journal al-Manar (The Lighthouse) di 1898, yang he edited hingga wafatnya. Al-Manar became most berpengaruh reformasi Islam journal dari its era, circulating di seluruh dunia Islam dan shaping discourse dari Islamic intellectual life untuk decades. It addressed teologi, fikih, social issues, dan political questions dari reformist perspective, calling untuk renewal dari independent reasoning (ijtihad) dan reform dari pendidikan Islam.

His most important ulamaly work adalah Tafsir al-Manar ( Manar Commentary), continuation dan completion dari Muhammad Abduh's tafsir Al-Al-Qur', yang adalah most important exegetical product dari Islamic modernist movement.

He juga mengarang al-Khilafah aw al-Imamah al-Uzma pada Islamic khalifahate, Yusr al-Islam (The Ease dari Islam), dan numerous other works. Rashid Rida's thought evolved over his career, moving dari pure Islamic modernism toward engagement dengan Salafi dan Wahhabi thought di tahun-tahun akhir hidupnya. He wafat di Kairo di 1935, having been one dari central figures di modern Islamic intellectual history.

name_full_en: Muhammad at-Tahir ibn Muhammad ibn Muhammad at-Tahir ibn Ashur
title: Sheikh
bio_short_en: Tunisian Maliki scholar and Grand Mufti, author of 'at-Tahrir wat-Tanwir,' a major modern Quran commentary, and pioneering work on maqasid ash-shari'ah.
bio_en:
