At the Battle of Badr, Utbah was one of the three Meccan champions who came forward for single combat. He was killed by Hamza, Ali, and Ubaydah ibn al-Harith. His daughter Hind had his liver eaten after Uhud — one of the most notorious acts of the Seerah.
bio_id:
Utbah ibn Rabi'ah adalah salah satu pemimpin senior Quraysh dan ayah dari Hind bint Utbah yang kelak menikah dengan Abu Sufyan. Ia dikenal sebagai sosok yang bijaksana dan cerdas dalam berpolitik di antara penduduk Mekkah, dan setidaknya sekali mencoba bernegosiasi dengan Nabi ﷺ alih-alih sekadar menentangnya. Pertukaran yang paling terkenal terjadi ketika Utbah mendatangi Nabi ﷺ dan menawarkan kekayaan, kepemimpinan, dan pengobatan atas apa pun yang mendorongnya mengaku sebagai nabi — asalkan beliau menghentikan dakwahnya.

Nabi ﷺ diam mendengarkan hingga Utbah selesai bicara, lalu bertanya: "Sudahkah engkau selesai?" Kemudian beliau membacakan ayat-ayat awal Surah Fussilat (41). Utbah duduk mendengarkan, lalu kembali kepada para pemimpin Quraysh dengan ekspresi yang berubah drastis. Ia berkata kepada mereka bahwa ia telah mendengar perkataan yang tidak pernah ia dengar sebelumnya — bukan puisi, bukan sihir, bukan ramalan — dan ia menyarankan agar mereka membiarkan Muhammad ﷺ dan menunggu apa yang akan terjadi. Para pemimpin Quraysh menolak sarannya.

Utbah mengikuti Perang Badar (2 H) sebagai salah satu pimpinan Quraysh. Ia tewas dalam perang itu — dibunuh oleh Hamzah ibn Abd al-Muttalib dan Ubaidah ibn al-Harits radhiyallahu 'anhuma dalam pertempuran satu lawan satu (mubarazah) yang menjadi salah satu adegan paling dramatik dalam perang tersebut. Anaknya, Abu Hudzaifah ibn Utbah, adalah seorang Muslim taat dan turut hadir dalam Perang Badar di pihak kaum Muslimin — sehingga ia menyaksikan ayahnya sendiri terbunuh, sebuah tragedi yang menggambarkan betapa dalamnya perpecahan yang ditimbulkan oleh dakwah Islam dalam keluarga-keluarga Quraysh.

name_en_alt: the one of the wealth and sons
bio_short_en: A powerful Meccan elder who was directly addressed in the Quran (Surah al-Muddaththir) for his arrogant rejection of the Quran after privately acknowledging it was extraordinary.
bio_en: Al-Walid ibn al-Mughirah was one of the most powerful and wealthy elders of the Quraysh, from the prestigious Banu Makhzum clan. He is most remarkable in Islamic history for his private acknowledgment of the Quran's greatness followed by his public refusal to accept it — an act driven purely by arrogance and tribal pride. When the pilgrimage season approached in the early years of Islam, the Quraysh gathered to decide what to say to pilgrims who would ask about Muhammad. Al-Walid listened to the Quran and told the Quraysh: 'By Allah, among you there is no one more knowledgeable about poetry, prose, and the poetry of the jinn than I am. By Allah, what he says does not resemble any of these things. By Allah, what he recites has sweetness and beauty. The top of it is fruitful and the bottom of it is abundant. It surpasses everything and nothing surpasses it.' The Quraysh were confused — then he said: 'I have thought about it — say that it is sorcery.' He chose to call the Quran magic despite his private belief in its extraordinary nature. Allah responded directly in Surah al-Muddaththir (74:11-26), one of the sharpest rebukes in the Quran, addressed to one who was given 'wealth and children' and still rejected the truth: 'Leave Me with the one I created alone, and to whom I granted extensive wealth, and children present with him, and I smoothed his path. Then he desires that I should add more. No!' He died before the Battle of Badr. His son Khalid ibn al-Walid later became one of the greatest Muslim commanders.
bio_id: Al-Walid ibn al-Mughirah adalah one dari most powerful dan wealthy elders dari Quraysh, dari prestigious Banu Makhzum clan. Ia adalah most remarkable di sejarah Islam untuk his private acknowledgment dari Al-Al-Qur''s greatness followed oleh his public refusal ke accept it — act driven purely oleh arrogance dan tribal pride. When haji season approached di early tahun dari Islam, Quraysh gathered ke decide what ke say ke pilgrims yang akan ask sekitar Muhammad. Al-Walid listened ke Al-Al-Qur' dan told Quraysh: 'By Allah, among you there adalah tidak one more ilmu pengetahuanable sekitar penyairry, prose, dan penyairry dari jinn than I am. By Allah, what he says does tidak resemble any dari these things. By Allah, what he recites memiliki sweetness dan beauty. The top dari it adalah fruitful dan bottom dari it adalah abundant. It surpasses everything dan nothing surpasses it.' The Quraysh adalah confused — then he said: 'I memiliki thought sekitar it — say yang it adalah sorcery.' He chose ke call Al-Al-Qur' magic meskipun his private belief di its extraordinary nature. Allah responded directly di Surah al-Muddaththir (74:11-26), one dari sharpest rebukes di Al-Al-Qur', addressed ke one yang adalah given 'wealth dan children' dan still rejected truth: 'Leave Me dengan one I created alone, dan ke whom I granted extensive wealth, dan children present dengan him, dan I smoothed his path. Then he desires yang I seharusnya add more. No!' He died sebelum Battle dari Badr. His son Khalid ibn al-Walid later became one dari greatest Muslim panglimas.

name_full_en: Abraha al-Ashram, Viceroy of Yemen for Abyssinia
bio_short_en: The Abyssinian viceroy of Yemen who marched with elephants to destroy the Ka'bah and was destroyed by Allah's army of birds — in the year the Prophet ﷺ was born.
bio_en: Abraha al-Ashram was the Abyssinian Christian viceroy of Yemen who led a massive military expedition against Mecca to destroy the Ka'bah in approximately 570 CE — the year the Prophet Muhammad ﷺ was born. This year became known as 'Am al-Fil (the Year of the Elephant) and was the standard dating reference for Arabs of that era. Abraha had built a magnificent cathedral called al-Qullays in Sana'a, Yemen, hoping to redirect the pilgrimage traffic of Arabia from Mecca to his church. When Arabs defiled it in protest, he led a massive army north to Mecca, including a war elephant (or several elephants) named Mahmud at the front. The Quraysh evacuated Mecca and fled to the hills, unable to resist the army. Abd al-Muttalib, the Prophet's grandfather and chief of the Quraysh, went to Abraha to negotiate the return of camels that had been seized. Abraha asked why he came only about camels and not about the Ka'bah — to which Abd al-Muttalib reportedly said: 'I am the lord of the camels. The Ka'bah has its own Lord who will protect it.' On the day Abraha's army prepared to march on Mecca, the lead elephant Mahmud refused to move toward Mecca. When turned in other directions, it ran; when turned toward Mecca, it sat down. Then came the army of Ababil — birds carrying stones of baked clay, dropping them on the army until they were destroyed 'like eaten straw' (Surah al-Fil 105). Abraha himself is reported to have fled back to Yemen, diseased and dying. The Quran commemorates this event in Surah al-Fil (Chapter 105): 'Have you not seen how your Lord dealt with the companions of the elephant?'
bio_id: Abraha al-Ashram adalah Abyssinian Christian viceroy of Yaman who led a massive military expedition against Mekkah to destroy the Ka'bah in sekitar 570 M — the year Nabi Muhammad ﷺ adalah lahir. This year became dikenal sebagai 'Am al-Fil (the Year of the Elephant) and adalah standard dating reference for Arabs of that era. Abraha memiliki built a magnificent cathedral disebut al-Qullays in Sana'a, Yaman, hoping to redirect the haji traffic of Arabia from Mekkah to his church. When Arabs defiled it in protest, he led a massive army north to Mekkah, including a war elephant (or several elephants) dinamai Mahmud at the front. The Quraysh evacuated Mekkah and fled to the hills, unable to resist the army. Abd al-Muttalib, Nabi ﷺ's grandfather and chief of the Quraysh, went to Abraha to negotiate the return of camels that telah menjadi seized. Abraha asked why he came only about camels and not about the Ka'bah — to which Abd al-Muttalib reportedly said: 'I am the lord of the camels. The Ka'bah memiliki its own Lord who will protect it.' On the day Abraha's army prepared to march on Mekkah, the lead elephant Mahmud refused to move toward Mekkah. When turned in other directions, it ran; when turned toward Mekkah, it sat down. Then came the army of Ababil — birds carrying stones of baked clay, dropping them on the army until they adalah destroyed 'like eaten straw' (Surah al-Fil 105). Abraha himself adalah reported to memiliki fled back to Yaman, diseased and dying. The Al-Al-Qur'an commemorates this event in Surah al-Fil (Chapter 105): 'Have you not seen how your Lord dealt with the companions of the elephant?'

name_full_en: Heraclius, Emperor of the Byzantine Empire
title: Emperor of Byzantium
bio_short_en: The Byzantine Emperor who received the Prophet's letter inviting him to Islam and privately acknowledged the signs of prophethood.
bio_en: Heraclius (575-641 CE) was the Emperor of the Byzantine Empire who received a letter from the Prophet Muhammad ﷺ in 7 AH / 628 CE inviting him to Islam. This letter — carried by the companion Dihyah al-Kalbi — is preserved in hadith collections and begins: 'In the name of Allah, the Most Beneficent, the Most Merciful. From Muhammad, the servant and messenger of Allah, to Heraclius, the leader of the Byzantines. Peace be upon those who follow guidance. I invite you to accept Islam. If you accept Islam, you will find safety, and Allah will give you a double reward. But if you reject this invitation, you will bear the sin of your subjects.' The hadith in Sahih al-Bukhari narrates that Heraclius responded to the letter by summoning Abu Sufyan (who was then in Syria on a trade mission before his conversion) to question him about the Prophet. The entire exchange is one of the most detailed portraits in the hadith of a non-Muslim ruler's private acknowledgment of the truth of the Prophet's message. Heraclius asked Abu Sufyan whether the Prophet was from noble lineage (yes), whether any of his family had claimed prophethood before (no), whether his followers increased or decreased (increased), whether anyone left his religion after entering it (no), whether he betrayed treaties (no), and what he commanded (prayer, charity, chastity, honoring bonds). Heraclius then said: 'If what you have told me is true, he will certainly come to control what is beneath my feet. I knew a prophet was coming, but I did not know he would be from among the Arabs.' Despite this private acknowledgment, Heraclius did not embrace Islam publicly, fearing his empire would reject it. This is one of the Quran's great lessons: knowing the truth is not enough — one must submit to it.
bio_id: Heraclius (575–641 M) adalah Emperor dari Byzantine Empire yang received letter dari Nabi Muhammad ﷺ di 7 AH / 628 M inviting him ke Islam. This letter — carried oleh companion Dihyah al-Kalbi — adalah preserved di koleksi-koleksi hadits dan begins: 'In name dari Allah, Most Beneficent, Most Merciful. From Muhammad, servant dan messenger dari Allah, ke Heraclius, leader dari Byzantines. Peace be upon those yang follow guidance. I invite you ke accept Islam. If you accept Islam, you akan find safety, dan Allah akan give you double reward. But if you reject this invitation, you akan bear sin dari your subjects.' The hadith di Sahih al-Bukhari narrates yang Heraclius responded ke letter oleh summoning Abu Sufyan (yang adalah then di Suriah pada trade mission sebelum his konversi) ke question him sekitar Nabi ﷺ. The entire exchange adalah one dari most detailed portraits di hadith dari non-Muslim ruler's private acknowledgment dari truth dari Nabi ﷺ's message. Heraclius asked Abu Sufyan whether Nabi ﷺ adalah dari noble lineage (yes), whether any dari keluarganya memiliki claimed prophethood sebelum (tidak), whether his pengikuts increased atau decreased (increased), whether anyone left his religion setelah entering it (tidak), whether he betrayed treaties (tidak), dan what he commanded (shalat, charity, chastity, honoring bonds). Heraclius then said: 'If what you memiliki told me adalah true, he akan certainly come ke control what adalah beneath my feet. I knew prophet adalah coming, but I did tidak know he akan be dari among Arabs.' Despite this private acknowledgment, Heraclius did tidak embrace Islam publicly, fearing his empire akan reject it. This adalah one dari Al-Al-Qur''s great lessons: knowing truth adalah tidak enough — one harus submit ke it.

name_full_en: Khosrow II Parviz, King of Persia (Sasanid Empire)
title: King of Persia
bio_short_en: The Persian emperor who tore up the Prophet's letter — and was himself torn apart, his empire dismembered within his own lifetime.
bio_en: Khosrow II (Chosroes, known in Arabic as Kisra) was the King of the Sasanid Persian Empire who received the Prophet Muhammad's letter inviting him to Islam in 7 AH / 628 CE. The letter was carried by the companion Abdullah ibn Hudhafah al-Sahmi. According to the most widely narrated account, Khosrow tore up the letter in contempt and arrogance, throwing it aside. When the Prophet ﷺ was informed of this, he said: 'May Allah tear his kingdom apart, just as he tore my letter.' The prophecy was fulfilled with remarkable speed. Khosrow II was one of the most powerful kings in the world at that time, his empire stretching from Iraq to Central Asia. Yet within months of receiving the Prophet's letter, his own son Sheroe (Kavadh II) had him arrested and executed in 628 CE — the very same year. His kingdom subsequently fractured: within twenty years, the entire Sasanid Empire had fallen to the Muslim armies. The contrast between Heraclius and Khosrow in the Seerah literature is deliberate: both received the same letter, but Heraclius privately acknowledged the truth while Khosrow responded with physical arrogance. The Prophet ﷺ had predicted that the Persian kingdom would end while the Roman (Byzantine) kingdom would endure longer — a prediction that was fulfilled. The fall of the Persian empire and its transformation into an Islamic civilization is one of the greatest historical changes of the 7th century.
bio_id:
Khosrow II (Chosroes, dikenal dalam bahasa Arab sebagai Kisra) adalah Raja Kekaisaran Persia Sasaniyah yang menerima surat Nabi Muhammad ﷺ yang mengajaknya masuk Islam pada tahun 7 H/628 M. Surat itu dibawa oleh Sahabat Abdullah ibn Hudhafah al-Sahmi. Menurut riwayat yang paling banyak dikutip, Khosrow merobek surat itu dengan penuh hina dan angkuh, membuangnya begitu saja. Ketika Nabi ﷺ diberitahu tentang hal ini, beliau bersabda: "Semoga Allah merobek-robek kerajaannya, sebagaimana ia merobek suratku." Nubuat tersebut terpenuhi dengan sangat cepat.

Khosrow II adalah salah satu raja paling berkuasa di dunia pada saat itu, penguasa sebuah kekaisaran yang membentang dari Mesopotamia hingga tepi-tepi India, dan yang pada puncak kekuasaannya telah menaklukkan Palestina, Mesir, dan Anatolia dari Kekaisaran Byzantium. Namun ia digulingkan dan dibunuh oleh putranya sendiri, Kavad II (Shiruyeh), pada tahun 628 M — hanya beberapa bulan setelah mengirim jawabannya yang hina atas surat Nabi ﷺ. Setelah itu, Kekaisaran Sasaniyah mengalami kekacauan politik yang berkepanjangan, dengan delapan raja berbeda berkuasa dalam waktu empat tahun, sebelum akhirnya runtuh total di tangan pasukan Muslim pada 633–651 M di bawah komando para Sahabat seperti Khalid ibn al-Walid dan Sa'd ibn Abi Waqqash. Kerajaan Persia pun benar-benar "dirobek" dalam waktu kurang dari dua puluh tahun setelah peristiwa itu.

name_full_en: Muqawqis — the Coptic ruler of Egypt
title: Muqawqis
bio_short_en: The ruler of Egypt who received the Prophet's letter, responded respectfully, and sent gifts including Maria al-Qibtiyyah.
bio_en: Muqawqis was the Coptic Christian ruler (or Byzantine governor) of Egypt who received the Prophet Muhammad's letter in 7 AH / 628 CE. The letter was carried by the companion Hatib ibn Abi Balta'ah. Muqawqis is identified by most modern scholars as Cyrus of Alexandria, the Chalcedonian bishop and Byzantine governor of Egypt, though some scholars offer alternative identifications. His response to the Prophet's letter was the most respectful of any of the major rulers who received it. He read it, placed it carefully in an ivory box, and dictated a diplomatic reply acknowledging the Prophet's message with respect but declining to convert, citing his position and the difficulty of changing his religion. He sent valuable gifts: two Coptic women (Maria and her sister Sirin), a thousand gold pieces, twenty fine garments, a mule named Duldul, a donkey named Ya'fur, and a large amount of honey. The Prophet ﷺ accepted the gifts. Maria al-Qibtiyyah became the mother of the Prophet's son Ibrahim. Muqawqis's respectful response contrasts sharply with the arrogance of Khosrow and is part of the Seerah's portrayal of the different ways rulers of the world responded to the final message. When the Muslim armies under Amr ibn al-As conquered Egypt in 641 CE, the Coptic Christian population — who had long been oppressed under Byzantine rule — largely welcomed the conquest.
bio_id: Muqawqis adalah Coptic Christian ruler (atau Byzantine governor) dari Mesir yang received Nabi Muhammad ﷺ's letter di 7 AH / 628 M. The letter adalah carried oleh companion Hatib ibn Abi Balta'ah. Muqawqis adalah identified oleh most modern para ulama as Cyrus dari Aleksandria, Chalcedonian bishop dan Byzantine governor dari Mesir, though some para ulama offer alternative identifications. His response ke Nabi ﷺ's letter adalah most respectful dari any dari major rulers yang received it. He read it, placed it carefully di ivory box, dan dictated diplomatic reply acknowledging Nabi ﷺ's message dengan respect but declining ke mualaf, citing his position dan difficulty dari changing his religion. He sent valuable gifts: two Coptic women (Maria dan her sister Sirin), thousand gold pieces, twenty fine garments, mule named Duldul, donkey named Ya'fur, dan large amount dari honey. The Prophet ﷺ accepted gifts. Maria al-Qibtiyyah became mother dari Nabi ﷺ's son Ibrahim. Muqawqis's respectful response contrasts sharply dengan arrogance dari Khosrow dan adalah part dari Seerah's portrayal dari different ways rulers dari dunia responded ke final message. When Muslim armies under Amr ibn al-As conquered Mesir di 641 M, Coptic Christian population — yang memiliki long been oppressed under Byzantine rule — largely welcomed conquest.

name_full_en: Ka'b ibn al-Ashraf al-Ta'i (Jewish tribal leader of Medina)
bio_short_en: A Jewish leader of Medina who incited war against the Muslims, mocked the Prophet in poetry, and was killed after the Battle of Badr.
bio_en: Ka'b ibn al-Ashraf was a leader of the Banu Nadir Jewish tribe in Medina and one of the most actively hostile opponents of the Prophet ﷺ among the Jewish communities of Medina. He was of mixed Arab and Jewish ancestry — his father was from the Arab tribe of Tayy, his mother from Banu Nadir. He was wealthy, poetically gifted, and influential. After the Battle of Badr (2 AH), when the Muslims achieved their decisive first victory, Ka'b ibn al-Ashraf traveled to Mecca to mourn the Qurayshi dead with elegies (marthiya poems) that glorified the enemies of Islam and incited the Quraysh to renewed war against the Muslims. He composed provocative erotic poetry about the Muslim women — a serious breach of tribal honor norms in Arabia that amounted to a declaration of hostility. When the Prophet ﷺ asked: 'Who will take care of Ka'b ibn al-Ashraf, for he has harmed Allah and His messenger?', the companion Muhammad ibn Maslamah volunteered, along with a small group. They obtained permission to use stratagem, approached Ka'b under the pretense of friendship, and killed him at night near his fortification in 3 AH / 625 CE. His death was a turning point — the other Jewish tribes of Medina recognized that active incitement against the Muslims had serious consequences. The Prophet ﷺ is reported to have said: 'Who killed Ka'b?' When told it was Ibn Maslamah and his companions, he praised them. Ka'b's story illustrates the Prophet's approach: patience with criticism and theological disagreement, but firm response to active incitement of war and violation of the treaty of Medina.
bio_id: Ka'b ibn al-Ashraf adalah seorang leader dari Banu Nadir Jewish tribe di Madinah dan one dari most actively hostile opponents dari Nabi ﷺ among Jewish komunitas-komunitas dari Madinah. Ia adalah dari mixed Arab dan Jewish ancestry — ayahnya adalah dari Arab tribe dari Tayy, ibunya dari Banu Nadir. Ia adalah wealthy, penyairically berbakat, dan berpengaruh. After Battle dari Badr (2 AH), ketika kaum Muslimin achieved their decisive first victory, Ka'b ibn al-Ashraf melakukan perjalanan ke Mekkah ke mourn Qurayshi dead dengan elegies (marthiya poems) yang glorified enemies dari Islam dan incited Quraysh ke renewed war against kaum Muslimin. Ia mengarang provocative erotic penyairry sekitar Muslim women — serious breach dari tribal honor norms di Arabia yang amounted ke declaration dari hostility. When Nabi ﷺ asked: 'Who akan take care dari Ka'b ibn al-Ashraf, untuk he memiliki harmed Allah dan His messenger?', companion Muhammad ibn Maslamah volunteered, sepanjang dengan small group. They obtained permission ke use stratagem, approached Ka'b under pretense dari friendship, dan killed him di night near his fortification di 3 AH / 625 M. His death adalah seorang turning point — other Jewish tribes dari Madinah recognized yang active incitement against kaum Muslimin memiliki serious consequences. The Prophet ﷺ adalah reported ke memiliki said: 'Who killed Ka'b?' When told it adalah Ibn Maslamah dan his companions, he praised them. Ka'b's story illustrates Nabi ﷺ's pendekatan: patience dengan criticism dan theological disagreement, but firm response ke active incitement dari war dan violation dari treaty dari Madinah.

title: Dhul-Qarnayn (the Two-Horned)
bio_short_en: The righteous king who journeyed to the east and west of the earth and built the iron barrier against Ya'juj and Ma'juj — as narrated in Surah al-Kahf.
bio_en: Dhul-Qarnayn (the Two-Horned) is a figure narrated in the Quran (Surah al-Kahf 18:83-98) as a powerful and righteous king whom Allah established on earth and gave the means to accomplish all things. His story was revealed in response to the Qurayshi challenge — they asked the Prophet ﷺ about a man who traveled all over the earth, having been advised by Jews of Medina who knew of such a figure from their scriptures. Dhul-Qarnayn made three great journeys. First, he traveled west until he reached 'a setting place of the sun' — a muddy spring, interpreted as a large body of dark water at the western edge of the known world — and found a people whom he judged: those who did wrong would be punished, those who believed would receive good reward. Second, he traveled to the east until he reached 'a rising place of the sun' — the furthest eastern lands. Third, he traveled between two mountain barriers until he found a people who could barely speak his language (perhaps due to extreme remoteness). They told him of Ya'juj and Ma'juj who were causing destruction in their land and offered tribute if he would build a barrier. Dhul-Qarnayn refused payment and built the great iron and molten copper barrier that sealed them. He declared: 'This is a mercy from my Lord. But when the promise of my Lord comes, He will make it level, and the promise of my Lord is ever true.' His identity has been debated by scholars for centuries. The majority of classical Islamic scholars identify him with a pre-Islamic king who was both powerful and righteous — some say Cyrus the Great of Persia, others say Alexander the Great of Macedon, others say a South Arabian king. The Quran presents him primarily as a moral example: worldly power used righteously, in the service of justice and protection of the weak.
bio_id: Dhul-Qarnayn ( Two-Horned) adalah seorang figure meriwayatkan di Al-Al-Qur' (Surah al-Kahf 18:83-98) as powerful dan righteous king whom Allah established pada earth dan gave means ke accomplish all things. His story adalah revealed di response ke Qurayshi challenge — they asked Nabi ﷺ sekitar man yang traveled all over earth, having been advised oleh Jews dari Madinah yang knew dari such figure dari their scriptures. Dhul-Qarnayn made three great journeys. First, ia melakukan perjalanan west hingga he reached ' setting place dari sun' — muddy spring, interpreted as large body dari dark water di western edge dari known dunia — dan found people whom he hakimd: those yang did wrong akan be punished, those yang believed akan receive good reward. Second, ia melakukan perjalanan ke east hingga he reached ' rising place dari sun' — furthest eastern lands. Third, ia melakukan perjalanan between two mountain barriers hingga he found people yang dapat barely speak his language (perhaps due ke extreme remoteness). They told him dari Ya'juj dan Ma'juj yang adalah causing destruction di their land dan offered tribute if he akan build barrier. Dhul-Qarnayn refused payment dan built great iron dan molten copper barrier yang sealed them. He declared: 'This adalah seorang mercy dari my Lord. But ketika promise dari my Lord comes, He akan make it level, dan promise dari my Lord adalah ever true.' His identity telah menjadi debated oleh para ulama untuk centuries. The majority dari klasik ulama Islams identify him dengan pre-Islamic king yang adalah both powerful dan righteous — some say Cyrus Great dari Persia, others say Alexander Great dari Macedon, others say South Arabian king. The Al-Al-Qur' presents him terutama as moral example: worldly power used righteously, di service dari justice dan protection dari weak.

title: al-Hakim (the Wise)
bio_short_en: The wise man honored in the Quran (Surah Luqman) for his counsel to his son — a model of tawحid, gratitude, prayer, and character.
bio_en: Luqman is one of the figures specifically honored in the Quran — an entire surah (Surah Luqman, Chapter 31) is named after him and records his counsel to his son. He is not a prophet in the mainstream scholarly opinion, but rather a wise man (hakim) to whom Allah gave wisdom: 'And We gave Luqman wisdom: Be grateful to Allah. And whoever is grateful — his gratitude is only for himself. And whoever is ungrateful — then indeed, Allah is Free of need and Praiseworthy' (Surah Luqman 31:12). The Quran then records his advice to his son across five magnificent verses that cover the entirety of Islamic moral and spiritual instruction: do not associate partners with Allah, be grateful to your parents, know that nothing escapes Allah even if it is the weight of a mustard seed hidden in a rock, establish prayer, enjoin good and forbid evil, be patient with what befalls you, do not be arrogant toward people, do not walk in pride upon the earth. This counsel is among the most comprehensive summaries of Islamic character in the entire Quran. The scholars have debated whether Luqman was an Ethiopian, a Nubian, a Sudanese, or a Yemeni man — and whether he was a prophet or a wali. The majority view (Ibn Kathir, al-Qurtubi) is that he was a wise man, not a prophet, elevated by his wisdom and gratitude. He is traditionally identified as a contemporary of the Prophet Dawud AS. Some link him with Aesop of the Greek tradition — a slave from Africa known for his wisdom — though this identification is not established. His counsel remains the Quran's most complete articulation of a father's legacy to his child.
bio_id:
Luqman adalah salah satu tokoh yang secara khusus dimuliakan dalam Al-Qur'an — sebuah surah penuh (Surah Luqman, surah ke-31) dinamai sesuai namanya dan merekam nasihat-nasihatnya kepada putranya. Ia bukan nabi menurut pendapat ilmiah yang dominan, melainkan seorang hamba yang bijaksana (hakim) yang dianugerahi hikmah oleh Allah: "Dan sungguh, telah Kami berikan hikmah kepada Luqman, yaitu: Bersyukurlah kepada Allah. Dan barang siapa bersyukur, maka sesungguhnya dia bersyukur untuk dirinya sendiri; dan barang siapa tidak bersyukur (kufur), maka sesungguhnya Allah Maha Kaya, Maha Terpuji" (Surah Luqman 31:12).

Al-Qur'an kemudian merekam nasihatnya kepada putranya dalam lima ayat yang agung yang mencakup keseluruhan akidah dan akhlak Islam: larangan syirik (31:13), perintah berbakti kepada kedua orang tua (31:14-15), keyakinan bahwa Allah mengetahui segala sesuatu sekecil apa pun (31:16), kewajiban shalat, amar makruf nahi mungkar, dan sabar menghadapi ujian (31:17), serta larangan sombong dan perintah bersikap sederhana dalam berjalan dan berbicara (31:18-19).

Para ulama berbeda pendapat tentang identitas Luqman. Pendapat terkuat adalah bahwa ia seorang hamba (budak) yang berasal dari Afrika — kemungkinan Sudan atau Etiopia — yang hidup pada masa pra-Islam, kemungkinan di zaman Nabi Dawud AS. Beberapa berpendapat ia adalah seorang nabi, namun mayoritas ulama — termasuk Ibnu Katsir — menolak pandangan ini karena tidak ada dalil tegas yang mendukungnya. Kisah-kisah hikmah yang dinisbahkan kepada Luqman sangat banyak dalam literatur Arab, menjadikannya sosok legendaris kearifan dan kesederhanaan yang tulus.

name_full_en: The Seven Sleepers of the Cave
bio_short_en: The group of young men who took refuge in a cave from a pagan king and were caused to sleep by Allah for 309 years — narrated in Surah al-Kahf.
bio_en: Ashab al-Kahf (the Companions of the Cave, also known in Christian tradition as the Seven Sleepers of Ephesus) are a group of young men narrated in the Quran (Surah al-Kahf 18:9-26) as a sign of the resurrection and the power of Allah. Their story was one of the three questions posed to the Prophet ﷺ by the Quraysh (on the advice of the Jews of Medina) to test his prophethood. The young men lived during the reign of a pagan king who forced his subjects to worship idols under penalty of death. These youths were among the few monotheists who refused. They sought refuge in a cave outside the city, praying: 'Our Lord, grant us from Yourself mercy and prepare for us from our affair right guidance.' Allah caused them to sleep in the cave. A dog accompanied them at the entrance — mentioned specifically in the Quran, which notes three, five, or seven sleepers with the eighth being the dog, though stating certainty lies with Allah. They slept for 309 years (Quran: 300 solar years, which equals 309 lunar years — a Quranic mathematical miracle noted by classical scholars). When they awoke, they thought they had slept a day or half a day. One went to buy food in the city and was discovered when he produced centuries-old coins. The whole city was by then largely Christian, and the king received the news with joy as a confirmation of the resurrection. The young men then died. Their story is one of the Quran's supreme examples of tawakkul (reliance on Allah), the reality of the afterlife, and the protection Allah provides for those who flee persecution to preserve their faith.
bio_id: Ashab al-Kahf (para Sahabat of the Cave, also dikenal in Christian tradisi as the Seven Sleepers of Ephesus) adalah a group of young men meriwayatkan in Al-Al-Qur'an (Surah al-Kahf 18:9-26) as a sign of the resurrection and the power of Allah. Their story adalah salah satu yang three questions posed to Nabi ﷺ by the Quraysh (on the advice of the Jews of Madinah) to test his prophethood. The young men lived during the reign of a pagan king who forced his subjects to ibadah idols under penalty of death. These youths adalah among the few monotheists who refused. They sought refuge in a cave outside the city, praying: 'Our Lord, grant us from Yourself mercy and prepare for us from our affair right guidance.' Allah caused them to sleep in the cave. A dog accompanied them at the entrance — mentioned specifically in Al-Al-Qur'an, which notes three, five, or seven sleepers with the eighth being the dog, though stating certainty lies with Allah. They slept for 309 tahun (Al-Al-Qur'an: 300 solar tahun, which equals 309 lunar tahun — a Al-Al-Qur'anic mathematical miracle noted by klasik para ulama). When they awoke, they thought they memiliki slept a day or half a day. One went to buy food in the city and adalah discovered when ia menghasilkan abad-abad-old coins. The whole city adalah by then largely Christian, and the king received the news with joy as a confirmation of the resurrection. The young men then wafat. Their story adalah salah satu yang Al-Al-Qur'an's supreme examples of tawakkal (reliance on Allah), the reality of the afterlife, and the protection Allah provides for those who flee persecution to preserve their faith.

name_full_en: Ya'juj and Ma'juj — the peoples behind the barrier of Dhul-Qarnayn
bio_short_en: The destructive peoples sealed behind the iron barrier of Dhul-Qarnayn who will be released near the Day of Judgment as a major sign.
bio_en: Ya'juj and Ma'juj (Gog and Magog) are peoples mentioned in the Quran (Surah al-Anbiya 21:96 and Surah al-Kahf 18:94-98) and extensively in hadith as one of the major signs of the Day of Judgment. In the story of Dhul-Qarnayn, a people came to him complaining of these destructive peoples — Ya'juj and Ma'juj — who were causing corruption in the land. Dhul-Qarnayn built a massive iron and molten copper barrier to contain them. The barrier is described as preventing their exit until the appointed time of Allah. The Prophet Muhammad ﷺ described Ya'juj and Ma'juj in detail in numerous hadith. A hole in the barrier grows each night until one day they will say 'We will complete this tomorrow, inshallah,' and return to find it as they left it. On that day they will pour through, filling the earth. The Prophet ﷺ described them as coming in such numbers that they will drink all the rivers dry, including the Tigris and the Euphrates and the Sea of Tiberias. After Isa AS kills the Dajjal and the Muslims pray with him, Ya'juj and Ma'juj will be released and no army will be able to withstand them. Isa AS and the believers will retreat to Mount Sinai and supplicate to Allah. Allah will send a worm (like the worm of Nuh's ark, al-Nawaf) that will kill them all overnight. Their decomposing bodies will cause a terrible smell until Allah sends rain to cleanse the earth. They are also referenced in Biblical prophecy in the books of Ezekiel and Revelation. Islamic tradition places their eventual release as a sign of the imminent end of the world.
bio_id:
Ya'juj dan Ma'juj (Gog dan Magog) adalah kaum yang disebutkan dalam Al-Qur'an (Surah Al-Anbiya 21:96 dan Surah Al-Kahf 18:94-98) dan secara luas dalam hadits sebagai salah satu tanda-tanda besar Hari Kiamat. Dalam kisah Dzulqarnayn, suatu kaum datang kepadanya mengadu tentang dua kaum perusak ini — Ya'juj dan Ma'juj — yang menyebarkan kerusakan di muka bumi. Dzulqarnayn membangun penghalang besar dari besi dan tembaga yang meleleh untuk mengurung mereka. Penghalang itu digambarkan akan mencegah keluarnya mereka hingga waktu yang telah ditetapkan Allah.

Nabi Muhammad ﷺ menggambarkan Ya'juj dan Ma'juj secara rinci dalam banyak hadits. Salah satu tanda mendekati Hari Kiamat adalah keluarnya mereka setelah Isa AS membunuh Dajjal. Mereka akan muncul dalam jumlah yang sangat besar dan menutupi bumi, minum habis air-air besar, dan menyebabkan kehancuran massal. Nabi Isa AS dan orang-orang beriman akan berlindung di Gunung Thursina (Sinai), dan Allah akan membinasakan Ya'juj dan Ma'juj melalui suatu wabah yang menembus leher mereka sehingga mereka mati semuanya dalam satu malam.

Para ulama berbeda pendapat tentang lokasi penghalang Dzulqarnayn, apakah ia masih ada atau sudah hilang, serta tentang sifat Ya'juj dan Ma'juj. Mayoritas ulama Salaf menyatakan bahwa mereka adalah kaum manusia nyata yang terpenjarakan di balik penghalang tersebut. Beberapa ulama mengaitkan mereka dengan bangsa-bangsa dari wilayah utara yang pernah menyerbu dunia Islam pada masa lalu. Walau bagaimanapun, hadits tentang mereka bersifat mutawatir dan keberadaannya serta kemunculannya di akhir zaman termasuk dalam akidah Islam yang wajib diimani.

name_full_en: Uzayr ibn Sarhiyyah — identified with Ezra the scribe
bio_short_en: A righteous man whom Allah caused to die for 100 years and then resurrected as a sign, mentioned in the Quran and venerated by some Jews as the son of God — a claim the Quran firmly rejects.
bio_en: Uzayr is mentioned in the Quran in two places. The first (Surah al-Baqarah 2:259) narrates a man who passed by a ruined town and asked: 'How will Allah bring this to life after its death?' Allah caused him to die for 100 years, then resurrected him and showed him how his food had not rotted but his donkey's bones had turned to dust, then were restored before his eyes. The experience was a direct, personal proof of resurrection. Most scholars identify this man as Uzayr (Ezra). The second mention (Surah al-Tawbah 9:30) is more pointed: 'The Jews say: Uzayr is the son of Allah' — a claim the Quran attributes to some Jews of that era and firmly rejects, equating it with the Christian claim about Jesus. 'Allah's curse be upon them — how are they deluded?' The Jewish veneration of Ezra as the preserver and restorer of the Torah after the Babylonian captivity appears to have been extreme enough in some communities to border on divine attribution. Uzayr is identified in Islamic tradition with Ezra of the Biblical record — the scribe-priest who led Jews from Babylon back to Jerusalem after the Persian conquest and reconstituted the Torah. He is one of the figures whose exact status in Islam (prophet, wali, or just a righteous man) is not definitively settled by the Quran or Sunnah. The Quran's primary use of him is as evidence of the resurrection and as a rebuttal of the claim of divine sonship.
bio_id: Uzayr adalah mentioned in Al-Al-Qur'an in two places. The first (Surah al-Baqarah 2:259) narrates a man who passed by a ruined town and asked: 'How will Allah bring this to life after its death?' Allah caused him to die for 100 tahun, then resurrected him and showed him how his food memiliki not rotted but his donkey's bones memiliki turned to dust, then adalah restored before his eyes. The experience adalah seorang direct, personal proof of resurrection. Most para ulama identify this man as Uzayr (Ezra). The second mention (Surah al-Tawbah 9:30) adalah more pointed: 'The Jews say: Uzayr adalah son of Allah' — a claim Al-Al-Qur'an attributes to some Jews of that era and firmly rejects, equating it with the Christian claim about Jesus. 'Allah's curse be upon them — how adalah they deluded?' The Jewish veneration of Ezra as the preserver and restorer of the Torah after the Babylonian captivity appears to telah menjadi extreme enough in some komunitas-komunitas to border on divine attribution. Uzayr adalah identified in tradisi Islam with Ezra of the Biblical record — the scribe-priest who led Jews from Babylon back to Yerusalem after the Persian conquest and reconstituted the Torah. He adalah salah satu yang figures whose exact status in Islam (prophet, wali, or just a righteous man) adalah not definitively settled by Al-Al-Qur'an or Sunnah. The Al-Al-Qur'an's primary use of him adalah as evidence of the resurrection and as a rebuttal of the claim of divine sonship.

title: al-Masih al-Dajjal
bio_short_en: The greatest trial before the Day of Judgment — the one-eyed False Messiah who will claim divinity and be killed by the return of Isa AS.
bio_en: Al-Masih al-Dajjal (the False Messiah) is the greatest trial that humanity will face before the Day of Judgment, described in extensive detail in the hadith of the Prophet Muhammad ﷺ. The Prophet ﷺ warned: 'There is no prophet between Adam and myself except that he warned his people about al-Dajjal.' He is described as a man, one-eyed (his right eye is like a floating grape, blind and discolored), with the word 'kafir' (disbeliever) written between his eyes — which every Muslim, literate or not, will be able to read. He will emerge from between Syria and Iraq (according to some narrations, from Khorasan — the east), followed initially by 70,000 Jews of Isfahan wearing Persian shawls. He will travel the entire earth in forty days (the first day like a year, the second like a month, the third like a week, then normal days). He will command rain and crops to grow, command the earth to yield its treasures, and command the sky to withhold rain — trials that will deceive many. He will claim to be the Messiah, then to be Lord. He will be given power to produce fire and apparent paradise and hell — which are actually the reverse. The Prophet ﷺ advised: 'Whoever hears of the Dajjal, let him flee from him. By Allah, a man will think he is a believer and follow him because of his confusing trials.' His only limits: he cannot enter Mecca or Medina. He will be killed by Isa ibn Maryam AS at the gate of Ludd (Lod, Israel) at the end of the battle. The Prophet ﷺ said: 'From the time of Adam until the Hour, there is no creation greater in trial than al-Dajjal.'
bio_id: Al-Masih al-Dajjal (the False Messiah) adalah greatest trial that humanity will face before the Day of Judgment, digambarkan in extensive detail in the hadith of Nabi Muhammad ﷺ. The Nabi ﷺ warned: 'There adalah no prophet between Adam and myself except that he warned his people about al-Dajjal.' He adalah digambarkan sebagai a man, one-eyed (his right eye adalah like a floating grape, blind and discolored), with the word 'kafir' (disbeliever) written between his eyes — which every Muslim, literate or not, will be able to read. He will emerge from between Suriah and Irak (according to some riwayat-riwayat, from Khorasan — the east), followed pada awalnya by 70,000 Jews of Isfahan wearing Persian shawls. He will travel the entire earth in forty days (the first day like a year, the second like a month, the third like a week, then normal days). He will command rain and crops to grow, command the earth to yield its treasures, and command the sky to withhold rain — trials that will deceive many. He will claim to be the Messiah, then to be Lord. He will be given power to produce fire and apparent paradise and hell — which adalah actually the reverse. The Nabi ﷺ advised: 'Whoever hears of the Dajjal, let him flee from him. By Allah, a man will think he adalah seorang believer and follow him because of his confusing trials.' His only limits: he cannot enter Mekkah or Madinah. He will be killed by Isa ibn Maryam AS at the gate of Ludd (Lod, Israel) at the end of the battle. The Nabi ﷺ said: 'From the time of Adam until the Hour, there adalah no creation greater in trial than al-Dajjal.'

name_full_en: al-Aziz — the chief minister of Egypt in the story of Yusuf
title: al-Aziz (the Chief Minister)
bio_short_en: The Egyptian minister who purchased Yusuf AS from the slave traders and gave him a position of trust in his household.
bio_en: Al-Aziz was the chief minister or treasurer of Egypt during the time of Prophet Yusuf AS. He is identified in Islamic tradition as holding the title al-Aziz (meaning 'the mighty one' or 'the minister'), and classical sources including Ibn Kathir name him Qitfir ibn Rahib. He corresponds to Potiphar in the Biblical account. When the slave traders who pulled Yusuf AS from the well brought him to Egypt and sold him, al-Aziz purchased him. The Quran records his purchase and his instructions to his wife: 'Make his stay comfortable. Perhaps he will benefit us or we will adopt him as a son' (Surah Yusuf 12:21). Al-Aziz recognized something extraordinary in the young Yusuf. His household prospered with Yusuf's presence. The central trial of Yusuf's time in al-Aziz's house was the attempt by al-Aziz's wife Zulaykha to seduce him, and Yusuf's flight and her false accusation. When al-Aziz came upon the scene — his wife blaming Yusuf, Yusuf protesting innocence — it was al-Aziz's own cousin who solved the case with the logic of the torn shirt. Al-Aziz acknowledged the truth: 'Indeed, it is of the women's plan. Indeed, your plan is great.' He asked Yusuf to keep quiet about the incident. When the women of the city later gossiped about Zulaykha, al-Aziz attended the dinner she arranged as a demonstration. After Yusuf was imprisoned (at Zulaykha's insistence), al-Aziz fades from the narrative. He is a figure of measured justice — not a villain but a man whose weakness in handling his wife's infatuation led to an innocent man's imprisonment. He serves as the backdrop against which Yusuf's extraordinary character shines.
bio_id: Al-Aziz adalah ketua minister atau treasurer dari Mesir selama time dari Prophet Yusuf AS. Ia adalah identified di tradisi Islam as holding title al-Aziz (meaning ' mighty one' atau ' minister'), dan klasik sources including Ibn Kathir name him Qitfir ibn Rahib. He corresponds ke Potiphar di Biblical account. When slave traders yang pulled Yusuf AS dari well brought him ke Mesir dan sold him, al-Aziz purchased him. The Al-Al-Qur' records his purchase dan his instructions ke istrinya: 'Make his stay comfortable. Perhaps he akan benefit us atau we akan adopt him as son' (Surah Yusuf 12:21). Al-Aziz recognized something extraordinary di young Yusuf. His household prospered dengan Yusuf's presence. The central trial dari Yusuf's time di al-Aziz's house adalah attempt oleh al-Aziz's wife Zulaykha ke seduce him, dan Yusuf's flight dan her false accusation. When al-Aziz came upon scene — istrinya blaming Yusuf, Yusuf protesting innocence — it adalah al-Aziz's own cousin yang solved case dengan logic dari torn shirt. Al-Aziz acilmu pengetahuand truth: 'Indeed, it adalah dari women's plan. Indeed, your plan adalah great.' He asked Yusuf ke keep quiet sekitar incident. When women dari city later gossiped sekitar Zulaykha, al-Aziz attended dinner she arranged as demonstration. After Yusuf adalah imprisoned (di Zulaykha's insistence), al-Aziz fades dari narrative. Ia adalah figure dari measured justice — tidak villain but man whose weakness di handling istrinya's infatuation led ke innocent man's imprisonment. He serves as backdrop against yang Yusuf's extraordinary character shines.

name_full_en: Rahil (Rachel), wife of Prophet Yaqub and mother of Yusuf and Binyamin
bio_short_en: The beloved wife of Prophet Yaqub AS and mother of Yusuf and Binyamin — the wife for whom Yaqub worked fourteen years.
bio_en: Rachel (Rahil in Arabic Islamic tradition) was the most beloved wife of Prophet Yaqub AS (Jacob) and the mother of his two most beloved sons: Yusuf AS and Binyamin (Benjamin). Her story is part of the background of Surah Yusuf and is known more fully through Islamic historical tradition and the commentary of classical scholars including Ibn Kathir in al-Bidaya wal-Nihaya. When Yaqub AS fled from his brother Esau and traveled to his uncle Laban in Padan-Aram (Haran), he fell in love with Rachel, the younger and more beautiful daughter of Laban. He worked for his uncle for seven years in exchange for her hand. Laban deceived him on the wedding night by substituting his older daughter Leah (Liya in Arabic tradition). Yaqub had to work another seven years to marry Rachel as well — making fourteen years of labor for the wife he loved. Rachel bore Yaqub two sons: Yusuf AS, the most beloved, and Binyamin, her last son, during whose birth she died. The death of Rachel in childbirth left Yaqub AS in profound grief and intensified his love for her sons Yusuf and Binyamin. Yusuf's half-brothers' jealousy was fueled in part by the favoritism they perceived Yaqub showed toward Rachel's children. Rachel's death before the great events of the Yusuf story means she never witnessed her son's rise to power in Egypt or the family reunion. She is one of the most beloved figures of the Abrahamic tradition across Islam, Judaism, and Christianity.
bio_id: Rachel (Rahil di Arabic tradisi Islam) adalah most beloved wife dari Prophet Yaqub AS (Jacob) dan mother dari his two most beloved sons: Yusuf AS dan Binyamin (Benjamin). Her story adalah part dari background dari Surah Yusuf dan adalah known more fully melalui Islamic historis tradisi dan commentary dari klasik para ulama including Ibn Kathir di al-Bidaya wal-Nihaya. When Yaqub AS fled dari saudaranya Esau dan melakukan perjalanan ke pamannya Laban di Padan-Aram (Haran), he fell di love dengan Rachel, younger dan more beautiful daughter dari Laban. He worked untuk pamannya untuk seven tahun di exchange untuk her hand. Laban deceived him pada wedding night oleh substituting his older daughter Leah (Liya di Arabic tradisi). Yaqub memiliki ke work another seven tahun ke marry Rachel as well — making fourteen tahun dari labor untuk wife he loved. Rachel bore Yaqub two sons: Yusuf AS, most beloved, dan Binyamin, her last son, selama whose birth she died. The death dari Rachel di childbirth left Yaqub AS di profound grief dan intensified his love untuk her sons Yusuf dan Binyamin. Yusuf's half-brothers' jealousy adalah fueled di part oleh favoritism they perceived Yaqub showed toward Rachel's children. Rachel's death sebelum great events dari Yusuf story means she never witnessed her son's rise ke power di Mesir atau family reunion. She adalah one dari most beloved figures dari Abrahamic tradisi di seluruh Islam, Judaism, dan Christianity.

name_full_en: al-Hawariyyun — the disciples of Prophet Isa AS
title: al-Hawariyyun (the Purified Supporters)
bio_short_en: The dedicated companions of Prophet Isa AS who declared 'We are the helpers of Allah' — mentioned in the Quran as a model of full commitment to a prophet.
bio_en: Al-Hawariyyun (the Disciples) were the close companions of Prophet Isa AS who believed in him and supported his mission. They are mentioned multiple times in the Quran (Surah Ali Imran 3:52, Surah al-Maidah 5:111-115, Surah al-Saff 61:14) and are presented as the model of full and unconditional commitment to a prophet. When Isa AS sensed disbelief around him, he called out: 'Who are my helpers toward Allah?' The Hawariyyun responded: 'We are the helpers of Allah. We believed in Allah, and bear witness that we are Muslims.' Their response to Isa's question in Surah al-Saff (61:14) is used by the Quran to call the believers to the same level of commitment: 'O you who have believed, be helpers of Allah as when Isa said to the disciples: Who are my helpers for Allah? The disciples said: We are the helpers of Allah.' In Surah al-Maidah (5:111-115), the disciples requested a table spread from heaven — a miraculous meal — as a confirmation of their faith and as a sign. Isa AS warned them of severe consequences if they disbelieved after seeing it, and Allah sent it. This table (al-Maidah) is what the fifth chapter of the Quran is named after. The Islamic understanding of the Hawariyyun differs from the Christian account in that Islam does not affirm the crucifixion, so the events surrounding the arrest and claimed death of Jesus are understood differently. The Hawariyyun continued spreading the original message of Isa AS after his elevation, though their teachings were subsequently altered. The number of twelve disciples is not specified in the Quran; that comes from Biblical tradition.
bio_id: Al-Hawariyyun (the Disciples) adalah close companions of Nabi Isa AS who believed in him and supported his mission. They adalah mentioned multiple times in Al-Al-Qur'an (Surah Ali Imran 3:52, Surah al-Maidah 5:111-115, Surah al-Saff 61:14) and adalah presented as the model of full and unconditional commitment to a prophet. When Isa AS sensed disbelief around him, he disebut out: 'Who adalah my helpers toward Allah?' The Hawariyyun responded: 'We adalah helpers of Allah. We believed in Allah, and bear witness that we adalah kaum Muslimin.' Their response to Isa's question in Surah al-Saff (61:14) adalah used by Al-Al-Qur'an to call the believers to the same level of commitment: 'O you who memiliki believed, be helpers of Allah as when Isa said to the murids: Who adalah my helpers for Allah? The murids said: We adalah helpers of Allah.' In Surah al-Maidah (5:111-115), the murids requested a table spread from heaven — a miraculous meal — as a confirmation of their faith and as a sign. Isa AS warned them of severe consequences if they disbelieved after seeing it, and Allah sent it. This table (al-Maidah) adalah what the fifth bab of Al-Al-Qur'an adalah dinamai after. The Islamic understanding of the Hawariyyun differs from the Christian account in that Islam does not affirm the crucifixion, so the events surrounding the arrest and claimed death of Jesus adalah understood differently. The Hawariyyun continued spreading the original message of Isa AS after his elevation, though their ajaran-ajaran adalah kemudian altered. The number of twelve murids adalah not specified in Al-Al-Qur'an; that comes from Biblical tradisi.

name: Zaynab bint Muhammad
bio_short_en: Eldest daughter of Prophet Muhammad and Khadijah. Married Abu al-As ibn al-Rabi before Islam; he later embraced Islam and they were reunited in Madinah. Died 8 AH.
bio_en: Zaynab bint Muhammad was the eldest daughter of Prophet Muhammad and Khadijah bint Khuwaylid, born approximately 13 years before the hijra. She was the first of the Prophet's children. She married Abu al-As ibn al-Rabi, a respected Meccan merchant and nephew of Khadijah, before the revelation of Islam. When the Prophet received revelation and Khadijah embraced Islam, Zaynab followed in faith. Abu al-As did not initially accept Islam, creating a painful separation. After the Battle of Badr, Abu al-As was captured by the Muslims. Zaynab sent her necklace — a gift from her mother Khadijah — as ransom. The Prophet was visibly moved and requested the companions to release Abu al-As without ransom, on the condition that he return Zaynab to Madinah. Abu al-As fulfilled this promise, and Zaynab emigrated to Madinah. Years later, Abu al-As embraced Islam sincerely, and the Prophet reunited them. Zaynab died in 8 AH, leaving behind a daughter Umamah bint Abi al-As, who was beloved by the Prophet — he is reported to have carried her on his shoulder during prayers when she was young.
bio_id: Zaynab bint Muhammad adalah eldest daughter of Nabi Muhammad ﷺ and Khadijah bint Khuwaylid, lahir sekitar 13 tahun before the hijra. She adalah first of Nabi ﷺ's children. She married Abu al-As ibn al-Rabi, a respected Mekkahn merchant and nephew of Khadijah, before the revelation of Islam. When Nabi ﷺ received revelation and Khadijah embraced Islam, Zaynab followed in faith. Abu al-As did not pada awalnya accept Islam, creating a painful separation. After the Battle of Badr, Abu al-As adalah captured by the kaum Muslimin. Zaynab sent her necklace — a gift from ibunya Khadijah — as ransom. The Nabi adalah visibly moved and requested the companions to release Abu al-As without ransom, on the condition that he return Zaynab to Madinah. Abu al-As fulfilled this promise, and Zaynab emigrated to Madinah. Years later, Abu al-As embraced Islam sincerely, and Nabi ﷺ reunited them. Zaynab wafat di 8 AH, leaving behind a daughter Umamah bint Abi al-As, who adalah beloved by Nabi ﷺ — he adalah reported to memiliki carried her on his shoulder during shalats when she adalah young.

name: Ruqayyah bint Muhammad
bio_short_en: Second daughter of Prophet Muhammad and Khadijah. Married Uthman ibn Affan and emigrated with him to Abyssinia, then Madinah. Died 2 AH while the Prophet was at Badr.
bio_en: Ruqayyah bint Muhammad was the second daughter of Prophet Muhammad and Khadijah bint Khuwaylid. She was initially betrothed to Utbah ibn Abi Lahab before the Prophet's mission. When Surah al-Masad (111) was revealed, Abu Lahab ordered his son to divorce her. This freed her to marry Uthman ibn Affan, who would later become the third caliph. Ruqayyah and Uthman were among the earliest Muslims and were among the first to emigrate to Abyssinia (Habasha) in the first hijra, seeking refuge from Meccan persecution under the Christian king Najashi. They later returned to Makkah and then emigrated to Madinah. Ruqayyah fell ill during the period of the Battle of Badr, and Uthman stayed behind to care for her at the Prophet's instruction. She died while the Muslims were still at Badr, and the Prophet received the news of victory alongside the news of his daughter's death. She was buried in al-Baqi cemetery in Madinah.
bio_id: Ruqayyah bint Muhammad adalah second daughter of Nabi Muhammad ﷺ and Khadijah bint Khuwaylid. She adalah pada awalnya betrothed to Utbah ibn Abi Lahab before Nabi ﷺ's mission. When Surah al-Masad (111) adalah revealed, Abu Lahab ordered putranya to divorce her. This freed her to marry Uthman ibn Affan, who would later become the third khalifah. Ruqayyah and Uthman adalah among the earliest kaum Muslimin and adalah among the first to emigrate to Abyssinia (Habasha) in the first hijra, seeking refuge from Mekkahn persecution under the Christian king Najashi. They later returned to Makkah and then emigrated to Madinah. Ruqayyah fell ill during the periode of the Battle of Badr, and Uthman stayed behind to care for her at Nabi ﷺ's instruction. She wafat while the kaum Muslimin adalah still at Badr, and Nabi ﷺ received the news of victory alongside the news of putrinya's death. She adalah buried in al-Baqi cemetery in Madinah.

name: Umm Kulthum bint Muhammad
name_en: Umm Kulthum, daughter of the Prophet
bio_short_en: Third daughter of Prophet Muhammad and Khadijah. After sister Ruqayyah died, she married Uthman ibn Affan, earning him the epithet Dhul-Nurayn (Possessor of Two Lights) for marrying two daughters of the Prophet.
bio_en: Umm Kulthum bint Muhammad was the third daughter of Prophet Muhammad and Khadijah bint Khuwaylid. Like her sister Ruqayyah, she was initially betrothed to one of Abu Lahab's sons (Utaybah) before Islam. When Surah al-Masad was revealed, Abu Lahab ordered the betrothals dissolved. Umm Kulthum embraced Islam with her family and emigrated to Madinah. After her sister Ruqayyah died in 2 AH while married to Uthman ibn Affan, the Prophet offered Umm Kulthum in marriage to Uthman. This marriage gave Uthman his famous epithet 'Dhul-Nurayn' — possessor of two lights — for having the honor of marrying two daughters of the Prophet consecutively. Umm Kulthum lived with Uthman until her death in 9 AH without leaving surviving children. The Prophet personally participated in preparing her body for burial. She was buried in al-Baqi cemetery in Madinah.
bio_id: Umm Kulthum bint Muhammad adalah third daughter of Nabi Muhammad ﷺ and Khadijah bint Khuwaylid. Like her sister Ruqayyah, she adalah pada awalnya betrothed to one of Abu Lahab's sons (Utaybah) before Islam. When Surah al-Masad adalah revealed, Abu Lahab ordered the betrothals dissolved. Umm Kulthum embraced Islam with keluarganya and emigrated to Madinah. After her sister Ruqayyah wafat di 2 AH while married to Uthman ibn Affan, Nabi ﷺ offered Umm Kulthum in marriage to Uthman. This marriage gave Uthman his famous epithet 'Dhul-Nurayn' — possessor of two lights — for having the honor of marrying two daughters of Nabi ﷺ consecutively. Umm Kulthum lived with Uthman until wafatnya in 9 AH without leaving surviving children. The Nabi personally participated in preparing her body for burial. She adalah buried in al-Baqi cemetery in Madinah.

name: al-Qasim ibn Muhammad
bio_short_en: The first son of Prophet Muhammad and Khadijah, who died in infancy. The Prophet was given his kunya Abu al-Qasim because of him. Enemies who mocked the Prophet as 'abtar' (without legacy) after his sons' deaths were answered by Surah al-Kawthar.
bio_en: Al-Qasim ibn Muhammad was the firstborn son of Prophet Muhammad and Khadijah bint Khuwaylid, born before the revelation of Islam in Makkah. He is the reason the Prophet carried the kunya 'Abu al-Qasim' — Father of Qasim. Al-Qasim died in infancy before he could walk or speak, according to the most accepted reports. His death was a source of grief for both the Prophet and Khadijah. The Arabs of the time called a man whose sons died in infancy 'abtar' (cut off without legacy), and it was partly in response to taunts from the Prophet's enemies after the deaths of his sons that Surah al-Kawthar (108) was revealed: 'Indeed, your enemy is the one who is cut off.' The Prophet's true legacy was the entire Muslim ummah. Al-Qasim was the eldest of the Prophet's children and the first to precede him in death. He was buried in Makkah.
bio_id: Al-Qasim ibn Muhammad adalah firstborn son dari Nabi Muhammad ﷺ dan Khadijah bint Khuwaylid, born sebelum revelation dari Islam di Makkah. Ia adalah reason Nabi ﷺ carried kunya 'Abu al-Qasim' — Father dari Qasim. Al-Qasim wafat di infancy sebelum he dapat walk atau speak, according ke most accepted reports. His death adalah seorang source dari grief untuk both Nabi ﷺ dan Khadijah. The Arabs dari time called man whose sons wafat di infancy 'abtar' (cut off tanpa warisan), dan it adalah partly di response ke taunts dari Nabi ﷺ's enemies setelah deaths dari putranyas yang Surah al-Kawthar (108) adalah revealed: 'Indeed, your enemy adalah one yang adalah cut off.' The Prophet's true warisan adalah entire Muslim ummah. Al-Qasim adalah eldest dari Nabi ﷺ's children dan first ke precede him di death. Ia adalah buried di Makkah.

name: Ibrahim ibn Muhammad
bio_short_en: The last child of Prophet Muhammad, born to Maria al-Qibtiyyah in 8 AH. Died as an infant at 18 months. On the day of his death a solar eclipse occurred; the Prophet clarified it was a sign of Allah's majesty, not connected to Ibrahim's death.
bio_en: Ibrahim ibn Muhammad was the youngest child and last son of Prophet Muhammad, born to Maria al-Qibtiyyah — the Coptic concubine gifted to the Prophet by the ruler of Egypt — in 8 AH in Madinah. His birth brought immense joy to the Prophet, who named him Ibrahim after the great patriarch. Ibrahim was nursed by a woman named Umm Sayf (wife of Abu Sayf, a blacksmith of Madinah) as well as by Maria. The Prophet visited him regularly and was deeply attached to this son. Ibrahim died in infancy at approximately 16-18 months in 10 AH. On the day of his death, a solar eclipse occurred. Some companions thought the eclipse was a sign of mourning for the Prophet's son. The Prophet addressed this directly in a sermon: 'The sun and the moon are two of Allah's signs. They do not eclipse because of the death or life of anyone. When you see them, supplicate to Allah and pray.' Upon visiting his dying son, the Prophet's eyes filled with tears and he said: 'The eyes shed tears and the heart grieves, but we say only what pleases our Lord. O Ibrahim, we are grieved by your parting.' Ibrahim's death meant the Prophet left this world with no surviving male descendants — fulfilling Surah al-Kawthar's promise that it is his enemy who is truly cut off. He was buried in al-Baqi cemetery.
bio_id: Ibrahim ibn Muhammad adalah youngest child dan last son dari Nabi Muhammad ﷺ, born ke Maria al-Qibtiyyah — Coptic concubine berbakat ke Nabi ﷺ oleh ruler dari Mesir — di 8 AH di Madinah. His birth brought immense joy ke Nabi ﷺ, yang named him Ibrahim setelah great patriarch. Ibrahim adalah nursed oleh woman named Umm Sayf (wife dari Abu Sayf, blacksmith dari Madinah) serta oleh Maria. The Prophet visited him regularly dan adalah deeply attached ke tputranya. Ibrahim wafat di infancy di sekitar 16-18 bulan di 10 AH. On day dari wafatnya, solar eclipse occurred. Some companions thought eclipse adalah seorang sign dari mourning untuk Nabi ﷺ's son. The Prophet addressed this directly di sermon: 'The sun dan moon adalah two dari Allah's signs. They do tidak eclipse because dari death atau life dari anyone. When you see them, supplicate ke Allah dan shalat.' Upon visiting his dying son, Nabi ﷺ's eyes filled dengan tears dan he said: 'The eyes shed tears dan heart grieves, but we say hanya what pleases our Lord. O Ibrahim, we adalah grieved oleh your parting.' Ibrahim's death meant Nabi ﷺ left this dunia dengan tidak surviving male descendants — fulfilling Surah al-Kawthar's promise yang it adalah his enemy yang adalah truly cut off. Ia adalah buried di al-Baqi cemetery.

name: Thuwayba al-Aslamiyyah
name_en: Thuwayba, first wet nurse of the Prophet
bio_short_en: The first wet nurse of Prophet Muhammad, a slave of Abu Lahab who nursed him for several days before Halimah al-Sa'diyyah took over. Abu Lahab freed her upon hearing the news of the Prophet's birth. Also nursed Hamza ibn Abd al-Muttalib, making him the Prophet's milk-brother.
bio_en: Thuwayba al-Aslamiyyah was a slave-woman belonging to Abu Lahab, the Prophet's uncle. She was the first woman to nurse the infant Muhammad after his mother Aminah bint Wahb, nursing him for several days in the earliest period of his life. According to the biographical sources, when Abu Lahab heard the news of his nephew Muhammad's birth through his slave Thuwayba, he was so pleased that he freed her on the spot. This act of joy would have consequences: it is reported that after Abu Lahab's death, he was seen in a dream in a pitiful state. When asked if he had any relief from punishment, he replied that he sucked water from his finger — the finger with which he had pointed to free Thuwayba. This narration, mentioned in Sahih al-Bukhari in a chapter on wet-nursing, is cited as evidence that even a disbeliever's joy at the Prophet's birth brought some diminishment of punishment. Thuwayba also nursed Hamza ibn Abd al-Muttalib, the Prophet's uncle, making Hamza and Muhammad milk-brothers — a bond the Prophet acknowledged throughout his life. She had a son named Masruh who died before her. After Thuwayba, Halimah al-Sa'diyyah took over as the Prophet's primary wet nurse and raised him in the desert. Thuwayba is thought to have embraced Islam and died around 7 AH. The Prophet showed her kindness throughout his life, sending her gifts from Madinah, and continued to care for her memory after her death.
bio_id: Thuwayba al-Aslamiyyah adalah seorang slave-woman belonging ke Abu Lahab, Nabi ﷺ's uncle. She adalah yang pertama woman ke nurse infant Muhammad setelah ibunya Aminah bint Wahb, nursing him untuk several days di earliest periode dari kehidupannya. According ke biographical sources, ketika Abu Lahab heard news dari his nephew Muhammad's birth melalui his slave Thuwayba, ia adalah so pleased yang he freed her pada spot. This act dari joy akan memiliki consequences: it adalah reported yang setelah Abu Lahab's death, ia adalah seen di dream di pitiful state. When asked if he memiliki any relief dari punishment, he replied yang he sucked water dari his finger — finger dengan yang he memiliki pointed ke free Thuwayba. This riwayat, mentioned di Sahih al-Bukhari di bab pada wet-nursing, adalah cited as evidence yang bahkan disbeliever's joy di Nabi ﷺ's birth brought some diminishment dari punishment. Thuwayba juga nursed Hamza ibn Abd al-Muttalib, Nabi ﷺ's uncle, making Hamza dan Muhammad milk-brothers — bond Nabi ﷺ acilmu pengetahuand throughout kehidupannya. She memiliki son named Masruh yang died sebelum her. After Thuwayba, Halimah al-Sa'diyyah took over as Nabi ﷺ's primary wet nurse dan raised him di desert. Thuwayba adalah thought ke memiliki embraced Islam dan wafat sekitar 7 AH. The Prophet showed her kindness throughout kehidupannya, sending her gifts dari Madinah, dan continued ke care untuk her memory setelah her death.

name: Liya bint Laban
bio_short_en: The elder daughter of Laban and first wife of Prophet Yaqub AS (Jacob), mother of Lawi (Levi) and Yahudha (Judah). Ancestor of Musa AS through Lawi and of Dawud AS through Yahudha.
bio_en: Liya bint Laban (known in the Bible as Leah) was the elder daughter of Laban, the uncle of Prophet Yaqub AS (Jacob), and became his first wife. In Islamic tradition, Yaqub AS had two wives who were sisters: Liya (Leah) and Rahil (Rachel). The Quran does not narrate Yaqub's marriage directly, but Islamic scholars have preserved the story through the Isra'iliyyat — reports from Jewish and Christian sources considered permissible to narrate when they do not contradict Islamic theology. According to this account, Yaqub traveled to his maternal uncle Laban after leaving his home. He fell in love with Rahil and agreed to work seven years for her hand. When the time came, Laban substituted Liya as the bride instead, and Yaqub had to work another seven years for Rahil. Liya was the mother of several of Yaqub's sons: Lawi (Levi), Yahudha (Judah), Rubil (Reuben), Shamun (Simeon), Yassakhar (Issachar), and Zabulun (Zebulun), among others. These sons became the patriarchs of tribes that formed Banu Isra'il (the Children of Israel). Lawi was an ancestor of Musa AS and Harun AS, and Yahudha was an ancestor of Dawud AS and the Jewish royal line. In Islamic theology, all the wives and families of the Prophets are respected as essential to the chain through which prophethood was transmitted.
bio_id: Liya bint Laban (known di Bible as Leah) adalah elder daughter dari Laban, uncle dari Prophet Yaqub AS (Jacob), dan became his first wife. In tradisi Islam, Yaqub AS memiliki two wives yang adalah sisters: Liya (Leah) dan Rahil (Rachel). The Al-Al-Qur' does tidak narrate Yaqub's marriage directly, but ulama Islams memiliki preserved story melalui Isra'iliyyat — reports dari Jewish dan Christian sources dianggap permissible ke narrate ketika they do tidak contradict Islamic teologi. According ke this account, Yaqub melakukan perjalanan ke his maternal uncle Laban setelah leaving his home. He fell di love dengan Rahil dan agreed ke work seven tahun untuk her hand. When time came, Laban substituted Liya as bride instead, dan Yaqub memiliki ke work another seven tahun untuk Rahil. Liya adalah mother dari several dari Yaqub's sons: Lawi (Levi), Yahudha (Judah), Rubil (Reuben), Shamun (Simeon), Yassakhar (Issachar), dan Zabulun (Zebulun), among others. These sons became patriarchs dari tribes yang formed Banu Isra'il ( Children dari Israel). Lawi adalah seorang ancestor dari Musa AS dan Harun AS, dan Yahudha adalah seorang ancestor dari Dawud AS dan Jewish royal line. In Islamic teologi, all wives dan families dari Nabi ﷺs adalah respected as essential ke chain melalui yang prophethood adalah meriwayatkan.

name: Abdullah ibn Muhammad
bio_short_en: The second son of Prophet Muhammad and Khadijah, known by the kunyahs al-Tayyib (the Pure) and al-Tahir (the Purified). He died in infancy in Makkah before the hijra.
bio_en: Abdullah ibn Muhammad was the second son of Prophet Muhammad and Khadijah bint Khuwaylid. He was given two honorary names: al-Tayyib (the Pure One) and al-Tahir (the Purified), both of which are sometimes treated as separate children in older accounts, though the majority of scholars hold they refer to the same person. He was born in Makkah before the hijra and died in infancy, like his elder brother al-Qasim. The deaths of the Prophet's sons caused his enemies — particularly al-As ibn Wa'il — to taunt him as 'abtar' (cut off, without male legacy). This mockery was addressed directly by Allah in Surah al-Kawthar (108): 'Indeed, your enemy is the one who is cut off.' The Prophet ﷺ is the father of the entire ummah, and it is his detractors whose names have faded while his name is proclaimed in every corner of the earth. Abdullah is buried in Makkah.
bio_id: Abdullah ibn Muhammad adalah second son dari Nabi Muhammad ﷺ dan Khadijah bint Khuwaylid. Ia adalah given two honorary names: al-Tayyib ( Pure One) dan al-Tahir ( Purified), both dari yang adalah sometimes treated as separate children di older accounts, though majority dari para ulama hold they refer ke same person. Ia adalah lahir di Makkah sebelum hijra dan wafat di infancy, like his elder brother al-Qasim. The deaths dari Nabi ﷺ's sons caused his enemies — khususnya al-As ibn Wa'il — ke taunt him as 'abtar' (cut off, tanpa male warisan). This mockery adalah addressed directly oleh Allah di Surah al-Kawthar (108): 'Indeed, your enemy adalah one yang adalah cut off.' The Prophet ﷺ adalah father dari entire ummah, dan it adalah his detractors whose names memiliki faded while his name adalah proclaimed di every corner dari earth. Abdullah adalah buried di Makkah.

name: al-Miqdad ibn Amr
bio_short_en: One of the earliest Muslims and among the Seven Pioneers who publicly declared Islam. He was the first Muslim horseman to fight in the way of Allah, and at Badr he gave one of the most courageous speeches of loyalty to the Prophet.
bio_en: Al-Miqdad ibn Amr, known as al-Miqdad ibn al-Aswad (through his adoptive relationship), was one of the earliest converts to Islam and one of the Seven Pioneers — the first Muslims to publicly declare their faith at Makkah despite the hostility of Quraysh. He emigrated to Abyssinia in the first hijra and later to Madinah. He holds the distinction of being the first Muslim to fight on horseback in the path of Allah — all other companions fought on foot in the early battles. At the Battle of Badr, when the Prophet consulted the companions about whether to engage the Quraysh force (which was far larger than expected), al-Miqdad spoke with famous courage: 'O Messenger of Allah, proceed to what Allah has commanded you. We will not say to you as Banu Isra'il said to Musa — 'Go you and your Lord and fight, while we sit here' — rather, we will fight at your right and your left, before you and behind you.' This declaration, reportedly bringing tears to the Prophet's eyes, preceded the Ansar's equally moving pledge. Al-Miqdad was known for his asceticism, his deep faith, and his sharp tongue against hypocrisy. He died in 33 AH near Madinah.
bio_id: Al-Miqdad ibn Amr, dikenal sebagai al-Miqdad ibn al-Aswad (melalui his adoptive relationship), adalah one dari earliest converts ke Islam dan one dari Seven Pioneers — first kaum Muslimin ke publicly declare their faith di Makkah meskipun hostility dari Quraysh. He emigrated ke Abyssinia di first hijra dan later ke Madinah. He holds distinction dari being first Muslim ke fight pada horseback di path dari Allah — all other companions fought pada foot di early battles. At Battle dari Badr, ketika Nabi ﷺ consulted companions sekitar whether ke engage Quraysh force (yang adalah far larger than expected), al-Miqdad spoke dengan famous courage: 'O Rasulullah ﷺ, proceed ke what Allah memiliki commanded you. We akan tidak say ke you as Banu Isra'il said ke Musa — 'Go you dan your Lord dan fight, while we sit here' — rather, we akan fight di your right dan your left, sebelum you dan behind you.' This declaration, reportedly bringing tears ke Nabi ﷺ's eyes, preceded Ansar's equally moving pledge. Al-Miqdad adalah dikenal karena his zahidism, his deep faith, dan his sharp tongue against hypocrisy. He wafat di 33 AH near Madinah.

name: Zayd ibn Haritha
bio_short_en: The adopted son of Prophet Muhammad ﷺ and commander of the Muslim forces at the Battle of Mu'tah, where he was martyred in 8 AH. He was the only Companion mentioned by name in the Quran (33:37). His son was Usama ibn Zayd.
bio_en: Zayd ibn Haritha al-Kalbi was the adopted son of Prophet Muhammad ﷺ before adoption was redefined in Islam. Born in the tribe of Kalb, he was captured as a child and enslaved, eventually coming to be owned by Khadijah bint Khuwaylid, who gave him to the Prophet as a gift. When his biological family came to ransom him, the young Zayd chose to remain with the Prophet — a remarkable act of devotion. The Prophet freed him and called him 'Zayd ibn Muhammad' until Surah al-Ahzab (33:37) revealed that adopted sons must retain their biological fathers' names. He was among the first converts to Islam. The Prophet married him to his cousin Zaynab bint Jahsh, but the marriage was troubled. After their divorce, the Prophet married Zaynab — an event directly addressed in the Quran (33:37) to dissolve the cultural taboo against marrying the ex-wife of an adopted son. Zayd was appointed commander of the army sent to Mu'tah in 8 AH, the first major clash with Byzantine forces. He was martyred in the battle, followed by Ja'far ibn Abi Talib and then Abdullah ibn Rawahah as commanders in succession. The Prophet wept upon receiving news of his death — the only Companion whose death caused the Prophet to openly weep in front of the Ansar. His son Usama ibn Zayd was also a beloved Companion and commander.
bio_id: Zayd ibn Haritha al-Kalbi adalah adopted son of Nabi Muhammad ﷺ before adoption adalah redefined in Islam. Lahir di the tribe of Kalb, he adalah captured as a child and enslaved, akhirnya coming to be owned by Khadijah bint Khuwaylid, who gave him to Nabi ﷺ as a gift. When his biological family came to ransom him, the young Zayd chose to remain with Nabi ﷺ — a remarkable act of devotion. The Nabi freed him and disebut him 'Zayd ibn Muhammad' until Surah al-Ahzab (33:37) revealed that adopted sons must retain their biological fathers' names. He adalah among the first converts to Islam. The Nabi married him to his cousin Zaynab bint Jahsh, but the marriage adalah troubled. After their divorce, Nabi ﷺ married Zaynab — an event directly addressed in Al-Al-Qur'an (33:37) to dissolve the cultural taboo against marrying the ex-wife of an adopted son. Zayd adalah appointed panglima of the army sent to Mu'tah in 8 AH, the first major clash with Byzantine forces. He adalah syahided in the battle, followed by Ja'far ibn Abi Talib and then Abdullah ibn Rawahah as panglimas in succession. The Nabi wept upon receiving news of wafatnya — the only Sahabat whose death caused Nabi ﷺ to openly weep in front of the Ansar. His son Usama ibn Zayd adalah also a beloved Sahabat and panglima.

name: Abdullah ibn Rawahah
bio_short_en: A great Ansari poet and companion who was one of the representatives at the first Bay'at al-Aqabah. Commander at Mu'tah after Ja'far ibn Abi Talib, where he was martyred in 8 AH. The Prophet praised his poetry as filled with divine truth.
bio_en: Abdullah ibn Rawahah was one of the most celebrated poets of the Ansar and a companion of the Prophet ﷺ. He was among the twelve Ansari representatives who pledged allegiance at the first Bay'at al-Aqabah, before the hijra. He was a devoted companion who would recite poetry in praise of the Prophet in the streets of Madinah. At the Battle of Mu'tah in 8 AH, the Prophet designated three commanders in succession: Zayd ibn Haritha, then Ja'far ibn Abi Talib, then Abdullah ibn Rawahah. When both Zayd and Ja'far fell, Ibn Rawahah took command. Before advancing, he composed verses exhorting himself to fight for Allah — the poetry of a warrior-poet heading into his final battle. He was martyred there. The Prophet ﷺ said of him that he was praised in the three heavens: by Allah, by the angels, and by the believers on earth. His poetry was noted for being truthful in its imagery even when using the poetic conventions of hyperbole.
bio_id: Abdullah ibn Rawahah adalah salah satu yang paling terkenal penyairs dari Ansar dan companion dari Nabi ﷺ. Ia adalah among twelve Ansari representatives yang pledged allegiance di first Bay'di al-Aqabah, sebelum hijra. Ia adalah devoted companion yang akan recite penyairry di praise dari Nabi ﷺ di streets dari Madinah. At Battle dari Mu'tah di 8 AH, Nabi ﷺ designated three panglimas di succession: Zayd ibn Haritha, then Ja'far ibn Abi Talib, then Abdullah ibn Rawahah. When both Zayd dan Ja'far fell, Ibn Rawahah took command. Before advancing, ia mengarang verses exhorting himself ke fight untuk Allah — penyairry dari pejuang-penyair heading ke dalam his final battle. Ia adalah syahided there. The Prophet ﷺ said dari him yang ia adalah praised di three heavens: oleh Allah, oleh angels, dan oleh believers pada earth.
