bio_id: Abd al-Wahhab al-Subki (727–771 H), putra Taqi al-Din al-Subki, adalah ahli fikih Syafi'i Syria. Ia terkenal karena menulis Tabaqat al-Syafi'iyya al-Kubra, ensiklopedi biografi ulama Syafi'i yang tak tertandingi.

title: Shafi'i Imam
bio_short_en: Known as 'the Small Shafi'i' (al-Shafi'i al-Saghir), author of Nihayat al-Muhtaj, the definitive Shafi'i reference in the Egyptian tradition.
bio_en: Muhammad ibn Ahmad al-Ramli (919–1004 AH / 1513–1596 CE) was a leading Egyptian Shafi'i scholar, nicknamed 'the Small Shafi'i' for his mastery of the school. His father Shihab al-Din al-Ramli was also a major Shafi'i scholar. His most important work, Nihayat al-Muhtaj ila Sharh al-Minhaj, is regarded in the Egyptian Shafi'i tradition as the most authoritative later Shafi'i reference, alongside Ibn Hajar al-Haytami's Tuhfat al-Muhtaj. Together, these two scholars (al-Ramli for Egypt, Ibn Hajar al-Haytami for the Hijaz) define the authoritative opinions in the later Shafi'i school. Students sought him from across the Muslim world.
bio_id: Muhammad al-Ramli (919–1004 H) dijuluki 'al-Syafi'i kecil' atas penguasaannya terhadap mazhab. Karyanya Nihayat al-Muhtaj adalah rujukan utama mazhab Syafi'i di tradisi Mesir.

title: Shaykh
bio_short_en: Major Shafi'i scholar of Cairo, author of Mughni al-Muhtaj, the most widely taught Shafi'i fiqh commentary.
bio_en: Muhammad ibn Ahmad al-Shirbini al-Qahiri (906–977 AH), known as al-Khatib al-Shirbini, was a leading Egyptian Shafi'i jurist of the 10th century AH. His major work, Mughni al-Muhtaj ila Ma'rifat Ma'ani Alfaz al-Minhaj, is the most widely used commentary on Imam al-Nawawi's Minhaj al-Talibin. It is distinguished for its balance between detail and accessibility, making it the preferred teaching text in Shafi'i faculties and Islamic universities. He also wrote al-Iqna' (a condensed Shafi'i fiqh text) and Tafsir al-Siraj al-Munir. He taught at al-Azhar and trained many prominent scholars.
bio_id: Muhammad al-Shirbini al-Qahiri (906–977 H) adalah ahli fikih Syafi'i terkemuka di Mesir. Karyanya Mughni al-Muhtaj adalah syarah Minhaj al-Nawawi yang paling banyak diajarkan di universitas Islam.

name_full_en: Muhammad ibn Bahadur ibn Abdullah al-Zarkashi
title: Imam
bio_short_en: Encyclopedic Shafi'i scholar, author of al-Bahr al-Muhit fi Usul al-Fiqh and al-Burhan fi Ulum al-Quran.
bio_en: Muhammad ibn Bahadur al-Zarkashi (745–794 AH / 1344–1392 CE) was one of the most learned Shafi'i scholars of the 8th century AH. Of Turkic origin, born in Egypt, he produced encyclopedic works in multiple fields. His al-Bahr al-Muhit fi Usul al-Fiqh is one of the most comprehensive works in Islamic legal theory (usul al-fiqh). His al-Burhan fi Ulum al-Quran is a landmark encyclopedia of the Quranic sciences. He also wrote al-Ijabah li ma Istadrakathu Aisha ala al-Sahabah, a specialized work on Aisha's criticisms of other companions. Despite his relatively short life (he died at about 48), his scholarly output was remarkable in scope and depth.
bio_id: Muhammad al-Zarkashi (745–794 H) adalah ulama Syafi'i ensiklopedis abad ke-8 H. Karyanya al-Bahr al-Muhit adalah ensiklopedi usul fikih terlengkap, dan al-Burhan fi Ulum al-Quran adalah rujukan utama ilmu-ilmu Al-Quran.

title: Shaykh
bio_short_en: Major Egyptian Shafi'i scholar and legal theorist, known for al-Tamhid fi Takhrij al-Furu' ala al-Usul.
bio_en: Jamal al-Din Abd al-Rahim al-Isnawi (704–772 AH / 1305–1370 CE) was a leading Shafi'i jurist and legal theorist of 8th-century AH Egypt. His most important work, al-Tamhid fi Takhrij al-Furu' ala al-Usul, is a landmark study connecting specific legal rulings (furu') to their theoretical foundations (usul), an approach that influenced subsequent legal theory. He also wrote al-Nihayah al-Sul fi Sharh Minhaj al-Wusul and al-Muhimmat, an addendum to al-Rafi'i and al-Nawawi's works. He was the chief teacher at al-Azhar and trained prominent scholars including al-Subki.
bio_id: Jamal al-Din al-Isnawi (704–772 H) adalah ahli fikih Syafi'i dan pakar usul fikih terkemuka di Mesir abad ke-8 H, kepala pengajar al-Azhar.

name_full_en: Muhammad ibn Umar Nawawi al-Bantani al-Jawi
title: Shaykh
bio_short_en: Prolific Indonesian-Meccan Shafi'i scholar, author of numerous widely-read Arabic works on fiqh, tafsir, and hadith.
bio_en: Muhammad ibn Umar Nawawi al-Bantani (1230–1316 AH / 1813–1898 CE), known as Nawawi al-Jawi, was the most prolific Indonesian Islamic scholar of the 19th century. Born in Banten, Java, he traveled to Mecca and settled there, spending his life teaching and writing. He produced over 100 works in Arabic on fiqh, tafsir, hadith, usul, and tasawwuf, making him among the most prolific Arabic-language authors of his century. His tafsir Marah Labid (Tafsir al-Munir) is a comprehensive Shafi'i commentary widely read in Southeast Asia. His works on fiqh, including his commentary on Safinah al-Najah, are standard texts in Indonesian Islamic schools (pesantren). He trained generations of Indonesian scholars in Mecca.
bio_id: Muhammad Nawawi al-Bantani (1230–1316 H) adalah ulama Indonesia paling produktif abad ke-19, menulis lebih dari 100 karya dalam bahasa Arab di bidang fikih, tafsir, dan hadis. Guru banyak ulama Indonesia di Mekah.

name_full_en: Abu Bakr ibn Muhammad Shata al-Dimyati
title: Sayyid
bio_short_en: Late 19th-century Egyptian Shafi'i scholar, author of I'anat al-Talibin, the most popular commentary on the Shafi'i primer Fath al-Mu'in.
bio_en: Abu Bakr ibn Muhammad Shata al-Dimyati (1266–1310 AH / 1843–1892 CE), known as Sayyid al-Bakri, was an Egyptian Shafi'i scholar who spent much of his life in Mecca teaching at the Grand Mosque. His most famous work, I'anat al-Talibin (Assistance for the Students), is a commentary on Fath al-Mu'in by al-Malibari, itself a commentary on Qurrat al-'Ayn. This four-volume work became the standard Shafi'i fiqh reference in Southeast Asian Islamic schools. It addresses legal questions relevant to everyday Muslim life with clarity and accessibility. He trained many students who returned to Southeast Asia and spread classical Shafi'i scholarship.
bio_id: Abu Bakr al-Dimyati (1266–1310 H) adalah ulama Syafi'i Mesir abad ke-19. Karyanya I'anat al-Talibin menjadi rujukan fikih Syafi'i standar di pesantren-pesantren Asia Tenggara.

name_full_en: Zayn al-Din ibn Abd al-Aziz al-Malibari
title: Shaykh
bio_short_en: South Indian Shafi'i scholar, author of Fath al-Mu'in, the foundational Shafi'i fiqh text in South Asia and Southeast Asia.
bio_en: Zayn al-Din ibn Abd al-Aziz al-Malibari (873–928 AH / 1468–1522 CE) was a Shafi'i scholar from Kerala (Malabar), South India. His most influential work, Fath al-Mu'in bi Sharh Qurrat al-'Ayn bi Muhimmat al-Din, is a concise yet comprehensive Shafi'i fiqh manual that became the foundational text for Shafi'i jurisprudence in South Asia and Southeast Asia. It is the text upon which Sayyid al-Bakri's I'anat al-Talibin is based. His work is still the standard fiqh reference in traditional Islamic schools of Kerala, Tamil Nadu, and across maritime Southeast Asia. He also wrote Irshad al-'Ibad, a popular work on Islamic practice.
bio_id: Zayn al-Din al-Malibari (873–928 H) adalah ulama Syafi'i dari Kerala, India Selatan. Karyanya Fath al-Mu'in menjadi teks fikih Syafi'i dasar di Asia Selatan dan Asia Tenggara.

title: Shaykh
bio_short_en: Shafi'i scholar of 10th-century AH Egypt, wrote marginal notes (hashiya) on Ibn Hajar al-Haytami's Tuhfah and Shihab al-Din al-Ramli's works.
bio_en: Ahmad ibn Qasim al-Abbadi (952–994 AH / 1545–1600 CE) was an Egyptian Shafi'i jurist who served as a teacher at al-Azhar. He is known for his scholarly glosses (hawashi) on the major Shafi'i references of his time, particularly his commentary on Ibn Hajar al-Haytami's Tuhfat al-Muhtaj. These glosses clarify difficult passages and note divergent opinions, making them valuable study aids in the later Shafi'i curriculum.
bio_id: Ahmad ibn Qasim al-Abbadi (952–994 H) adalah ulama Syafi'i Mesir abad ke-10 H, dikenal karena hasyiyah-hasyiyahnya atas karya-karya Ibn Hajar al-Haytami.

name_full_en: Abd al-Salam ibn Sa'id ibn Habib al-Tanukhi
title: Imam
bio_short_en: Chief student of Ibn al-Qasim (student of Malik), compiler of al-Mudawwana, the foundational text of the Maliki school.
bio_en: Abd al-Salam ibn Sa'id, known as Sahnun (160–240 AH / 776–854 CE), was the foremost transmitter of the Maliki school in North Africa. He traveled from Qayrawan to study under Ibn al-Qasim in Egypt, who was the principal student of Imam Malik. Together with Ibn al-Qasim, he compiled al-Mudawwana al-Kubra (The Great Compilation), the foundational legal text of the Maliki school. Al-Mudawwana contains Sahnun's questions posed to Ibn al-Qasim about Maliki legal positions, and Ibn al-Qasim's answers based on Malik's teachings. This became the primary reference of the Maliki school for centuries. Sahnun served as the chief judge (qadi) of Qayrawan and trained generations of Maliki jurists in North Africa.
bio_id: Sahnun ibn Sa'id (160–240 H) adalah penyusun al-Mudawwana al-Kubra, teks dasar mazhab Maliki, bersama Ibn al-Qasim. Ia berperan besar dalam penyebaran mazhab Maliki di Afrika Utara.

name_full_en: Abd al-Rahman ibn al-Qasim al-Atqi al-Misri
title: Imam
bio_short_en: Imam Malik's foremost student, principal transmitter of Maliki jurisprudence and co-author of al-Mudawwana.
bio_en: Abd al-Rahman ibn al-Qasim al-Atqi (128–191 AH / 745–806 CE) was the closest and most important student of Imam Malik ibn Anas. He studied under Malik for approximately 20 years in Medina. After Malik's death, he became the principal reference point for Maliki jurisprudence in Egypt. Al-Mudawwana, compiled in dialogue with Sahnun, preserves his transmission of Maliki positions on hundreds of legal questions. His opinions formed the core of what became standard Maliki doctrine. He was based in Egypt and trained many scholars who spread the Maliki school across North Africa and al-Andalus. Imam al-Shafi'i also benefited from studying with him when he came to Egypt.
bio_id: Ibn al-Qasim (128–191 H) adalah murid utama Imam Malik dan perawi terpenting mazhab Maliki. Ia menjadi rujukan utama fikih Maliki di Mesir setelah wafat Imam Malik.

name_full_en: Abd al-Malik ibn Habib al-Sulami al-Andalusi
title: Imam
bio_short_en: The first great Maliki scholar of al-Andalus, who transmitted the school from the East and established it firmly in Spain.
bio_en: Abd al-Malik ibn Habib al-Sulami (174–238 AH / 790–853 CE) was the founding figure of Maliki jurisprudence in al-Andalus. He traveled east, studying under Imam Malik's students including Ibn al-Qasim and Mutarrif ibn Abd Allah, and returned to Andalusia with a thorough mastery of the school. He was the most learned jurist in Andalusia of his generation and is credited with establishing the dominance of the Maliki school there. He wrote prolifically on fiqh, history, and Quran commentary. His work al-Wadi'a is an early Maliki legal manual. He trained many scholars who carried on the Maliki tradition in Andalusia.
bio_id: Ibn Habib al-Maliki (174–238 H) adalah tokoh perintis mazhab Maliki di Andalusia. Setelah belajar dari murid-murid Imam Malik di Timur, ia kembali dan menetapkan dominasi mazhab Maliki di Spanyol Islam.

name_full_en: Abdullah ibn Abi Zayd al-Nafzawi al-Qayrawani
title: Imam
bio_short_en: Called 'the Small Malik,' the leading Maliki scholar of his age, author of al-Risalah, the most popular Maliki fiqh primer in history.
bio_en: Abdullah ibn Abi Zayd al-Nafzawi al-Qayrawani (310–386 AH / 922–996 CE), called 'the Small Malik' (Malik al-Saghir) by his contemporaries, was the undisputed leader of the Maliki school in his generation. His al-Risalah fi al-Fiqh al-Maliki is one of the most famous and widely-studied primers of Islamic law in history. Written as a comprehensive introduction to Maliki fiqh, from personal purification to commercial transactions, it is still memorized and taught in Maliki-dominant regions today. His Mukhtasar al-Mudawwana is an abridgment of the foundational Maliki text. Based in Qayrawan (modern Tunisia), he headed the Maliki scholarly establishment of the Islamic West.
bio_id: Ibn Abi Zayd al-Qayrawani (310–386 H), dijuluki 'Malik kecil', adalah pemimpin mazhab Maliki di zamannya. Karyanya al-Risalah fi al-Fiqh al-Maliki adalah primer fikih Maliki paling populer sepanjang sejarah.

name_full_en: Ahmad ibn Idris al-Sanhaji al-Buhuti al-Misri
title: Imam
bio_short_en: Leading Maliki jurist and legal theorist of 7th-century AH Egypt, author of al-Dhakhirah and al-Furuq.
bio_en: Ahmad ibn Idris al-Qarafi (626–684 AH / 1228–1285 CE) was the preeminent Maliki jurist and legal theorist of 7th-century AH Egypt. His encyclopedic al-Dhakhirah (The Treasure) is a comprehensive Maliki fiqh reference covering all areas of Islamic law. His al-Furuq (The Distinctions) is a landmark work in legal theory, identifying subtle but crucial distinctions between similar legal concepts. He also wrote al-Tanqih al-Fusul in usul al-fiqh, which remains a standard text. Al-Qarafi's works demonstrate the sophisticated analytical tradition of the Maliki school and are studied in Maliki academic institutions to this day.
bio_id: Ahmad al-Qarafi (626–684 H) adalah ahli fikih Maliki dan pakar usul fikih terkemuka di Mesir abad ke-7 H. Karyanya al-Dhakhirah adalah ensiklopedi fikih Maliki yang komprehensif.

name_full_en: Muhammad ibn Ahmad ibn Rushd al-Qurtubi (the grandfather)
title: Imam
bio_short_en: Grandfather of the philosopher Averroes, the leading Maliki jurist of al-Andalus, author of al-Muqaddimat al-Mumahhidat.
bio_en: Muhammad ibn Ahmad ibn Rushd (450–520 AH / 1058–1126 CE), known as Ibn Rushd al-Jadd (the grandfather) to distinguish him from his famous grandson, was the foremost Maliki jurist of al-Andalus in his generation. He served as chief judge (qadi) of Cordoba. His most important work, al-Muqaddimat al-Mumahhidat (Preliminary Introductions), provides detailed jurisprudential analyses of the subjects in Ibn Abi Zayd's al-Nawadir. His al-Bayan wa al-Tahsil is a major commentary on Ibn Rushd's own al-Utbiyya, providing extensive analysis of Maliki legal questions.
bio_id: Ibn Rushd al-Jadd (450–520 H), kakek filsuf Averroes, adalah ahli fikih Maliki utama Andalusia. Ia menjabat hakim kepala Cordoba dan menulis al-Muqaddimat al-Mumahhidat.

title: Imam
bio_short_en: The leading Maliki scholar of his era in North Africa, called 'Imam of Imams,' known for his authoritative commentary on Sahih Muslim.
bio_en: Muhammad ibn Ali al-Maziri (453–536 AH / 1059–1141 CE) was the foremost Maliki scholar in North Africa and Sicily of the 6th century AH, called 'Imam al-Aimmah' (Imam of Imams). His major work, al-Mu'lim bi Fawa'id Muslim, is the earliest and one of the most authoritative commentaries on Sahih Muslim. He was also a physician and is credited with remarkable medical observations. Born in Sicily (under Islamic rule at the time), he later settled in al-Mahdiyyah in Tunisia. He taught al-Qadi Iyad and other major scholars who would carry the Maliki tradition.
bio_id: al-Maziri (453–536 H) dijuluki 'Imam al-Aimmah', pemimpin ulama Maliki di Afrika Utara abad ke-6 H. Karyanya al-Mu'lim adalah syarah tertua dan paling otoritatif atas Sahih Muslim.

title: Imam
bio_short_en: Leading Maliki scholar of 15th-century Morocco, author of al-Mi'yar al-Mu'rib, the largest collection of Maliki fatwas ever compiled.
bio_en: Ahmad ibn Yahya al-Wansharisi (834–914 AH / 1430–1508 CE) was the most important Maliki jurist of 9th-century AH Morocco. His monumental work al-Mi'yar al-Mu'rib wa al-Jami' al-Mughrib is the most comprehensive collection of Maliki fatwas (legal opinions) ever assembled, spanning 13 volumes and covering centuries of legal opinions from scholars across al-Andalus, North Africa, and the Islamic West. It remains an invaluable source for Maliki legal history. He also played a crucial role in encouraging Muslims in Granada to resist religious persecution under Spanish Christian rule rather than comply with demands to outwardly practice Christianity.
bio_id: al-Wansharisi (834–914 H) adalah ulama Maliki terkemuka di Maroko abad ke-9 H. Karyanya al-Mi'yar al-Mu'rib adalah koleksi fatwa Maliki terlengkap yang pernah disusun, mencakup 13 jilid.

name_full_en: Khalil ibn Ishaq ibn Musa al-Jundi al-Maliki
title: Shaykh
bio_short_en: Author of Mukhtasar Khalil, the most authoritative and widely-studied Maliki fiqh text, a concise summary of the entire Maliki school.
bio_en: Khalil ibn Ishaq al-Jundi (714–776 AH / 1314–1374 CE) was an Egyptian Maliki jurist whose Mukhtasar (The Compendium) became the single most important text in Maliki jurisprudence. Known simply as Mukhtasar Khalil, this concise but extraordinarily dense work summarizes the Maliki school's positions on every area of Islamic law. It was written so concisely that students required extensive commentaries to understand it, leading to the production of numerous influential sharhs (commentaries), the most famous being those of al-Kharashi, al-Zurqani, al-Hatab, and al-Tawudi. Mukhtasar Khalil is still the primary fiqh text in West African and North African madrasas today.
bio_id: Khalil ibn Ishaq al-Jundi (714–776 H) adalah penulis Mukhtasar Khalil, teks fikih Maliki paling otoritatif. Ringkasannya yang padat tentang seluruh mazhab Maliki masih menjadi teks utama di madrasah-madrasah Afrika Barat dan Afrika Utara.

name_full_en: Muhammad ibn Muhammad ibn Abd al-Rahman al-Hatab al-Ru'ayni
title: Imam
bio_short_en: Maliki scholar of the 10th century AH, author of Mawahib al-Jalil, the most comprehensive commentary on Mukhtasar Khalil.
bio_en: Muhammad ibn Muhammad al-Hatab al-Ru'ayni (902–954 AH / 1497–1547 CE) was a Moroccan-born Maliki scholar who spent much of his life in Tripoli and eventually settled in Mecca. His most important work, Mawahib al-Jalil fi Sharh Mukhtasar Khalil (The Gifts of the Majestic in Commentary on the Compendium of Khalil), is a six-volume commentary on Mukhtasar Khalil and is considered the most comprehensive and authoritative commentary on that foundational Maliki text. His work synthesizes views from across the Maliki tradition and is a major reference in Maliki jurisprudence.
bio_id: al-Hatab al-Ru'ayni (902–954 H) adalah ulama Maliki Maroko abad ke-10 H. Karyanya Mawahib al-Jalil adalah syarah paling komprehensif atas Mukhtasar Khalil.

name_full_en: Abd al-Baqi ibn Yusuf al-Zurqani al-Misri
title: Shaykh
bio_short_en: Egyptian Maliki scholar, author of important commentaries on Mukhtasar Khalil and Muwatta of Imam Malik.
bio_en: Abd al-Baqi ibn Yusuf al-Zurqani (1020–1099 AH / 1611–1688 CE) was a major Egyptian Maliki scholar of the 11th century AH. He wrote an important commentary on Mukhtasar Khalil and also a widely-used commentary on Muwatta of Imam Malik called Sharh al-Zurqani ala al-Muwatta. Both are standard references in the Maliki school. His commentary on the Muwatta is particularly valued for its clear explanations and engagement with the different narrations and their legal implications.
bio_id: al-Zurqani al-Maliki (1020–1099 H) adalah ulama Maliki Mesir abad ke-11 H, dikenal karena syarah-syarahnya atas Mukhtasar Khalil dan Muwatta Imam Malik.

name_full_en: Ibrahim ibn Ali ibn Muhammad ibn Farhun al-Ya'muri al-Madani
title: Qadi
bio_short_en: Maliki judge of Medina, author of Tabsirat al-Hukkam, a landmark work on Islamic judicial procedure and legal methodology.
bio_en: Ibrahim ibn Ali ibn Farhun (702–799 AH / 1302–1397 CE) was a Maliki jurist from the Arabian Peninsula who served as chief judge of Medina. His most important work, Tabsirat al-Hukkam fi Usul al-Aqdiyya wa Manahij al-Ahkam (The Illumination of Judges in the Principles of Adjudication), is a comprehensive treatise on judicial procedure and methodology in Islamic law. It is a primary reference for the history of Islamic judiciary and legal procedure. He also wrote al-Dibaj al-Mudhahhab, an important biographical dictionary of Maliki scholars.
bio_id: Ibn Farhun (702–799 H) adalah hakim Maliki Madinah. Karyanya Tabsirat al-Hukkam adalah risalah komprehensif tentang prosedur peradilan Islam dan metodologi hukum.

name_full_en: Ali ibn Abd al-Salam al-Tasuli al-Fasi
title: Shaykh
bio_short_en: Major 19th-century Moroccan Maliki scholar, author of al-Bahjah fi Sharh al-Tuhfah, a key commentary in the Moroccan Maliki tradition.
bio_en: Ali ibn Abd al-Salam al-Tasuli (1204–1258 AH / 1789–1842 CE) was a leading Moroccan Maliki scholar who taught at the Qarawiyyin mosque-university in Fez. His al-Bahjah fi Sharh al-Tuhfah is one of the most important commentaries on Ibn Asim's al-Tuhfah, the foundational legal poem of the Moroccan Maliki tradition. His work is a key text in the transmission of the Maliki school in Morocco.
bio_id: al-Tasuli (1204–1258 H) adalah ulama Maliki Maroko terkemuka yang mengajar di Qarawiyyin Fez. Karyanya al-Bahjah fi Syarh al-Tuhfah adalah syarah penting dalam tradisi Maliki Maroko.

title: Imam
bio_short_en: Andalusian Maliki scholar who settled in Alexandria, author of Siraj al-Muluk and al-Hawadith wa al-Bida'.
bio_en: Muhammad ibn al-Walid al-Fihri al-Tartushi (451–520 AH / 1059–1126 CE) was an Andalusian Maliki jurist from Tortosa who traveled east, studied in Iraq and Egypt, and eventually settled in Alexandria where he taught and produced important works. His Siraj al-Muluk (Lamp of Kings) is a treatise on statecraft from an Islamic perspective, addressing the duties and conduct of rulers. His al-Hawadith wa al-Bida' (Occurrences and Innovations) is an important early work criticizing blameworthy innovations in religious practice. He was a significant figure in the Maliki scholarly tradition and trained important scholars in Alexandria.
bio_id: al-Tartushi (451–520 H) adalah ulama Maliki dari Andalusia yang menetap di Iskandariyah. Karyanya Siraj al-Muluk membahas tata kelola pemerintahan Islami.

title: Imam
bio_short_en: Major Andalusian Maliki scholar and hadith master, author of al-Muntaqa fi Sharh al-Muwatta and al-Istifa.
bio_en: Sulayman ibn Khalaf al-Baji (403–474 AH / 1013–1081 CE) was the leading Maliki scholar of al-Andalus in the 5th century AH. He traveled east and studied under major scholars before returning to Andalusia. His monumental al-Muntaqa fi Sharh al-Muwatta is one of the most important commentaries on Imam Malik's Muwatta, covering its hadith with legal analysis and comparative jurisprudence. He also wrote al-Isyarah fi Usul al-Fiqh and al-Minhaj fi Tartib al-Hujaj. He was appointed chief judge (qadi) of Almeria and was a key figure in defending and expanding the Maliki school in Andalusia.
bio_id: al-Baji (403–474 H) adalah ulama Maliki dan ahli hadis terkemuka di Andalusia abad ke-5 H. Karyanya al-Muntaqa fi Syarh al-Muwatta adalah salah satu syarah terpenting atas Muwatta Imam Malik.

name_full_en: Ahmad ibn Muhammad al-Sawi al-Maliki al-Azhari
title: Shaykh
bio_short_en: Major Egyptian Maliki scholar, known for his glosses (hawashi) on the major Maliki texts including Sharh al-Sawi ala Kifayat al-Talib.
bio_en: Ahmad ibn Muhammad al-Sawi al-Maliki (1175–1241 AH / 1761–1825 CE) was a major Egyptian Maliki scholar who taught at al-Azhar. He is known for his scholarly glosses (hawashi) on the major Maliki texts. His Hashiyat al-Sawi ala al-Sharh al-Saghir (known as Bulghat al-Salik) is a widely-used commentary on Khalil's text. He was among the most respected Maliki authorities of 13th-century AH Egypt and trained many students who went on to teach at al-Azhar.
bio_id: Ahmad al-Sawi (1175–1241 H) adalah ulama Maliki Mesir terkemuka yang mengajar di al-Azhar. Dikenal karena hasyiyah-hasyiyahnya atas teks-teks Maliki utama.

title: Shaykh
bio_short_en: Leading Maliki scholar of 18th-century Egypt, author of al-Sharh al-Kabir and al-Sharh al-Saghir on Khalil's Mukhtasar.
bio_en: Ahmad ibn Muhammad al-Dardiri (1127–1201 AH / 1731–1786 CE) was one of the most important Maliki scholars of 18th-century Egypt. He taught at al-Azhar and is celebrated for his two commentaries on Mukhtasar Khalil: al-Sharh al-Kabir (the large commentary) and al-Sharh al-Saghir (the small commentary, also called Aqrab al-Masalik). These are among the most widely-used Maliki fiqh references today, particularly in West Africa and Egypt. He was known for his piety, simple lifestyle, and his defense of the poor against oppression by Ottoman authorities. Al-Sawi wrote his famous gloss on al-Sharh al-Saghir.
bio_id: Ahmad al-Dardiri (1127–1201 H) adalah ulama Maliki Mesir terkemuka abad ke-18. Dua syarahnya atas Mukhtasar Khalil (al-Sharh al-Kabir dan al-Sharh al-Saghir) adalah referensi fikih Maliki yang paling banyak digunakan saat ini.

name_full_en: Ali ibn Muhammad ibn Khalaf al-Ma'afiri al-Qayrawani al-Lakhmi
title: Imam
bio_short_en: Major Maliki jurist of 5th-century AH Qayrawan, author of al-Tabsirah, an influential Maliki legal encyclopedia.
bio_en: Ali ibn Muhammad al-Lakhmi (402–478 AH / 1012–1085 CE) was a prominent Maliki jurist of Qayrawan (Tunisia) in the 5th century AH. His most important work, al-Tabsirah (The Illumination), is an extensive Maliki legal encyclopedia dealing with all areas of Islamic law. It presents the Maliki school's positions with extensive analysis and comparison with other schools. Al-Lakhmi was known for his independent legal thinking and his ability to go beyond the standard Maliki positions when the evidence warranted. He was one of the major figures of the Maliki school in the Islamic West.
bio_id: al-Lakhmi (402–478 H) adalah ulama Maliki terkemuka di Qayrawan abad ke-5 H. Karyanya al-Tabsirah adalah ensiklopedi fikih Maliki yang luas.

name_full_en: Ahmad ibn Muhammad ibn Sallama al-Azdi al-Tahawi
title: Imam
bio_short_en: Great Hanafi scholar and hadith master, author of al-Aqidah al-Tahawiyya, the most widely accepted creedal text in Sunni Islam.
bio_en: Ahmad ibn Muhammad al-Tahawi (239–321 AH / 853–933 CE) was one of the greatest scholars in Islamic history, a master of Hanafi jurisprudence and an exceptional hadith scholar. Born in Egypt, he began his studies under his maternal uncle al-Muzani (the Shafi'i scholar), but eventually embraced the Hanafi school. He is most celebrated for al-Aqidah al-Tahawiyya (The Creed of al-Tahawi), a concise but comprehensive statement of Sunni theological beliefs that has been accepted by all major schools of Sunni Islam and has become the most widely-studied creedal text in the Islamic tradition. He also wrote the monumental Sharh Mushkil al-Athar (explaining apparently contradictory hadiths) and Sharh Ma'ani al-Athar (explaining the meanings of narrations), both encyclopedic works in hadith scholarship. His works demonstrate that the Hanafi school is rooted in hadith scholarship.
bio_id: al-Tahawi (239–321 H) adalah ulama Hanafi dan ahli hadis besar. Karyanya al-Aqidah al-Tahawiyya adalah teks akidah Sunni yang paling banyak diterima dan dipelajari sepanjang sejarah Islam.

name_full_en: Muhammad ibn Ahmad ibn Abi Sahl al-Sarakhsi
title: Shams al-A'immah
bio_short_en: Called 'Sun of the Imams,' the most important Hanafi jurist of the 5th century AH, author of the encyclopedic al-Mabsut.
bio_en: Muhammad ibn Ahmad al-Sarakhsi (430–483 AH / 1037–1096 CE), titled Shams al-A'immah (Sun of the Imams), was the foremost Hanafi jurist of the 5th century AH and one of the greatest legal scholars in Islamic history. His magnum opus, al-Mabsut (The Extensive Work), is a thirty-volume encyclopedic commentary on al-Kafi of al-Hakim al-Shahid that covers every area of Islamic law in extraordinary depth. A celebrated story holds that he dictated much of al-Mabsut from memory to his students while imprisoned in a well by the Sultan. He also wrote Usul al-Sarakhsi, one of the foundational works of Hanafi legal theory. His analysis is characterized by rigorous logical reasoning grounded in the classical Hanafi tradition.
bio_id: al-Sarakhsi (430–483 H), bergelar 'Matahari para Imam', adalah ahli fikih Hanafi terpenting abad ke-5 H. Karyanya al-Mabsut, ensiklopedi hukum Islam 30 jilid, didiktekan dari ingatan saat ia dalam penjara.

name_full_en: Fakhr al-Din Uthman ibn Ali al-Zayla'i al-Hanafi
title: Imam
bio_short_en: Hanafi jurist and major hadith scholar, author of Tabyin al-Haqa'iq and Nasb al-Rayah, an essential reference for hadith evidence in Hanafi fiqh.
bio_en: Fakhr al-Din Uthman ibn Ali al-Zayla'i (648–743 AH / 1250–1342 CE) was a Hanafi jurist of East African/Somali origin (Zayla is in Somalia) who became a major scholar in Cairo. His most important works are Tabyin al-Haqa'iq fi Sharh Kanz al-Daqa'iq (a commentary on the Hanafi text Kanz al-Daqa'iq by al-Nasafi) and Nasb al-Rayah li Ahadith al-Hidayah. Nasb al-Rayah is a landmark work documenting the hadith evidence for the legal positions taken in al-Hidayah, the foundational Hanafi text. It is an essential reference for understanding the hadith foundations of Hanafi jurisprudence.
bio_id: al-Zayla'i (648–743 H) adalah ahli fikih Hanafi dan ulama hadis terkemuka. Karyanya Nasb al-Rayah adalah referensi penting yang mendokumentasikan dalil hadis untuk posisi-posisi hukum dalam al-Hidayah.

name_full_en: Muwaffaq al-Din Abdullah ibn Ahmad ibn Qudama al-Maqdisi
title: Imam
bio_short_en: The foremost Hanbali jurist of his era, author of al-Mughni, the most comprehensive treatise of comparative Islamic jurisprudence.
bio_en: Muwaffaq al-Din Abdullah ibn Qudama al-Maqdisi (541–620 AH / 1147–1223 CE) was the greatest Hanbali jurist of the 6th–7th century AH and one of the most important scholars in Islamic legal history. Born in Nablus (Palestine), his family migrated to Damascus where he spent his scholarly career. His al-Mughni is a ten-volume encyclopedic work of comparative Islamic law (fiqh al-muqarin), covering every area of Islamic law and comparing the Hanbali positions with those of other schools, with evidence from Quran, hadith, and scholarly consensus. It remains the most comprehensive work of comparative fiqh in the Islamic tradition. He also wrote al-Kafi, al-Muqni', Umdat al-Fiqh (a primer), and al-Rawdah al-Naziriyyah in theology.
bio_id: Ibn Qudama al-Maqdisi (541–620 H) adalah ahli fikih Hanbali terbesar zamannya. Karyanya al-Mughni adalah ensiklopedi fikih perbandingan terlengkap dalam tradisi Islam, mencakup semua bidang hukum dengan perbandingan antar mazhab.

name_full_en: Shams al-Din Muhammad ibn Muflih al-Maqdisi
title: Imam
bio_short_en: Outstanding Hanbali scholar and close student of Ibn Taymiyya, author of al-Furu' and al-Adab al-Shar'iyya.
bio_en: Muhammad ibn Muflih al-Maqdisi (708–763 AH / 1318–1362 CE) was a distinguished Hanbali scholar of Damascus and one of the most important students of Ibn Taymiyya and Ibn al-Qayyim. Ibn Taymiyya reportedly said of him: 'There is not under the dome of heaven a greater scholar of fiqh than Ibn Muflih.' His al-Furu' (The Branches) is one of the most comprehensive Hanbali fiqh references, covering all areas of Islamic law with detailed analysis. His al-Adab al-Shar'iyya (Shariah Etiquettes) is a three-volume work on Islamic manners and etiquette in all aspects of life, drawing on Quran, hadith, and scholarly traditions.
bio_id: Ibn Muflih al-Maqdisi (708–763 H) adalah ulama Hanbali terkemuka dan murid Ibn Taimiyah yang dekat. Ibn Taimiyah berkata tentangnya: 'Tidak ada di bawah kubah langit ulama fikih yang lebih besar dari Ibn Muflih.'

title: Shaykh
bio_short_en: 10th-century AH Hanbali scholar, author of Muntaha al-Iradat, a foundational text of the later Hanbali school in the Hijaz.
bio_en: Muhammad ibn Ahmad al-Futuhi, known as Ibn al-Najjar (898–972 AH / 1498–1564 CE), was a Hanbali scholar of Cairo. His Muntaha al-Iradat fi Jam' al-Muqni' ma al-Tanqih wa Ziyada became one of the most important reference texts of the later Hanbali school in the Hijaz alongside al-Iqna'. He synthesized the major Hanbali positions and served as the basis for several major later commentaries.
bio_id: Ibn al-Najjar al-Futuhi (898–972 H) adalah ulama Hanbali abad ke-10 H. Karyanya Muntaha al-Iradat menjadi salah satu teks rujukan terpenting mazhab Hanbali pada masa akhir di Hijaz.

name_full_en: Jamal al-Din Abdullah ibn Yusuf al-Zayla'i al-Hanafi
title: Hafiz
bio_short_en: Hanafi hadith master, author of Nasb al-Rayah li Ahadith al-Hidayah, the definitive reference for the hadith evidence of the Hanafi school.
bio_en: Jamal al-Din Abdullah ibn Yusuf al-Zayla'i (672–762 AH / 1274–1360 CE) was a major Hanafi hadith scholar of Cairo. His Nasb al-Rayah li Ahadith al-Hidayah is a monumental four-volume work that documents all the hadith cited in the foundational Hanafi text al-Hidayah, evaluating their chains of transmission, identifying stronger and weaker versions, and providing comparative analysis. This work demonstrates the hadith foundations of Hanafi jurisprudence and remains an essential reference for researchers. Note: not to be confused with Fakhr al-Din al-Zayla'i who has a similar name but different first name.
bio_id: Jamal al-Din al-Zayla'i (672–762 H) adalah ahli hadis Hanafi terkemuka di Kairo. Karyanya Nasb al-Rayah mendokumentasikan semua hadis yang dikutip dalam al-Hidayah dengan analisis riwayat yang komprehensif.

title: Imam
bio_short_en: Leading Hanafi scholar of Baghdad, author of al-Mukhtasar (known as Mukhtasar al-Quduri), the most widely memorized Hanafi primer in Islamic history.
bio_en: Ahmad ibn Muhammad al-Quduri (362–428 AH / 972–1037 CE) was the foremost Hanafi scholar of Baghdad in his generation and was known as Shaykh al-Madhab (Chief of the School). His al-Mukhtasar (Compendium), known as Mukhtasar al-Quduri, is a concise but comprehensive introduction to Hanafi fiqh that became the most widely memorized Hanafi primer in Islamic history. It covers the basics of Islamic practice from ritual purity to commercial transactions in clear, accessible language. It remains in active use in traditional Islamic schools (madrasas) across South Asia, Central Asia, and among Hanafi Muslims worldwide.
bio_id: al-Quduri (362–428 H) adalah ulama Hanafi terkemuka di Baghdad. Karyanya al-Mukhtasar (Mukhtasar al-Quduri) adalah primer fikih Hanafi yang paling banyak dihafal dalam sejarah Islam.

name_full_en: Abdullah ibn Ahmad ibn Mahmud al-Nasafi
title: Hafiz
bio_short_en: Major Hanafi jurist and Quran scholar, author of Kanz al-Daqa'iq (a foundational Hanafi text) and Tafsir al-Nasafi (Madarik al-Tanzil).
bio_en: Abdullah ibn Ahmad al-Nasafi (630–710 AH / 1234–1310 CE) was a major Hanafi scholar and Quran commentator of the 7th century AH. His Kanz al-Daqa'iq fi Furu' al-Hanafiyya is a concise but comprehensive summary of Hanafi jurisprudence that became one of the standard teaching texts of the school, generating several major commentaries (including al-Bahr al-Ra'iq by Ibn Nujaym and Tabyin al-Haqa'iq by al-Zayla'i). His Tafsir al-Nasafi, known as Madarik al-Tanzil wa Haqa'iq al-Ta'wil, is a respected Quran commentary that combines Ash'ari theology with Hanafi jurisprudential perspectives.
bio_id: Abu al-Barakat al-Nasafi (630–710 H) adalah ulama Hanafi dan mufassir terkemuka abad ke-7 H. Karyanya Kanz al-Daqa'iq menjadi teks pengajaran standar Hanafi, dan tafsirnya Madarik al-Tanzil adalah komentar Quran yang dihormati.

name_full_en: Ala al-Din Abu Bakr ibn Mas'ud al-Kasani al-Hanafi
title: Malik al-Ulama
bio_short_en: Titled 'King of the Scholars,' Hanafi jurist of the 6th century AH, author of Bada'i' al-Sana'i', a systematic masterpiece of Hanafi law.
bio_en: Abu Bakr ibn Mas'ud al-Kasani (514–587 AH / 1120–1189 CE), titled Malik al-Ulama (King of the Scholars), was one of the most gifted Hanafi jurists of the 6th century AH. His Bada'i' al-Sana'i' fi Tartib al-Shara'i' (Wonders of the Arts in the Ordering of the Laws) is a seven-volume systematic masterpiece of Hanafi law, presenting the legal rules in a beautifully organized manner that shows the logical interconnections between different rules. It stands out for its clear arrangement and the way it presents the underlying rationale (ta'lil) for legal rules. It is a landmark work in the development of the Hanafi tradition. Note: may already exist in DB under slightly different name.
bio_id: al-Kasani (514–587 H), bergelar 'Raja Ulama', adalah ahli fikih Hanafi berbakat abad ke-6 H. Karyanya Bada'i' al-Sana'i' adalah mahakarya sistematis fikih Hanafi yang terorganisir dengan indah.

title: Imam
bio_short_en: First Hanbali scholar to systematically compile the school's positions in written form, his al-Mukhtasar was foundational for the school.
bio_en: Umar ibn al-Husayn al-Khiraqi (267–334 AH / 880–945 CE) was a Baghdadi Hanbali scholar who is credited with being the first to systematically compile the positions of the Hanbali school in written legal form. His al-Mukhtasar (Compendium) was the first comprehensive Hanbali fiqh manual and became the foundation upon which later Hanbali scholars built. Ibn Qudama's famous al-Mughni is written as a commentary on al-Khiraqi's al-Mukhtasar. By reducing the oral teachings of the school to written form, al-Khiraqi preserved and systematized the Hanbali tradition.
bio_id: al-Khiraqi (267–334 H) adalah ulama Hanbali Baghdad yang pertama kali menyusun posisi-posisi mazhab Hanbali secara sistematis dalam tulisan. Al-Mughniy Ibn Qudama ditulis sebagai syarah atas al-Mukhtasar al-Khiraqi.

name_full_en: Muhammad ibn al-Husayn ibn Muhammad al-Farra' al-Baghdadi
title: Qadi
bio_short_en: Chief judge and leading Hanbali scholar of 5th-century AH Baghdad, author of al-Mu'tamad fi Usul al-Din and al-Ahkam al-Sultaniyyah.
bio_en: Muhammad ibn al-Husayn al-Farra', known as Abu Ya'la (380–458 AH / 990–1066 CE), was the foremost Hanbali scholar of 5th-century AH Baghdad. He served as chief judge of Baghdad. His al-Mu'tamad fi Usul al-Din (The Relied-Upon in Principles of Religion) is an important Hanbali theological work. His al-Ahkam al-Sultaniyyah (Sultanical Rulings) is a significant work on Islamic political theory and public law. He played a crucial role in developing the Hanbali school's systematic approach to theology and jurisprudence and trained many important scholars including his student al-Qadi Abu Ya'la.
bio_id: Abu Ya'la al-Farra' (380–458 H) adalah hakim kepala dan ulama Hanbali terkemuka Baghdad abad ke-5 H. Karyanya al-Ahkam al-Sultaniyyah adalah karya penting tentang teori politik Islam.

name_full_en: Najm al-Din Sulayman ibn Abd al-Qawi al-Tufi al-Sarsari
title: Shaykh
bio_short_en: Hanbali jurist and legal theorist, known for his controversial theory that maslaha (public interest) can override textual evidence, debated extensively by scholars.
bio_en: Najm al-Din al-Tufi (657–716 AH / 1259–1316 CE) was a Hanbali jurist who is best known in Islamic legal theory for his controversial treatise arguing that maslaha (public interest or welfare) can override texts (Quran and hadith) in non-devotional matters. This view was rejected by the majority of Islamic legal scholars as it could potentially undermine the primacy of revealed text. However, al-Tufi's detailed analysis of the issue has made his treatise an important text in discussions of Islamic legal theory. He also wrote works on usul al-fiqh and logic.
bio_id: al-Tufi (657–716 H) adalah ahli fikih Hanbali dan pakar usul fikih, dikenal karena teorinya yang kontroversial bahwa maslaha dapat mengesampingkan teks dalam perkara non-ibadah, yang ditolak mayoritas ulama.

name_full_en: Dawud ibn Ali ibn Khalaf al-Isbahani al-Zahiri
title: Imam
bio_short_en: Founder of the Zahiri school of Islamic jurisprudence, which adheres strictly to the apparent (zahir) meaning of texts without qiyas.
bio_en: Dawud ibn Ali al-Isbahani (200–270 AH / 815–883 CE), known as Dawud al-Zahiri, was the founder of the Zahiri (literalist) school of Islamic jurisprudence. Born in Kufa and based in Baghdad, he initially studied Shafi'i fiqh but developed his own distinct methodology: adhering strictly to the apparent (zahir) meaning of Quran and hadith and rejecting analogical reasoning (qiyas). His school attracted followers but eventually dwindled, with the Andalusian scholar Ibn Hazm being its most famous later representative. Dawud al-Zahiri trained many scholars and was well respected even by those who disagreed with his methodology.
bio_id: Dawud al-Zahiri (200–270 H) adalah pendiri mazhab Zahiri dalam fikih Islam, yang berpegang teguh pada makna zahir (lahiriah) teks tanpa qiyas. Muridnya adalah Ibn Hazm al-Andalusi.

name_full_en: Ali ibn Abdullah ibn Ja'far al-Sa'di al-Basri
title: Imam
bio_short_en: The greatest hadith scholar of his era after Imam Ahmad, teacher of Imam al-Bukhari, master of rijal criticism ('ilm al-rijal).
bio_en: Ali ibn Abdullah ibn al-Madini (161–234 AH / 778–849 CE) was the foremost hadith scholar of his generation and is considered by many to be the greatest scholar of hadith criticism (ilm al-rijal) after Imam Ahmad ibn Hanbal. He was the teacher of Imam al-Bukhari, and al-Bukhari reportedly said: 'I never felt humble before anyone as I felt humble before Ali ibn al-Madini.' He wrote on the illnesses (ilal) of hadith and on the evaluation of narrators, establishing critical tools that became foundational for later hadith scholarship. He was the leading authority on hadith chains and narrators in Basra and later Baghdad.
bio_id: Ali ibn al-Madini (161–234 H) adalah ahli hadis terbesar zamannya setelah Imam Ahmad. Guru Imam al-Bukhari ini adalah otoritas utama dalam kritik periwayat (ilmu rijal).

name_full_en: Ubaydullah ibn Abd al-Karim al-Razi al-Qurashi
title: Imam
bio_short_en: Major hadith scholar and critic of 3rd-century AH, hadith expert of Ray (Iran), close associate of Imam al-Bukhari and Abu Hatim al-Razi.
bio_en: Ubaydullah ibn Abd al-Karim Abu Zur'ah al-Razi (200–264 AH / 817–878 CE) was a leading hadith scholar of the 3rd century AH, based in Ray (near modern Tehran). He was a close associate of Imam al-Bukhari and a peer of Abu Hatim al-Razi. Together, Abu Zur'ah and Abu Hatim represent the leading hadith criticism school of the Razi (Persian) region. His collection of narrators' evaluations (known as Su'alat Abu Zur'ah) is an important reference in hadith criticism. Imam al-Bukhari showed him drafts of the Sahih and benefited from his opinions.
bio_id: Abu Zur'ah al-Razi (200–264 H) adalah ahli hadis terkemuka abad ke-3 H di Ray, Persia. Ia adalah sahabat dekat Imam al-Bukhari dan salah satu otoritas utama dalam kritik periwayat.

name_full_en: Muhammad ibn Idris ibn al-Mundhir al-Razi al-Hanzali
title: Imam
bio_short_en: One of the most rigorous hadith critics of the 3rd century AH, his collected evaluations of narrators are among the most authoritative in the science.
bio_en: Muhammad ibn Idris Abu Hatim al-Razi (195–277 AH / 798–875 CE) was one of the most rigorous and strict hadith critics of the classical period. Based in Ray, he traveled extensively to collect hadiths and evaluate narrators. His collected judgments, preserved by his son Ibn Abi Hatim in al-Jarh wa al-Ta'dil (a massive encyclopedic work on narrator evaluation), are among the most authoritative and widely-cited in hadith scholarship. He was known for the strictness of his standards, accepting fewer narrators as fully reliable than some of his peers. His son's encyclopedia based on his evaluations is a primary reference in hadith criticism.
bio_id: Abu Hatim al-Razi (195–277 H) adalah salah satu kritikus hadis paling ketat abad ke-3 H. Penilaiannya atas periwayat, yang dipreservasi oleh putranya Ibn Abi Hatim, adalah rujukan utama dalam ilmu jarh wa ta'dil.

name_full_en: Abd al-Rahman ibn Muhammad ibn Idris al-Razi
title: Imam
bio_short_en: Major hadith scholar and Quran commentator, author of al-Jarh wa al-Ta'dil and an important Quran tafsir, son of Abu Hatim al-Razi.
bio_en: Abd al-Rahman ibn Abi Hatim al-Razi (240–327 AH / 854–938 CE), son of Abu Hatim al-Razi, was a major hadith scholar and Quran commentator. His most important contribution is the encyclopedic al-Jarh wa al-Ta'dil (Impugning and Validating), a nine-volume work that documents the evaluations of thousands of hadith narrators by the major critics of the classical period, including his father's judgments. It is one of the most comprehensive and important references in hadith criticism. He also wrote a tafsir (Quran commentary) preserved in fragmentary form that is an important source for early Quran interpretation.
bio_id: Ibn Abi Hatim al-Razi (240–327 H) adalah ahli hadis dan mufassir terkemuka, penulis al-Jarh wa al-Ta'dil, ensiklopedi penilaian periwayat hadis yang komprehensif.

name_full_en: Ali ibn Muhammad ibn Abd al-Malik al-Qattan al-Fasi
title: Imam
bio_short_en: Leading Maliki hadith critic and legal scholar of 7th-century AH Morocco, author of Bayan al-Wahm wa al-Iham, a critique of hadith evaluations.
bio_en: Ali ibn Muhammad al-Qattan al-Fasi (562–628 AH / 1150–1231 CE) was the foremost Maliki hadith critic of the Islamic West in the 7th century AH. His Bayan al-Wahm wa al-Iham is a critique of hadith evaluations in Ibn al-Hazm's and al-Hazimi's works, correcting what he saw as errors in their judgment. He also wrote al-Iqtinah' fi Ahkam al-Istibra', demonstrating mastery of both Maliki fiqh and hadith criticism. He represents the Maliki tradition's serious engagement with hadith methodology.
bio_id: Ibn al-Qattan al-Fasi (562–628 H) adalah kritikus hadis Maliki terkemuka di Maroko abad ke-7 H, dikenal karena karyanya Bayan al-Wahm wa al-Iham yang mengkoreksi penilaian hadis.

title: Imam
bio_short_en: The greatest Arabic grammarian of the Kufa school, author of Ma'ani al-Quran, a foundational work for Quranic linguistics.
bio_en: Yahya ibn Ziyad al-Farra' (144–207 AH / 761–822 CE) was the foremost grammarian of the Kufa school and one of the greatest Arabic linguists in Islamic history. His Ma'ani al-Quran (The Meanings of the Quran) is an early and foundational work that analyzes the grammatical and linguistic aspects of every verse in the Quran. Unlike the Basra school (represented by Sibawayhi), the Kufa school had its own analytical approach to Arabic grammar. Al-Farra''s work is an essential reference for understanding the linguistic foundations of Quranic interpretation.
bio_id: al-Farra' (144–207 H) adalah ahli tata bahasa Arab terbesar dari mazhab Kufah. Karyanya Ma'ani al-Quran adalah karya dasar untuk linguistik Quranik.

title: Imam
bio_short_en: Major Quran commentator of the 5th century AH, author of al-Kashf wa al-Bayan, an early comprehensive tafsir.
bio_en: Ahmad ibn Muhammad al-Tha'labi (350–427 AH / 960–1035 CE) was a major Quran commentator of the 5th century AH based in Nishapur. His al-Kashf wa al-Bayan 'an Tafsir al-Quran (The Revelation and Clarification Concerning the Interpretation of the Quran) is a large early tafsir that became a source for later commentators. He is also known for Ara'is al-Majalis (known in English as Lives of the Prophets), a compilation of stories about Quranic figures. His tafsir quotes many early authorities and is a valuable source for the history of Quranic interpretation.
bio_id: al-Tha'labi (350–427 H) adalah mufassir terkemuka abad ke-5 H di Nishapur. Karyanya al-Kashf wa al-Bayan adalah tafsir komprehensif awal yang menjadi sumber bagi para mufassir kemudian.

name_full_en: Muhammad ibn Umar ibn al-Husayn Fakhr al-Din al-Razi
title: Imam
bio_short_en: Brilliant Ash'ari theologian and polymath, author of Mafatih al-Ghayb (the Great Commentary), one of the most encyclopedic Quran commentaries.
bio_en: Muhammad ibn Umar Fakhr al-Din al-Razi (544–606 AH / 1149–1209 CE) was one of the most brilliant polymaths of Islamic civilization. His Quran commentary Mafatih al-Ghayb (Keys of the Unseen), also called al-Tafsir al-Kabir (The Great Commentary), is a 32-volume work that is encyclopedic in its treatment, engaging philosophy, theology, science, and law alongside the traditional tafsir approach. It is the primary reference for Ash'ari theological interpretation of the Quran. He was also one of the greatest Ash'ari theologians, writing extensive works in kalam (Islamic theology). His al-Arba'in fi Usul al-Din and other theological works represent the peak of Ash'ari scholastic theology.
bio_id: Fakhr al-Din al-Razi (544–606 H) adalah salah satu ilmuwan terbesar peradaban Islam. Tafsirnya Mafatih al-Ghayb (32 jilid) adalah ensiklopedi tafsir Quran paling komprehensif dari perspektif Asy'ari.

name_full_en: Muhammad al-Tahir ibn Muhammad ibn Muhammad al-Tahir ibn Ashur
title: Shaykh al-Islam
bio_short_en: Tunisia's greatest 20th-century scholar, author of al-Tahrir wa al-Tanwir (the most significant modern Quran commentary) and Maqasid al-Shariah.
bio_en: Muhammad al-Tahir ibn Ashur (1296–1393 AH / 1879–1973 CE) was the Shaykh al-Islam of Tunisia and the foremost Islamic scholar of the 20th century Arab world. He served as the rector of the Zaytuna University in Tunis and wrote prolifically on Islamic law, Quran commentary, and legal philosophy. His al-Tahrir wa al-Tanwir (Liberation and Enlightenment) is a 30-volume Quran commentary widely regarded as the most significant modern Arabic tafsir for its engagement with contemporary issues and linguistic depth. His Maqasid al-Shari'ah al-Islamiyya (The Objectives of Islamic Law) is a landmark work that reexamined the theory of Islamic legal objectives (maqasid) and influenced modern Islamic legal thought.
bio_id: Ibn Ashur (1296–1393 H) adalah ulama Tunisia terbesar abad ke-20 dan Syaikh al-Islam. Karyanya al-Tahrir wa al-Tanwir adalah tafsir Arab modern yang paling signifikan, dan Maqasid al-Syariah mempengaruhi pemikiran hukum Islam kontemporer.

name_full_en: Ibrahim Mustafa Sayyid Qutb Ibrahim al-Shadhili
title: Shahid
bio_short_en: Egyptian literary critic turned Islamic thinker, author of Fi Zilal al-Quran and Milestones; highly influential but also highly controversial in his later writings.
bio_en: Sayyid Qutb (1324–1386 AH / 1906–1966 CE) was an Egyptian literary critic who became an Islamic thinker and leading ideologue of the Muslim Brotherhood. His tafsir Fi Zilal al-Quran (In the Shade of the Quran) is widely read for its literary and spiritual reflections. However, his later works, particularly Ma'alim fi al-Tariq (Milestones), have been criticized by major Islamic scholars for promoting ideas about takfir (declaring Muslims apostates) and jihad against Muslim governments that many scholars consider to be outside mainstream Sunni thought. His works require careful reading with awareness of the scholarly critiques. He was executed by the Egyptian government in 1966.
bio_id: Sayyid Qutb (1324–1386 H) adalah kritikus sastra Mesir yang kemudian menjadi pemikir Islam dan ideolog Ikhwanul Muslimin. Tafsirnya Fi Zilal al-Quran dibaca luas, namun karya-karya belakangnya dikritik para ulama karena gagasan takfir dan jihad yang dianggap di luar manhaj Sunni.

name_full_en: Muhammad al-Amin ibn Muhammad al-Mukhtar al-Jakani al-Shinqiti
title: Shaykh
bio_short_en: Mauritanian scholar who taught in Medina and authored Adwa al-Bayan, a unique tafsir that explains the Quran through other Quranic verses.
bio_en: Muhammad al-Amin al-Shinqiti (1325–1393 AH / 1905–1974 CE) was a Mauritanian Maliki scholar who traveled to Arabia and was appointed professor at the Islamic University of Medina and the Prophet's Mosque. His Adwa al-Bayan fi Idah al-Quran bil-Quran (Lights of Clarification in Explaining the Quran by the Quran) is a unique tafsir that primarily uses other Quranic verses to explain each verse being commented upon. This approach, rooted in the principle that the Quran is its own best interpreter, makes it a distinctive and valuable contribution to tafsir literature. He was known for his encyclopedic knowledge of the Arabic language and classical Islamic sciences. Note: this scholar may already be in the DB.
bio_id: al-Shinqiti (1325–1393 H) adalah ulama Mauritania yang mengajar di Madinah. Karyanya Adwa al-Bayan adalah tafsir unik yang menjelaskan Al-Quran melalui ayat-ayat Al-Quran lainnya.

name_full_en: Ismail ibn Umar ibn Kathir al-Qurashi al-Dimashqi
title: Imam
bio_short_en: Student of Ibn Taymiyya, author of the most widely-read Quran tafsir (Tafsir Ibn Kathir) and al-Bidayah wa al-Nihayah (history).
bio_en: Ismail ibn Umar ibn Kathir al-Dimashqi (700–774 AH / 1300–1373 CE) was a Shafi'i scholar and one of the most important students of Ibn Taymiyya. His Tafsir al-Quran al-Azim (known as Tafsir Ibn Kathir) is the most widely read and translated Quran commentary in the modern era, valued for its clear explanations, extensive citation of earlier authorities, and strong emphasis on hadith. It is considered the most accessible of the major classical tafsirs for general readers. His al-Bidayah wa al-Nihayah (The Beginning and the End) is a comprehensive Islamic history from creation to his own time. Note: may already be in DB.
bio_id: Ibn Kathir al-Dimashqi (700–774 H) adalah murid Ibn Taimiyah dan penulis tafsir Quran yang paling banyak dibaca di era modern (Tafsir Ibn Kathir) serta al-Bidayah wa al-Nihayah, sejarah Islam yang komprehensif.

bio_short_en: One of the seven canonical Quran reciters, Imam of Madinah in recitation for over sixty years.
bio_en: Nafi' ibn Abi Nu'aym al-Madani (d. 169H) was one of the Seven Canonical Reciters (al-Qurra' al-Sab'ah) and the undisputed authority of Quranic recitation in Madinah. Of Abyssinian origin, he studied under more than seventy companions and their followers, including Abdur-Rahman ibn Hurmuz al-A'raj and Abu Ja'far Yazid ibn al-Qa'qa'. He served as the Imam of the Quranic recitation of Madinah for over sixty years. His two primary transmitters were Warsh (d. 197H) and Qalun (d. 220H), whose recitations remain widely used today in North and West Africa and parts of the Arab world. Imam Malik reportedly said about Nafi': 'He is the Imam of the Muslims in recitation.'
bio_id: Nafi' al-Madani (w. 169H) adalah salah satu dari Tujuh Qari Kanonik (al-Qurra' al-Sab'ah) dan otoritas tak terbantahkan dalam tilawah Quran di Madinah. Ia belajar dari lebih dari tujuh puluh sahabat dan tabi'in. Dua perawi utamanya adalah Warsh dan Qalun, yang bacaannya masih digunakan luas di Afrika Utara dan Barat.

bio_short_en: One of the seven canonical Quran reciters of Kufa; his recitation via Hafs is the most widely used in the world today.
bio_en: Asim ibn Abi al-Najud al-Asadi al-Kufi (d. 127H) was one of the Seven Canonical Reciters and the most important reciter of Kufa. He learned the Quran from Abu Abd al-Rahman al-Sulami and Zirr ibn Hubaysh, who both traced their chains back to Uthman ibn Affan and Ali ibn Abi Talib respectively. His two primary transmitters were Hafs ibn Sulayman and Shu'bah ibn 'Ayyash. The recitation of Hafs 'an Asim became the dominant standard in the Muslim world, used across the Middle East, most of Asia, Africa, and beyond. It is the recitation of the mushaf distributed by Saudi Arabia.
bio_id: Asim ibn Abi al-Najud (w. 127H) adalah salah satu dari Tujuh Qari Kanonik dan qari paling penting dari Kufah. Dua perawi utamanya adalah Hafs ibn Sulayman dan Shu'bah. Bacaan Hafs 'an Asim menjadi standar dominan di dunia Muslim dan merupakan bacaan mushaf yang didistribusikan secara luas.

bio_short_en: One of the seven canonical Quran reciters of Kufa, known for precise elongation rules and asceticism.
bio_en: Hamzah ibn Habib al-Zayyat al-Kufi (80-156H) was one of the Seven Canonical Reciters, representing Kufa alongside Asim. He was a student of al-A'mash and Ja'far al-Sadiq among others, and transmitted the recitation from Humran ibn A'yan. Known for his strict elongations (madd) and precise articulation points, his recitation was transmitted through Khalaf and Khallad. Despite being a merchant (al-Zayyat = oil merchant), he was renowned for asceticism and worship. Imam Ahmad praised his recitation highly.
bio_id: Hamzah al-Zayyat (80-156H) adalah salah satu dari Tujuh Qari Kanonik, mewakili Kufah. Dikenal karena pemanjangan madd yang ketat dan artikulasi yang tepat. Bacaannya ditransmisikan melalui Khalaf dan Khallad.

bio_short_en: One of the seven canonical Quran reciters; also the leading grammarian of the Kufi school of Arabic linguistics.
bio_en: Ali ibn Hamzah al-Kisa'i al-Kufi (119-189H) was both one of the Seven Canonical Reciters and the leading grammarian of the Kufi school of Arabic linguistics. He was the personal teacher of Harun al-Rashid's sons and served as chief tutor at the Abbasid court. In recitation, he studied under Hamzah al-Zayyat and others. His recitation is transmitted through Abu al-Harith and Hafs al-Du'ri. In grammar, he famously debated Sibawayhi, the founder of the Basri school. His breadth — recitation, grammar, and literature — made him one of the most versatile scholars of early Islam.
bio_id: Al-Kisa'i al-Kufi (119-189H) adalah salah satu dari Tujuh Qari Kanonik sekaligus ahli nahwu terkemuka dari mazhab Kufah. Ia menjadi guru pribadi putra-putra Harun al-Rashid dan dikenal karena keluasan ilmu dalam bidang tilawah, tata bahasa, dan sastra.

bio_short_en: Primary transmitter of Asim's recitation; his chain (Hafs 'an Asim) is the most widely used Quranic recitation in the world.
bio_en: Hafs ibn Sulayman ibn al-Mughirah al-Asadi al-Kufi (90-180H) was the stepson and primary student of Asim ibn Abi al-Najud. He transmitted Asim's recitation with such precision and authority that the chain Hafs 'an Asim became the global standard. While early hadith scholars were cautious about his reliability as a hadith narrator, his mastery of Quranic recitation was never questioned. The mushaf distributed by the Saudi government and used across the Muslim world follows Hafs 'an Asim. Every Muslim who recites the Quran from a standard printed copy is, in a sense, following his transmission.
bio_id: Hafs ibn Sulayman (90-180H) adalah anak tiri dan murid utama Asim ibn Abi al-Najud. Ia mentransmisikan bacaan Asim dengan presisi tinggi, sehingga sanad Hafs 'an Asim menjadi standar global. Setiap mushaf yang dicetak dan didistribusikan secara luas mengikuti riwayatnya.

bio_short_en: Prolific hadith compiler known for his three Mu'jam collections covering thousands of companions' narrations.
bio_en: Sulayman ibn Ahmad al-Tabarani (260-360H) was one of the most prolific hadith collectors in Islamic history. Born in Tabariyyah (Tiberias) and having lived to the remarkable age of 100, he traveled widely and compiled three monumental Mu'jam (dictionary-arranged) collections: al-Mu'jam al-Kabir (massive, containing narrations of over a thousand companions), al-Mu'jam al-Awsat (arranged by teachers), and al-Mu'jam al-Saghir. Together these collections contain tens of thousands of hadith and serve as primary sources for many narrations found nowhere else. He was highly regarded but hadith critics note that his chains occasionally include weak narrators.
bio_id: Al-Tabarani (260-360H) adalah salah satu pengumpul hadis paling produktif dalam sejarah Islam. Ia menyusun tiga koleksi Mu'jam: al-Kabir, al-Awsat, dan al-Saghir, yang bersama-sama memuat puluhan ribu hadis.

bio_short_en: Major hadith scholar known as 'The Master of Hadith'; author of al-Mustadrak 'ala al-Sahihayn.
bio_en: Abu Abdallah Muhammad ibn Abdallah al-Hakim al-Naysaburi (321-405H) was the leading hadith authority of his era in Khurasan, known as al-Hakim (The Master). His most important work, al-Mustadrak 'ala al-Sahihayn, collects hadiths he considered met the criteria of Bukhari and Muslim but were not included in their collections — a claim that subsequent scholars have assessed with varying degrees of agreement. He also wrote al-Madkhal ila 'Ilm al-Sahih, Ma'rifat 'Ulum al-Hadith, and biographies of Shafi'i scholars of Naysabur. Critics, including al-Dhahabi, noted he was sometimes lenient in authenticating hadiths.
bio_id: Al-Hakim al-Naysaburi (321-405H) adalah otoritas hadis terkemuka di Khurasan, dikenal sebagai 'al-Hakim'. Karyanya al-Mustadrak mengumpulkan hadis yang dianggap memenuhi kriteria Bukhari dan Muslim namun tidak dimasukkan dalam koleksi mereka.

bio_short_en: Foremost Islamic scholar of the Indian subcontinent; synthesizer of the four madhabs and author of Hujjat Allah al-Balighah.
bio_en: Ahmad ibn Abd al-Rahim al-Dihlawi, known as Shah Wali Allah (1703-1762 CE / 1114-1176H), was the most important Islamic scholar of the Indian subcontinent in the 18th century. Born in Delhi, he studied under his father before traveling to Makkah and Madinah, where he received ijazas from major scholars. His masterpiece, Hujjat Allah al-Balighah (The Conclusive Argument from God), is a comprehensive work explaining the wisdom and social principles underlying Islamic law. He sought to reconcile differences between the four madhabs, to revive hadith studies in India, and to translate the Quran into Persian to make it accessible. His intellectual tradition profoundly influenced later movements including the Deobandis and Barelwis.
bio_id: Shah Wali Allah al-Dihlawi (1703-1762 M) adalah ulama Islam terpenting di anak benua India abad ke-18. Karyanya Hujjat Allah al-Balighah merupakan karya komprehensif yang menjelaskan hikmah dan prinsip sosial di balik hukum Islam. Ia berupaya merekonsiliasi perbedaan empat mazhab.

bio_short_en: Leading female scholar of hadith and fiqh among the Tabi'in; her legal opinions were cited by major Damascene scholars.
bio_en: Umm al-Darda' al-Sughra (Hujayma bint Huyayy al-Awsabiyyah, d. 81H) was the leading female scholar of the generation after the Companions (Tabi'in) in Syria. She studied hadith from Abu Darda' (whom she later married), Abu Hurayra, 'Aisha, Mu'awiyah ibn Abi Sufyan, and other companions. She was known to sit in the men's learning circles (halaqas) in the mosque and issue legal opinions. The Caliph Abd al-Malik ibn Marwan reportedly studied under her. Her legal opinions circulated among Damascene scholars. She represents a documented precedent of women teaching men in Islamic scholarship.
bio_id: Umm al-Darda' al-Sughra (w. 81H) adalah ulama wanita terkemuka di kalangan Tabi'in di Suriah. Ia belajar hadis dari Abu Darda', Abu Hurayra, 'Aisyah, dan sahabat lainnya. Khalifah Abd al-Malik ibn Marwan dilaporkan belajar darinya.

bio_short_en: Preeminent female hadith scholar of the 5th century; the highest authority for transmitting Sahih al-Bukhari in her era.
bio_en: Karimah bint Ahmad al-Marwaziyyah (d. 463H) was the foremost female hadith scholar of the 5th century of Islam and the most authoritative transmitter of Sahih al-Bukhari in her time. She received a continuous chain of transmission for Sahih al-Bukhari going back to al-Kushmayhani (a student of al-Firabri, who was a direct student of al-Bukhari). Al-Khatib al-Baghdadi and al-Khatib al-Tibrizi and other major scholars traveled to Makkah to hear Sahih al-Bukhari from her. She lived well past 100 years. Ibn Hajar al-Asqalani praised her chain as the highest (a'la) available for Sahih al-Bukhari.
bio_id: Karimah al-Marwaziyyah (w. 463H) adalah ulama hadis wanita terkemuka abad ke-5 dan otoritas tertinggi dalam mentransmisikan Sahih al-Bukhari pada zamannya. Para ulama besar bepergian ke Makkah untuk mendengar Sahih al-Bukhari darinya.

bio_short_en: Chief Mufti (Sheikh al-Islam) of the Ottoman Empire for 30 years; author of a major Quran commentary.
bio_en: Ebussuud Efendi (Muhammad ibn Muhammad al-Amadi, 1490-1574 CE) served as the Sheikh al-Islam (Chief Mufti) of the Ottoman Empire for thirty years under Suleiman the Magnificent and his son Selim II — the longest tenure in Ottoman history. He was responsible for legitimizing many of Suleiman's legal reforms, famously harmonizing Hanafi jurisprudence with Ottoman administrative law (kanun). His Quranic commentary, Irshad al-'Aql al-Salim ila Mazaya al-Kitab al-Karim, is one of the most important Ottoman tafsirs. He also wrote fatwas that integrated traditional Islamic law with the practical needs of a vast empire.
bio_id: Ebussuud Efendi (1490-1574 M) adalah Sheikh al-Islam (Mufti Agung) Kesultanan Ottoman selama tiga puluh tahun di bawah Suleiman al-Qanuni. Ia bertanggung jawab menyelaraskan fikih Hanafi dengan hukum administratif Ottoman, dan menulis tafsir Quran Irshad al-'Aql al-Salim.

bio_short_en: Central Asian polymath who wrote authoritative commentaries on kalam, usul al-fiqh, and Arabic rhetoric.
bio_en: Sa'd al-Din Masud ibn Umar al-Taftazani (722-792H / 1322-1390 CE) was a Central Asian polymath who served at the courts of the Timurids and other rulers. He wrote authoritative commentaries on foundational texts in multiple Islamic sciences: Sharh al-Aqaid al-Nasafiyyah (theology), Sharh al-Talwih (Hanafi usul al-fiqh), Sharh Mukhtasar Ibn al-Hajib (Maliki usul), Sharh al-Maqasid (kalam), and al-Mutawwal and al-Mukhtasar (Arabic rhetoric). These commentaries became standard teaching texts in madrasas across the Islamic world for centuries. He famously debated al-Jurjani at Timur's court on questions of theology.
bio_id: Al-Taftazani (722-792H) adalah sarjana polimatis Asia Tengah yang menulis komentar otoritatif dalam berbagai ilmu Islam: kalam, usul al-fiqh, dan retorika Arab. Komentar-komentarnya menjadi teks pengajaran standar di madrasah di seluruh dunia Islam.

bio_short_en: Shafi'i judge and biographer; author of Wafayat al-A'yan, one of the most important biographical dictionaries of Islamic civilization.
bio_en: Ahmad ibn Muhammad ibn Khallikan (608-681H / 1211-1282 CE) was a Kurdish Shafi'i jurist who served as chief judge (qadi al-qudat) of Damascus and Egypt. His major work, Wafayat al-A'yan wa Anba' Abna' al-Zaman (Deaths of Notable Men), is one of the most important biographical dictionaries of Islamic civilization, containing entries on over 800 notable scholars, poets, rulers, and intellectuals from the first through seventh Islamic centuries. He provides biographical detail, death dates, and anecdotes not found elsewhere. It was translated into French by de Slane in the 19th century.
bio_id: Ibn Khallikan (608-681H) adalah hakim Syafi'i Kurdi yang menjabat sebagai qadi al-qudat Damaskus dan Mesir. Karyanya Wafayat al-A'yan adalah salah satu kamus biografi terpenting peradaban Islam, memuat entri lebih dari 800 tokoh terkemuka.
works: Wafayat al-A'yan wa Anba' Abna' al-Zaman

bio_short_en: Major hadith scholar and biographer; student of Ibn Hajar al-Asqalani; author of al-Daw' al-Lami' biography of 15th-century scholars.
bio_en: Shams al-Din Muhammad ibn Abd al-Rahman al-Sakhawi (831-902H / 1428-1497 CE) was a major hadith scholar and biographer of Cairo, and the foremost student of Ibn Hajar al-Asqalani. His massive biographical dictionary al-Daw' al-Lami' li Ahl al-Qarn al-Tasi' (The Brilliant Light on the People of the 9th Century) contains over 11,000 entries on scholars and notables of the 9th/15th century — an incomparable source for that period. He also wrote al-Maqasid al-Hasanah on commonly circulated hadiths, al-Qawl al-Badi' on blessings upon the Prophet, and a multi-volume biography of Ibn Hajar al-Asqalani.
bio_id: Al-Sakhawi (831-902H) adalah ahli hadis dan biografi terkemuka Kairo, murid utama Ibn Hajar al-Asqalani. Kamus biografinya al-Daw' al-Lami' memuat lebih dari 11.000 entri tentang ulama abad ke-9 H dan merupakan sumber tak tertandingi untuk periode tersebut.

bio_short_en: Andalusian philosopher and jurist; his commentaries on Aristotle shaped medieval European philosophy.
bio_en: Abu al-Walid Muhammad ibn Ahmad ibn Rushd (1126-1198 CE / 520-595H), known in Europe as Averroes, was an Andalusian philosopher, jurist, physician, and polymath. He served as court physician and chief judge (qadi) in Seville and Cordoba. His extensive commentaries on Aristotle — preserved largely through Latin translations — were foundational for medieval European Scholastic philosophy, earning him the title 'The Commentator' in the Latin world. In Islamic jurisprudence, his Bidayat al-Mujtahid wa Nihayat al-Muqtasid is a landmark comparative fiqh text presenting the legal opinions of all four madhabs with their evidential bases. Late in life he was briefly exiled by the Almohad Caliph due to philosophical controversies.
bio_id: Ibn Rushd (1126-1198 M), dikenal di Eropa sebagai Averroes, adalah filsuf, fuqaha, dan dokter dari Andalus. Komentar-komentarnya atas Aristoteles sangat mempengaruhi filsafat skolastik Eropa abad pertengahan. Dalam fikih, karyanya Bidayat al-Mujtahid adalah teks fikih perbandingan yang monumental.

bio_short_en: Maliki jurist of Fez; compiler of al-Mi'yar al-Mu'rib, the most comprehensive collection of Maliki fatwas from North Africa and al-Andalus.
bio_en: Ahmad ibn Yahya al-Wansharisi (834-914H / 1430-1508 CE) was a Maliki scholar who fled from Tlemcen (Algeria) to Fez (Morocco) and became a leading figure of Moroccan scholarship. His monumental work al-Mi'yar al-Mu'rib wa al-Jami' al-Mughrib 'an Fatawi 'Ulama' Ifriqiyyah wa al-Andalus wa al-Maghrib, comprising 13 volumes, is the largest single collection of Maliki fatwas, gathering legal opinions from scholars of North Africa and al-Andalus across several centuries. It is an invaluable primary source for the social, legal, and economic history of the Maghreb.
bio_id: Al-Wansharisi (834-914H) adalah fuqaha Maliki dari Fez, Maroko. Karyanya al-Mi'yar al-Mu'rib (13 jilid) adalah koleksi fatwa Maliki terbesar, mengumpulkan pendapat hukum dari ulama Afrika Utara dan al-Andalus selama beberapa abad.

bio_short_en: West African Maliki scholar and Sufi leader who unified scholarly and spiritual authority across the Sahara and Sahel.
bio_en: Sidi al-Mukhtar ibn Ahmad al-Kunti (1729-1811 CE / 1142-1226H) was a Maliki scholar, poet, and Sufi leader of the Kunta tribe in the western Sahara. Through his extensive scholarly network — he corresponded with rulers and scholars from Timbuktu to Morocco — he exercised enormous religious and political influence across the Sahara-Sahel region for decades. His works include poetry, fiqh, and Sufi devotional literature. He emphasized the Qadiriyya Sufi order and was a stabilizing force among nomadic tribes during a period of significant conflict. Islam.wiki note: his Sufi institutional affiliations should be contextualized per the theological guidelines.
bio_id: Al-Mukhtar al-Kunti (1729-1811 M) adalah ulama Maliki, penyair, dan pemimpin sufi dari suku Kunta di Sahara barat. Melalui jaringan keilmuannya yang luas, ia memberikan pengaruh besar di kawasan Sahara-Sahel selama beberapa dekade.

bio_short_en: Leading jurist of Kufa in the generation of the Tabi'in; primary teacher of Hammad ibn Abi Sulayman, who taught Abu Hanifah.
bio_en: Ibrahim ibn Yazid al-Nakha'i al-Kufi (50-96H) was the leading jurist of Kufa in the generation after the Companions (Tabi'in). He studied under Abd al-Rahman ibn Yazid (his uncle), al-Aswad ibn Yazid, and transmitted extensively from the scholars of Kufa who had learned directly from Abdullah ibn Masud. He was the primary teacher of Hammad ibn Abi Sulayman, who was in turn the teacher of Abu Hanifah — placing al-Nakha'i as a key link in the Hanafi chain of transmission. His legal opinions were extensively documented and formed the basis of early Kufan jurisprudence.
bio_id: Ibrahim al-Nakha'i (50-96H) adalah fuqaha terkemuka di Kufah dari generasi Tabi'in. Ia adalah guru utama Hammad ibn Abi Sulayman, yang kemudian mengajar Abu Hanifah — menjadikannya mata rantai penting dalam sanad hukum Hanafi.

bio_short_en: Major Tabi'i scholar of Kufa; key transmitter of Abdullah ibn Masud's legal tradition.
bio_en: Al-Aswad ibn Yazid ibn Qays al-Nakha'i (d. 74H) was one of the foremost Tabi'in of Kufa and a key transmitter of the legal and hadith tradition of Abdullah ibn Masud. He was the maternal uncle of Ibrahim al-Nakha'i. He performed Hajj 80 times according to some reports and was known for his extreme devotion and fasting. Imam Ahmad listed him among the most reliable narrators of Kufa. His opinions are frequently cited in Hanafi jurisprudence as representing the Kufan school.
bio_id: Al-Aswad ibn Yazid (w. 74H) adalah salah satu Tabi'in terkemuka di Kufah dan transmitter utama tradisi hukum Abdullah ibn Masud. Ia adalah paman dari pihak ibu Ibrahim al-Nakha'i dan terkenal karena ibadah dan puasa yang intens.

bio_short_en: Important early Tabi'i scholar of Basra; one of the earliest interpreters of the Quran among the Tabi'in.
bio_en: Abu al-Aliyah Rufay' ibn Mihran al-Riyahi al-Basri (d. 90H) was one of the earliest and most important Quran scholars among the Tabi'in. A freed slave who embraced Islam, he studied under Abu Bakr, Umar, Ali, Ubayy ibn Ka'b, Ibn Abbas, and many companions. Al-Hasan al-Basri said: 'Abu al-Aliyah is the most learned person after the Companions.' He was among the first to transmit Quranic exegesis (tafsir) from the companions and his opinions are frequently cited in tafsir works.
bio_id: Abu al-Aliyah al-Riyahi (w. 90H) adalah salah satu ulama tafsir Tabi'in yang paling awal dan paling penting. Al-Hasan al-Basri berkata tentangnya: 'Abu al-Aliyah adalah orang paling berilmu setelah para Sahabat.' Ia mempelajari tafsir dari banyak sahabat terkemuka.

bio_short_en: Important Tabi'i scholar of Madinah; one of the earliest Quran commentators and a key link in the Maliki chain.
bio_en: Zayd ibn Aslam al-'Adawi al-Madani (d. 136H) was a major scholar of Madinah in the Tabi'i generation, the son of Aslam (the freed slave of Umar ibn al-Khattab). He studied under his father, Ibn Umar, Ibn Abbas, Abu Hurayra, and other companions. Imam Malik considered him one of the reliable scholars and narrated from him extensively in the Muwatta'. He is noted for an early and independent approach to Quranic exegesis, using analogical reasoning ('aql) alongside transmitted reports — which some companions found novel. His son Abdur-Rahman ibn Zayd was also a scholar, though less reliable.
bio_id: Zayd ibn Aslam (w. 136H) adalah ulama terkemuka Madinah dari generasi Tabi'in, putra Aslam (mantan budak Umar ibn al-Khattab). Imam Malik mengandalkannya sebagai perawi terpercaya dan banyak meriwayatkan darinya dalam al-Muwatta'.

bio_short_en: Founder of Arabic prosody and compiler of al-'Ayn, the first Arabic dictionary; also credited with developing the Arabic vowel system.
bio_en: Abu Abd al-Rahman al-Khalil ibn Ahmad al-Farahidi al-Basri (100-175H) was one of the most original intellects in early Islamic civilization. He established the science of Arabic prosody ('ilm al-'arud), identifying 15 of the 16 meters of Arabic poetry. He compiled al-'Ayn, widely considered the first comprehensive Arabic dictionary, organized by the place of articulation of letters. He is also credited with systematizing the Arabic vowel marks (tashkil/harakaat) that allow the Quran and Arabic texts to be read accurately. His student Sibawayhi went on to write al-Kitab, the foundational Arabic grammar.
bio_id: Al-Khalil ibn Ahmad al-Farahidi (100-175H) adalah salah satu intelek paling orisinal dalam peradaban Islam awal. Ia mendirikan ilmu prosodi Arab ('arud), menyusun kamus Arab pertama (al-'Ayn), dan dikreditkan dengan menyistematisasi tanda baca Arab (tashkil) yang memungkinkan pembacaan akurat al-Quran.

bio_short_en: Author of al-Kitab, the foundational work of Arabic grammar; student of al-Khalil ibn Ahmad; considered 'the Imam of grammarians'.
bio_en: Abu Bishr Amr ibn Uthman ibn Qanbar al-Basri, known as Sibawayhi (c. 140-180H), was a Persian-origin scholar who wrote al-Kitab (The Book), the foundational work of Arabic grammar and arguably the most important linguistic treatise in Arabic history. A student of al-Khalil ibn Ahmad al-Farahidi, he systematized Arabic grammar with extraordinary precision and depth. Al-Kitab was so comprehensive and authoritative that it became the reference for all subsequent grammarians. He represents the Basri school of grammar, in contrast to the Kufi school led by al-Kisa'i.
bio_id: Sibawayhi (c. 140-180H) adalah penulis al-Kitab, karya dasar tata bahasa Arab dan mungkin risalah linguistik terpenting dalam sejarah Arab. Murid al-Khalil ibn Ahmad, ia mensistematisasi tata bahasa Arab dengan presisi dan kedalaman yang luar biasa.

bio_short_en: Major Arabic lexicographer; author of Tahdhib al-Lughah, an 15-volume dictionary of classical Arabic.
bio_en: Abu Mansur Muhammad ibn Ahmad al-Azhari al-Harawi (282-370H) was a major Arabic lexicographer from Herat (modern Afghanistan). His most important work, Tahdhib al-Lughah, is a 15-volume dictionary of classical Arabic drawing on the early works of al-Khalil, Ibn al-A'rabi, and others, with extensive attention to the roots and derivations of words. He had a remarkable life story: he was captured by the Qarmatians during a Hajj journey and spent years among the Bedouins, which deepened his knowledge of authentic spoken Arabic. His work remains an important reference for understanding classical and Quranic Arabic.
bio_id: Al-Azhari (282-370H) adalah leksikografer Arab terkemuka dari Herat. Karyanya Tahdhib al-Lughah, kamus Arab 15 jilid, tetap menjadi referensi penting untuk memahami bahasa Arab klasik dan Quranik.

bio_short_en: Arabic literary genius; author of the Maqamat al-Hariri, one of the most celebrated works of Arabic prose literature.
bio_en: Abu Muhammad al-Qasim ibn Ali al-Hariri al-Basri (446-516H) was one of the greatest Arabic prose stylists of the Islamic era. His Maqamat (Sessions) — fifty dramatic prose-poem anecdotes featuring the roguish Abu Zayd of Saruj and his narrator al-Harith — is one of the most celebrated works in Arabic literary history, known for its wordplay, puns, rhyming prose, and extraordinary display of Arabic language virtuosity. He was also a scholar who wrote Durrat al-Ghawwas fi Awham al-Khawass, correcting common linguistic errors among educated people. He served as head of intelligence reports (sahib al-khabar) in Basra.
bio_id: Al-Hariri al-Basri (446-516H) adalah salah satu ahli prosa Arab terbesar. Karyanya Maqamat al-Hariri — 50 anekdot dramatis dengan permainan kata dan prosa berirama yang luar biasa — adalah salah satu karya sastra Arab paling terkenal dalam sejarah.

bio_short_en: Leading scholar of the Kufi grammatical school; teacher of al-Hariri's teachers and a legendary Arabic lexicographer.
bio_en: Abu al-Abbas Ahmad ibn Yahya al-Shaybani, known as Tha'lab (200-291H), was the foremost representative of the Kufi school of Arabic grammar in his era. He memorized poetry and grammar from the Bedouins and became the leading authority of the Kufi tradition after al-Kisa'i and al-Farra'. His Majalis (Sessions) recorded by his students, his Qawaid al-Shi'r (Rules of Poetry), and his Fasih al-Kalam (Correct Speech) are among his important works. He went deaf in old age but continued to dictate and teach. He died after falling from his mount, having been startled by a loud shout.
bio_id: Tha'lab (200-291H) adalah wakil terkemuka dari mazhab nahwu Kufah. Ia adalah otoritas utama tradisi Kufah setelah al-Kisa'i dan al-Farra', dan terkenal sebagai leksikografer Arab yang legendaris.

bio_short_en: Andalusian scholar who composed Hirz al-Amani (al-Shatibiyyah), the definitive didactic poem on the Seven Canonical Recitations.
bio_en: Abu Muhammad Qasim ibn Firruh al-Shatibi al-Ru'ayni (538-590H / 1144-1194 CE) was an Andalusian scholar who became blind in childhood but memorized the entire Quran and became a foremost authority in Quranic recitation and other sciences. His famous poem Hirz al-Amani wa Wajh al-Tahani (also called al-Shatibiyyah) contains 1,173 verses encoding the rules of all Seven Canonical Recitations — a feat of mnemonic Arabic poetry that has been memorized and taught for 800 years.
