He also composed a poem on Hadith methodology (Aqila Atrabi al-Qasa'id). He moved from al-Andalus to Egypt and became a teacher at the Fatimid Azhar.
bio_id: Al-Shatibi (538-590H) adalah ulama Andalusia yang buta dari kecil tetapi menghafal al-Quran dan menjadi otoritas terkemuka dalam ilmu qiraat. Qasidahnya Hirz al-Amani (al-Shatibiyyah) — 1.173 bait yang mengkodekan aturan Tujuh Bacaan Kanonik — telah dihafal dan diajarkan selama 800 tahun.

bio_short_en: Greatest authority on Quranic recitation of the medieval era; author of al-Nashr fi al-Qiraat al-Ashr and al-Tayybah.
bio_en: Abu al-Khayr Muhammad ibn Muhammad ibn Muhammad ibn al-Jazari al-Dimashqi (751-833H / 1350-1429 CE) was the undisputed authority on Quranic recitation of his era. He memorized the Quran as a child, studied all Ten Canonical Recitations, and went on to write the most comprehensive works on Quranic recitation in Islamic history. His al-Nashr fi al-Qiraat al-Ashr is the authoritative two-volume reference on the Ten Recitations. His didactic poem al-Tayyibah (al-Tayyibat al-Nashr) — modeled on al-Shatibi's work but covering ten recitations — became the higher-level memorization text for advanced students. He also wrote al-Muqaddimah (al-Jazariyyah), a brief poem on the rules of tajwid that is memorized by millions.
bio_id: Ibn al-Jazari (751-833H) adalah otoritas tak tertandingi dalam ilmu qiraat pada zamannya. Al-Nashr fi al-Qiraat al-Ashr-nya adalah referensi komprehensif dua jilid tentang Sepuluh Bacaan. Al-Muqaddimah (al-Jazariyyah)-nya, sajak ringkas tentang tajwid, dihafal oleh jutaan orang.

bio_short_en: Early Islamic scholar of spiritual psychology and self-examination; his Ri'ayah li Huquq Allah is the first systematic work on Islamic spirituality.
bio_en: Abu Abdallah al-Harith ibn Asad al-Muhasibi al-Basri (165-243H) was an early Islamic scholar of Baghdad known for his pioneering work in Islamic psychology and spiritual ethics. His name al-Muhasibi (self-accountant) reflects his central teaching: constant self-examination (muhasabah). His major work, al-Ri'ayah li Huquq Allah, is considered the first systematic Islamic treatise on spiritual psychology and moral psychology. He was a student of Imam Ahmad ibn Hanbal's contemporaries and taught al-Junayd al-Baghdadi. Note: Imam Ahmad criticized some of his works for using philosophical terminology, so his writings should be read with scholarly guidance.
bio_id: Al-Muhasibi (165-243H) adalah ulama Islam awal dari Baghdad yang terkenal dalam psikologi spiritual dan etika Islam. Karyanya al-Ri'ayah li Huquq Allah dianggap risalah sistematis pertama tentang psikologi spiritual Islam. Catatan: Imam Ahmad mengkritik beberapa karyanya karena penggunaan terminologi filosofis.

bio_short_en: Yemeni jurist and hadith scholar who advocated direct derivation from Quran and Sunnah without strict adherence to a single madhab.
bio_en: Muhammad ibn Ali al-Shawkani al-San'ani (1173-1250H / 1759-1834 CE) was a Yemeni jurist, hadith scholar, and reformer who rose to become the Chief Judge of Yemen under the Zaydi Qasimid imams. Despite his Zaydi upbringing, he moved toward Sunni positions and advocated independent legal reasoning (ijtihad) based directly on Quran and Sunnah, rejecting strict adherence (taqlid) to any single madhab. His Nayl al-Awtar (an explanation of Muntaqa al-Akhbar) is a widely read collection of hadith with legal commentary. His Fath al-Qadir is an important Quran commentary. He is influential among 20th-century Salafi scholars who cite his legal methodology.
bio_id: Al-Shawkani (1173-1250H) adalah fuqaha, ahli hadis, dan reformis Yaman yang menjadi Qadi Agung Yaman. Ia menganjurkan ijtihad langsung dari al-Quran dan Sunnah tanpa taklid ketat pada satu mazhab. Nayl al-Awtar-nya adalah koleksi hadis dengan komentar hukum yang banyak dibaca.

bio_short_en: Levantine hadith scholar; author of Kashf al-Khafa', the standard reference for investigating commonly circulated hadiths.
bio_en: Ismail ibn Muhammad al-Ajluni al-Dimashqi (1087-1162H / 1676-1749 CE) was a Shafi'i hadith scholar and teacher from the town of Ajlun in modern Jordan who settled and taught in Damascus. His major work, Kashf al-Khafa' wa Muzil al-Ilbas 'amma Ishtahara min al-Ahadith 'ala Alsinat al-Nas, is the standard reference for investigating the authenticity and sources of hadiths that have become widely circulated on people's tongues. It examines over 3,000 commonly quoted expressions and provides their sources, grades, and any scholarly evaluations. It is complementary to al-Sakhawi's al-Maqasid al-Hasanah.
bio_id: Ismail al-Ajluni (1087-1162H) adalah ahli hadis Syafi'i dari Levant. Karyanya Kashf al-Khafa' adalah referensi standar untuk menyelidiki keaslian dan sumber hadis yang beredar luas di kalangan masyarakat, memuat lebih dari 3.000 ungkapan.

bio_short_en: Pioneer of hadith studies in India; author of Ashi'at al-Lam'at (commentary on Mishkat) and introducer of formal hadith scholarship to the subcontinent.
bio_en: Abd al-Haqq ibn Sayf al-Din al-Dihlawi (1551-1642 CE / 958-1052H) was the first major hadith scholar of the Indian subcontinent in the modern sense, earning the title Muhaddith of Delhi. He studied hadith in Makkah and Madinah under major Hijazi scholars and returned to India to introduce formal hadith transmission (isnad-based) and commentary. His major works include Ashi'at al-Lam'at, a Persian commentary on Mishkat al-Masabih; al-Lam'at fi Sharh al-Mishkat; and Madarij al-Nubuwwah, a comprehensive seerah work. He also wrote Persian translations of hadith collections to make them accessible to Indian Muslims.
bio_id: Abd al-Haqq al-Dihlawi (1551-1642 M) adalah ulama hadis pertama yang signifikan di anak benua India dalam pengertian modern, memperoleh gelar Muhaddith Delhi. Ia memperkenalkan transmisi hadis formal berbasis sanad ke India. Karyanya termasuk komentar atas Mishkat al-Masabih dan Madarij al-Nubuwwah.

bio_short_en: West African Islamic scholar, jihadist, and ruler; leader of a major Islamic reform movement in 19th-century Senegambia-Sudan.
bio_en: Al-Hajj Umar ibn Sa'id Taal al-Futi (c. 1797-1864 CE) was a West African Islamic scholar, warrior, and founder of the Tijaniyya-based Tukolor Empire in what is now Senegal, Mali, and Guinea. He studied in Madinah and became the West African representative (Khalifa) of the Tijani Sufi order. His major Islamic work, Rimah Hizb al-Rahim 'ala Nuhur Hizb al-Rajim (Spears of God's Mercy against the Armies of Satan), is a defense of the Tijaniyya order. He led military campaigns against the Bambara kingdoms (which had resisted Islam) and the French colonial presence. Islam.wiki note: his Tijani affiliations are contextualized per the theological guidelines on Sufi orders.
bio_id: Al-Hajj Umar Taal (c. 1797-1864 M) adalah ulama, pejuang, dan pendiri Kekaisaran Tukolor di Afrika Barat. Ia menjadi wakil Tijaniyya di Afrika Barat dan menulis Rimah Hizb al-Rahim sebagai pembelaan atas tarikat Tijaniyah. Ia memimpin kampanye militer melawan kerajaan Bambara dan kehadiran kolonial Prancis.

bio_short_en: West African scholar and founder of the Imamate of Futa Jallon; led the first major Islamic reform state in West Africa.
bio_en: Ibrahim Musa Kaba, known as Karamokho Alfa (c. 1698-1762 CE), was a Fula Islamic scholar and the first Imam of Futa Jallon (modern Guinea), where he established one of the first Islamic reform states in West Africa. He led the Fulani jihad of 1725-1727, transforming Futa Jallon from a predominantly animist region into an Islamic state governed by Maliki jurisprudence. He established a system of local mosques and Quranic schools. His leadership established a pattern of Islamic governance that influenced subsequent West African reform movements.
bio_id: Karamokho Alfa (c. 1698-1762 M) adalah ulama Fula dan Imam pertama Futa Jallon (Guinea modern), di mana ia mendirikan salah satu negara reformasi Islam pertama di Afrika Barat. Ia memimpin jihad Fulani 1725-1727 yang mengubah Futa Jallon menjadi negara Islam yang diatur oleh fikih Maliki.

bio_short_en: Leading contemporary Deobandi jurist; authority on Islamic finance and a widely respected Hanafi scholar of fiqh and hadith.
bio_en: Muhammad Taqi Usmani (born 1943 CE / 1362H) is a Pakistani Deobandi scholar widely considered the foremost living authority on Islamic commercial law and finance. He served as a Sharia board member for major Islamic financial institutions globally and as a judge on Pakistan's Federal Sharia Court. His Takmilah Fath al-Mulhim is a major completion of the commentary on Sahih Muslim. He has written extensively on Islamic finance, Hanafi fiqh, hadith, and tafsir. His book Islam and Modernism addresses contemporary challenges from a traditional scholarly perspective. He was educated at Darul Uloom Karachi and studied under his father Mufti Muhammad Shafi'.
bio_id: Muhammad Taqi Usmani (lahir 1943 M) adalah ulama Pakistan yang dianggap otoritas terkemuka dalam hukum komersial dan keuangan Islam. Ia menjabat di dewan syariah lembaga keuangan Islam global dan sebagai hakim Pengadilan Syariah Federal Pakistan. Takmilah Fath al-Mulhim-nya adalah penyelesaian penting dari komentar Sahih Muslim.

bio_short_en: 17th-century Moroccan scholar and advisor to Sultan Moulay Ismail; known for his Qanun letter rebuking royal oppression.
bio_en: Muhammad al-Hasan ibn Masud al-Yusi (1040-1102H / 1631-1691 CE) was one of the most prominent Moroccan scholars of the 17th century, serving as professor at the Qarawiyyin and as a court scholar under multiple rulers. He is most famous for his bold letter (Qanun) to Sultan Moulay Ismail, rebuking him for oppression and reminding him of his obligations to justice — an act of scholarly courage that became legendary. He also wrote encyclopedic works on rhetoric, logic, Maliki fiqh, and natural sciences. His Muhadarat fi al-Adab wa al-Lughah is an encyclopedic collection on Arabic adab.
bio_id: Al-Hasan al-Yusi (1040-1102H) adalah ulama Maroko terkemuka abad ke-17, pengajar di Qarawiyyin dan penasihat istana. Ia terkenal karena surat berani (Qanun) kepada Sultan Moulay Ismail yang menegur kezalimannya dan mengingatkan kewajibannya terhadap keadilan.

bio_short_en: Prolific Hanafi scholar from Herat who settled in Makkah; known for commentary on Mishkat al-Masabih and works on aqeedah.
bio_en: Nur al-Din Abu al-Hasan Ali ibn Sultan Muhammad al-Qari al-Harawi al-Makki (930-1014H / 1524-1606 CE), known as Mulla Ali al-Qari, was a prolific Hanafi scholar originally from Herat who settled in Makkah and died there. He wrote over 100 works across Quranic recitation, hadith, fiqh, aqeedah, and language. His most important work is Mirqat al-Mafatih, an eight-volume commentary on Mishkat al-Masabih. He also wrote Sharh al-Fiqh al-Akbar (commentary on Abu Hanifah's theological tract), al-Asrar al-Marfu'ah (on fabricated hadiths), and Jam' al-Wasa'il. He was a calligrapher who funded his studies by writing Qurans.
bio_id: Mulla Ali al-Qari (930-1014H) adalah ulama Hanafi produktif dari Herat yang menetap di Makkah. Ia menulis lebih dari 100 karya di berbagai bidang. Karyanya yang terpenting adalah Mirqat al-Mafatih, komentar delapan jilid atas Mishkat al-Masabih.

bio_short_en: 19th-century Indian Hanafi scholar and hadith expert; author of al-Raf' wa al-Takmil, a major work on hadith criticism methodology.
bio_en: Abu al-Hasanat Muhammad Abd al-Hayy al-Lacknawi al-Hindi (1264-1304H / 1848-1886 CE) was a prolific Indian Hanafi scholar of Lucknow who combined mastery of Hanafi jurisprudence with deep hadith scholarship. His al-Raf' wa al-Takmil fi al-Jarh wa al-Ta'dil is a major work on the methodology of hadith narrator criticism (rijal), explaining the technical terminology and reconciling apparent contradictions in classical judgments. He also wrote al-Fawaid al-Bahiyyah on Hanafi jurists, al-Ajwibah al-Fadilah on hadith science, and Zafar al-Amani on hadith terminology.
bio_id: Abd al-Hayy al-Lacknawi (1264-1304H) adalah ulama Hanafi produktif dari Lucknow yang menggabungkan penguasaan fikih Hanafi dengan keilmuan hadis yang mendalam. Karyanya al-Raf' wa al-Takmil adalah karya penting tentang metodologi kritik perawi hadis.

bio_short_en: Companion entrusted by the Prophet with knowledge of hypocrites and signs of the Last Hour; known as 'Keeper of the Secret'.
bio_en: Hudhayfa ibn al-Yaman al-Absi al-Ansari (d. 36H) was one of the most trusted companions of the Prophet Muhammad, peace be upon him. The Prophet entrusted him with the names of the hypocrites (munafiqun) of Madinah — a secret he never revealed in full. He is also extensively cited in hadith about the trials (fitan) and signs of the Last Hour. He served as governor of al-Mada'in (Ctesiphon) under Umar ibn al-Khattab. He is reported to have asked the Prophet: 'We were in ignorance and evil, then Allah brought us this goodness. Will there be evil after this good?' — leading to an extended exchange about future tribulations.
bio_id: Hudhayfa ibn al-Yaman (w. 36H) adalah salah satu sahabat yang paling dipercaya Nabi, dikenal sebagai 'Penjaga Rahasia'. Nabi mempercayakan kepadanya nama-nama orang munafik di Madinah. Ia banyak dikutip dalam hadis tentang fitnah dan tanda-tanda Hari Kiamat.

bio_short_en: Early martyr and husband of Sumayyah; father of Ammar ibn Yasir; tortured to death for refusing to leave Islam.
bio_en: Yasir ibn Amir al-Ansi al-Madhhiji (d. c. 615 CE) was an early companion of the Prophet Muhammad from Yemen who had come to Makkah searching for a lost brother. He embraced Islam early and married Sumayyah bint Khayyat. Together they and their son Ammar faced severe torture at the hands of the Makkans, particularly Abu Jahl. Yasir died from the torture before the early Muslims emigrated to Abyssinia or Madinah. He is honored as one of the earliest male martyrs of Islam and is remembered together with his wife Sumayyah as the 'family of Yasir' (Al Yasir), whose patience was praised by the Prophet.
bio_id: Yasir ibn Amir (w. c. 615 M) adalah sahabat awal Nabi dari Yaman yang memeluk Islam pada masa awal. Bersama istrinya Sumayyah dan putra mereka Ammar, mereka menghadapi penyiksaan berat dari orang-orang Makkah. Yasir meninggal akibat penyiksaan dan dihormati sebagai salah satu martir pria paling awal dalam Islam.

bio_short_en: Wife of the Prophet; one of the most learned of his wives; her patience in widowhood before marrying the Prophet is legendary.
bio_en: Umm Salama Hind bint Abi Umayyah al-Makhzumiyyah (d. 62H) was one of the most learned and wisest of the Mothers of the Believers. She and her first husband Abu Salama were among the earliest Muslims and emigrated to Abyssinia then Madinah. After Abu Salama's death from wounds at Uhud, she was proposed to by Abu Bakr, then Umar, but declined, until the Prophet himself proposed and she accepted. She was known for her wisdom and often consulted on matters of significance — it was she who, after the Treaty of Hudaybiyyah, advised the Prophet on how to handle the companions' reluctance to shave. She transmitted nearly 400 hadiths.
bio_id: Umm Salama (w. 62H) adalah salah satu Ummul Mu'minin yang paling berilmu dan bijaksana. Ia dan suami pertamanya Abu Salama termasuk Muslim paling awal yang berhijrah ke Habasyah kemudian Madinah. Setelah suaminya wafat, ia dinikahi Nabi. Ia terkenal karena kebijaksanaannya dan meriwayatkan hampir 400 hadis.

bio_short_en: Wife of the Prophet; her marriage to the Prophet led to the freeing of 100 Muslim households of the Banu Mustaliq.
bio_en: Juwayriyyah bint al-Harith ibn Abi Dirar al-Khuza'iyyah (d. 56H) was the daughter of the chief of the Banu Mustaliq tribe, taken captive after the Battle of al-Muraisi (5H). She came to the Prophet seeking help to ransom herself, and the Prophet himself paid her ransom and married her. Aisha narrated: 'I know of no woman who was a greater blessing to her people than Juwayriyyah. When the companions heard the Prophet had married her, they released all the captives of Banu Mustaliq, saying: They are now relatives of the Messenger of Allah — thus 100 households were freed.' She transmitted several hadiths and lived until approximately 56H.
bio_id: Juwayriyyah bint al-Harith (w. 56H) adalah putri kepala suku Banu Mustaliq yang ditawan setelah Pertempuran al-Muraisi. Ketika Nabi menikahinya, para sahabat membebaskan semua tawanan Banu Mustaliq karena mereka kini menjadi kerabat Rasulullah — sehingga 100 rumah tangga dibebaskan.

bio_short_en: The first major Muslim philosopher and 'Philosopher of the Arabs'; pioneer in logic, mathematics, music theory, and medicine.
bio_en: Abu Yusuf Ya'qub ibn Ishaq al-Kindi (c. 801-873 CE / 185-260H) was the first major Muslim philosopher and is known as 'The Philosopher of the Arabs' (Faylasuf al-Arab). He served at the Abbasid court in Baghdad and was prolific across philosophy, mathematics, astronomy, medicine, music theory, and cryptography. He was among the first to attempt a synthesis of Greek philosophy with Islamic thought, but took a more theologically conservative approach than later philosophers. He wrote over 260 works, most of which are lost; surviving works include On First Philosophy and treatises on music theory where he extended Aristotelian physics. He was a devout Muslim who believed philosophy and theology were compatible.
bio_id: Al-Kindi (c. 801-873 M) adalah filsuf Muslim besar pertama, dikenal sebagai 'Filsuf Arab'. Ia bertugas di istana Abbasiyah dan produktif di berbagai bidang: filsafat, matematika, astronomi, kedokteran, teori musik, dan kriptografi. Ia menulis lebih dari 260 karya.

bio_short_en: The 'Second Teacher' (after Aristotle); systematized Aristotelian logic for Arabic-speaking world and integrated it with Islamic political thought.
bio_en: Abu Nasr Muhammad ibn Muhammad al-Farabi al-Turki (c. 872-950 CE / 257-339H) was a Central Asian philosopher known as 'the Second Teacher' (al-Mu'allim al-Thani), after Aristotle. He was the first to comprehensively systematize Aristotelian logic and philosophy for Arabic readers and integrated it with Neoplatonic thought. His Mabadi Ara' Ahl al-Madinah al-Fadilah (Opinions of the Inhabitants of the Virtuous City) developed a theory of the ideal Islamic polity. He also wrote extensively on music (Kitab al-Musiqa al-Kabir). Islam.wiki note: some of his philosophical positions diverge from mainstream Islamic theology; he is significant historically as a major transmitter of Greek thought to the Islamic world.
bio_id: Al-Farabi (c. 872-950 M) adalah filsuf dari Asia Tengah yang dikenal sebagai 'Guru Kedua' setelah Aristoteles. Ia adalah orang pertama yang secara komprehensif mensistematisasi logika dan filsafat Aristotelian untuk pembaca Arab. Catatan: beberapa posisi filosofisnya menyimpang dari teologi Islam arus utama.

bio_short_en: The greatest physician of the medieval world; his al-Hawi (Continens) was a medical encyclopedia used in European universities until the 17th century.
bio_en: Abu Bakr Muhammad ibn Zakariyya al-Razi (c. 854-925 CE / 240-313H), known in Europe as Rhazes, was the greatest physician of the medieval world. Born in Ray (near modern Tehran), he headed hospitals in Ray and Baghdad. His encyclopedic al-Hawi (Continens) — 23 volumes summarizing Greek, Syriac, and his own medical knowledge — was used as a textbook in European universities until the 17th century. He made the first clear clinical description distinguishing smallpox from measles. He was also a pioneer of experimental medicine, testing medications on animals before humans. Note: his philosophical writings (particularly on religion and prophecy) are considered heretical by Islamic scholars and are not endorsed here.
bio_id: Al-Razi al-Thabib (c. 854-925 M), dikenal di Eropa sebagai Rhazes, adalah dokter terbesar abad pertengahan. Ensiklopedi medisnya al-Hawi (23 jilid) digunakan sebagai buku teks di universitas-universitas Eropa hingga abad ke-17. Ia membuat deskripsi klinis pertama yang membedakan cacar dari campak. Catatan: tulisan-tulisan filosofisnya dianggap tidak ortodoks oleh para ulama Islam.

bio_short_en: Father of modern surgery; his al-Tasrif introduced surgical instruments that remained in use for 500 years.
bio_en: Abu al-Qasim Khalaf ibn Abbas al-Zahrawi al-Ansari (c. 936-1013 CE / 324-404H), known in Europe as Abulcasis, was the greatest surgeon of the medieval world and is often called the Father of Modern Surgery. Born near Cordoba in al-Andalus, he spent his career there. His encyclopedic medical work al-Tasrif liman Ajiza an al-Ta'lif contains the first illustrated surgical manual in history, describing and illustrating over 200 surgical instruments — many of his own invention — and pioneering techniques in wound management, obstetrics, and dental surgery. European surgeons relied on al-Tasrif as their primary surgical reference until the 16th century.
bio_id: Al-Zahrawi (c. 936-1013 M), dikenal di Eropa sebagai Abulcasis, adalah ahli bedah terbesar abad pertengahan dan disebut Bapak Bedah Modern. Karyanya al-Tasrif memuat panduan bedah bergambar pertama dalam sejarah, mendeskripsikan lebih dari 200 instrumen bedah, banyak di antaranya penemuannya sendiri.

bio_short_en: The greatest prose stylist of classical Arabic; polymath who wrote on theology, zoology, rhetoric, and social history.
bio_en: Abu Uthman Amr ibn Bahr al-Kinani al-Basri, known as al-Jahiz (c. 776-869 CE / 163-255H), was the greatest prose writer of classical Arabic literature and a towering polymath of the Abbasid era. Born of African origin in Basra, he was a Mu'tazili theologian but his wide-ranging curiosity led him to write on zoology (Kitab al-Hayawan), rhetoric (al-Bayan wa al-Tabyin), misers (Kitab al-Bukhala'), and dozens of other subjects with wit, observation, and extraordinary prose style. He reportedly died at nearly 100, crushed by his books. Islam.wiki note: his Mu'tazili theology is not endorsed; his literary and cultural works are historically significant.
bio_id: Al-Jahiz (c. 776-869 M) adalah penulis prosa Arab klasik terbesar dan polimat era Abbasiyah. Berasal dari Afrika, ia menulis tentang zoologi (Kitab al-Hayawan), retorika, dan puluhan subjek lainnya dengan kecerdasan dan gaya prosa yang luar biasa. Catatan: teologi Mu'tazili-nya tidak disetujui; karya-karya sastra dan budayanya signifikan secara historis.

bio_short_en: The greatest poet in the Arabic language; his panegyric and philosophical verse represents the pinnacle of Arabic classical poetry.
bio_en: Abu al-Tayyib Ahmad ibn al-Husayn al-Ju'fi al-Kindi, known as al-Mutanabbi (303-354H / 915-965 CE), is widely considered the greatest poet in the Arabic language. Born in Kufa, he served as court poet for the Hamdanid ruler Sayf al-Dawla of Aleppo for nine years — producing some of the most celebrated Arabic verse. His poetry covers panegyric, self-praise, wisdom, and lament, and is notable for its philosophical depth and memorable formulations. Countless Arabic expressions, proverbs, and idioms derive from his lines. His name al-Mutanabbi ('the one who claimed prophethood') refers to a youthful claim he reportedly made and later recanted.
bio_id: Al-Mutanabbi (303-354H) secara luas dianggap sebagai penyair terbesar dalam bahasa Arab. Ia bertugas sebagai penyair istana Sayf al-Dawla dari Aleppo. Puisinya mencakup pujian, kebijaksanaan, dan ratapan, dengan kedalaman filosofis yang khas. Tak terhitung ungkapan Arab, peribahasa, dan idiom berasal dari bait-baitnya.

bio_short_en: Major Abbasid poet and compiler of al-Hamasa, an important anthology of classical Arabic poetry.
bio_en: Abu Tammam Habib ibn Aws al-Ta'i (c. 188-231H / 804-845 CE) was one of the leading poets of the Abbasid era and a court poet for al-Mu'tasim. He is best remembered as the compiler of al-Hamasa (the anthology of valor poetry), one of the most important collections of early Arabic verse. As a poet himself, he was noted for elaborate metaphors (badi' style) and philosophical themes. He served as postmaster (sahib al-barid) in Mosul. The hadith about reciting poetry in the mosque — with his conversation with the caliph — is often cited to show that poetry was part of Islamic civilization.
bio_id: Abu Tammam (c. 188-231H) adalah salah satu penyair terkemuka era Abbasiyah dan penyair istana al-Mu'tasim. Ia paling dikenang sebagai penyusun al-Hamasa, salah satu koleksi syair Arab klasik terpenting.

bio_short_en: Ottoman polymath and bibliographer; his Kashf al-Zunun is the most comprehensive Islamic bibliographic encyclopedia.
bio_en: Mustafa ibn Abdullah, known as Katip Celebi (1017-1067H / 1609-1657 CE) or Haji Khalifa, was one of the most remarkable Ottoman scholars. A professional accountant (katip) who educated himself through voracious self-study, he wrote Kashf al-Zunun 'an Asami al-Kutub wa al-Funun, the most comprehensive Islamic bibliographic encyclopedia, listing some 15,000 book titles across all Islamic sciences with brief descriptions — an incomparable reference tool. He also wrote Jihannuma (on geography and cartography, incorporating European maps), Fezleke (Ottoman history), and other works. He advocated engagement with European sciences and is considered a precursor of Ottoman reform.
bio_id: Katip Celebi (1017-1067H) adalah polimat dan bibliografer Ottoman. Karyanya Kashf al-Zunun adalah ensiklopedia bibliografi Islam paling komprehensif, mendaftarkan sekitar 15.000 judul buku di semua ilmu Islam. Ia juga menulis Jihannuma tentang geografi dan kartografi.

bio_short_en: Ottoman admiral and cartographer; his 1513 world map includes what may be the earliest known depiction of the Americas.
bio_en: Ahmad Muhiddin Piri ibn Haji Mehmed, known as Piri Reis (c. 1465-1554 CE / 876-961H), was an Ottoman admiral and cartographer. He is best known for his Bahriye (Book of Navigation), a comprehensive guide to the Mediterranean with detailed coastal charts, and for his 1513 world map (now in the Topkapi Museum), which is notable for appearing to show South America and possibly Antarctica with unusual accuracy for its era. He compiled his maps from over 20 Arabic, Spanish, and Portuguese sources, including charts reportedly from Christopher Columbus's expedition. He served as Admiral of the Indian Ocean Fleet but was executed for abandoning the siege of Hormuz.
bio_id: Piri Reis (c. 1465-1554 M) adalah laksamana dan kartografer Ottoman. Ia terkenal dengan Bahriye (Buku Navigasi) yang komprehensif dan peta dunia tahun 1513 yang tampaknya menggambarkan Amerika Selatan dan mungkin Antartika dengan akurasi yang luar biasa untuk zamannya.

bio_short_en: Ottoman astronomer and mathematician; director of Samarkand observatory under Ulugh Beg, later chief teacher at Istanbul.
bio_en: Ala al-Din Ali ibn Muhammad al-Qushji (805-879H / 1403-1474 CE) was an Ottoman astronomer, mathematician, and theologian who began his career at the Samarkand observatory of Ulugh Beg. After Ulugh Beg's assassination, he traveled to Rome and then settled in Istanbul under Sultan Mehmed II, who appointed him head of the Sahn-i Thaman (the eight medrese complex). He is significant for separating astronomy from natural philosophy in Islamic intellectual tradition — arguing that astronomy can reach its conclusions without metaphysical assumptions. He also wrote important works on theology and mathematics.
bio_id: Ali Qushji (805-879H) adalah astronom, matematikawan, dan teolog Ottoman. Ia memulai karirnya di observatorium Samarkand milik Ulugh Beg, kemudian menetap di Istanbul sebagai kepala kompleks madrasah Sahn-i Thaman. Ia penting karena memisahkan astronomi dari filsafat alam.

bio_short_en: Timurid ruler of Samarkand and leading astronomer; his star catalog remained the most accurate until Tycho Brahe.
bio_en: Muhammad Taraghay ibn Shahrukh, known as Ulugh Beg (1394-1449 CE / 796-853H), was the grandson of Tamerlane and ruler of Samarkand. More significant for science than for politics, he built the famous Samarkand Observatory (1428-1429) — the largest and most advanced of its era — where he and his team of astronomers compiled a star catalog (Zij-i Sultani) measuring the positions of over 1,000 stars with unprecedented precision. His catalog remained the most accurate available until Tycho Brahe's work in the 16th century. He also taught mathematics and astronomy at the Ulugh Beg Madrasa he built. He was assassinated in 1449.
bio_id: Ulugh Beg (1394-1449 M) adalah cucu Tamerlane dan penguasa Samarkand. Lebih penting dalam sains daripada politik, ia membangun Observatorium Samarkand (1428-1429) yang menghasilkan katalog bintang (Zij-i Sultani) yang tetap menjadi yang paling akurat hingga Tycho Brahe di abad ke-16.

bio_short_en: Hadith scholar and compiler of the Sunan al-Darimi (Musnad al-Darimi); one of the important early hadith collections.
bio_en: Abu Muhammad Abdallah ibn Abd al-Rahman al-Darimi al-Samarqandi (181-255H) was a major hadith scholar of Khorasan. He compiled the Sunan al-Darimi (also called Musnad al-Darimi), which is sometimes counted as one of the seven major canonical collections. He was a teacher of Imam al-Bukhari and Muslim and was known for extreme piety — declining positions of power he was offered. He traveled extensively for hadith, studying under al-Shafi'i, Ahmad ibn Hanbal, and other leading scholars of his era. His Sunan is notable for beginning with a chapter on the importance of knowledge.
bio_id: Al-Darimi (181-255H) adalah ahli hadis terkemuka dari Khorasan. Ia menyusun Sunan al-Darimi, yang kadang dihitung sebagai salah satu dari tujuh koleksi kanonik utama. Ia adalah guru al-Bukhari dan Muslim, dan dikenal karena kezuhudan ekstrem.

bio_short_en: The greatest Maliki scholar of North Africa. He compiled al-Mudawwanah al-Kubra, the most authoritative Maliki legal reference, from the teachings of Ibn al-Qasim.
bio_en: Sahnun ibn Sa'id al-Tanukhi al-Qayrawani was the foremost Maliki scholar of the Maghreb and the greatest compiler of Maliki fiqh. He traveled to Madinah and Egypt to study, receiving from Ibn al-Qasim the teachings of Imam Malik. He compiled the al-Mudawwanah al-Kubra, a comprehensive record of Maliki legal opinions that became the primary reference of the Maliki school. He served as the chief judge (qadi) of Kairouan (Qayrawan) in Tunisia. He died in 240 AH. The Maliki school's dominance in North Africa is largely due to his efforts.
bio_id: Sahnun ibn Sa'id al-Tanukhi al-Qayrawani adalah ahli fiqh Maliki terkemuka di Maghreb. Beliau menyusun al-Mudawwanah al-Kubra, catatan komprehensif pendapat hukum Maliki yang menjadi referensi utama mazhab Maliki.

bio_short_en: A major Hanafi hadith scholar from Zayla (Somalia). Author of Nasb al-Rayah, the most comprehensive collection of hadith evidence for Hanafi positions.
bio_en: Jamal al-Din Abu Muhammad Abd Allah ibn Yusuf al-Zayla'i al-Hanafi was born in Zayla (modern Somalia/Ethiopia). He was one of the greatest Hanafi hadith scholars. His most famous work, Nasb al-Rayah li-Ahadith al-Hidayah, is a comprehensive collection of the hadith evidences cited in the Hanafi legal manual al-Hidayah, with critical analysis of each narration's authenticity. He also wrote a major commentary on the Kanz al-Daqa'iq. He died in Cairo in 762 AH and is buried there.
bio_id: Jamal al-Din Abu Muhammad Abd Allah ibn Yusuf al-Zayla'i al-Hanafi lahir di Zayla (Somalia/Ethiopia modern). Nasb al-Rayah-nya adalah koleksi komprehensif bukti hadis untuk posisi hukum Hanafi dalam al-Hidayah.

bio_short_en: A major Hanafi jurist of Central Asia, teacher of al-Kasani. Author of Tuhfat al-Fuqaha', a foundational Hanafi legal text.
bio_en: 'Ala al-Din Muhammad ibn Ahmad al-Samarqandi al-Hanafi was one of the leading Hanafi jurists of Central Asia. He was the teacher of al-Kasani, who later married his daughter. His major work Tuhfat al-Fuqaha' is an important foundational text in Hanafi jurisprudence. He also wrote on usul al-fiqh and aqeedah. He died in Samarqand around 540 AH.
bio_id: 'Ala al-Din Muhammad ibn Ahmad al-Samarqandi al-Hanafi adalah salah satu ahli fiqh Hanafi terkemuka di Asia Tengah. Beliau adalah guru al-Kasani. Tuhfat al-Fuqaha'-nya adalah teks dasar penting dalam yurisprudensi Hanafi.

bio_short_en: The greatest Islamic scholar of the Indian subcontinent. Author of Hujjat Allah al-Balighah, a landmark work reconciling the four madhabs through hadith.
bio_en: Qutb al-Din Ahmad ibn Abd al-Rahim al-Faruqi al-Dihlawi, known as Shah Wali Allah, was the foremost Islamic scholar of the Indian subcontinent. He studied in Madinah under the hadith master Abu Tahir al-Kurdi. He wrote Hujjat Allah al-Balighah (The Conclusive Argument from God), a landmark work explaining the rational and spiritual wisdom behind Islamic rulings and seeking to reconcile the four madhabs through hadith methodology. He also translated the Quran into Persian (Fath al-Rahman) and Urdu (Fathu al-Aziz). He revived Islamic scholarship in India and his school influenced all later Indian Islamic movements. He died in Delhi in 1176 AH.
bio_id: Qutb al-Din Ahmad ibn Abd al-Rahim al-Faruqi al-Dihlawi, dikenal sebagai Shah Wali Allah, adalah ulama Islam terkemuka di anak benua India. Hujjat Allah al-Balighah-nya adalah karya monumental yang menjelaskan hikmah di balik hukum Islam.

bio_short_en: The greatest Islamic geographer. His Mu'jam al-Buldan (Dictionary of Countries) is the most comprehensive geographical encyclopedia of the medieval Islamic world.
bio_en: Shihab al-Din Abu Abd Allah Yaqut ibn Abd Allah al-Hamawi al-Rumi was born in Anatolia (hence 'al-Rumi') and was captured as a child, becoming a slave in Baghdad. He gained freedom through his learning and traveled extensively throughout the Islamic world, visiting Khorasan, Transoxiana, and the Hejaz. His Mu'jam al-Buldan (Dictionary of Countries) is a 5-volume geographical encyclopedia covering thousands of places in the Islamic world with historical, etymological, and cultural information. He also wrote Mu'jam al-Udaba' (a biographical dictionary of scholars). He died in Aleppo in 626 AH.
bio_id: Shihab al-Din Abu Abd Allah Yaqut ibn Abd Allah al-Hamawi al-Rumi lahir di Anatolia. Mu'jam al-Buldan-nya (Kamus Negara-negara) adalah ensiklopedi geografis 5 jilid yang mencakup ribuan tempat di dunia Islam.

bio_short_en: The great Andalusian traveler and poet. His Rihlah (travel account) is one of the most vivid descriptions of the Islamic world in the 12th century CE.
bio_en: Abu al-Husayn Muhammad ibn Ahmad ibn Jubayr al-Kinani al-Andalusi was a scholar, poet, and traveler from Valencia, Al-Andalus. His famous Rihlah (travel account) documents his journey from Andalusia to Mecca for Hajj and back (1183-1185 CE), passing through Sicily, Egypt, the Hejaz, Iraq, and Syria. It is one of the most detailed and vivid accounts of the medieval Islamic world, describing mosques, markets, rulers, and daily life. It influenced later travelers including Ibn Battuta. He died in Alexandria in 614 AH.
bio_id: Abu al-Husayn Muhammad ibn Ahmad ibn Jubayr al-Kinani al-Andalusi adalah ulama, penyair, dan pelancong dari Valencia, Al-Andalus. Rihlah-nya mendokumentasikan perjalanannya dari Andalusia ke Mekah untuk haji dan kembali.

bio_short_en: A daughter of 'Ali ibn Abi Talib from a secondary wife. A transmitter of hadith from her father and Imam al-Husayn.
bio_en: Fatimah bint 'Ali ibn Abi Talib was a daughter of the Companion 'Ali ibn Abi Talib. She was a hadith transmitter from her father and brother Imam al-Husayn. She is mentioned in biographical dictionaries of hadith transmitters (rijal) as a reliable narrator. She represents the many female scholars of the Tabi'in generation who transmitted knowledge from the Companions.
bio_id: Fatimah bint 'Ali ibn Abi Thalib adalah putri sahabat 'Ali ibn Abi Thalib. Beliau adalah perawi hadis dari ayah dan saudaranya Imam al-Husain.

bio_short_en: Known as 'the Pride of Women' and 'the Female Bukhari of her time.' A celebrated female hadith scholar of Baghdad who taught in the Abbasid capital.
bio_en: Fatimah bint Ahmad ibn Yahya al-Dinawari al-Baghdadiyyah, known as al-Shuhda al-Katibah (the Calligraphist), was one of the most celebrated female hadith scholars of the medieval period. She was called 'Fakhr al-Nisa'' (the Pride of Women) and 'Bukhari al-Asr' (the Bukhari of her era) for her mastery of hadith. She held public hadith sessions in Baghdad with thousands of students attending, including major scholars. She was given an ijazah by her father and transmitted hadith chains that many scholars sought for their high isnad. She died in Baghdad in 574 AH.
bio_id: Fatimah bint Ahmad ibn Yahya al-Dinawari al-Baghdadiyyah, dikenal sebagai al-Syuhda al-Katibah, adalah salah satu ulama hadis wanita paling terkenal pada periode abad pertengahan. Beliau disebut 'Fakhr al-Nisa'' dan 'Bukhari al-Asr' atas penguasaan hadisnya.

bio_short_en: The great Islamic reformer of West Africa. He founded the Sokoto Caliphate in northern Nigeria, one of the largest states in African history.
bio_en: Uthman ibn Fudi (dan Fodio in Hausa) was a Fulani Islamic scholar, reformer, and founder of the Sokoto Caliphate in what is now northern Nigeria. He conducted a successful jihad against the corrupt Hausa states, establishing a caliphate based on Maliki law. He was a prolific writer on Islamic law, theology, and governance, and championed women's education and rights. The Sokoto Caliphate he founded became one of the largest states in African history. His scholarship influenced Islamic movements across West Africa to this day. He died in 1232 AH.
bio_id: Uthman ibn Fudi (dan Fodio dalam bahasa Hausa) adalah ulama, reformis, dan pendiri Kesultanan Sokoto di Nigeria utara. Beliau melancarkan jihad yang berhasil terhadap kerajaan Hausa yang korup, mendirikan kekhalifahan berdasarkan hukum Maliki.

bio_short_en: The leading Hanafi jurist of the Ottoman Empire under Sultan Mehmed II. His Durar al-Hukkam and Mir'at al-Usul are standard Ottoman legal texts.
bio_en: Muhammad ibn Faramurz ibn Ali al-Hanafi, known as Mulla Khusraw, was the Shaykh al-Islam (chief religious authority) of the Ottoman Empire under Sultan Mehmed II (the Conqueror). He was the leading Hanafi jurist of his era and wrote Durar al-Hukkam fi Sharh Ghurar al-Ahkam (a major Hanafi legal text) and Mir'at al-Usul (on usul al-fiqh). These became standard texts in Ottoman madrasas. He died in Istanbul in 885 AH.
bio_id: Muhammad ibn Faramurz ibn Ali al-Hanafi, dikenal sebagai Mulla Khusraw, adalah Syaikh al-Islam (otoritas agama tertinggi) Kekaisaran Utsmani di bawah Sultan Mehmed II. Durar al-Hukkam-nya menjadi teks hukum standar di madrasah Utsmani.

bio_short_en: An Ottoman jurist and theologian, author of Nazm al-Faraid (a summary of Hanafi theology) and a commentary on al-Manar in usul al-fiqh.
bio_en: Muhammad ibn Muslihuddeen Mustafa al-Qojavee al-Hanafi, known as Shaykhzadah, was an Ottoman scholar who wrote important works in Hanafi jurisprudence and theology. His commentary Majma al-Anhur fi Sharh Multaqa al-Abhur became one of the most used references in Ottoman legal education. He died in 951 AH.
bio_id: Muhammad ibn Muslihuddeen Mustafa al-Qojavee al-Hanafi, dikenal sebagai Shaykhzadah, adalah ulama Utsmani yang menulis karya-karya penting dalam yurisprudensi dan teologi Hanafi.
works: Majma al-Anhur fi Sharh Multaqa al-Abhur

bio_short_en: Founder of the Ash'ari school of theology, the most widespread Sunni theological school. He was initially a student of the Mu'tazilite al-Jubbai before returning to the way of the Salaf.
bio_en: Abu al-Hasan Ali ibn Isma'il al-Ash'ari al-Basri al-Baghdadi was the founder of the Ash'ari school of Islamic theology (kalam). He studied under the Mu'tazilite theologian al-Jubbai for 40 years before famously debating and refuting him and publicly declaring his return to the way of Imam Ahmad ibn Hanbal and the Salaf. He then formulated theological responses to the Mu'tazilites and other deviant groups using the tools of kalam while affirming the beliefs of Ahl us-Sunnah. His works include al-Ibanah 'an Usul al-Diyanah, al-Luma', and Maqalat al-Islamiyyin. Note: Athari scholars hold that his final and correct position was expressed in al-Ibanah, which aligns with the way of the Salaf. He died in Baghdad around 324 AH.
bio_id: Abu al-Hasan Ali ibn Ismail al-Asy'ari al-Basri al-Baghdadi adalah pendiri mazhab teologi Asy'ariyah. Beliau awalnya murid Mu'tazilah al-Jubba'i selama 40 tahun sebelum kembali ke jalan Imam Ahmad ibn Hanbal dan Salaf.

bio_short_en: One of the earliest and most important Islamic historians. His Kitab al-Maghazi is the earliest extant comprehensive account of the Prophet's military expeditions.
bio_en: Muhammad ibn Umar al-Waqidi al-Aslami was one of the earliest Islamic historians. Born in Madinah, he settled in Baghdad under Abbasid patronage. His most important work, Kitab al-Maghazi (Book of Military Expeditions), is the earliest surviving comprehensive account of the Prophet's ﷺ military campaigns. Although hadith scholars were critical of his chain of narration methodology (some considering him weak in hadith), his historical works are invaluable to Islamic historiography. He also wrote Futuh al-Sham and other historical works. He died in Baghdad in 207 AH.
bio_id: Muhammad ibn Umar al-Waqidi al-Aslami adalah salah satu sejarawan Islam tertua dan terpenting. Kitab al-Maghazi-nya (Buku Ekspedisi Militer) adalah akun komprehensif pertama yang bertahan dari kampanye militer Nabi ﷺ.

bio_short_en: Author of al-Tabaqat al-Kubra, the most comprehensive biographical dictionary of the Prophet's Companions and early Muslim generations.
bio_en: Muhammad ibn Sa'd ibn Mani' al-Zuhri al-Basri al-Baghdadi was the secretary (katib) of al-Waqidi. His masterwork al-Tabaqat al-Kubra (The Great Classes) is the most comprehensive biographical dictionary of the Prophet ﷺ, his Companions, the Tabi'in, and subsequent generations. It runs to 8 volumes (in modern editions, much more in manuscript) and is an essential source for Islamic biographical literature (tarjama). He died in Baghdad in 230 AH.
bio_id: Muhammad ibn Sa'd ibn Mani' al-Zuhri al-Basri al-Baghdadi adalah sekretaris (katib) al-Waqidi. Al-Tabaqat al-Kubra-nya (Kelas-kelas Besar) adalah kamus biografi paling komprehensif dari Nabi ﷺ, para sahabatnya, Tabi'in, dan generasi berikutnya.

bio_short_en: The greatest authority on Quranic recitation (qira'at) in the last 700 years. His al-Nashr fi al-Qira'at al-'Ashr and al-Tayyibah are the definitive references for the ten canonical recitations.
bio_en: Shams al-Din Abu al-Khayr Muhammad ibn Muhammad ibn al-Jazari al-Dimashqi al-Misri was the foremost authority on Quranic recitation (qira'at) of the post-classical period. He received chains of transmission for all seven (and ten) canonical recitations. His works al-Nashr fi al-Qira'at al-'Ashr and its summary al-Tayyibah define the ten canonical recitations and their rules. He also wrote al-Durrah al-Mudiyyah (on the three additional recitations). He traveled extensively to teach — from Damascus to Egypt to Anatolia to Persia and back. He died in Shiraz in 833 AH. Every present-day Quran teacher traces their chain of recitation through him.
bio_id: Shams al-Din Abu al-Khayr Muhammad ibn Muhammad ibn al-Jazari al-Dimasyqi adalah otoritas terkemuka dalam bacaan Al-Quran (qira'at) pada periode pasca-klasik. Al-Nashr fi al-Qira'at al-'Asyr-nya adalah referensi definitif untuk sepuluh bacaan kanonik.

bio_short_en: One of the four pillars of hadith criticism alongside Yahya ibn Ma'in, Yahya al-Qattan, and Ahmad ibn Hanbal. Imam al-Shafi'i said: 'No one knows hadith like Ibn Mahdi.'
bio_en: Abu Sa'id Abd al-Rahman ibn Mahdi al-'Anbari al-Basri was one of the greatest hadith scholars of his era. He studied under Sufyan al-Thawri, Sufyan ibn 'Uyaynah, and Malik ibn Anas. Imam al-Shafi'i wrote his famous Risalah (the first work on Islamic jurisprudential theory) as a response to a request by Ibn Mahdi. Ahmad ibn Hanbal ranked him alongside Yahya al-Qattan as the two greatest hadith critics of their time. He died in Basra in 198 AH.
bio_id: Abu Sa'id Abd al-Rahman ibn Mahdi al-'Anbari al-Basri adalah salah satu ulama hadis terbesar di eranya. Imam al-Syafi'i menulis Risalah-nya yang terkenal sebagai respons atas permintaan Ibn Mahdi.

bio_short_en: The greatest hadith scholar of Yemen. His al-Musannaf is one of the earliest preserved hadith collections and a major source for the Sunnan literature.
bio_en: Abu Bakr Abd al-Razzaq ibn Hammam al-Himyari al-San'ani al-Yamani was the foremost hadith scholar of Yemen. Scholars from across the Islamic world — including Imam Ahmad ibn Hanbal, Yahya ibn Ma'in, and Yahya al-Qattan — traveled to Sana'a to study under him. His al-Musannaf (11 volumes in modern editions) is one of the oldest preserved hadith collections, predating the six canonical collections. It is a primary source for Islamic jurisprudence. He also wrote Tafsir Abd al-Razzaq. He died in Sana'a in 211 AH.
bio_id: Abu Bakr Abd al-Razzaq ibn Hammam al-Himyari al-Shan'ani al-Yamani adalah ulama hadis terkemuka Yaman. Al-Musannaf-nya (11 jilid) adalah salah satu koleksi hadis tertua yang masih tersimpan.

bio_short_en: The most distinguished student of Sufyan ibn 'Uyaynah in Mecca and one of the earliest teachers of Imam al-Bukhari. Author of al-Musnad al-Humaydi.
bio_en: Abu Bakr Abd Allah ibn al-Zubayr al-Qurashi al-Humaydi al-Makki was the leading student of Sufyan ibn 'Uyaynah and one of the earliest teachers of Imam al-Bukhari. He also studied under Imam al-Shafi'i. His al-Musnad al-Humaydi is an important early hadith collection organized by Companion. He was the first to bring Imam al-Shafi'i's works to Mecca. He died in Mecca in 219 AH. Imam al-Bukhari considered him his most important teacher in the Hejaz.
bio_id: Abu Bakr Abd Allah ibn al-Zubayr al-Qurashi al-Humaydi al-Makki adalah murid terkemuka Sufyan ibn 'Uyainah dan salah satu guru paling awal Imam al-Bukhari. Al-Musnad al-Humaydi-nya adalah koleksi hadis awal yang penting.

bio_short_en: Known as 'the little al-Shafi'i,' one of the two supreme Shafi'i authorities of the 16th century alongside Ibn Hajar al-Haytami. His Nihayat al-Muhtaj is a standard reference in Greater Syria and Egypt.
bio_en: Shams al-Din Muhammad ibn Ahmad ibn Hamzah al-Ramli al-Ansari al-Misri was known as 'the little al-Shafi'i' (al-Shafi'i al-Saghir) for his mastery of the Shafi'i school. He was the student of Zakariyya al-Ansari. His Nihayat al-Muhtaj ila Sharh al-Minhaj (a commentary on al-Nawawi's Minhaj) became a standard Shafi'i legal reference in Egypt and Greater Syria (though Ibn Hajar al-Haytami's Tuhfah is preferred in Southeast Asia and East Africa). He and Ibn Hajar al-Haytami were considered the two supreme authorities in the Shafi'i school of their era. He died in Cairo in 1004 AH.
bio_id: Shams al-Din Muhammad ibn Ahmad al-Ramli al-Ansari al-Misri dikenal sebagai 'al-Syafi'i Kecil' atas penguasaannya terhadap mazhab Syafi'i. Nihayat al-Muhtaj-nya menjadi referensi hukum Syafi'i standar di Mesir dan Greater Syria.

bio_short_en: One of the greatest Hanafi jurists of the 15th century. His Fath al-Qadir (commentary on al-Hidayah) is the most analytically rigorous Hanafi legal work.
bio_en: Kamal al-Din Muhammad ibn Abd al-Wahid al-Siwasi al-Iskandari, known as Ibn al-Humam, was the leading Hanafi jurist of his era. Born in Alexandria, he studied in Egypt and settled in Cairo. His Fath al-Qadir (commentary on al-Marghinani's al-Hidayah) is considered the most analytically rigorous and comprehensive commentary in Hanafi jurisprudence. He also wrote Tahrir al-Usul (on usul al-fiqh) and al-Musayarah (on Maturidi theology). He died in Cairo in 861 AH.
bio_id: Kamal al-Din Muhammad ibn Abd al-Wahid al-Siwasi al-Iskandari (Ibn al-Humam) adalah ahli fiqh Hanafi terkemuka di eranya. Fath al-Qadir-nya (komentar atas al-Hidayah al-Marghinani) dianggap komentar paling analitis dan komprehensif dalam yurisprudensi Hanafi.

bio_short_en: One of the greatest Islamic scholars of Aceh (Indonesia). His Sirat al-Mustaqim was the first comprehensive Islamic legal text written in Malay.
bio_en: Nuruddin Muhammad ibn Ali ibn Hasanji ibn Muhammad Hamid al-Raniri al-Qurashi was born in Ranir (Rander, Gujarat, India) and became the court scholar (shaykh al-islam) of the Acehnese Sultanate in Sumatra. His Sirat al-Mustaqim (The Straight Path) was the first comprehensive Islamic legal text written in Malay, making it foundational for Islamic jurisprudence in the Malay Archipelago. He also wrote Bustan al-Salatin (a major Malay historical and literary work) and Hujjat al-Siddiq li Daf' al-Zindiq (a refutation of pantheistic Sufism). He died in India in 1068 AH.
bio_id: Nuruddin Muhammad ibn Ali ibn Hasanji al-Raniri al-Qurashi lahir di Ranir (Rander, Gujarat, India) dan menjadi ulama istana Kesultanan Aceh di Sumatera. Sirat al-Mustaqim-nya adalah teks hukum Islam komprehensif pertama yang ditulis dalam bahasa Melayu.

bio_short_en: The greatest Quranic exegete of the 20th century. His Adwa' al-Bayan is the most thorough modern tafsir that explains the Quran solely by the Quran and Sunnah.
bio_en: Muhammad al-Amin ibn Muhammad al-Mukhtar al-Shinqiti was born in Mauritania (Shinqit, West Africa) and became one of the greatest Islamic scholars of the 20th century. He settled in Saudi Arabia and taught at the Islamic University of Madinah. His masterwork Adwa' al-Bayan fi Idah al-Quran bil-Quran (Lights of Explanation: Clarifying the Quran by the Quran) is a 10-volume tafsir that follows the methodology of the Salaf, interpreting the Quran primarily through the Quran itself and authentic Sunnah. He also wrote Manc' Jawaz al-Majaz (on figures of speech in the Quran). He died in Mecca in 1393 AH.
bio_id: Muhammad al-Amin ibn Muhammad al-Mukhtar al-Shinqiti lahir di Mauritania (Shinqit, Afrika Barat) dan menjadi salah satu ulama Islam terbesar abad ke-20. Adwa' al-Bayan-nya adalah tafsir modern paling menyeluruh yang menjelaskan Al-Quran semata-mata dengan Al-Quran dan Sunnah.
works: Adwa' al-Bayan fi Idah al-Quran bil-Quran

bio_short_en: One of the foremost Islamic scholars of the 20th century. Known for his accessible fatwas and his extraordinary ability to explain complex Islamic issues to ordinary Muslims.
bio_en: Muhammad ibn Salih ibn Muhammad al-'Uthaymin al-Wuhaybi al-Tamimi was one of the most beloved and prolific Islamic scholars of the late 20th century. He was a student of Abd al-Aziz ibn Baz and 'Abd al-Rahman al-Sa'di. He taught in 'Unayzah, Saudi Arabia and at the Grand Mosque in Mecca. He wrote over 50 books on aqeedah, fiqh, and Islamic education, and his recorded lessons run to tens of thousands of hours. He was known for his warmth, accessibility, and ability to provide clear guidance grounded in evidence. He died in 1421 AH.
bio_id: Muhammad ibn Salih ibn Muhammad al-'Utsaimin al-Wuhaybi al-Tamimi adalah salah satu ulama Islam paling dicintai dan produktif pada akhir abad ke-20. Beliau menulis lebih dari 50 buku tentang aqidah, fiqh, dan pendidikan Islam.

bio_short_en: A leading early scholar of Arabic linguistics, hadith, and Quranic studies. His Gharib al-Hadith and Fada'il al-Quran are among the most important early works in these fields.
bio_en: Abu 'Ubayd al-Qasim ibn Sallam al-Baghdadi al-Harawi was one of the leading early scholars combining expertise in Arabic linguistics, hadith, Quranic sciences, and jurisprudence. He studied under Imam al-Shafi'i, Sufyan ibn 'Uyaynah, and other great scholars. His Gharib al-Hadith (Rare Expressions in Hadith) explains difficult vocabulary in hadith, while his Fada'il al-Quran covers the merits and virtues of the Quran. He also wrote Kitab al-Amwal (on Islamic economics and finance), an early and influential work. He died in Mecca in 224 AH.
bio_id: Abu 'Ubayd al-Qasim ibn Sallam al-Baghdadi al-Harawi adalah salah satu ulama awal terkemuka yang menggabungkan keahlian dalam linguistik Arab, hadis, ilmu Al-Quran, dan yurisprudensi.

bio_short_en: Author of al-Sihah, the most widely used classical Arabic dictionary for 500 years. His lexicographic work defined the standard for Arabic language scholarship.
bio_en: Abu Nasr Isma'il ibn Hammad al-Jawhari al-Farrabi al-Naysaburi was a linguist and lexicographer from Farab (Central Asia, in modern Kazakhstan). His al-Sihah Taj al-Lugha wa-Sihah al-'Arabiyyah (The Correct: Crown of Language and Correct Arabic) was for centuries the most widely used Arabic dictionary. It is organized by root and covers approximately 40,000 words with examples from classical poetry and the Quran. He died around 400 AH in Naysabur. His dictionary served as the foundation for later Arabic lexicography including Ibn Manzur's Lisan al-Arab.
bio_id: Abu Nasr Isma'il ibn Hammad al-Jawhari al-Farrabi al-Naysaburi adalah ahli bahasa dan leksikografi dari Farab (Asia Tengah). Al-Sihah-nya selama berabad-abad merupakan kamus Arab yang paling banyak digunakan.

bio_short_en: A physically deformed Companion who died as a martyr. The Prophet ﷺ personally arranged his marriage and wept over his body, saying 'He is of me and I am of him.'
bio_en: Julaybib was a Companion of the Prophet ﷺ whose lineage was unknown and whose physical appearance was considered unappealing. The Prophet ﷺ personally arranged a marriage for him, insisting to a family who initially refused due to Julaybib's appearance. He participated in a military expedition and was found killed having slain seven enemies before dying. When the Prophet ﷺ found his body surrounded by the seven he had killed, he said: 'He killed seven and then was killed? This is of me and I am of him.' He was among the rare companions of unknown lineage whom the Prophet ﷺ gave special honors, showing Islam's rejection of lineage-based discrimination.
bio_id: Julaybib adalah sahabat Nabi ﷺ yang nasabnya tidak diketahui. Nabi ﷺ secara pribadi mengatur pernikahannya. Beliau terbunuh dalam ekspedisi militer setelah membunuh tujuh musuh. Nabi ﷺ bersabda: 'Dia membunuh tujuh orang lalu terbunuh? Dia dari-ku dan aku dari-nya.'

bio_short_en: One of the four Companions the Prophet ﷺ commanded Muslims to learn the Quran from. A freed slave who became one of the leading Quran reciters of the early Muslims.
bio_en: Salim Mawla Abi Hudhayfa was a freed slave of Abu Hudhayfa ibn 'Utbah who became one of the most eminent Companions in Quranic recitation. The Prophet ﷺ named him as one of the four Companions from whom Muslims should learn the Quran: 'Learn the Quran from four: Ibn Mas'ud, Salim Mawla Abi Hudhayfa, Ubayy ibn Ka'b, and Mu'adh ibn Jabal.' He led the early Muslims in Mecca in prayer due to his Quran mastery. He was martyred at the Battle of Yamama (12 AH) fighting the false prophet Musaylimah.
bio_id: Salim Mawla Abi Hudhayfa adalah mantan budak Abu Hudhayfa ibn 'Utbah yang menjadi salah satu sahabat paling terkemuka dalam bacaan Al-Quran. Nabi ﷺ menyebutnya sebagai salah satu dari empat sahabat yang darinya umat Islam harus mempelajari Al-Quran.

bio_short_en: Wife of Abu al-Darda' and a distinguished female Companion known for her knowledge and piety. Not to be confused with Umm al-Darda' al-Sughra (the Tabi'iyyah).
bio_en: Umm al-Darda' al-Kubra (the Elder) was the first wife of the Companion Abu al-Darda' al-Ansari. She was known for her knowledge, piety, and eloquence. She is to be distinguished from Umm al-Darda' al-Sughra (the Younger, a Tabi'iyyah scholar who was Abu al-Darda's second wife after the first's death and who was the more famous scholar). The elder Umm al-Darda' is mentioned in early biographical works as a woman of knowledge and virtue. She died before or shortly after the Prophet ﷺ.
bio_id: Umm al-Darda' al-Kubra (yang Tua) adalah istri pertama sahabat Abu al-Darda' al-Ansari. Beliau dikenal karena ilmu, kesalehan, dan kefasihannya. Jangan disamakan dengan Umm al-Darda' al-Sughra, seorang ulama Tabi'iyyah.

bio_short_en: A leading female hadith scholar of the Tabi'in generation. Granddaughter of Asma' bint Abi Bakr and wife of Hisham ibn 'Urwah, a great Tabi'i scholar.
bio_en: Fatimah bint al-Mundhir ibn al-Zubayr ibn al-Awwam was a leading female scholar of the Tabi'in generation. She was the granddaughter of Asma' bint Abi Bakr and married her cousin Hisham ibn 'Urwah (the hadith scholar and son of 'Urwah ibn al-Zubayr). She transmitted hadith from her grandmother Asma', from Hisham, and from other Companions and Tabi'in. She is cited as a narrator in major hadith collections and is praised for her piety, learning, and reliability as a narrator.
bio_id: Fatimah bint al-Mundhir ibn al-Zubayr ibn al-Awwam adalah ulama wanita terkemuka dari generasi Tabi'in. Beliau adalah cucu perempuan Asma' bint Abi Bakr dan istri Hisyam ibn 'Urwah.

bio_short_en: The greatest hadith scholar in Islamic history. His Sahih al-Bukhari is considered the most authentic book after the Quran. He memorized 600,000 hadiths.
bio_en: Muhammad ibn Isma'il ibn Ibrahim al-Bukhari al-Ju'fi was born in Bukhara (Uzbekistan) and became the greatest hadith scholar of all time. He began traveling for knowledge at age 16 and spent decades gathering hadiths across the Islamic world. He memorized over 600,000 hadiths with their chains and selected 7,275 unique hadiths (with repetitions: ~9,082) for his Sahih — spending 16 years on its composition and reportedly making ghusl and praying two rak'ahs before writing each hadith. His Jami' al-Sahih (known as Sahih al-Bukhari) is universally accepted as the most rigorously authenticated hadith collection and the most authentic book after the Quran. He also wrote al-Tarikh al-Kabir (a 9-volume biographical dictionary of narrators). He died in Khartank, near Samarqand, in 256 AH.
bio_id: Muhammad ibn Isma'il ibn Ibrahim al-Bukhari al-Ju'fi lahir di Bukhara (Uzbekistan) dan menjadi ulama hadis terbesar sepanjang masa. Jami' al-Sahih-nya (Sahih al-Bukhari) secara universal diterima sebagai koleksi hadis yang paling ketat diautentikasi, kitab paling sahih setelah Al-Quran.

bio_short_en: Compiler of Sunan Abu Dawud, one of the six canonical hadith collections focusing on Islamic law. He reviewed 500,000 hadiths and selected 4,800 for his Sunan.
bio_en: Sulayman ibn al-Ash'ath ibn Ishaq al-Azdi al-Sijistani was born in Sijistan (modern Afghanistan/Iran border region). He traveled extensively, studying under Imam Ahmad ibn Hanbal, Yahya ibn Ma'in, Ali ibn al-Madini, and hundreds of other scholars. He reviewed 500,000 hadiths and selected approximately 4,800 for his Sunan — the third of the six canonical hadith collections. The Sunan focuses predominantly on legal (fiqhi) hadiths and is the primary reference for deriving legal rulings. He wrote of his book: 'I collected 500,000 hadiths, then selected what is here. It is sufficient for a person's religious needs.' He died in Basra in 275 AH.
bio_id: Sulayman ibn al-Asy'ats ibn Ishaq al-Azdi al-Sijistani lahir di Sijistan (kawasan perbatasan Afghanistan/Iran modern). Sunan-nya adalah koleksi hadis kanonik ketiga dari enam kitab hadis utama, dengan fokus pada hadis-hadis hukum (fiqhi).

bio_short_en: Compiler of Jami' al-Tirmidhi (Sunan al-Tirmidhi), one of the six canonical hadith collections. Unique for including grades on each hadith and noting scholarly disagreements.
bio_en: Muhammad ibn 'Isa ibn Sawra ibn Musa al-Tirmidhi al-Dabbi was born near Tirmidh (modern Uzbekistan). He was a student of Imam al-Bukhari and Abu Dawud. His Jami' al-Tirmidhi (also called Sunan al-Tirmidhi) is one of the six canonical hadith collections. It is distinctive for including: after each hadith, al-Tirmidhi's own grading (hasan, sahih, da'if, etc.) and notes on which scholars held which legal opinions based on the hadith. This makes it uniquely valuable as a jurisprudential resource. He also wrote al-Shama'il al-Muhammadiyyah (a description of the Prophet's ﷺ physical and moral characteristics). He died in or near Tirmidh in 279 AH and was reportedly blind in his later years.
bio_id: Muhammad ibn 'Isa ibn Sawra ibn Musa al-Tirmidhi al-Dabbi lahir dekat Tirmidz (Uzbekistan modern). Jami' al-Tirmidhi-nya adalah salah satu dari enam koleksi hadis kanonik, unik karena menyertakan penilaian al-Tirmidhi sendiri pada setiap hadis.

bio_short_en: Compiler of Sunan al-Nasa'i (al-Mujtaba), one of the six canonical hadith collections. Considered by some the strictest hadith critic of his era after al-Bukhari and Muslim.
bio_en: Ahmad ibn Shu'ayb ibn Ali ibn Sinan al-Nasa'i al-Khurasani was born in Nasa (modern Turkmenistan). He traveled extensively through the Islamic world, collecting hadiths. He settled in Egypt for most of his life. His al-Mujtaba (known as Sunan al-Nasa'i or al-Sunan al-Sughra) is one of the six canonical hadith collections. He first wrote a larger collection (al-Sunan al-Kubra) and then condensed it to al-Mujtaba, selecting what he considered most reliable. He is known for particularly strict standards in evaluating narrators. He died in Palestine (some say Mecca) in 303 AH, reportedly after being beaten by supporters of Mu'awiyah for writing a book on the virtues of 'Ali.
bio_id: Ahmad ibn Syu'ayb ibn Ali al-Nasa'i al-Khurasani lahir di Nasa (Turkmenistan modern). Al-Mujtaba-nya (Sunan al-Nasa'i) adalah salah satu dari enam koleksi hadis kanonik. Beliau dikenal karena standar evaluasi perawi yang sangat ketat.

bio_short_en: One of the greatest scholars in Islamic history. His Tafsir al-Tabari and Tarikh al-Tabari are the foundational works of Quranic exegesis and Islamic history respectively.
bio_en: Muhammad ibn Jarir ibn Yazid al-Tabari al-Amuli was born in Amul (Tabaristan, modern Iran) and became one of the most encyclopedic scholars in Islamic history. He traveled across the Islamic world and settled in Baghdad. He reportedly offered to write 30 volumes of Quran commentary and 30 volumes of history but his students said they could not write so much — so he condensed each to the known versions. His Jami' al-Bayan fi Ta'wil al-Quran (Tafsir al-Tabari) is the most comprehensive early Quran commentary, running to 30 volumes, and is the foundational reference for classical tafsir methodology. His Tarikh al-Rusul wal-Muluk (History of Prophets and Kings) is the foundational work of Islamic historiography. He also independently derived legal principles (and his school, the Jariri madhab, existed briefly). He died in Baghdad in 310 AH.
bio_id: Muhammad ibn Jarir ibn Yazid al-Tabari al-Amuli lahir di Amul (Tabaristan, Iran modern) dan menjadi salah satu ulama paling ensiklopedis dalam sejarah Islam. Tafsir al-Tabari-nya adalah komentar Al-Quran awal yang paling komprehensif, dan Tarikh al-Tabari adalah karya dasar historiografi Islam.
works: Jami' al-Bayan fi Ta'wil al-Quran (Tafsir al-Tabari)

bio_short_en: One of the 'Ashara Mubashshara (Ten Given Glad Tidings of Paradise). He famously shielded the Prophet ﷺ at Uhud with his body, suffering a paralyzed hand in doing so.
bio_en: Talha ibn 'Ubayd Allah al-Taymi al-Qurayshi was one of the most honored Companions, among the 'Ashara Mubashshara (Ten Given Glad Tidings of Paradise). He was among the earliest converts to Islam and suffered greatly for it. At the Battle of Uhud, he stood firm in protecting the Prophet ﷺ when others fled, receiving over 70 wounds and having his hand permanently paralyzed. The Prophet ﷺ named him 'Talha the Generous' (Talha al-Khayr) for his extraordinary generosity — he was famously wealthy and would give away large fortunes. He was killed at the Battle of the Camel (36 AH) near Basra.
bio_id: Talha ibn 'Ubayd Allah al-Taymi al-Qurayshi adalah salah satu sahabat yang paling dihormati, termasuk dalam Sepuluh Orang yang Dijanjikan Surga. Pada Perang Uhud, beliau tetap teguh melindungi Nabi ﷺ, menerima lebih dari 70 luka.

bio_short_en: One of the 'Ashara Mubashshara (Ten Given Glad Tidings of Paradise). The Apostle's ﷺ 'disciple' and cousin. He was the first to draw a sword in defense of Islam.
bio_en: Abu Abd Allah al-Zubayr ibn al-'Awwam ibn Khuwaylid al-Asadi al-Qurayshi was one of the ten Companions promised Paradise and one of the most distinguished fighters in early Islam. He was the Prophet's ﷺ cousin (his mother was Safiyyah bint Abd al-Muttalib, the Prophet's aunt) and son-in-law (he married Asma' bint Abi Bakr). The Prophet ﷺ called him 'my disciple' (hawari). He was known for his extraordinary bravery in battle. He is considered the first person to draw a sword in defense of Islam. He participated in all major battles. He was killed at the Battle of Wadi al-Siba' (36 AH) during the conflict of the Camel. His son 'Urwah ibn al-Zubayr became one of the Seven Jurists of Madinah.
bio_id: Abu Abd Allah al-Zubayr ibn al-'Awwam ibn Khuwaylid al-Asadi al-Qurayshi adalah salah satu dari sepuluh sahabat yang dijanjikan surga. Nabi ﷺ menyebutnya 'murid saya' (hawari). Beliau dikenal karena keberaniannya yang luar biasa dalam pertempuran.

bio_short_en: One of the earliest converts and first horsemen in Islam. Famous for his bold speech at the Battle of Badr — the Prophet ﷺ praised him highly for it.
bio_en: Al-Miqdad ibn 'Amr al-Bahrani al-Kindi, known as al-Miqdad ibn al-Aswad (having been adopted by al-Aswad), was one of the earliest and most dedicated Companions. He was the first Muslim cavalryman. At the Battle of Badr, before the battle, 'Umar ibn al-Khattab and others suggested they should think carefully, but al-Miqdad stood and said: 'O Messenger of Allah, go where your Lord commands you, for we are with you. By Allah, we will not say what the Companions of Musa said to Musa: go you and your Lord and fight while we sit here. Rather, we will fight on your right and your left, in front of you and behind you.' The Prophet ﷺ was overjoyed. He died in Madinah in 33 AH.
bio_id: Al-Miqdad ibn 'Amr al-Bahrani al-Kindi, dikenal sebagai al-Miqdad ibn al-Aswad, adalah salah satu sahabat paling awal dan paling berdedikasi. Beliau adalah penunggang kuda Muslim pertama. Pidatonya yang berani sebelum Perang Badar sangat membahagiakan Nabi ﷺ.

bio_short_en: One of the greatest Companions and jurists. The Prophet ﷺ said: 'Whoever wants to recite the Quran fresh as when it was revealed, let him recite as Ibn Umm 'Abd recites.' Foundation of the Hanafi school.
bio_en: Abu Abd al-Rahman Abd Allah ibn Mas'ud ibn Ghafil al-Hudhali was among the earliest and most learned Companions. He was the sixth person to accept Islam. He served as the Prophet's ﷺ personal attendant and was so close that people thought he was part of the Prophet's household. The Prophet ﷺ said to learn the Quran from four Companions, placing Ibn Mas'ud first. He was the primary teacher of the Kufan school of Quranic recitation and jurisprudence. After the Prophet's death, he settled in Kufa where his legal methodology became the foundation of the Hanafi school (through Ibrahim al-Nakha'i, Hammad ibn Abi Sulayman, and Abu Hanifa). He died in Madinah in 32 AH.
bio_id: Abu Abd al-Rahman Abd Allah ibn Mas'ud ibn Ghafil al-Hudhali adalah salah satu sahabat paling awal dan paling berilmu. Beliau orang keenam yang masuk Islam. Metodologi hukumnya di Kufah menjadi fondasi mazhab Hanafi.

name_ar: عبد الله بن عمرو بن العاص
bio_short_en: One of the most prolific narrators of hadith. The Prophet ﷺ gave him special permission to write down hadiths. Known for his extreme piety and long worship.
bio_en: Abu Muhammad Abd Allah ibn 'Amr ibn al-'As al-Sahmi al-Qurayshi was the son of the Companion 'Amr ibn al-'As. He was one of the most prolific narrators of hadith, with over 700 narrations. The Prophet ﷺ gave him special permission to write down hadiths — his collection was called al-Sahifah al-Sadiqah (the Truthful Document), considered the oldest written hadith collection. He was known for his extreme piety, fasting constantly and praying all night, for which the Prophet ﷺ counselled moderation. He died in 63 AH.
bio_id: Abu Muhammad Abd Allah ibn 'Amr ibn al-'As al-Sahmi al-Qurayshi adalah salah satu perawi hadis paling produktif, dengan lebih dari 700 riwayat. Nabi ﷺ memberinya izin khusus untuk menulis hadis — koleksinya disebut al-Sahifah al-Shadiqah.

bio_short_en: One of the greatest scholars of the 2nd century AH in hadith and fiqh. Imam al-Shafi'i said: 'If Sufyan al-Thawri had not been, jurisprudence would have died in Kufa.'
bio_en: Abu Abd Allah Sufyan ibn Sa'id ibn Masruq al-Thawri al-Kufi was the greatest scholar of Kufa in the 2nd century AH, combining mastery of hadith with deep knowledge of Islamic law. He briefly founded his own legal school (the Thawri madhab), which survived for about two centuries. Imam al-Shafi'i said: 'If it were not for Malik and Sufyan al-Thawri, knowledge would have disappeared.' He was known for his deep piety and his flight from worldly power — he refused a judgeship and went into hiding to avoid it. He died in Basra in 161 AH.
bio_id: Abu Abd Allah Sufyan ibn Sa'id ibn Masruq al-Thawri al-Kufi adalah ulama terbesar Kufah pada abad ke-2 H, menggabungkan penguasaan hadis dengan pengetahuan mendalam tentang hukum Islam. Imam al-Syafi'i berkata: 'Jika bukan karena Malik dan Sufyan al-Thawri, ilmu akan hilang.'

bio_short_en: Author of al-Mustadrak 'ala al-Sahihayn, a collection of hadiths he claimed met the criteria of al-Bukhari and Muslim. A major authority on hadith sciences.
bio_en: Abu Abd Allah Muhammad ibn Abd Allah ibn Muhammad al-Hakim al-Naysaburi al-Shafi'i was the leading hadith scholar of Khorasan in the 4th century AH. His major work al-Mustadrak 'ala al-Sahihayn (The Supplement to the Two Sahihs) contains approximately 9,000 hadiths which he claimed met al-Bukhari's or Muslim's criteria but were not included in their collections. Al-Dhahabi abridged it with critical comments. He also wrote Ma'rifat 'Ulum al-Hadith (an important work on hadith sciences), al-Amali, and a biography of al-Shafi'i. He served as chief judge (qadi) of Nishapur. He died in 405 AH.
bio_id: Abu Abd Allah Muhammad ibn Abd Allah al-Hakim al-Naysaburi al-Syafi'i adalah ulama hadis terkemuka Khurasan pada abad ke-4 H. Al-Mustadrak 'ala al-Shahihain-nya berisi sekitar 9.000 hadis yang ia klaim memenuhi kriteria al-Bukhari atau Muslim.

bio_short_en: The most prolific narrator of hadith, with over 5,000 narrations. He devoted himself entirely to accompanying the Prophet ﷺ and memorizing his sayings.
bio_en: Abu Hurayrah Abd al-Rahman ibn Sakhr al-Dawsi al-Yamani was the most prolific narrator of prophetic hadith, with over 5,374 narrations attributed to him. Born in Yemen, he accepted Islam in 7 AH and devoted himself entirely to accompanying the Prophet ﷺ, joining the Ahl al-Suffah (the people of the mosque's covered platform who dedicated themselves to learning). He said: 'My Ansar brothers were busy with their farms, and my Muhajirun brothers were busy in the markets, but I had no worry except filling my belly — so I stayed with the Prophet.' His memory was so extraordinary that some doubted him, which the Prophet ﷺ himself addressed. He served as governor of Bahrain under 'Umar. He died in Madinah in 57 AH.
bio_id: Abu Hurayrah Abd al-Rahman ibn Sakhr al-Dawsi al-Yamani adalah perawi hadis Nabi yang paling produktif, dengan lebih dari 5.374 riwayat yang dinisbatkan kepadanya. Beliau mendedikasikan dirinya sepenuhnya untuk menemani Nabi ﷺ dan menghafal sabda-sabdanya.

bio_short_en: The greatest scholar of Islamic legal theory (usul al-fiqh) after al-Ghazali. His al-Muwafaqat revolutionized usul al-fiqh by developing the concept of maqasid al-shariah (objectives of Islamic law).
bio_en: Abu Ishaq Ibrahim ibn Musa ibn Muhammad al-Lakhmi al-Shatibi al-Andalusi al-Maliki was born in Granada, Al-Andalus, and became the most important Maliki usul al-fiqh scholar of the medieval period. His al-Muwafaqat fi Usul al-Shari'ah (Harmonizations in the Foundations of Islamic Law) is a revolutionary work that systematized and expanded the concept of maqasid al-shariah (the objectives and purposes behind Islamic law), going beyond previous usul works by explaining the higher aims behind legal rulings. He identified five universal necessities (daruriyyat): religion, life, lineage, intellect, and property. His al-I'tisam is the most important classical work on bid'ah (religious innovation). He died in Granada in 790 AH.
bio_id: Abu Ishaq Ibrahim ibn Musa ibn Muhammad al-Lakhmi al-Syathibi al-Andalusi al-Maliki lahir di Granada, Al-Andalus. Al-Muwafaqat-nya adalah karya revolusioner yang mensistematisasikan konsep maqasid al-syariah (tujuan dan maksud di balik hukum Islam). Al-I'tisam-nya adalah karya klasik paling penting tentang bid'ah.

bio_short_en: The most intellectually gifted Hanbali scholar of his era. His al-Funun is said to be the largest book ever written in Islamic civilization — reportedly 800 volumes.
bio_en: Abu al-Wafa' Ali ibn Aqil ibn Muhammad ibn Aqil al-Baghdadi al-Hanbali was the most brilliant Hanbali jurist of the 5th-6th century AH. He studied under al-Qadi Abu Ya'la and was known for his extraordinary intellect and intellectual courage. He wrote al-Funun — described as 800 volumes in his own lifetime — an encyclopedic work covering theology, jurisprudence, linguistics, and literature. Only fragments survive. He was persecuted for some of his earlier theological views (influenced by the Mu'tazilah) but repented and remained Hanbali. He also wrote al-Wadih fi Usul al-Fiqh. He died in Baghdad in 513 AH.
bio_id: Abu al-Wafa' Ali ibn Aqil ibn Muhammad ibn Aqil al-Baghdadi al-Hanbali adalah ahli fiqh Hanbali paling cemerlang pada abad ke-5-6 H. Al-Funun-nya — yang dikatakan berjumlah 800 jilid — adalah karya ensiklopedis mencakup teologi, yurisprudensi, linguistik, dan sastra.

bio_short_en: The son of Husayn ibn Ali and grandson of Ali ibn Abi Talib. Survived Karbala. Known as 'Zayn al-Abidin' (the Ornament of the Worshippers) for his extraordinary worship and piety.
bio_en: Ali ibn al-Husayn ibn Ali ibn Abi Talib, known as Zayn al-Abidin and Abu al-Husayn, was the son of the martyred Imam al-Husayn. He was present at Karbala but was too ill to fight, and thus survived the massacre. He returned to Madinah and spent the rest of his life in devotion, hadith transmission, and teaching. He is revered by all Muslims for his extraordinary piety and worship — historians note he performed 1,000 rak'ahs of voluntary prayer daily. He is an important hadith transmitter from his father al-Husayn and grandfather Ali. He is considered the fourth Imam in Shi'a theology, though Sunni scholars revere him as a pious Tabi'i. He died in Madinah in 94 AH.
bio_id: Ali ibn al-Husayn ibn Ali ibn Abi Thalib, dikenal sebagai Zayn al-Abidin, adalah putra Imam al-Husain yang syahid. Beliau hadir di Karbala tetapi terlalu sakit untuk bertempur, sehingga selamat. Beliau kembali ke Madinah dan menghabiskan sisa hidupnya dalam pengabdian, periwayatan hadis, dan pengajaran.

bio_short_en: The leading Maliki scholar of Qayrawan (Tunisia) in the 4th century AH. His al-Risalah al-Mufassalah is the foundational classical text on Islamic education.
bio_en: Abu al-Hasan Ali ibn Muhammad ibn Khalaf al-Qabisi al-Qayrawani was the leading Maliki scholar of Qayrawan (Tunisia) and one of the most influential scholars in Islamic educational theory. His al-Risalah al-Mufassalah (the Detailed Treatise) on education is the earliest and most comprehensive classical text on Islamic primary education (kuttab) — covering the proper way to teach children, the Quran curriculum, moral education, and teacher conduct. He also wrote on Maliki fiqh, hadith, and aqeedah. He died in Qayrawan in 403 AH.
bio_id: Abu al-Hasan Ali ibn Muhammad al-Qabisi al-Qayrawani adalah ulama Maliki terkemuka di Qayrawan (Tunisia). Al-Risalah al-Mufassalah-nya adalah teks klasik tertua dan paling komprehensif tentang pendidikan Islam dasar.

bio_short_en: The greatest Islamic philosopher and Aristotle's chief commentator. A practicing Maliki jurist. His philosophical works profoundly influenced medieval European scholasticism.
bio_en: Abu al-Walid Muhammad ibn Ahmad ibn Rushd al-Qurtubi al-Maliki, known in the West as Averroes, was the grandson of Ibn Rushd al-Jadd (the leading Maliki jurist of Cordoba). He was born in Cordoba, Al-Andalus, and was both a practicing Maliki jurist and the most important Islamic philosopher, serving as the chief commentator on Aristotle's works. His commentaries on Aristotle were translated into Latin and profoundly influenced European medieval scholasticism, including Thomas Aquinas. He also wrote Bidayat al-Mujtahid (a comparative Maliki fiqh work). He served as the court physician of the Almohad Caliph. He died in Marrakesh in 595 AH. Note: Some of his philosophical positions were criticized by Islamic scholars for potential conflict with orthodox theology.
bio_id: Abu al-Walid Muhammad ibn Ahmad ibn Rushd al-Qurtubi al-Maliki, dikenal di Barat sebagai Averroes, adalah filosof Islam terpenting dan komentator utama Aristoteles. Komentar-komentarnya tentang Aristoteles diterjemahkan ke dalam bahasa Latin dan sangat mempengaruhi skolastisisme Eropa abad pertengahan.

bio_short_en: Rector of al-Azhar University (1958-1963). A leading reformist scholar who worked on pan-Islamic unity and issued the controversial fatwa recognizing Ja'fari (Shia) fiqh as valid.
bio_en: Mahmud Shaltut ibn Ahmad al-Azhari was the Grand Imam and Rector of al-Azhar University in Egypt from 1958-1963. He was one of the leading modernist-reformist scholars who sought to make Islamic law responsive to contemporary conditions. He issued the famous (and controversial among Sunnis) fatwa in 1959 declaring that teaching the Ja'fari (Twelver Shia) school of jurisprudence is permissible for Sunnis — intended to promote Muslim unity. He also worked on Quranic tafsir and wrote al-Islam 'Aqidah wa-Shari'ah. He died in 1383 AH.
bio_id: Mahmud Syaltut ibn Ahmad al-Azhari adalah Grand Imam dan Rektor Universitas al-Azhar Mesir dari tahun 1958-1963. Beliau mengeluarkan fatwa terkenal (dan kontroversial di kalangan Sunni) yang menyatakan bahwa mengajarkan mazhab fiqh Ja'fari (Syiah Dua Belas Imam) diperbolehkan bagi umat Sunni.

bio_short_en: One of the greatest Islamic scholars of the 20th-21st centuries. Author of al-Fiqh al-Islami wa-Adillatuh, the most comprehensive modern encyclopedia of comparative Islamic law.
bio_en: Wahba Mustafa al-Zuhayli al-Hanafi was born in Dair 'Atiyyah, Syria, and became the most prolific Hanafi scholar of the 20th-21st centuries. He was a professor at Damascus University for decades. His al-Fiqh al-Islami wa-Adillatuh (Islamic Jurisprudence and Its Evidence) is an 8-volume (originally, later expanded to 11) encyclopedia covering all branches of Islamic law comparatively across the four madhabs — the most comprehensive modern work of its kind. He also wrote al-Tafsir al-Munir (16-volume modern tafsir), Usul al-Fiqh al-Islami, and al-Fiqh al-Hanbali al-Muyassar. He died in 1436 AH in Damascus.
bio_id: Wahba Mustafa al-Zuhayli al-Hanafi lahir di Dair 'Atiyyah, Suriah, dan menjadi ulama Hanafi paling produktif pada abad ke-20-21. Al-Fiqh al-Islami wa-Adillatuh-nya adalah ensiklopedi hukum Islam komparatif modern yang paling komprehensif.

bio_short_en: One of the greatest Hanafi jurists of the 20th century. His al-Madkhal al-Fiqhi al-'Amm is the most authoritative modern introduction to Islamic jurisprudence and civil law.
bio_en: Mustafa Ahmad al-Zarqa al-Halabi al-Hanafi was born in Aleppo, Syria, and became one of the most authoritative Islamic jurists of the 20th century. His al-Madkhal al-Fiqhi al-'Amm (General Introduction to Islamic Jurisprudence) is the most systematic and comprehensive modern treatment of Islamic jurisprudential theory and its relationship to civil law.
