He served as a professor at Damascus University and the University of Jordan. He was a member of the OIC Fiqh Academy. He died in Amman in 1420 AH.
bio_id: Mustafa Ahmad al-Zarqa al-Halabi al-Hanafi lahir di Aleppo, Suriah, dan menjadi salah satu ahli hukum Islam paling otoritatif abad ke-20. Al-Madkhal al-Fiqhi al-'Amm-nya adalah pengantar modern paling sistematis dan komprehensif tentang teori yurisprudensi Islam.

bio_short_en: The greatest universal scholar of the classical Islamic world. Contributed to astronomy, mathematics, physics, geography, history, pharmacology, and linguistics.
bio_en: Abu al-Rayhan Muhammad ibn Ahmad al-Biruni al-Khwarazmi was one of the greatest scholars in human history. Born in Khwarazm (modern Uzbekistan), he traveled extensively and produced groundbreaking works in nearly every field of knowledge: astronomy (al-Qanun al-Mas'udi), geography (Kitab al-Hind), history (al-Athar al-Baqiyah), pharmacology (Kitab al-Saydanah), and mathematics. His al-Qanun al-Mas'udi (dedicated to Sultan Mahmud of Ghazni) is an astronomical encyclopedia of the highest order. His Kitab al-Hind is the most important medieval description of Indian civilization and religion. He calculated the circumference of the Earth with remarkable accuracy. He died in Ghazni in 440 AH.
bio_id: Abu al-Rayhan Muhammad ibn Ahmad al-Biruni al-Khwarazmi adalah salah satu ulama universal terbesar dalam sejarah manusia. Beliau memberikan kontribusi revolusioner pada hampir setiap bidang ilmu pengetahuan: astronomi, geografi, sejarah, farmakologi, dan matematika.

bio_short_en: The master of hadith sciences in the 5th century AH. His Tarikh Baghdad (History of Baghdad) is the most comprehensive biographical dictionary of scholars associated with Baghdad.
bio_en: Abu Bakr Ahmad ibn Ali ibn Thabit al-Khatib al-Baghdadi was the foremost expert on hadith sciences of the 5th century AH. His Tarikh Baghdad (History of Baghdad) is a massive 14-volume biographical dictionary containing over 7,000 entries of scholars, poets, governors, and others connected to Baghdad — an indispensable source for Islamic biographical scholarship. He also wrote al-Kifayah fi 'Ilm al-Riwayah and al-Jami' li-Akhlaq al-Rawi, foundational works on hadith criticism. He died in Baghdad in 463 AH.
bio_id: Abu Bakr Ahmad ibn Ali ibn Tsabit al-Khatib al-Baghdadi adalah pakar ilmu hadis terkemuka pada abad ke-5 H. Tarikh Baghdad-nya (Sejarah Baghdad) adalah kamus biografi 14 jilid yang berisi lebih dari 7.000 entri ulama yang terhubung dengan Baghdad.

bio_short_en: A Mu'tazili scholar and literary giant of the Abbasid period. Author of the famous Sharh Nahj al-Balaghah, a literary and historical commentary on 'Ali ibn Abi Talib's sayings.
bio_en: Abu Hamid Abd al-Hamid ibn Hibat Allah ibn Muhammad ibn Abi al-Hadid al-Mada'ini was a Mu'tazili scholar, poet, and administrator under the Abbasid Caliph al-Nasir li-Din Allah. He is best known for his Sharh Nahj al-Balaghah, a 20-volume literary, historical, and philosophical commentary on the Nahj al-Balaghah (collection of speeches and letters attributed to 'Ali ibn Abi Talib). Though from a Mu'tazili perspective, it contains valuable historical and literary analysis. He died in Baghdad in 656 AH, the same year as the Mongol sack. Note: His Mu'tazili methodology means his theological conclusions should be evaluated carefully.
bio_id: Abu Hamid Abd al-Hamid ibn Hibat Allah ibn Muhammad ibn Abi al-Hadid al-Mada'ini adalah ulama Mu'tazilah, penyair, dan administrator. Sharh Nahj al-Balaghah-nya adalah komentar sastra, sejarah, dan filosofis 20 jilid. Catatan: metodologi Mu'tazilah-nya berarti kesimpulan teologisnya harus dievaluasi dengan hati-hati.

bio_short_en: One of the most prolific Companion narrators of hadith with over 1,500 narrations. An Ansari companion who participated in nearly all major events of early Islam.
bio_en: Abu Abd Allah Jabir ibn Abd Allah ibn 'Amr al-Khazraji al-Ansari was one of the most prolific narrators of prophetic hadith among the Companions, with approximately 1,540 narrations. He was an Ansari Companion from Madinah who participated in the second pledge at al-'Aqabah, the battles of Badr (though young), Uhud, and all subsequent battles. His father was killed at Uhud. The Prophet ﷺ was particularly kind to him and once offered to compensate him for his father's debts. He transmitted many hadith on fiqh, seerah, and tafsir. He died in Madinah around 74 AH.
bio_id: Abu Abd Allah Jabir ibn Abd Allah ibn 'Amr al-Khazraji al-Ansari adalah salah satu perawi hadis Nabi yang paling produktif di antara para sahabat, dengan sekitar 1.540 riwayat. Beliau adalah sahabat Anshar dari Madinah.

bio_short_en: The foremost Quranic scholar among the Companions. Known as 'the Sea' of knowledge and 'Tarjuman al-Quran' (Interpreter of the Quran). The Prophet ﷺ prayed for him to be given understanding of religion.
bio_en: Abu al-Abbas Abd Allah ibn Abbas ibn Abd al-Muttalib al-Hashimi al-Qurayshi was the Prophet's ﷺ cousin (the son of his uncle al-'Abbas). The Prophet ﷺ embraced him as a child and prayed: 'O Allah, grant him deep understanding in religion and teach him the interpretation of the Quran.' He became the foremost Quranic scholar, known as 'the Sea' (al-Bahr) of knowledge for his depth, 'Habr al-Ummah' (the Scholar of the Ummah), and 'Tarjuman al-Quran' (the Interpreter of the Quran). He narrated over 1,660 hadith. He was the ancestor of the Abbasid dynasty. He died in Ta'if in 68 AH.
bio_id: Abu al-Abbas Abd Allah ibn Abbas ibn Abd al-Muttalib al-Hashimi al-Qurayshi adalah sepupu Nabi ﷺ. Nabi mendoakannya: 'Ya Allah, berikan dia pemahaman mendalam dalam agama dan ajari dia tafsir Al-Quran.' Beliau menjadi ulama Al-Quran terkemuka, dikenal sebagai 'al-Bahr' (Lautan) ilmu.

bio_short_en: Author of al-Jami' li-Ahkam al-Quran (Tafsir al-Qurtubi), the most comprehensive Maliki Quranic commentary focusing on legal rulings extracted from the Quran.
bio_en: Abu Abd Allah Muhammad ibn Ahmad ibn Abi Bakr al-Ansari al-Qurtubi al-Maliki was born in Cordoba, Al-Andalus, and later settled in Egypt. His al-Jami' li-Ahkam al-Quran (Comprehensive Collection of Quranic Legal Rulings), known as Tafsir al-Qurtubi, is a 20-volume Quranic commentary focusing on extracting legal rulings from each verse. It is one of the most referenced classical tafsir works. He also wrote al-Tadhkirah fi Ahwal al-Mawta wa-Umur al-Akhirah (an important eschatological work). He was known for his piety and asceticism. He died in Munya Bani Khasib, Egypt, in 671 AH.
bio_id: Abu Abd Allah Muhammad ibn Ahmad al-Ansari al-Qurtubi al-Maliki lahir di Cordoba, Al-Andalus, dan kemudian menetap di Mesir. Al-Jami' li-Ahkam al-Quran-nya (Tafsir al-Qurtubi), terdiri dari 20 jilid, berfokus pada ekstraksi hukum syariat dari setiap ayat Al-Quran.

bio_short_en: One of the greatest usul al-fiqh scholars. His al-Ihkam fi Usul al-Ahkam is the most technically rigorous classical work in Islamic jurisprudential theory.
bio_en: Abu al-Hasan Sayf al-Din 'Ali ibn Abi 'Ali ibn Muhammad al-Amidi al-Hanbali (later Shafi'i) was one of the greatest scholars of Islamic jurisprudential theory (usul al-fiqh). His al-Ihkam fi Usul al-Ahkam (Verdicts on the Foundations of Legal Rulings) is considered the most technically rigorous and analytically sophisticated work in classical usul al-fiqh, engaging with legal theory at the level of systematic philosophy. He also wrote al-Mubin fi Sharh Alfaz al-Hukama' (on philosophical terminology). He was born in Amidah (modern Diyarbakir, Turkey) and died in Damascus in 631 AH.
bio_id: Abu al-Hasan Sayf al-Din 'Ali ibn Abi 'Ali ibn Muhammad al-Amidi al-Hanbali (kemudian Syafi'i) adalah salah satu ulama terbesar teori yurisprudensi Islam (usul al-fiqh). Al-Ihkam fi Usul al-Ahkam-nya dianggap sebagai karya paling teknis dan analitis dalam usul al-fiqh klasik.

bio_short_en: Known as 'Hakim al-Ummat' (The Sage of the Ummah). The most prolific Islamic scholar of the Indian subcontinent, writing over 1,000 works on every Islamic science.
bio_en: Ashraf 'Ali ibn Abd al-Haqq al-Thanawi al-Hanafi was the most influential Deobandi scholar of the 20th century and is known as 'Hakim al-Ummat' (The Sage of the Ummah) and 'Mujaddid al-Milla' (Renewer of the Community). Born in Thana Bhawan, India, he wrote over 1,000 works in Arabic, Urdu, and Persian. His most famous work Bahishti Zewar (Heavenly Ornaments) is a comprehensive guide to Islamic practice for Muslim women — one of the most widely read Islamic books in South Asia. He also wrote an Urdu Quran translation and tafsir (Bayan al-Quran) and numerous works on Sufi spiritual methodology (with emphasis on remaining within Shari'ah). He died in 1362 AH.
bio_id: Ashraf 'Ali ibn Abd al-Haqq al-Thanawi al-Hanafi adalah ulama Deobandi paling berpengaruh pada abad ke-20 dan dikenal sebagai 'Hakim al-Ummat' (Orang Bijak dari Umat). Beliau menulis lebih dari 1.000 karya dalam bahasa Arab, Urdu, dan Persia.

bio_short_en: The greatest hadith scholar of the Indian subcontinent in the 20th century. Known for his extraordinary memory — reportedly could recall the exact location of any hadith in the classical collections.
bio_en: Muhammad Anwar Shah ibn Mu'azzam Shah al-Kashmiri al-Hanafi was the foremost hadith scholar of the Indian subcontinent. He taught at Darul Uloom Deoband for many years. He was known for an extraordinary encyclopedic memory of hadith — scholars say he could cite the exact page and volume of any hadith in the classical collections from memory. He wrote Fayd al-Bari (dictated lectures on Sahih al-Bukhari, recorded by students), Ikfar al-Mulhidin, and 'Arf al-Shadhi (Tirmidhi commentary). He died in Deoband in 1352 AH.
bio_id: Muhammad Anwar Shah ibn Mu'azzam Shah al-Kashmiri al-Hanafi adalah ulama hadis terkemuka di anak benua India. Beliau dikenal dengan memori ensiklopedis yang luar biasa tentang hadis — dikatakan dapat mengutip halaman dan volume persis dari hadis mana pun dalam koleksi klasik dari ingatan.

bio_short_en: Son of the Caliph 'Umar ibn al-Khattab and one of the most prolific Companion narrators with over 2,600 hadiths. Known for his extreme devotion to following the Sunnah precisely.
bio_en: Abu Abd al-Rahman Abd Allah ibn Umar ibn al-Khattab al-'Adawi al-Qurayshi was the son of the Caliph 'Umar ibn al-Khattab. He was one of the most devoted Companions in following the Sunnah — he would attempt to retrace every step the Prophet ﷺ took, praying in the same spots and following his habits precisely. He narrated over 2,630 hadith — making him the fourth most prolific narrator among the Companions after Abu Hurayrah, Ibn 'Abbas, and Anas ibn Malik. He refused to take sides in the civil wars (refusing both 'Ali and Mu'awiyah and later Husayn and Yazid). He died in Mecca in 73 AH after being wounded by a poisoned spear.
bio_id: Abu Abd al-Rahman Abd Allah ibn Umar ibn al-Khattab al-'Adawi al-Qurayshi adalah putra Khalifah 'Umar ibn al-Khattab. Beliau adalah salah satu sahabat yang paling berdedikasi dalam mengikuti Sunnah. Beliau meriwayatkan lebih dari 2.630 hadis.

bio_short_en: Granddaughter of the Prophet ﷺ and daughter of Ali and Fatimah. She was also present at Karbala.
bio_en: Umm Kulthum bint Ali ibn Abi Talib (9–61 AH) was the granddaughter of the Prophet Muhammad ﷺ and the daughter of Ali ibn Abi Talib and Fatimah al-Zahra. She was present alongside her brother Husayn and sister Zaynab at Karbala. Like her sister Zaynab, she was taken captive following the tragedy of Karbala and is remembered as a witness to those events. She is among the Ahl al-Bayt revered by Muslims.
bio_id: Umm Kulthum bint Ali adalah cucu Nabi ﷺ yang hadir pada peristiwa Karbala.

bio_short_en: One of the greatest hadith critics of the classical period, known for his encyclopedic knowledge of narrators.
bio_en: Yahya ibn Ma'een al-Ghatafani (158–233 AH) was one of the foremost hadith scholars and narrator critics (rijal critics) of the classical period. Born in Niqya near Baghdad, he inherited wealth which he spent entirely on acquiring hadith knowledge, reportedly traveling extensively and spending over a million dirhams on this pursuit. He was a student of Yahya al-Qattan and a close contemporary of Ahmad ibn Hanbal. His evaluations of narrators are considered definitive in the science of hadith criticism ('ilm al-rijal), and his assessments are preserved in works like Ta'rikh ibn Ma'een. He evaluated hundreds of thousands of narrators and is one of the founding authorities of hadith criticism.
bio_id: Yahya ibn Ma'een (158–233 H) adalah salah satu ahli hadith terbesar yang dikenal dengan kritik perawinya.

bio_short_en: Imam al-Haramayn, teacher of al-Ghazali and one of the greatest Ash'ari theologians and Shafi'i jurists.
bio_en: Abd al-Malik ibn Abdallah ibn Yusuf al-Juwayni (419–478 AH), known as Imam al-Haramayn (Imam of the Two Sanctuaries), was one of the greatest Shafi'i jurists and Ash'ari theologians of the medieval period. Born in Juwain near Nishapur, he succeeded his father as a leading scholar. He taught in Makkah and Madinah for several years (earning the title Imam al-Haramayn), then returned to Nishapur where he headed the Nizamiyya madrasa. His most famous student was Abu Hamid al-Ghazali. His major works include al-Irshad in theology and al-Burhan in usul al-fiqh, both foundational texts in Ash'ari-Shafi'i tradition.
bio_id: al-Juwayni (419–478 H) adalah Imam al-Haramayn, guru al-Ghazali dan salah satu teolog Asy'ari terbesar.

bio_short_en: North African Maliki scholar whose Sanusiyya creed became the standard Ash'ari theological text in the Muslim world.
bio_en: Muhammad ibn Yusuf al-Sanusi al-Tilimsani (837–895 AH) was a Maliki scholar and Ash'ari theologian from Tlemcen (modern Algeria). His brief theological treatise Umm al-Barahin (also known as al-Sanusiyya) became one of the most widely studied texts in Islamic theology across North Africa, the Arab world, and West Africa, and was the subject of numerous commentaries. He also wrote on logic, hadith, and Sufism. al-Sanusi's clear systematization of the obligatory, impossible, and permissible divine attributes made his work accessible for teaching at all levels.
bio_id: al-Sanusi (837–895 H) adalah ulama Maliki dari Afrika Utara yang risalah aqidahnya digunakan di seluruh dunia Muslim.

bio_short_en: 19th century Moroccan scholar who settled in Arabia and founded an influential spiritual and scholarly tradition.
bio_en: Ahmad ibn Idris al-Fasi al-Hasani (1173–1253 AH / 1760–1837 CE) was a Moroccan-born scholar who became one of the most influential Islamic scholars of the 19th century. After studying in Fez, he traveled to Cairo and eventually settled in the Hijaz and Yemen. He emphasized direct study of hadith and Quran over medieval school texts, and his approach attracted many students who went on to found movements in Sudan (Muhammad al-Mirghani, Khatmiyya), Libya (Muhammad al-Sanusi, Sanusiyya), and Somalia. He is not associated with tariqah innovation but with hadith-focused scholarly renewal.
bio_id: Ahmad ibn Idris al-Fasi (1173–1253 H) adalah ulama Maroko yang berpengaruh di abad ke-19.

bio_short_en: One of the first seven Muslims, participated in all major battles including Badr while riding a horse.
bio_en: al-Miqdad ibn Amr al-Bahrani al-Kindi (died 33 AH), also known as al-Miqdad ibn al-Aswad, was one of the earliest Muslims and among the first seven to accept Islam. He was the only horseman at the Battle of Badr, riding into combat on horseback — a distinction highlighted by the Prophet ﷺ. He participated in all the major battles alongside the Prophet ﷺ. At the consultation before Badr, he delivered one of the most famous statements: 'We will not say as the Children of Israel said to Moses... Rather we will fight on your right and your left, in front of you and behind you.' He narrated hadiths and is counted among the senior companions.
bio_id: al-Miqdad ibn Amr adalah salah satu tujuh Muslim pertama dan satu-satunya penunggang kuda di Badar.

bio_short_en: Imam of Sicily, leading Maliki jurist and physician of the medieval period.
bio_en: Muhammad ibn Ali ibn Umar al-Tamimi al-Mazari (453–536 AH) was a Maliki scholar and physician from Mazara in Sicily. Known as 'the Imam' in the Maliki school of his time, he was a jurist, hadith scholar, and theologian as well as a practicing physician. He authored a major commentary on Sahih Muslim called al-Mu'lim bi-Fawa'id Muslim, which was later summarized and built upon by al-Qadi Iyad. His medical writing was also noted. He represents the flourishing of Islamic scholarship in Sicily and North Africa during the medieval period.
bio_id: al-Mazari (453–536 H) adalah Imam Sisilia, ahli fiqh Maliki dan dokter terkemuka.

bio_short_en: Leading Maliki jurist of al-Andalus and grandfather of the philosopher Averroes (Ibn Rushd).
bio_en: Muhammad ibn Ahmad ibn Muhammad ibn Rushd al-Qurtubi (450–520 AH), known as Ibn Rushd al-Jadd (the grandfather) to distinguish him from his famous philosopher grandson, was the leading Maliki jurist of al-Andalus of his time. He served as the chief qadi (judge) of Cordoba and as the prayer leader of its Great Mosque. His major work al-Bayan wa-al-Tahsil is a monumental 20-volume collection of legal questions and responses, and his Muqaddimat is an important introduction to Maliki jurisprudence. He is considered one of the greatest Maliki scholars of Andalus.
bio_id: Ibn Rushd al-Jadd (450–520 H) adalah ahli fiqh Maliki terkemuka di Andalusia.

bio_short_en: One of the Seven Fuqaha' of Madinah, nephew of Aisha and son of al-Zubayr ibn al-Awwam. Pioneer of seerah writing.
bio_en: Urwah ibn al-Zubayr ibn al-Awwam (23–94 AH) was a leading Tabi'i scholar and one of the Seven Fuqaha' (jurists) of Madinah. He was the son of the companion al-Zubayr ibn al-Awwam and the nephew of Aisha bint Abi Bakr. He narrated extensively from Aisha and preserved much of the seerah. He is considered one of the first to write about the life of the Prophet ﷺ systematically, making him a pioneer in seerah scholarship. His students included al-Zuhri, who transmitted much of this knowledge to later generations.
bio_id: Urwah ibn al-Zubayr (23–94 H) adalah salah satu Tujuh Fuqaha Madinah dan pelopor penulisan sirah.

name_ar: سالم بن عبد الله بن عمر
bio_short_en: One of the Seven Fuqaha' of Madinah, son of Ibn Umar. Most authentic transmitter of his father's hadiths.
bio_en: Salim ibn Abdallah ibn Umar ibn al-Khattab (40–106 AH) was one of the Seven Fuqaha' (jurists) of Madinah and the son of the companion Abdullah ibn Umar. He was the most authentic transmitter of his father's hadiths and is described by scholars as one of the most trustworthy narrators of his generation. He was renowned for his piety, refusal of worldly offices, and literal adherence to his father's practice. al-Zuhri said: 'The fuqaha' of Madinah in my time were four: Sa'id ibn al-Musayyib, Urwah ibn al-Zubayr, al-Qasim ibn Muhammad, and Salim ibn Abdallah.'
bio_id: Salim ibn Abdallah ibn Umar (40–106 H) adalah salah satu Tujuh Fuqaha Madinah dan putra Ibn Umar.

bio_short_en: One of the Seven Fuqaha' of Madinah and the last of them to survive. Son of Abd al-Rahman ibn al-Harith.
bio_en: Abu Bakr ibn Abd al-Rahman ibn al-Harith ibn Hisham al-Makhzumi (22–94 AH) was one of the Seven Fuqaha' of Madinah and the last of them to die. He went blind in his later years but continued to teach and issue fatwas. He is counted among the leading scholars of Madinah in the generation between the companions and the classical scholars. He narrated from many companions including Abu Hurayrah and Aisha.
bio_id: Abu Bakr ibn Abd al-Rahman (22–94 H) adalah salah satu Tujuh Fuqaha Madinah yang terakhir wafat.

bio_short_en: Most important hadith transmitter of the Tabi'un. First to systematically collect and write down hadith by order of Umar ibn Abd al-Aziz.
bio_en: Muhammad ibn Muslim ibn Ubaydallah ibn Shihab al-Zuhri (51–124 AH), known as al-Zuhri, was the most important hadith scholar of the Tabi'un generation and is universally regarded as one of the greatest scholars in Islamic history. By order of Caliph Umar ibn Abd al-Aziz, he undertook the first systematic collection and recording of hadith. Ahmad ibn Hanbal said: 'The isnad of al-Zuhri is the most authentic of all isnads.' He narrated from many companions and Tabi'un, and was narrated from by Imam Malik, Sufyan ibn Uyaynah, and the greatest scholars of the next generation. He is credited with being the first to formally write down hadith on a large scale.
bio_id: Ibn Shihab al-Zuhri (51–124 H) adalah transmiter hadith terpenting generasi Tabi'in yang pertama menulis hadith secara sistematis.

bio_short_en: Direct teacher of Abu Hanifah and key transmitter of the Kufa jurisprudential tradition.
bio_en: Hammad ibn Abi Sulayman al-Ash'ari al-Kufi (50–120 AH) was the direct and primary teacher of Abu Hanifah al-Nu'man. He transmitted the Kufa jurisprudential tradition from Ibrahim al-Nakha'i, who transmitted from Alqamah, who transmitted from Abdullah ibn Mas'ud, who narrated from the Prophet ﷺ. This chain gives the Hanafi school its primary connection to Prophetic practice. Hammad was recognized as the leading jurist of Kufa in his time, and Abu Hanifah studied under him for 18 years. He was known for his mastery of fiqh and hadith and was trusted by the scholars of his era.
bio_id: Hammad ibn Abi Sulayman (50–120 H) adalah guru langsung Abu Hanifah dan perantara tradisi fiqh Kufah.

bio_short_en: Mother of the Prophet Muhammad ﷺ. She died when he was approximately six years old.
bio_en: Aminah bint Wahb al-Zuhri was the mother of the Prophet Muhammad ﷺ and a woman of noble lineage from the Quraysh tribe. She married Abdullah ibn Abd al-Muttalib, the Prophet's father, who died before the Prophet was born. Aminah died when Muhammad ﷺ was approximately six years old, while returning from a visit to her family in Yathrib (Madinah). She passed away at a place called al-Abwa'. Her death left the young Muhammad ﷺ an orphan, which is referenced in the Quran (93:6): 'Did He not find you an orphan and give [you] refuge?'
bio_id: Aminah bint Wahb adalah ibu Nabi Muhammad ﷺ yang wafat saat beliau berusia sekitar enam tahun.

bio_short_en: Sister of Ali ibn Abi Talib who gave refuge to two men during the Conquest of Makkah, which the Prophet ﷺ honored.
bio_en: Fakhtah bint Abi Talib, known as Umm Hani' (died approximately 50 AH), was a companion of the Prophet ﷺ and the sister of Ali ibn Abi Talib. During the Conquest of Makkah (8 AH), she gave refuge to two men from her former husband's family. When Ali ibn Abi Talib intended to kill them, Umm Hani' went to the Prophet ﷺ who said: 'We have given protection to those whom you have given protection, O Umm Hani'.' She also reported the Isra' and Mi'raj, as the Prophet ﷺ departed from her house. She is counted among those who narrated hadiths from the Prophet ﷺ.
bio_id: Umm Hani' bint Abi Talib adalah sahabat wanita yang memberikan perlindungan kepada dua orang saat Fathu Makkah.

bio_short_en: Son of al-Abbas ibn Abd al-Muttalib and cousin of the Prophet ﷺ. Narrated hadith from the Prophet and performed key roles at Hajjat al-Wada'.
bio_en: al-Fadl ibn al-Abbas ibn Abd al-Muttalib (died 18 AH) was the cousin of the Prophet Muhammad ﷺ and the eldest son of al-Abbas ibn Abd al-Muttalib. He was with the Prophet ﷺ during Hajjat al-Wada' (the Farewell Pilgrimage) and performed significant services including riding with the Prophet ﷺ and helping the Prophet ﷺ perform ghusl at death. He narrated hadiths from the Prophet ﷺ and was martyred during the plague of Amwas (18 AH) in Syria. He is counted among the companions who participated in the battles.
bio_id: al-Fadl ibn al-Abbas adalah sepupu Nabi ﷺ yang memainkan peran penting saat Haji Wada'.

bio_short_en: Primary student of Ibn Taymiyya and prolific author on fiqh, spirituality, hadith, and Quranic sciences.
bio_en: Shams al-Din Abu Abdallah Muhammad ibn Abi Bakr ibn Ayyub al-Zar'i al-Dimashqi (691–751 AH), known as Ibn al-Qayyim al-Jawziyya, was the most important student of Ibn Taymiyya and one of the most prolific scholars in Islamic history. He wrote on fiqh, hadith, tafsir, spirituality, medicine, logic, and Islamic psychology. His major works include Zad al-Ma'ad (a comprehensive study of the prophetic biography and practice), Madarij al-Salikin (on spiritual development), I'lam al-Muwaqqi'in (on Islamic legal theory and jurisprudence), and al-Tibyan fi Aqsam al-Quran. He was imprisoned alongside his teacher Ibn Taymiyya for their views. His synthesis of spiritual refinement with strict adherence to Quran and Sunnah is deeply influential.
bio_id: Ibn al-Qayyim al-Jawziyya (691–751 H) adalah murid utama Ibn Taimiyah dan penulis karya fiqh, spiritualitas, dan hadith yang produktif.

bio_short_en: Co-founder of Darul Uloom Deoband and leading Hanafi scholar of 19th-century India.
bio_en: Rashid Ahmad al-Gangohi al-Hanafi (1244–1323 AH / 1829–1905 CE) was one of the founding figures of the Deoband movement and the leading Hanafi scholar of 19th-century India. A student of the great hadith master Muhammad Ishaq al-Dehlawi and spiritual disciple of Hajji Imdadullah Makki, he cofounded Darul Uloom Deoband in 1867 alongside Muhammad Qasim Nanawtawi. He was revered as a jurist, hadith scholar, and spiritual guide. His fatawa collection (Fatawa Rashidiyya) is a major reference in Hanafi fiqh for the subcontinent. He is considered the foundational authority of the Deobandi school.
bio_id: Rashid Ahmad Gangohi (1244–1323 H) adalah salah satu pendiri Darul Uloom Deoband dan ulama Hanafi terkemuka India abad ke-19.

bio_short_en: Greatest Islamic scholar of Timbuktu, prolific author of biographies and fiqh. Taken captive to Morocco but continued writing.
bio_en: Ahmad Baba ibn Ahmad ibn Ahmad al-Timbukti al-Sudani al-Sanhaji (963–1036 AH / 1556–1627 CE) was the most famous scholar of Timbuktu and one of the greatest African Islamic scholars of his era. He was born into a scholarly family that had produced generations of Islamic scholars. When the Moroccan Sa'dian army invaded Timbuktu (1591 CE), he was taken captive to Marrakesh where he continued to teach and write. He authored over 40 works, most notably Nayl al-Ibtihaj bi-Tatriz al-Dibaj, a biographical dictionary of Maliki scholars. He was a master of hadith, fiqh, and Maliki jurisprudence, and his work preserves invaluable knowledge about the Timbuktu scholarly tradition.
bio_id: Ahmad Baba al-Timbukti (963–1036 H) adalah ulama Islam terbesar Timbuktu dan penulis lebih dari 40 karya.

bio_short_en: West African Islamic scholar, mujahid, and founder of the Toucouleur Empire in the Senegambia region.
bio_en: Umar ibn Said al-Futi al-Toro, known as al-Hajj Umar Tal (1212–1281 AH / 1797–1864 CE), was a West African Islamic scholar, Sufi shaykh of the Tijaniyya order, and military leader who founded the Toucouleur Empire spanning modern-day Senegal, Guinea, and Mali. He performed Hajj three times and studied under the Tijaniyya shaykh Muhammad al-Ghali in Madinah. He wrote al-Rimah Hizb al-Rahim (Spears of the Party of the Merciful), a major theological and Sufi text. He led jihad against both French colonialism and pagan Bambara kingdoms. Note: He was associated with the Tijaniyya Sufi order.
bio_id: al-Hajj Umar Tal (1212–1281 H) adalah ulama Islam Afrika Barat dan pendiri Kekaisaran Toucouleur.

bio_short_en: Senegalese Islamic scholar with one of the largest followings in West Africa. President of the World Muslim League.
bio_en: Ibrahim ibn Abd Allah Niass al-Kawlakhi (1318–1395 AH / 1900–1975 CE) was a Senegalese Islamic scholar and one of the most influential figures in 20th-century West African Islam. He was a leader of the Tijaniyya Sufi order and is said to have had tens of millions of followers across West Africa, particularly in Senegal, Nigeria, and Ghana. He served as vice president of the World Muslim League (Rabitat al-Alam al-Islami) and was the first African Muslim leader to meet with the Saudi royal family in a context of mutual respect. He wrote extensively on Islam, Sufism, and theology. Note: He was a leader of the Tijaniyya Sufi order; his scholarship is recognized while tariqah practices are noted.
bio_id: Ibrahim Niass (1318–1395 H) adalah ulama Islam Senegal dengan jutaan pengikut di Afrika Barat.

bio_short_en: Greatest Malay-Patani Islamic scholar, prolific author of Malay-language religious texts.
bio_en: Dawud ibn Abdallah al-Fatani al-Shafi'i (1183–1263 AH / 1769–1847 CE) was the most prominent Islamic scholar from the Patani region of southern Thailand and is considered one of the greatest Islamic scholars in the history of the Malay world. He spent much of his life in Makkah, studying and teaching, and authored over 66 works in the Malay language on theology, fiqh, hadith, and Sufism. His works spread throughout the Malay archipelago and the Malay Peninsula, and he is credited with producing the most comprehensive Islamic library in the Malay language of his era. He is sometimes called 'al-Nawawi of the Malay world.'
bio_id: Dawud al-Fatani (1183–1263 H) adalah ulama Melayu-Patani terbesar dan penulis lebih dari 66 karya berbahasa Melayu.

bio_short_en: First major Malay poet and Islamic scholar from Aceh, pioneering Malay Islamic literature.
bio_en: Hamzah Fansuri al-Fansuri (c. 960–1027 AH / c. 1550–1620 CE) was an Islamic scholar and poet from Barus (Fansur), Aceh in northern Sumatra. He is considered the earliest significant Malay poet and the father of Malay Islamic mystical literature. He wrote extensively in Malay on Islamic theology and spiritual themes, producing the first systematic Malay Islamic poetry. His works include Asrar al-Arifin (Secrets of the Gnostics). His later writings were condemned by the Shafi'i scholar Nuruddin al-Raniri as containing pantheistic ideas influenced by Ibn Arabi. Note: Some of his theological positions were criticized; include only his role as a pioneering scholar of Malay Islamic literature.
bio_id: Hamzah Fansuri (c. 960–1027 H) adalah penyair Melayu pertama yang signifikan dan pelopor sastra Islam Melayu.

bio_short_en: Turkish Islamic scholar whose Risale-i Nur (Epistles of Light) is one of the most widely read Islamic works of the 20th century.
bio_en: Said Nursi (Bediüzzaman Said Nursi) (1290–1379 AH / 1873–1960 CE) was a Turkish Kurdish Islamic scholar who wrote the Risale-i Nur (Epistles of Light), a vast Quranic commentary addressing the crises of faith in the modern age. Born in eastern Anatolia, he first pursued traditional Islamic scholarship and engaged in political activism, later suffering imprisonment and exile under the Turkish secular republic. In exile, he produced the Risale-i Nur collection — over 6,000 pages addressing the relationship between faith, reason, science, and modern challenges. The collection has been translated into many languages and has influenced millions of Muslims worldwide. He is considered one of the most important Islamic thinkers of the 20th century.
bio_id: Said Nursi (1290–1379 H) adalah ulama Islam Turki yang Risale-i Nur-nya adalah salah satu karya Islam paling banyak dibaca abad ke-20.

bio_short_en: Turkish Islamic scholar and shaykh who revived Islamic learning in Turkey under the secular republic.
bio_en: Mehmed Zahid Kotku (1315–1400 AH / 1897–1980 CE) was a Turkish Islamic scholar and the last prominent shaykh of the Naqshbandi-Khalidi order in Turkey. He served as imam at the Süleymaniye and Iskender Pasa mosques in Istanbul. Despite Turkey's secular restrictions on Islamic education, he educated thousands of students in traditional Islamic sciences. Many of his students became influential figures in Turkish politics, business, and scholarship. He is credited with playing a key role in the revival of Islamic consciousness in Turkey during the 20th century.
bio_id: Mehmed Zahid Kotku (1315–1400 H) adalah ulama Turki yang menghidupkan kembali pembelajaran Islam di bawah republik sekuler.

bio_short_en: Persian polymath, astronomer, and mathematician who served the Mongols. Founded the Maragha observatory.
bio_en: Muhammad ibn Muhammad ibn al-Hasan al-Tusi, known as Nasir al-Din al-Tusi (597–672 AH / 1201–1274 CE), was a Persian polymath who made significant contributions to mathematics, astronomy, philosophy, and Islamic theology. He served the Ismaili order in Alamut before being captured by Hulagu Khan (the Mongol ruler), whom he then served. He founded the Maragha Observatory in Azerbaijan, which became the most advanced astronomical observatory of its time. His Zij-i Ilkhani remained the standard astronomical reference for centuries. He also wrote on ethics (Akhlaq-i Nasiri) and theology. His work preserved and transmitted much of the Islamic scientific tradition after the Mongol invasion. Note: His association with Ismailis and later Mongols was controversial; his scientific contributions are significant.
bio_id: Nasir al-Din al-Tusi (597–672 H) adalah polymath Persia dan astronom yang mendirikan observatorium Maragha.

bio_short_en: Major Islamic historian and biographer. His Futuh al-Buldan is the foundational history of the Islamic conquests.
bio_en: Ahmad ibn Yahya ibn Jabir al-Baladhuri (189–279 AH) was one of the most important historians of the classical Islamic period. His Futuh al-Buldan (Conquests of the Lands) is the primary source for the history of the Islamic conquests of Syria, Iraq, Persia, Egypt, and North Africa, and remains essential for historians of the early Islamic period. He also wrote Ansab al-Ashraf (Genealogies of the Nobles), a major biographical dictionary of companions, early Muslims, and notable figures. He served at the Abbasid court and was known for his translations of Persian texts into Arabic.
bio_id: al-Baladhuri (189–279 H) adalah sejarawan Islam terkemuka yang Futuh al-Buldan-nya adalah sumber utama sejarah penaklukan Islam.

bio_short_en: Arab historian and geographer known as the 'Herodotus of the Arabs'. His Muruj al-Dhahab is a universal history.
bio_en: Ali ibn al-Husayn ibn Ali al-Mas'udi (282–346 AH) was an Arab historian, geographer, and traveler of wide-ranging intellectual interests. Known as the 'Herodotus of the Arabs,' he traveled extensively across the Muslim world, India, East Africa, and other regions, recording his observations and compiling historical and geographical knowledge. His major work Muruj al-Dhahab wa-Ma'adin al-Jawhar (Meadows of Gold and Mines of Gems) is a universal history combining history, geography, natural science, and social observation. His work is a primary source for the history of the Islamic world in the 10th century CE.
bio_id: al-Mas'udi (282–346 H) adalah sejarawan dan ahli geografi Arab yang dikenal sebagai 'Herodotus orang Arab'.

bio_short_en: Companion known for wisdom, piety, and knowledge. Judge of Damascus and teacher of Quran to the Syrians.
bio_en: Uwaymir ibn Zayd ibn Qays al-Khazraji, known as Abu al-Darda' (died 32 AH), was a companion of the Prophet ﷺ known for his wisdom, asceticism, and deep spiritual insight. He was one of the companions who learned the Quran directly from the Prophet ﷺ and was appointed as judge of Damascus and teacher of Quran to the Syrians by Caliph Umar ibn al-Khattab. He narrated many hadiths and is famous for his sayings on wisdom and knowledge. His wife Umm al-Darda' was also a notable scholar. He said: 'The world is a prison for the believer and paradise for the unbeliever.'
bio_id: Abu al-Darda' (wafat 32 H) adalah sahabat yang dikenal karena kebijaksanaan dan kesalehannya, hakim Damaskus.

bio_short_en: First child born to the Muslims in Madinah after the Hijra. Led an independent caliphate against the Umayyads for nine years.
bio_en: Abd Allah ibn al-Zubayr ibn al-Awwam (1–73 AH) was the first Muslim child born in Madinah after the Hijra, an event celebrated by the companions as a good omen. He was the son of the companion al-Zubayr ibn al-Awwam and Asma' bint Abi Bakr, and grew up in the household of the companions. He became a great warrior and scholar. He opposed the Umayyad dynasty and established an independent caliphate from Makkah that controlled much of the Muslim world for nine years (64–73 AH). He rebuilt the Ka'bah and died defending Makkah when al-Hajjaj ibn Yusuf besieged the city. He is counted among the companions.
bio_id: Abd Allah ibn al-Zubayr (1–73 H) adalah anak Muslim pertama yang lahir di Madinah setelah Hijrah.

bio_short_en: Companion and one of the first seven Muslims. The only horseman at Badr, praised by the Prophet ﷺ for his loyalty.
bio_en: al-Miqdad ibn Amr ibn Tha'laba al-Bahrani, known as al-Miqdad ibn al-Aswad (died 33 AH), was among the first seven persons to embrace Islam and one of the earliest companions to display loyalty to the Prophet ﷺ. He was the only horseman at the Battle of Badr and gave the famous pledge before Badr: 'We will not say as the Children of Israel said... We will fight on your right and your left.' He was among the most loyal companions to Ali ibn Abi Talib. He was noted for the Prophet's special mention of him and his role as one of the earliest and most faithful Muslims.
bio_id: al-Miqdad ibn al-Aswad (wafat 33 H) adalah salah satu tujuh Muslim pertama dan satu-satunya penunggang kuda di Badar.

bio_short_en: Grandson of Ali ibn Abi Talib and survivor of Karbala. Known as Zayn al-Abidin (Ornament of the Worshippers) for his piety.
bio_en: Ali ibn al-Husayn ibn Ali ibn Abi Talib (38–94 AH), known as Zayn al-Abidin (Ornament of the Worshippers), was the son of Husayn ibn Ali and the grandson of Ali ibn Abi Talib. He survived the tragedy of Karbala (61 AH) by being ill and unable to fight. After Karbala, he returned to Madinah where he lived a life of intense worship, prayer, and scholarship. He is revered by all Muslims for his piety and his supplications (du'as), collected in al-Sahifa al-Sajjadiyya. He narrated from his father, his uncle al-Hasan, and other companions. He is counted among the Tabi'un scholars and his knowledge of Islamic sciences was widely recognized.
bio_id: Ali ibn Husayn Zayn al-Abidin (38–94 H) adalah cucu Ali ibn Abi Talib yang selamat dari Karbala dan dikenal karena kesalehannya.

bio_short_en: Mamluk-era Egyptian historian whose al-Nujum al-Zahirah chronicles Egypt's history year by year.
bio_en: Abu al-Mahasin Jamal al-Din Yusuf ibn Taghribirdi al-Atabaki (812–874 AH) was an Egyptian historian of the Mamluk period. Born to a Mamluk amir, he devoted himself to scholarship and writing history. His major work al-Nujum al-Zahirah fi Muluk Misr wa-al-Qahira (Brilliant Stars about the Kings of Egypt and Cairo) is a year-by-year chronicle of Egyptian history from the Arab conquest through his own era. He also wrote al-Manhal al-Safi, a biographical dictionary of Mamluk figures. His detailed records make him an invaluable source for Mamluk history.
bio_id: Ibn Taghribirdi (812–874 H) adalah sejarawan Mesir era Mamluk yang al-Nujum al-Zahirah-nya mencatat sejarah Mesir tahun per tahun.

bio_short_en: Swiss-Egyptian Islamic scholar and academic. Controversial figure who has written on Islam and Western Muslim identity.
bio_en: Tariq Ramadan (born 1382 AH / 1962 CE) is a Swiss-Egyptian Islamic scholar and academic, grandson of Hasan al-Banna (founder of the Muslim Brotherhood). He was a professor of Contemporary Islamic Studies at the University of Oxford and has written extensively on Muslim identity in the West, Islamic reform, and ethics. His major works include Western Muslims and the Future of Islam and Radical Reform. Note: He was suspended from academic positions following serious allegations of misconduct; his academic contributions are noted while acknowledging the controversies surrounding him.
bio_id: Tariq Ramadan (lahir 1382 H) adalah akademisi Islam Swiss-Mesir yang menulis tentang identitas Muslim Barat.
works: Western Muslims and the Future of Islam

bio_short_en: Lebanese-American scholar specializing in Islamic biographical literature and hadith sciences.
bio_en: Gibril Fouad Haddad (born 1379 AH / 1960 CE) is a Lebanese-American Islamic scholar specializing in hadith sciences and Islamic biographical literature. He studied under Sheikh Muhammad al-Yaqoubi and other traditional scholars. He has written numerous books on Islamic scholarship, including biographies of major scholars and works on Islamic theology. He is the author of The Four Imams, Sunna Notes (on hadith and practice), and many translations of classical Islamic texts. He teaches Islamic sciences and is known for his work preserving and transmitting traditional Islamic scholarship.
bio_id: Gibril Fouad Haddad (lahir 1379 H) adalah ulama Lebanon-Amerika yang mengkhususkan diri dalam literatur biografi Islam.

bio_short_en: Pre-Islamic poet who converted to Islam and wrote the famous qasida Banat Su'ad in praise of the Prophet ﷺ.
bio_en: Ka'b ibn Zuhayr ibn Abi Sulma (died approximately 26 AH) was a pre-Islamic Arab poet who eventually converted to Islam. He was initially hostile to the Prophet ﷺ and wrote poetry mocking him. Later, when he feared execution, he came to the Prophet ﷺ and recited his famous ode Banat Su'ad (Su'ad has departed), praising the Prophet ﷺ. The Prophet ﷺ was so pleased that he draped his mantle (burda) over Ka'b's shoulders. This poem, known as al-Burda (the Mantle), became one of the most celebrated poems in Arabic literature and sparked a tradition of prophetic panegyric poetry that continues to this day.
bio_id: Ka'b ibn Zuhayr adalah penyair Arab yang memeluk Islam dan menulis qasida Banat Su'ad yang terkenal.

bio_short_en: Tabi'iyya scholar who was the most important transmitter of Aisha's knowledge. Ibn Shihab al-Zuhri considered her a reliable ocean of knowledge.
bio_en: Amra bint Abd al-Rahman al-Ansariyya (25–98 AH) was one of the most respected female scholars of the Tabi'un generation. She was raised in the household of Aisha bint Abi Bakr and became her most important female student. Ibn Shihab al-Zuhri (the greatest hadith scholar of the Tabi'un) said about her: 'Amra is a vast ocean [of knowledge] that never dries up.' Umar ibn Abd al-Aziz specifically directed scholars to go to Amra to learn the hadith of Aisha. Her narrations form a major portion of the hadiths attributed to Aisha in the major hadith collections.
bio_id: Amra bint Abd al-Rahman (25–98 H) adalah ulama wanita Tabi'iyah dan transmiter paling penting pengetahuan Aisha.

bio_short_en: Imam of Khorasan in Shafi'i fiqh and usul al-fiqh. Pioneer of systematic legal methodology in the East.
bio_en: Muhammad ibn Ali ibn Ismail al-Qaffal al-Shashi (291–365 AH) was the Imam of Khorasan and the leading Shafi'i scholar of the Eastern Islamic world in his time. Born in Tashkent (Shash), he traveled to Baghdad to study under the major Shafi'i scholars and then became the foremost authority of his school in Central Asia and Persia. He wrote al-Malhuz fi al-Fiqh (the Abridged) which became a standard Shafi'i text, and a groundbreaking work on usul al-fiqh called Mahasin al-Shari'ah. He is credited with founding the eastern branch of the Shafi'i school.
bio_id: al-Qaffal al-Shashi (291–365 H) adalah Imam Khurasan dalam fiqh Syafi'i yang merintis metodologi hukum Islam di Timur.

bio_short_en: Leading Hanafi jurist of Baghdad. His Ahkam al-Quran is the primary Hanafi commentary on Quranic legal verses.
bio_en: Ahmad ibn Ali Abu Bakr al-Razi al-Jassas al-Hanafi (305–370 AH) was the leading Hanafi jurist of Baghdad in his era and the imam of the Hanafi school in Iraq. His major work Ahkam al-Quran (Rulings of the Quran) is the most comprehensive Hanafi commentary on the legal verses of the Quran and remains the primary reference for Hanafi interpretation of Quranic law. He also wrote Usul al-Fiqh (principles of Islamic jurisprudence) which was foundational in Hanafi legal methodology. He was a student of Abu al-Hasan al-Karkhi and transmitted the Hanafi tradition to later generations.
bio_id: al-Jassas (305–370 H) adalah ahli fiqh Hanafi terkemuka Baghdad yang Ahkam al-Quran-nya adalah tafsir hukum Hanafi utama.

bio_short_en: Towering Hanbali scholar of Damascus whose mastery of Islamic sciences and bold refutation of innovations made him uniquely influential.
bio_en: Taqi al-Din Ahmad ibn Abd al-Halim ibn Abd al-Salam ibn Taymiyya al-Harrani al-Dimashqi (661–728 AH) was a Syrian Hanbali scholar considered one of the most influential Islamic thinkers of all time. Born in Harran, he moved to Damascus where he studied under the leading scholars and produced an astonishing volume of scholarship across every Islamic discipline. He engaged in theological debates with Ash'aris, Sufis, Shi'a, and philosophers from within a strict Athari/Hanbali framework, arguing for direct return to Quran, Sunnah, and the way of the Salaf. He led the Muslims of Damascus during the Mongol invasions. He was imprisoned multiple times for his views on theological matters. His student Ibn al-Qayyim spread and developed his teachings. His work on aqeedah, usul al-fiqh, politics, and refutation of deviations is foundational in Salafi and many Sunni traditions.
bio_id: Ibn Taymiyya (661–728 H) adalah ulama Hanbali Suriah yang pengaruhnya luar biasa dalam pemikiran Islam.
works: al-Furqan bayna Awliya al-Rahman wa-Awliya al-Shaytan

bio_short_en: Grandson of the Prophet ﷺ and son of Ali and Fatimah. Briefly led the caliphate before abdicating to preserve Muslim unity.
bio_en: al-Hasan ibn Ali ibn Abi Talib (3–50 AH) was the grandson of the Prophet Muhammad ﷺ and the first son of Ali ibn Abi Talib and Fatimah al-Zahra. He is revered as one of the leaders of the youth of Paradise, as the Prophet ﷺ reportedly said. After the assassination of his father Ali in 40 AH, al-Hasan was acclaimed as caliph by the Kufans. However, to prevent further Muslim bloodshed, he negotiated an agreement with Mu'awiyah ibn Abi Sufyan and abdicated the caliphate after approximately six months — an act that fulfilled the Prophet's prophecy that al-Hasan would bring reconciliation between two large groups of Muslims. He retired to Madinah where he lived until his death.
bio_id: al-Hasan ibn Ali (3–50 H) adalah cucu Nabi ﷺ yang mengundurkan diri dari kekhalifahan untuk menjaga persatuan Muslim.

bio_short_en: Famous female saint and ascetic of Basra known for her poetry on divine love.
bio_en: Rabi'a al-Adawiyya al-Qaysiyya al-Basriyya (95–185 AH) was one of the most famous early female mystics and ascetics in Islamic history. Born as a slave in Basra, she was eventually freed and became renowned for her intense worship, asceticism, and spiritual poetry. She is famous for her concept of divine love — loving God for His own sake, not for fear of Hell or hope of Paradise. Her sayings and poetry are widely quoted in Islamic spiritual literature. Note: Rabi'a is an important historical figure; her emphasis on sincere worship and love of God is uncontroversially pious. The attribution of specific theological positions on sainthood to her should be read carefully within historical context.
bio_id: Rabi'a al-Adawiyya (95–185 H) adalah wali wanita terkenal dari Basra yang dikenal karena puisi cinta ilahi-nya.

bio_short_en: One of the major hadith scholars of Kufa. His al-Musannaf is one of the largest hadith collections with approximately 40,000 hadiths.
bio_en: Abu Bakr Abdallah ibn Muhammad ibn Abi Shayba al-Absi al-Kufi (159–235 AH) was one of the leading hadith scholars of the classical period. His al-Musannaf (Encyclopedia of Hadith arranged by topic) is one of the largest hadith compilations, containing approximately 40,000 hadith and athar (companion narrations), and is a primary source for Islamic legal and historical hadith. He was the teacher of al-Bukhari, Muslim, Ahmad ibn Hanbal, Ibn Majah, and Abu Dawud — making him one of the most important links in the hadith transmission chain. He is considered a major reference in hadith sciences.
bio_id: Ibn Abi Shayba (159–235 H) adalah ulama hadith terkemuka Kufah yang al-Musannaf-nya berisi sekitar 40.000 hadith.

bio_short_en: Tabi'i companion of the Prophet's companions, tribal leader of Basra, and exemplar of forbearance (hilm).
bio_en: al-Dahhak ibn Qays, known as al-Ahnaf ibn Qays al-Tamimi al-Basri (died approximately 67 AH), was one of the most respected leaders among the Tabi'un. He met and spoke with the Prophet ﷺ as a child, making him a junior companion or senior Tabi'i depending on the account. He led the tribe of Banu Sa'd in Basra and participated in the Muslim conquest of Persia. He is universally cited in Arabic literature as the supreme example of hilm (forbearance/clemency) — the ability to overlook insults and maintain composure. His sayings on wisdom and leadership are preserved in classical Arabic literature.
bio_id: al-Ahnaf ibn Qays (wafat 67 H) adalah pemimpin suku Basra dari generasi Tabi'in yang terkenal karena kesabarannya.

bio_short_en: The closest student of al-Shafi'i who inherited his teaching circle in Egypt. Died in prison for his refusal to accept the Mu'tazilite position.
bio_en: Abu Yaqub Yusuf ibn Yahya al-Qurashi al-Misri, known as al-Buwayti (160–231 AH), was the foremost student of Imam al-Shafi'i in Egypt and his appointed successor in transmitting his school. When al-Shafi'i died, al-Buwayti led his teaching circle in Egypt. He was imprisoned during the Mihna (the Mu'tazilite inquisition) under Caliph al-Wathiq for refusing to declare that the Quran was created. He died in prison in Baghdad as a result of mistreatment, becoming one of the martyrs of the Sunni cause. He transmitted al-Shafi'i's school and his abridgment al-Mukhtasar is a foundational Shafi'i text.
bio_id: al-Buwayti (160–231 H) adalah murid terdekat al-Syafi'i di Mesir yang meninggal dalam penjara karena menolak pandangan Mu'tazilah.

bio_short_en: Most reliable transmitter of Imam al-Shafi'i's works. Without him, the Shafi'i school's texts would be largely lost.
bio_en: al-Rabi' ibn Sulayman al-Muradi al-Misri (174–270 AH) was the primary transmitter of Imam al-Shafi'i's books and the most reliable source for the Shafi'i school's texts. He narrated al-Shafi'i's major works including Kitab al-Umm (the comprehensive fiqh encyclopedia), al-Risala (the foundational usul al-fiqh text), and the Musnad. Without al-Rabi's transmission, many of al-Shafi'i's works would be unknown today. He served as the muezzin of the Great Mosque of Fustat in Egypt. Al-Shafi'i reportedly said about him: 'al-Rabi' is my ear' — meaning he was the one through whom others would hear al-Shafi'i's knowledge.
bio_id: al-Rabi' ibn Sulayman (174–270 H) adalah transmiter utama karya-karya Imam al-Syafi'i yang menjaga warisan ilmunya.

bio_short_en: Andalusian Maliki scholar and the primary transmitter of the Muwatta in al-Andalus.
bio_en: Yahya ibn Yahya al-Laythi al-Masmoudi al-Andalusi (152–234 AH) was a Berber scholar from al-Andalus who traveled to Madinah to study under Imam Malik ibn Anas. He is the primary transmitter of the Muwatta in al-Andalus and his recension (riwayah) of the Muwatta is the most widely circulated version today — the version studied worldwide is his recension. He served as a judge in Cordoba and was the leading figure in spreading Maliki fiqh throughout al-Andalus. His role in establishing the Maliki school in Spain and North Africa was foundational.
bio_id: Yahya ibn Yahya al-Laythi (152–234 H) adalah ulama Maliki Andalusia dan transmiter utama Muwatta di al-Andalus.

bio_short_en: Greatest scholar of Kufa in the Tabi'un era. Expert in law, hadith, and history with an extraordinary memory.
bio_en: Abu Amr Amir ibn Sharahil al-Sha'bi al-Kufi (17–103 AH) was the leading scholar of Kufa among the Tabi'un and one of the most important scholars of the second generation. He met and narrated from over 500 companions of the Prophet ﷺ. He was renowned for his extraordinary memory, said to have never reviewed a book twice. He was knowledgeable in fiqh, hadith, history, genealogy, and Arabic poetry. Caliph Abd al-Malik ibn Marwan employed him as an envoy to Byzantine Emperor Justinian II due to his brilliance. Ibrahim al-Nakha'i and al-Hasan al-Basri were his contemporaries. He taught Abu Hanifah among others.
bio_id: al-Sha'bi (17–103 H) adalah ulama terbesar Kufah di era Tabi'in yang bertemu lebih dari 500 sahabat.

bio_short_en: Mother of Aisha and Abd al-Rahman ibn Abi Bakr. Wife of Abu Bakr al-Siddiq.
bio_en: Umm Rumman bint Amir ibn Uwaymr al-Kinaniyya (died 6 AH) was a companion of the Prophet ﷺ and the mother of Aisha bint Abi Bakr and Abd al-Rahman ibn Abi Bakr. She was the wife of Abu Bakr al-Siddiq. When the scandal involving Aisha (hadith al-ifk) occurred during the expedition to Banu al-Mustaliq, Umm Rumman's response and Aisha's response are both recorded in the authentic accounts of this event. She narrated a small number of hadiths. The Prophet ﷺ is reported to have praised her after her death.
bio_id: Umm Rumman adalah ibu Aisha bint Abi Bakr dan istri Abu Bakr al-Siddiq.

bio_short_en: Greatest Arab geographer, who created the most accurate map of the world in the 12th century for King Roger II of Sicily.
bio_en: Abu Abd Allah Muhammad ibn Muhammad ibn Abd Allah ibn Idris al-Sharif al-Idrisi (493–560 AH) was an Arab geographer, cartographer, and botanist who worked under the patronage of King Roger II of Sicily. He is considered the greatest Arab geographer of the medieval period. His major work Nuzhat al-Mushtaq fi Ikhtiraq al-Afaq (also known as the Book of Roger or Tabula Rogeriana) contains a detailed description of the world along with one of the most accurate world maps of the medieval period. He spent 15 years compiling information from travelers and explorers to create this comprehensive geographic work. His maps were used by explorers for centuries.
bio_id: al-Idrisi (493–560 H) adalah ahli geografi Arab terbesar yang menciptakan peta dunia paling akurat di abad ke-12.
works: Nuzhat al-Mushtaq fi Ikhtiraq al-Afaq (Tabula Rogeriana)

bio_short_en: Greatest Arab physician after Ibn Sina. Discovered the pulmonary circulation of blood 300 years before William Harvey.
bio_en: Ala' al-Din Abu al-Hasan Ali ibn Abi al-Hazm al-Qurashi al-Dimashqi, known as Ibn al-Nafis (607–687 AH), was a Syrian physician who worked in Egypt and is considered the greatest Arab physician after Ibn Sina. His most important contribution was the discovery and correct description of the pulmonary (minor) circulation of blood — the circulation of blood between the heart and lungs — nearly 300 years before William Harvey's work in the West. He also wrote a commentary on Ibn Sina's Canon of Medicine and was a Shafi'i jurist who wrote on Islamic law and philosophy. He was the chief physician at the Mansuri Hospital in Cairo.
bio_id: Ibn al-Nafis (607–687 H) adalah dokter Arab terbesar setelah Ibn Sina yang menemukan sirkulasi paru sebelum Harvey.

bio_short_en: The most famous Abbasid Caliph, during whose reign Baghdad became the most prosperous city in the world.
bio_en: Harun ibn Muhammad al-Mahdi al-Abbasi, known as Harun al-Rashid (148–193 AH), was the fifth Abbasid Caliph and the most famous ruler of the Abbasid golden age. Under his reign, Baghdad became the most prosperous and culturally vibrant city in the world. He patronized arts, scholarship, and learning, sponsoring the translation movement that brought Greek science and philosophy into Arabic. Imam al-Shafi'i and Imam Ahmad ibn Hanbal lived during his reign. He conducted annual Hajj journeys and fought against the Byzantines. The tales of the Arabian Nights (Alf Layla wa-Layla) are set in his court, though they are fictional. His reign is considered the peak of Abbasid civilization.
bio_id: Harun al-Rashid (148–193 H) adalah Khalifah Abbasiyah paling terkenal yang pada masanya Baghdad menjadi kota paling makmur.

bio_short_en: Founder of Baghdad and second Abbasid Caliph. He commissioned the first major Islamic translation movement and founded the Bayt al-Hikmah.
bio_en: Abu Jafar Abd Allah ibn Muhammad al-Abbasi, known as al-Mansur (95–158 AH), was the second Abbasid Caliph and the true consolidator of Abbasid power. He founded Baghdad (Madinat al-Salam) in 145 AH and made it the Abbasid capital. He patronized scholarship, commissioning translations of Persian, Greek, and Indian texts into Arabic — the beginning of the Islamic translation movement. He also built the Bayt al-Hikmah (House of Wisdom) which became the center of Islamic learning. He met with Imam Malik ibn Anas and urged him to compile the Muwatta as a standard legal code. He is credited with establishing the administrative and financial foundations of the Abbasid state.
bio_id: al-Mansur (95–158 H) adalah Khalifah Abbasiyah kedua dan pendiri Baghdad yang mensponsori gerakan terjemahan pertama Islam.

bio_short_en: Sultan of Egypt and Syria who recaptured Jerusalem in 1187 CE. Known equally for his military genius and his chivalry.
bio_en: Salah al-Din Yusuf ibn Ayyub al-Kurdi (532–589 AH / 1137–1193 CE), known in the West as Saladin, was the Sultan of Egypt and Syria and the founder of the Ayyubid dynasty. Born to a Kurdish family in Tikrit (modern Iraq), he rose through the ranks under Nur al-Din Zangi. He united Egypt and Syria under his rule and launched the campaign to recapture Jerusalem, which fell to the Crusaders in 1099 CE. On 27 Rajab 583 AH (2 October 1187 CE), his forces entered Jerusalem — ending 88 years of Crusader occupation. He was known for his magnanimity toward defeated enemies. He was a Sunni Muslim who suppressed the Fatimid Shi'a caliphate in Egypt and restored Sunni rule.
bio_id: Salah al-Din al-Ayyubi (532–589 H) adalah Sultan Mesir dan Suriah yang merebut kembali Yerusalem dari tentara Salib.

bio_short_en: Ruler of Syria who united the Levant against the Crusaders and paved the way for Saladin.
bio_en: Mahmud ibn Zangi ibn Aqsunqur, known as Nur al-Din (Light of the Religion) (511–569 AH / 1118–1174 CE), was the Seljuk governor who united Muslim Syria against the Crusaders. He captured Edessa, Antioch's surroundings, and much of the Levant. He was known as a just and pious ruler who built mosques, madrasas, and hospitals across Syria. His vizier Shirkuh and nephew Saladin (later) completed his work in Egypt. He is credited with creating the unified Muslim state that eventually enabled Saladin to recapture Jerusalem. He built the Nur al-Din hospital (bimaristan) in Damascus, one of the most advanced medical facilities of the medieval world.
bio_id: Nur al-Din Zangi (511–569 H) adalah penguasa Suriah yang mempersatukan kaum Muslim melawan tentara Salib.

bio_short_en: Mamluk Sultan who decisively defeated the Mongols at Ain Jalut and expelled the remaining Crusaders from the Levant.
bio_en: al-Malik al-Zahir Rukn al-Din Baybars al-Bunduqdari (625–676 AH / 1223–1277 CE) was the fourth Mamluk Sultan of Egypt and one of the most important rulers in medieval Islamic history. A Turkic slave-soldier who rose to become sultan, he is best remembered for two historic achievements: decisively defeating the Mongols at the Battle of Ain Jalut (658 AH / 1260 CE), which halted the Mongol advance into the Islamic heartland; and systematically dismantling the remaining Crusader states in the Levant. He also established postal relay systems across Egypt and Syria, promoted trade, and revived the Abbasid caliphate in Cairo after the Mongols destroyed Baghdad.
bio_id: al-Zahir Baybars (625–676 H) adalah Sultan Mamluk yang mengalahkan Mongol di Ain Jalut dan mengusir tentara Salib.

bio_short_en: Greatest historian of Mamluk Egypt. His Khitat is an encyclopedic topographical and historical description of Egypt.
bio_en: Ahmad ibn Ali ibn Abd al-Qadir al-Husayni al-Ubaydi al-Maqrizi (766–845 AH) was one of the greatest Islamic historians of the medieval period and the foremost historian of Mamluk Egypt. He served in various administrative positions before devoting himself entirely to scholarship. His major work al-Mawa'iz wa-al-I'tibar fi Dhikr al-Khitat wa-al-Athar (known as al-Khitat) is an encyclopedic topographical and historical survey of Egypt, covering its geography, monuments, buildings, religious sites, and history. He also wrote Itti'az al-Hunafa' (history of the Fatimid caliphate) and al-Suluk (a Mamluk history). He is an indispensable source for the history of medieval Egypt.
bio_id: al-Maqrizi (766–845 H) adalah sejarawan terbesar Mesir Mamluk yang al-Khitat-nya adalah survei ensiklopedik Mesir.

bio_short_en: Sabian mathematician and astronomer from Harran who made major contributions to geometry and number theory.
bio_en: Thabit ibn Qurra ibn Marwan al-Harrani al-Sabi' (221–288 AH / 836–901 CE) was a Sabian mathematician, physician, and astronomer from Harran (modern Turkey) who worked in Baghdad. Though a Sabian (not Muslim), he is a significant figure in Islamic intellectual history due to his contributions to the translation movement and original research. He translated Greek mathematical and astronomical texts including works of Euclid, Archimedes, Apollonius, and Ptolemy from Greek and Syriac into Arabic, making them available to Muslim scholars. His original contributions to mathematics include work on amicable numbers and non-Euclidean geometry concepts. He also wrote on medicine and astronomy.
bio_id: Thabit ibn Qurra (221–288 H) adalah matematikawan dan astronom dari Harran yang memberikan kontribusi besar pada geometri.

bio_short_en: Leading Maliki scholar of al-Andalus. His Ahkam al-Quran is the major Maliki commentary on Quranic legal verses.
bio_en: Abu Bakr Muhammad ibn Abd Allah ibn Muhammad ibn al-Arabi al-Ishbili al-Maliki (468–543 AH) was the leading Maliki jurist of al-Andalus of his era. Not to be confused with the Sufi mystic Ibn Arabi (560–638 AH), this Ibn al-Arabi was a strict Sunni Maliki scholar. He traveled to the East and studied under al-Ghazali in Baghdad and other leading scholars. His major works include Ahkam al-Quran (Rulings of the Quran from a Maliki perspective), Aridat al-Ahwadhi (commentary on Sahih al-Tirmidhi), and al-Qabs (commentary on Muwatta). He served as a judge in Seville.
bio_id: Ibn al-Arabi al-Maliki (468–543 H) adalah ahli fiqh Maliki terkemuka Andalusia, berbeda dari Ibn Arabi sang Sufi.

bio_short_en: Companion and brother of Ubadah ibn al-Samit. His zihar divorce case led to the revelation of Surah al-Mujadila.
bio_en: Aws ibn al-Samit ibn Qays al-Ansari al-Khazraji (died approximately 30 AH) was a companion of the Prophet ﷺ and brother of Ubadah ibn al-Samit. He is significant primarily because of the incident that led to the revelation of Surah al-Mujadila (The Pleading Woman). His wife Khawla bint Tha'laba complained to the Prophet ﷺ that Aws had pronounced zihar (a pre-Islamic form of divorce) against her, and the Quran revealed the rulings on this in 58:1-4. He is counted among the Ansari companions.
bio_id: Aws ibn al-Samit adalah sahabat yang kasus zihar-nya memicu turunnya Surah al-Mujadilah.

bio_short_en: Zahirite scholar of al-Andalus. His al-Muhalla is the encyclopedic fiqh text of the Zahiri school, and his al-Fisal is major comparative religion.
bio_en: Abu Muhammad Ali ibn Ahmad ibn Said ibn Hazm al-Andalusi al-Qurtubi (384–456 AH) was a scholar of al-Andalus known for his mastery of Islamic law, theology, and philosophy. A Zahirite (literalist) in fiqh, he wrote al-Muhalla bil-Athar, the most comprehensive encyclopedia of Zahirite jurisprudence. He also wrote al-Fisal fi al-Milal wa-al-Ahwa' wa-al-Nihal (Decisive Views on Religious Sects), a pioneering work of comparative religion. His Tawq al-Hamamah (Ring of the Dove) is a celebrated work on the nature of love. Despite controversy over some of his polemical positions, his scholarship is universally acknowledged. Note: He was distinct from Ibn al-Arabi the Maliki jurist of Seville.
bio_id: Ibn Hazm al-Andalusi (384–456 H) adalah ulama Zahiri Andalusia yang al-Muhalla-nya adalah ensiklopedi fiqh Zahiri.

bio_short_en: Companion who narrated from the Prophet ﷺ in many important hadith, including the description of Isra' and Mi'raj.
bio_en: al-Bara' ibn Azib ibn al-Harith al-Ansari al-Khazraji (died approximately 71 AH) was a companion of the Prophet ﷺ who participated in many battles and narrated a significant number of hadiths. He is a primary narrator of key hadiths including those on the Night Journey (Isra'), the questioning of the grave, and the description of the Prophet ﷺ. He was an Ansari from Madinah and was initially too young for Badr but participated in subsequent battles. He died in Kufa. His hadiths appear in all major collections.
bio_id: al-Bara' ibn Azib (wafat 71 H) adalah sahabat Anshari yang meriwayatkan hadith penting tentang Isra' dan alam kubur.

bio_short_en: Companion born in Madinah during the Prophet's lifetime. Known for narrating the hadith 'The halal is clear and the haram is clear.'
bio_en: al-Nu'man ibn Bashir ibn Sa'd al-Ansari al-Khazraji (2–65 AH) was a companion of the Prophet ﷺ, born in Madinah during the Prophet's lifetime and the first Ansar child named by the Prophet ﷺ. He is known for narrating several important hadiths including the famous hadith 'The halal is clear and the haram is clear, and between them are doubtful matters' (Bukhari and Muslim). He served as governor of Kufa and Homs under Muawiyah ibn Abi Sufyan. He was killed during the political turbulence of the Second Fitna.
bio_id: al-Nu'man ibn Bashir (2–65 H) adalah sahabat yang lahir di Madinah semasa Nabi ﷺ dan meriwayatkan hadith halal-haram yang terkenal.

bio_short_en: Companion who narrated the famous hadith on the Thaqalayn (Two Weighty Things: Quran and Ahl al-Bayt).
bio_en: Zayd ibn Arqam ibn Zayd al-Ansari al-Khazraji (died approximately 65 AH) was a companion of the Prophet ﷺ. He participated in many battles with the Prophet ﷺ including Khandaq. He is perhaps best known as the primary narrator of the Hadith al-Thaqalayn — the Prophet's statement at Ghadir Khumm: 'I am leaving among you two weighty things: the Book of Allah and my Ahl al-Bayt.' This hadith is in Sahih Muslim and is considered authentic by all Muslims. He narrated many other hadiths and lived in Kufa.
bio_id: Zayd ibn Arqam (wafat 65 H) adalah sahabat Anshari yang meriwayatkan hadith Thaqalayn yang penting.

bio_short_en: Female companion renowned for her knowledge of the rulings on washing the dead and participating in campaigns.
bio_en: Nusayba bint al-Harith al-Ansariyya, known as Umm Atiyya (died approximately 30 AH), was a female companion of the Prophet ﷺ from the Ansar of Madinah. She is one of the most frequently cited female authorities on practical Islamic matters, particularly the rulings on washing the dead (ghusl al-mayyit) and menstruation. She participated in military campaigns alongside the Prophet ﷺ, tending to the wounded and treating the sick. Her hadiths appear in Sahih al-Bukhari and Sahih Muslim, and she is a key narrator for the rulings related to women's worship.
bio_id: Umm Atiyya al-Ansariyya adalah sahabat wanita yang menjadi otoritas utama dalam hukum memandikan jenazah.

bio_short_en: Female warrior companion who protected the Prophet ﷺ at the Battle of Uhud with her sword and bow.
bio_en: Nusayba bint Ka'b al-Ansariyya al-Maziniyya, also known as Umm Umara (died approximately 13 AH), was one of the most celebrated female companions of the Prophet ﷺ for her physical bravery. She was among the women of the Second Aqabah pledge. At the Battle of Uhud, when many companions fled, she remained beside the Prophet ﷺ and fought to protect him with her sword and bow, receiving twelve wounds. The Prophet ﷺ reportedly said: 'I did not look to the right or left at Uhud except that I saw Nusayba fighting to protect me.' She also participated in other campaigns and lost a son in battle.
bio_id: Nusayba bint Ka'b (Umm Umara) adalah sahabat wanita pemberani yang melindungi Nabi ﷺ di Perang Uhud.

bio_short_en: Early female Muslim from Makkah who worked secretly to convert Qurayshi women to Islam.
bio_en: Ghuzayyah bint Jabir al-Qurayshiyya, known as Umm Sharik (died approximately 25 AH), was among the earliest Muslim women. Born in Makkah into the Quraysh, she secretly worked to convert other Qurayshi women to Islam during the early Makkan period, risking her life to spread the message. When discovered, she was tortured. She later emigrated to Madinah. She was known for her generosity and hosted guests extensively. She narrated hadiths from the Prophet ﷺ and is counted among the companions.
bio_id: Umm Sharik adalah sahabat wanita awal yang bekerja secara rahasia mengajak wanita Quraysh masuk Islam.

bio_short_en: Moroccan historian of al-Andalus. His Nafh al-Tib is the most comprehensive history of Islamic Spain and its scholars.
bio_en: Ahmad ibn Muhammad ibn Ahmad al-Maqqari al-Tilimisani (986–1041 AH / 1578–1632 CE) was a Moroccan-Algerian scholar and historian who wrote the most important historical work on Islamic Spain. His Nafh al-Tib min Ghusn al-Andalus al-Ratib (The Fragrant Breeze from the Moist Branch of al-Andalus) is an encyclopedic history of Islamic Spain covering its scholars, poets, rulers, and cultural achievements. Born in Tlemcen (Algeria), he worked in Morocco and Egypt and is considered the last great historian of al-Andalus. The work is invaluable for understanding Andalusian Islamic civilization.
bio_id: al-Maqqari (986–1041 H) adalah sejarawan Maroko yang Nafh al-Tib-nya adalah sejarah paling lengkap tentang Spanyol Islam.
works: Nafh al-Tib min Ghusn al-Andalus al-Ratib

bio_short_en: Andalusian Maliki scholar whose al-Muharrar al-Wajiz is the most scholarly Arabic-language Quranic commentary of al-Andalus.
bio_en: Abu Muhammad Abd al-Haqq ibn Ghalib ibn Atiyya al-Gharnatí al-Andalusí (480–541 AH) was a Maliki scholar and judge from Granada. His tafsir al-Muharrar al-Wajiz fi Tafsir al-Kitab al-Aziz is considered the most scholarly and well-organized Quranic commentary produced in al-Andalus, notable for its careful evaluation of narrations, grammatical analysis, and comparative jurisprudence. Ibn Khaldun praised it as superior to al-Zamakhshari's tafsir in methodology. He served as a judge and was known for combining legal expertise with encyclopedic knowledge of hadith and tafsir.
bio_id: Ibn Atiyya al-Andalusi (480–541 H) adalah ulama Maliki Granada yang al-Muharrar al-Wajiz-nya adalah tafsir Quran terbaik di al-Andalus.
works: al-Muharrar al-Wajiz fi Tafsir al-Kitab al-Aziz

bio_short_en: Syrian Hanafi scholar and hadith expert known for his biographical work and teaching of the Islamic sciences.
bio_en: Abd al-Fattah ibn Muhammad ibn Bishir Abu Ghudda al-Halabi al-Hanafi (1336–1417 AH / 1917–1997 CE) was a Syrian Hanafi scholar of Aleppo who became one of the foremost Islamic scholars of the 20th century. He studied under major scholars of Syria and Egypt and was a leading authority in hadith and Islamic biography. He is best known for his extensive critical editions and annotations of classical Islamic texts, bringing previously inaccessible works to a wider audience. His annotations on ibn al-Salah's Muqaddimah and other works are particularly valued. He taught in Saudi Arabia after leaving Syria and trained many students.
bio_id: Abd al-Fattah Abu Ghudda (1336–1417 H) adalah ulama Hanafi Suriah yang terkenal karena edisi kritis teks-teks Islam klasiknya.

bio_short_en: Syrian Islamic scholar, judge, and writer known for his accessible and eloquent Islamic literature.
bio_en: Ali ibn Mustafa al-Tantawi al-Dimashqi (1327–1420 AH / 1909–1999 CE) was a Syrian Shafi'i scholar, judge, and prolific writer. He served as a judge in Syria and later in Saudi Arabia. He is best known for his accessible and beautifully written Islamic literature — his Dhikrayat (Memoirs), Rijal min al-Ta'rikh (Men of History), and his works on Islamic jurisprudence and biography. He had a widely watched television program and his writing style influenced many contemporary Islamic writers. He was known for combining deep scholarship with literary excellence.
bio_id: Ali al-Tantawi (1327–1420 H) adalah ulama Islam Suriah, hakim, dan penulis yang dikenal atas karya sastra Islamnya yang mudah dipahami.

bio_short_en: Maliki scholar of Granada whose al-Muwafaqat is the foundational text on Maqasid al-Shariah (objectives of Islamic law).
bio_en: Abu Ishaq Ibrahim ibn Musa ibn Muhammad al-Lakhmi al-Gharnati al-Shatibi (720–790 AH) was a Maliki scholar from Granada who wrote the most influential work on the theory of Maqasid al-Shariah (the higher objectives of Islamic law). His al-Muwafaqat fi Usul al-Shariah (Consonances in the Foundations of Islamic Law) systematically developed the theory that Islamic law has overarching objectives — the preservation of religion, life, intellect, lineage, and wealth — and that rulings should be understood in light of these objectives. This theory has become foundational in modern Islamic legal theory. He also wrote al-I'tisam, a comprehensive work on bid'ah.
bio_id: al-Shatibi (720–790 H) adalah ulama Maliki Granada yang al-Muwafaqat-nya adalah teks dasar Maqasid al-Syariah.

bio_short_en: Renowned ascetic and scholar of Makkah. Former highway robber who repented and became one of the most pious Muslims of his era.
bio_en: Fudayl ibn Iyad ibn Masud al-Tamimi al-Khurasani (105–187 AH) was one of the most famous ascetics and scholars of his era. Born in Khorasan, he reportedly led a group of bandits in his youth before experiencing a moment of spiritual transformation when he heard Quranic recitation, leading to his complete repentance. He relocated to Makkah where he became one of the leading scholars, sitting with Sufyan ibn Uyaynah and teaching Imam al-Shafi'i. He was known for his extreme piety, weeping, and powerful exhortations on the Day of Judgment. Harun al-Rashid visited him and was moved by his directness.
bio_id: Fudayl ibn Iyad (105–187 H) adalah orang saleh dan ulama terkenal yang bertaubat dari menjadi perampok jalanan.

bio_short_en: Scholar of Khurasan combining hadith, fiqh, asceticism, and jihad. Called the prince of the believers in hadith.
bio_en: Abd Allah ibn al-Mubarak al-Marwazi (118–181 AH) was one of the most remarkable scholars of the Islamic classical period, combining mastery of hadith, fiqh, Arabic literature, and physical jihad. Born to a Turkish father and Khorasani mother, he studied under Imam Malik, Sufyan al-Thawri, and other leading scholars, and himself became a master of hadith. He is said to have been the first to compile a book specifically on the topic of jihad. He divided his time between scholarship and jihad on the frontier with the Byzantines. He was called 'the Prince of the Believers in hadith' by his contemporaries.
bio_id: Abd Allah ibn al-Mubarak (118–181 H) adalah ulama Khurasan yang memadukan hadith, fiqh, kezuhudan, dan jihad.

bio_short_en: One of the Ten Promised Paradise. Shielded the Prophet ﷺ with his own body at Uhud, earning the title 'Talha the Good.'
bio_en: Talha ibn Ubaydallah ibn Uthman al-Taymi al-Qurashi (died 36 AH) was one of the Ten Companions Promised Paradise (al-Asharah al-Mubashsharun) and among the earliest converts to Islam. At the Battle of Uhud, he sustained severe wounds including paralysis of his hand while shielding the Prophet ﷺ with his own body, earning the title 'Talha the Generous' and 'Talha the Good.' The Prophet ﷺ said he was destined for Paradise after this act. He was one of the six shura council members under Umar. He was killed at the Battle of the Camel (36 AH) in which he participated alongside Aisha and al-Zubayr.
bio_id: Talha ibn Ubaydallah (wafat 36 H) adalah salah satu Sepuluh yang dijanjikan surga yang melindungi Nabi ﷺ di Uhud.

bio_short_en: One of the first seven Muslims and the only horseman at Badr. His pledge before Badr is one of the most famous companion statements.
bio_en: al-Miqdad ibn Amr ibn Tha'labah al-Bahrani (died 33 AH), known as al-Miqdad ibn al-Aswad, was one of the first seven persons to publicly declare Islam and the only horseman at the Battle of Badr. Before Badr, when the Prophet ﷺ consulted his companions, al-Miqdad declared: 'O Messenger of Allah, go where your Lord has commanded you and we will be with you. By Allah, we will not say as the Children of Israel said to Moses: Go you and your Lord and fight, while we remain sitting here. Rather, we will fight on your right and your left, in front of you and behind you.' This pledge is preserved in both al-Bukhari and Muslim.
bio_id: al-Miqdad ibn Amr adalah salah satu tujuh Muslim pertama dan satu-satunya penunggang kuda di Badar.

bio_short_en: Hadith scholar of Kufa. His Kitab al-Kharaj is one of the earliest works on Islamic fiscal policy.
bio_en: Yahya ibn Adam ibn Sulayman al-Qurashi al-Umawi (140–203 AH) was a hadith scholar from Kufa who studied under Sufyan al-Thawri, Sufyan ibn Uyaynah, and other leading scholars. He is best known for his Kitab al-Kharaj, one of the earliest systematic works on Islamic fiscal policy and taxation, which is an important source for the economic history of early Islam. He also compiled works on hadith and fiqh. Ahmad ibn Hanbal narrated from him and praised his knowledge.
bio_id: Yahya ibn Adam (140–203 H) adalah ulama hadith Kufah yang Kitab al-Kharaj-nya adalah salah satu karya tertua tentang kebijakan fiskal Islam.

bio_short_en: Most important hadith transmitter among the Tabi'un. He was commissioned by Umar ibn Abd al-Aziz to formally record hadith.
bio_en: Muhammad ibn Muslim ibn Ubaydallah ibn Abd Allah ibn Shihab al-Zuhri (51–124 AH) was universally regarded as the most important hadith scholar among the Tabi'un. He narrated from over 200 companions and companion-students, and his hadiths form the backbone of the six canonical collections. By order of Caliph Umar ibn Abd al-Aziz (the 'fifth rightly guided caliph'), he undertook the first formal collection of hadith on a large scale — the first time hadith was officially written down in a systematic way. This is why his role in preserving the Prophetic tradition cannot be overstated. He was noted for his mastery of seerah, genealogy, and law alongside hadith.
bio_id: al-Zuhri (51–124 H) adalah transmiter hadith terpenting di antara Tabi'in yang diperintahkan menulis hadith secara resmi.

bio_short_en: Greatest Islamic scholar of 20th century India. His Bahishti Zewar is the most widely read Islamic guide for South Asian Muslims.
bio_en: Ashraf Ali al-Thanawi al-Imdadi al-Chishti (1280–1362 AH / 1863–1943 CE) was the most prolific and influential Islamic scholar of South Asia in the 20th century. Known as 'Hakim al-Ummah' (Physician of the Community) and 'Mujaddid al-Milla' (Reviver of the Community), he wrote over 1000 books and pamphlets. His most famous work, Bahishti Zewar (Heavenly Ornaments), is a comprehensive guide to Islamic practice for women that remains one of the most widely read Islamic books in South Asia. He was a Sufi shaykh of the Chishti-Imdadi order and was associated with Darul Uloom Deoband.
bio_id: Ashraf Ali Thanawi (1280–1362 H) adalah ulama Islam terbesar India abad ke-20 yang Bahishti Zewar-nya masih dibaca luas.

bio_short_en: Indian Islamic scholar whose 'What Has the World Lost due to the Decline of Muslims?' is one of the most widely read Islamic books globally.
bio_en: Ali ibn Abd al-Hayy al-Hasani, known as Abu al-Hasan al-Nadwi (1332–1420 AH / 1913–1999 CE), was one of the most influential Islamic scholars of the 20th century from India. President of the Islamic Academy in Lucknow (Nadwat al-Ulama), he wrote extensively in Arabic and Urdu on Islamic history, spirituality, and reform.
