His most influential work Madha Khasir al-Alam bi-Inhitat al-Muslimin (What Has the World Lost due to the Decline of Muslims?) translated into many languages and shaped the understanding of Islamic civilization's role in world history for millions of readers. He was a member of the Muslim World League's founding council.
bio_id: Abu al-Hasan al-Nadwi (1332–1420 H) adalah ulama India yang Madha Khasir al-Alam-nya dibaca jutaan orang.

bio_short_en: Deobandi scholar known for his works on Islamic economics and the history of Islamic philosophy in India.
bio_en: Manazir Ahsan Gilani al-Deobandi al-Hanafi (1310–1375 AH / 1892–1956 CE) was a scholar associated with Darul Uloom Deoband known for his scholarly contributions to Islamic intellectual history in India. His Islamiyat ma'ashiyyat (Islamic Economics) was one of the first systematic treatments of Islamic economic principles. He also wrote on the history of Islamic thought and education in India. He served as a teacher at Darul Uloom Deoband and Darul Uloom Hyderabad and contributed significantly to Islamic scholarship in Urdu.
bio_id: Manazir Ahsan Gilani (1310–1375 H) adalah ulama Deoband yang dikenal atas karya ekonomi Islam dan sejarah filsafat Islam.

bio_short_en: Founder of the Sanusiyya movement in Libya. Student of Ahmad ibn Idris who spread Islamic revival across North Africa.
bio_en: Muhammad ibn Ali al-Sanusi al-Khattabi al-Idrisi al-Hasani (1202–1276 AH / 1787–1859 CE) was a Moroccan-Algerian Islamic scholar who founded the Sanusiyya movement in Libya and the Sahara. He studied under Ahmad ibn Idris al-Fasi in Makkah and established his center in Jabal Akhdar in Cyrenaica (modern Libya). The Sanusiyya movement became a major force for Islamic revival and resistance to colonialism across Libya, Chad, Sudan, and the Sahara. His grandson Muhammad Idris became the first king of modern Libya. His scholarly works combined hadith methodology with spiritual development. Note: The Sanusiyya is classified as a tariqah; the founder's hadith scholarship is recognized.
bio_id: al-Sanusi (1202–1276 H) adalah pendiri gerakan Sanusiyya di Libya yang menyebarkan kebangkitan Islam di Afrika Utara.

bio_short_en: Khorasan Sufi scholar who compiled the earliest known Sufi tafsir of the Quran.
bio_en: Abu Abd al-Rahman Muhammad ibn al-Husayn al-Sulami al-Nisaburi was a Khorasan scholar known for his compilation of Haqa'iq al-Tafsir, an early compilation of spiritual and allegorical Quranic interpretations from Sufi figures. He was also the author of Tabaqat al-Sufiyya, an important biographical dictionary of early Sufi figures. His works require scholarly contextualisation as some esoteric interpretations diverge from mainstream Sunni tafsir.
bio_id: Al-Sulami adalah ulama Khorasan yang menyusun tafsir Sufi awal Al-Quran.

bio_short_en: Bukharan Hanafi scholar, author of al-Ta'arruf li-Madhhab Ahl al-Tasawwuf.
bio_en: Abu Bakr Muhammad ibn Ibrahim al-Kalabadhi al-Bukhari was a Hanafi scholar from Bukhara known for his work al-Ta'arruf li-Madhhab Ahl al-Tasawwuf, which attempts to show the compatibility of Sufi doctrine with orthodox Islamic theology. This work was influential in reconciling mystical tendencies within Sunni Islam. He represents a scholarly approach to evaluating early Sufi claims against the Quran and Sunnah.
bio_id: Al-Kalabadhi adalah ulama Hanafi dari Bukhara, penulis al-Ta'arruf li-Madhhab Ahl al-Tasawwuf.

bio_short_en: Prominent companion known for political acumen; governor of Basra and Kufa under Umar and Uthman.
bio_en: Al-Mughira ibn Shu'ba al-Thaqafi was a prominent Companion known for his sharp intelligence and political skill. He converted to Islam before the conquest of Khaybar and participated in many campaigns. Under Umar ibn al-Khattab, he served as governor of Basra and then Kufa. He was sent on diplomatic missions and was known for his shrewd negotiating abilities. He narrated approximately 136 hadiths.
bio_id: Al-Mughira ibn Shu'ba adalah sahabat terkemuka yang terkenal dengan kecerdasan politiknya.

bio_short_en: Prominent student of Ibn Taymiyya and Ibn Qayyim; author of al-Adab al-Shar'iyya.
bio_en: Shams al-Din Muhammad ibn Muflih al-Maqdisi al-Hanbali was a leading student of Ibn Taymiyya and Ibn al-Qayyim. He is best known for his comprehensive work al-Adab al-Shar'iyya wal-Minh al-Mar'iyya, a vast encyclopedia of Islamic manners and etiquette derived from hadith and legal sources. He also wrote al-Furu', a comprehensive Hanbali jurisprudence text. He was considered one of the best students of the Ibn Taymiyya-Ibn Qayyim tradition.
bio_id: Ibn Muflih al-Hanbali adalah murid terkemuka Ibn Taymiyya, penulis al-Adab al-Shar'iyya.

bio_short_en: Andalusian Maliki jurist who transmitted Malik's Muwatta to Spain; called 'the wise man of al-Andalus'.
bio_en: Abu Muhammad Yahya ibn Yahya al-Laythi al-Andalusi was the primary transmitter of Imam Malik's Muwatta in al-Andalus. He traveled to Medina and studied directly under Malik ibn Anas, and his transmission of the Muwatta became the most widespread version in the western Muslim world. He is credited with establishing the Maliki school as the dominant legal tradition in Spain and the Maghreb. He was called 'the sage of al-Andalus.'
bio_id: Yahya ibn Yahya al-Laythi adalah ahli fiqih Maliki Andalusia yang menyebarkan Muwatta di Spanyol.

bio_short_en: Khorasan Shafi'i scholar known for his tafsir Ma'alim al-Tanzil and the hadith collection Masabih al-Sunnah.
bio_en: Abu Muhammad al-Husayn ibn Masud al-Baghawi al-Farra was a prominent Khorasan scholar known for his Tafsir Ma'alim al-Tanzil (Signposts of Revelation), a highly regarded Sunni tafsir that relies primarily on authentic narrations from the Prophet and companions. He also compiled Masabih al-Sunnah, an important hadith collection. He was known as 'Reviver of the Sunnah' and 'Pillar of Religion' for his efforts in combating innovations in his region.
bio_id: Al-Baghawi adalah ulama Khorasan yang terkenal dengan tafsirnya Ma'alim al-Tanzil.

bio_short_en: Nigerian Islamic scholar and reformer who advocated for direct Quran-based guidance over traditional authority.
bio_en: Sheikh Abubakar Mahmud Gumi was one of the most influential Islamic scholars in Nigeria in the 20th century. He was known for his Quran commentary in Hausa (Rayyoyin Kur'ani) and his reformist stance challenging traditional hierarchies in northern Nigerian Islam. He advocated for direct engagement with the Quran and Sunnah over reliance on traditional religious authorities. His followers became known as 'Yan Izala' and his influence reshaped Islamic discourse in northern Nigeria.
bio_id: Abubakar Gumi adalah ulama dan reformis Islam Nigeria yang berpengaruh pada abad ke-20.

bio_short_en: Indian Deobandi scholar who spent years in Medina and opposed partition of India.
bio_en: Hussain Ahmad Madani was a prominent Indian Deobandi Islamic scholar who spent many years teaching in Medina, earning the title 'al-Madani.' He was a student of Mahmud al-Hasan (Shaykh al-Hind) and a senior teacher at Darul Uloom Deoband. He was politically active and controversially opposed the partition of India, arguing for composite Indian nationalism. His theological contributions and his impact on the Deobandi school in South Asia are widely acknowledged.
bio_id: Hussain Ahmad Madani adalah ulama Deobandi India yang menghabiskan bertahun-tahun di Madinah.

bio_short_en: East African Islamic scholar and founder of the Uwaysiyya order; spread Islam in Somalia and East Africa.
bio_en: Sheikh Uways ibn Muhammad al-Barawi was a Somali Islamic scholar who founded the Uwaysiyya Sufi order in East Africa. He was born in Barawa, Somalia and studied in Baghdad. He spread Islamic education throughout Somalia, Tanzania, Comoros and other East African countries through his network of students and scholars. He is considered a major figure in the Islamization of East Africa and is venerated by many East African Muslims.
bio_id: Uways al-Barawi adalah ulama Islam Afrika Timur yang mendirikan tarekat Uwaysiyya.

bio_short_en: Pakistani Islamic thinker and founder of Jamaat-e-Islami; controversial modernist-Islamist ideologue.
bio_en: Sayyid Abul A'la Mawdudi was a Pakistani Islamic scholar, journalist, and political thinker who founded the Jamaat-e-Islami in 1941. He developed a comprehensive Islamic political philosophy advocating for an Islamic state governed by divine law. His prolific writings, including his Quranic commentary Tafhim al-Quran, were widely influential in 20th century Islamist thought. Note: his views on Islamic governance, democracy, and jihad are debated among scholars, and he received criticism from traditional ulama on several theological issues.
bio_id: Sayyid Mawdudi adalah pemikir Islam Pakistan dan pendiri Jamaat-e-Islami.

bio_short_en: Companion who made the first female migration (hijra) from Mecca to Medina alone, prompting Quranic revelation.
bio_en: Umm Kulthum bint Uqba ibn Abi Muayt was a Companion of the Prophet known for her courageous solo hijra from Mecca to Medina. The Quraysh demanded her return as part of the Treaty of Hudaybiyya (which applied to men), but Allah revealed verses (60:10-11) distinguishing her case, establishing that female Muslim migrants need not be returned to disbelievers. She thus became the cause for important Quranic guidance on hijra and treaty obligations.
bio_id: Umm Kulthum bint Uqba adalah sahabat yang melakukan hijrah perempuan pertama sendirian dari Mekah ke Madinah.

bio_short_en: Genealogist and hadith scholar from Merv, author of al-Ansab (Genealogies).
bio_en: Abu Sa'd Abd al-Karim ibn Muhammad al-Sam'ani al-Marwazi was a Khorasan scholar known for his genealogical and biographical encyclopedias. His most important work, al-Ansab (Genealogies), is a comprehensive dictionary organized by the family names (nisba) of Islamic scholars and transmitters, providing crucial biographical information about thousands of individuals. He also authored Tarikh Marw about the scholars of Merv.
bio_id: Al-Sam'ani adalah ahli silsilah dan hadis dari Merv, penulis al-Ansab.

bio_short_en: Son of Abu Hatim al-Razi; major hadith critic and Quran commentator.
bio_en: Abu Muhammad Abd al-Rahman ibn Muhammad ibn Idris ibn al-Mundhir al-Razi, known as Ibn Abi Hatim, was a major hadith scholar and Quranic commentator from Rayy (modern Tehran). The son of the great hadith critic Abu Hatim al-Razi, he wrote extensively on hadith criticism in his al-Jarh wal-Ta'dil (Hadith Authentication), a multi-volume work assessing the reliability of thousands of hadith transmitters. He also authored a substantial Quranic commentary using hadith narrations.
bio_id: Ibn Abi Hatim al-Razi adalah kritikus hadis dan komentator Quran terkemuka dari Rayy.

bio_short_en: Companion commander who led the Muslim forces to victory at the decisive Battle of Nahavand.
bio_en: Al-Nu'man ibn Muqarrin al-Muzani was a Companion who converted to Islam early and participated in many campaigns. He was appointed by Caliph Umar ibn al-Khattab to command the Muslim forces at the Battle of Nihawand (642 CE/21 AH) — the decisive battle that effectively ended Sassanid Persian resistance. He was martyred in this battle, which Muslims called 'the Conquest of Conquests' (Fath al-Futuh) for its historical significance.
bio_id: Al-Nu'man ibn Muqarrin adalah komandan sahabat yang memimpin kemenangan di Pertempuran Nihawand.

bio_short_en: Egyptian Hanafi jurist, author of al-Bahr al-Ra'iq and the legal maxims al-Ashbah wal-Naza'ir.
bio_en: Zayn al-Din Ibrahim ibn Muhammad ibn Ibrahim ibn Nujaym al-Misri was a leading Egyptian Hanafi jurist of the Ottoman period. He is best known for two seminal works: al-Bahr al-Ra'iq fi Sharh Kanz al-Daqa'iq, a comprehensive Hanafi fiqh commentary, and al-Ashbah wal-Naza'ir, a collection of legal maxims that remains a standard reference in Islamic jurisprudence. He contributed greatly to the systematization of Hanafi legal theory.
bio_id: Ibn Nujaym adalah ahli fiqih Hanafi Mesir, penulis al-Bahr al-Ra'iq dan al-Ashbah wal-Naza'ir.

bio_short_en: Egyptian polymath who authored over 500 works spanning all Islamic sciences.
bio_en: Jalal al-Din al-Suyuti (Abd al-Rahman ibn Abi Bakr) was one of the most prolific Islamic scholars in history, authoring over 500 works on Quranic sciences, hadith, fiqh, history, linguistics, and other fields. His most famous works include al-Itqan fi Ulum al-Quran (a comprehensive encyclopedia of Quranic sciences), Tadrib al-Rawi (hadith methodology), and Tafsir al-Jalalayn (co-authored with al-Mahalli). He claimed to be a mujaddid (renewer) of Islam for his century.
bio_id: Al-Suyuti adalah ulama Mesir yang sangat produktif, menulis lebih dari 500 karya.

bio_short_en: Libyan historian known for extensive Islamic biographies and historical works.
bio_en: Ali Muhammad al-Sallabi is a contemporary Libyan Islamic historian and scholar. He is best known for his extensive biographical and historical works covering the lives of the rightly-guided caliphs, major companions, and historical figures. His multi-volume biographies of Abu Bakr, Umar, Uthman, Ali, Umar ibn Abd al-Aziz, Saladin, and others have become standard references for accessible Islamic history written from an Islamic perspective.
bio_id: Ali al-Sallabi adalah sejarawan Islam Libya yang terkenal dengan karya-karya biografi Islam.

bio_short_en: Major female hadith transmitter and one of the highest authorities for Sahih al-Bukhari.
bio_en: Karima bint Ahmad al-Marwaziyya was one of the most respected female hadith transmitters of the 5th century AH. She was a high authority for the transmission of Sahih al-Bukhari, and major scholars like al-Khatib al-Baghdadi, al-Bayhaqi, and others traveled to hear the book from her. She lived to over 100 years old and transmitted hadith with an extremely high chain (isnad ali). Her transmission of al-Bukhari is considered one of the most reliable chains.
bio_id: Karima al-Marwaziyya adalah perawi hadis perempuan terkemuka dan otoritas utama Shahih al-Bukhari.

bio_short_en: Founder of the University of al-Qarawiyyin in Fez, considered the oldest university in the world.
bio_en: Fatima bint Muhammad al-Fihri al-Qurayshiyya was a Muslim woman from Kairouan (Tunisia) who settled in Fez, Morocco. She founded the al-Qarawiyyin mosque and university in 859 CE (245 AH), which is recognized by UNESCO and the Guinness Book of Records as the oldest continuously operating degree-granting university in the world. Her founding of this institution made her one of the most significant patrons of education in Islamic history.
bio_id: Fatima al-Fihri adalah pendiri Universitas al-Qarawiyyin di Fez, universitas tertua di dunia.

bio_short_en: Chief judge under Harun al-Rashid and principal student of Abu Hanifa.
bio_en: Abu Yusuf Yaqub ibn Ibrahim al-Ansari was the chief judge (Qadi al-Qudat) under Caliph Harun al-Rashid and one of the most important students of Imam Abu Hanifa. He helped systematize and codify Hanafi jurisprudence and played a crucial role in its spread as the official legal school of the Abbasid Caliphate. His Kitab al-Kharaj (Book of Taxation) is an important early work on public finance in Islamic law.
bio_id: Abu Yusuf al-Qadi adalah hakim agung di bawah Harun al-Rashid dan murid utama Abu Hanifa.

bio_short_en: Student of Abu Hanifa and primary codifier of Hanafi fiqh in six foundational texts.
bio_en: Abu Abd Allah Muhammad ibn al-Hasan al-Shaybani was one of the most important jurists in Islamic history. A student of Abu Hanifa and then Abu Yusuf, he authored the six foundational texts of Hanafi jurisprudence. His Muwatta commentary and the Siyar al-Kabir (on international relations in Islamic law) are particularly significant. His works form the backbone of written Hanafi fiqh.
bio_id: Muhammad ibn al-Hasan al-Shaybani adalah kodifikator utama fiqih Hanafi.

bio_short_en: Persian theologian and philosopher, Hujjat al-Islam, author of Ihya Ulum al-Din.
bio_en: Abu Hamid Muhammad ibn Muhammad al-Ghazali al-Tusi, known as Hujjat al-Islam (Proof of Islam), was one of the most influential Muslim theologians, philosophers and mystics of the medieval period. He studied and taught at the Nizamiyya of Baghdad before a spiritual crisis led him to abandon his prestigious post. His most famous work, Ihya Ulum al-Din (Revival of the Religious Sciences), attempts to reconcile orthodox Islamic theology with spiritual refinement. Note: His more mystical works require contextual reading.
bio_id: Al-Ghazali, Hujjat al-Islam, adalah teolog dan filsuf Islam terkemuka, penulis Ihya Ulum al-Din.

bio_short_en: Son of Uthman dan Fodio and Sultan of Sokoto Caliphate, scholar and prolific author.
bio_en: Muhammad Bello ibn Uthman ibn Fodio was the son and successor of Uthman dan Fodio as Sultan of the Sokoto Caliphate in West Africa. He was both a political leader and a prolific Islamic scholar, authoring over 60 works on Islamic jurisprudence, governance, history, and other fields. His most famous work, Infaq al-Maysur, is an important history of the Sokoto jihad.
bio_id: Muhammad Bello adalah putra Uthman dan Fodio dan Sultan Kesultanan Sokoto.

bio_short_en: Syrian scholar and prolific author of al-Fiqh al-Islami wa Adillatuh and Tafsir al-Munir.
bio_en: Wahbah Mustafa al-Zuhayli was a contemporary Syrian Islamic scholar and one of the foremost jurists of the 20th century. He is best known for his encyclopedia of Islamic jurisprudence, al-Fiqh al-Islami wa Adillatuh, a comprehensive multi-volume work covering all areas of Islamic law with comparative analysis of all four madhabs.
bio_id: Wahbah al-Zuhayli adalah ulama Suriah kontemporer, penulis al-Fiqh al-Islami wa Adillatuh.

bio_short_en: Greatest hadith scholar in Islamic history; compiler of the most authentic book after the Quran.
bio_en: Abu Abd Allah Muhammad ibn Ismail al-Bukhari is universally regarded as the greatest hadith scholar in Islamic history. Born in Bukhara, he traveled extensively to collect hadiths, meeting over 1,000 hadith teachers. His Sahih al-Bukhari is considered by Sunni Muslims as the most authentic book after the Quran, containing approximately 7,275 hadiths selected from over 600,000 he evaluated.
bio_id: Imam al-Bukhari adalah ulama hadis terbesar dalam sejarah Islam.

bio_short_en: Second greatest hadith scholar; compiler of Sahih Muslim.
bio_en: Abu al-Husayn Muslim ibn al-Hajjaj al-Qushayri al-Nisaburi was one of the greatest hadith scholars of Islam. Born in Nishapur, he traveled widely to study hadith and became a student of Imam al-Bukhari. His Sahih Muslim is one of the two most authoritative hadith collections, containing approximately 3,033 hadiths selected with rigorous criteria.
bio_id: Imam Muslim adalah ulama hadis terbesar kedua dalam Islam, penyusun Sahih Muslim.

bio_short_en: Syrian Tabi'i scholar who advised the Umayyad caliphs and helped build the Dome of the Rock.
bio_en: Raja' ibn Haywa al-Kindi was a prominent Syrian scholar of the Tabi'een generation. He was a trusted advisor to several Umayyad caliphs. He played an important role in the construction of the Dome of the Rock in Jerusalem and was known for his piety and scholarly wisdom.
bio_id: Raja' ibn Haywa adalah ulama tabi'in dari Suriah yang memberikan nasihat kepada khalifah Umayyah.

bio_short_en: Leading hadith scholar of Egypt in the Tabi'een generation.
bio_en: Yazid ibn Abi Habib al-Azdi was the foremost hadith scholar of Egypt in the Tabi'een generation. He transmitted from many companions and major Tabi'een and was the teacher of the next generation of Egyptian scholars including al-Layth ibn Sa'd. He was appointed as an Islamic judge in Egypt.
bio_id: Yazid ibn Abi Habib adalah ulama hadis terkemuka Mesir dari generasi tabi'in.

bio_short_en: Primary Egyptian student of Malik whose transmissions form the core of the Maliki school.
bio_en: Abd al-Rahman ibn al-Qasim al-Utqi al-Masri was the most important Egyptian student of Imam Malik and the primary transmitter of the Maliki school in North Africa. He sat in Malik's circle for twenty years and his transmissions of Malik's legal opinions (in the Mudawwana) became the backbone of Maliki jurisprudence.
bio_id: Ibn al-Qasim al-Maliki adalah murid utama Imam Malik dari Mesir.

bio_short_en: Tunisian Maliki jurist who compiled al-Mudawwana, the foundational text of the Maliki school.
bio_en: Abd al-Salam ibn Sa'id ibn Habib al-Tanukhi, known as Sahnun, was the foremost jurist of Kairouan (Tunisia) and compiler of the Mudawwana al-Kubra. This work records the legal opinions of Ibn al-Qasim and became the most important reference text for Maliki jurisprudence. He is considered the chief imam of the Maliki school after Malik and Ibn al-Qasim.
bio_id: Sahnun adalah ahli fiqih Maliki Tunisia yang menyusun al-Mudawwana.

bio_short_en: Tunisian Islamic thinker and political leader, founder of the Ennahda Movement.
bio_en: Rashid al-Ghannushi is a Tunisian Islamic philosopher, political thinker, and co-founder of the Ennahda Movement. He is one of the most prominent contemporary Muslim intellectuals engaging with questions of Islam and democracy, human rights, and political participation.
bio_id: Rashid al-Ghannushi adalah pemikir Islam dan pemimpin politik Tunisia, pendiri Gerakan Ennahda.
works: Public Freedoms in the Islamic State

bio_short_en: Medieval female hadith scholar with high chains, praised by Ibn Hajar al-Asqalani.
bio_en: Zaynab bint Ahmad ibn Abd al-Rahim al-Shahriyya was a prominent medieval female hadith scholar. She was known for her high chains of transmission (isnad ali) and scholars traveled to hear hadith from her. Ibn Hajar al-Asqalani and other major scholars praised her reliability and learning.
bio_id: Zaynab bint Ahmad adalah ulama hadis perempuan abad pertengahan yang terkenal dengan sanad-sanad tingginya.

bio_short_en: Leading Maliki jurist of the Maghreb who influenced the Almoravid movement.
bio_en: Abu Imran Musa ibn Isa al-Fasi al-Ghafaqi was the foremost Maliki jurist of the Maghreb in his era. Born in Fez, he studied in Córdoba and Kairouan and eventually settled as a teacher in Kairouan. Among his students was Abd Allah ibn Yasin, who went on to found the Almoravid movement that Islamized West Africa.
bio_id: Abu Imran al-Fasi adalah ahli fiqih Maliki terkemuka dari Maghreb.

bio_short_en: Pakistani Islamic scholar and philosopher who developed neo-modernist approaches to Islamic thought.
bio_en: Fazlur Rahman Malik was a Pakistani Islamic philosopher and scholar who taught at the University of Chicago. He developed contextual and historical interpretation approaches to Quran and hadith. His works have been influential in academic Islamic studies, though controversial among traditionalist scholars.
bio_id: Fazlur Rahman adalah filsuf Islam Pakistan yang mengembangkan pendekatan neo-modernis.
